title: Add the Envelope Status Component to a Salesforce Layout
heading_path: Add the Envelope Status Component to a Salesforce Layout
text:
Learn to add the Docusign Envelope Status component to a Salesforce layout.

Docusign  Envelope Status is a component that you can add to your Salesforce layouts. The component displays the progress of all Docusign envelopes sent from a record. The component displays envelopes sent using the following methods:

- A Send with Docusign action
- An Envelope Template action
- [A Start with Docusign workflow button](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=tskcae26f13-f2e5-4afd-85a7-145a70b12977.html&_LANG=enus)
- [An Agreement Desk for Salesforce request](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=tsk151c1542-bdf4-4c8e-ba20-37affb7adc7e.html&_LANG=enus)
- [eSignature actions in Salesforce Flows](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=dzi1705696883404.html&_LANG=enus)

Salesforce users with Docusign access can view and act on envelopes without logging on to Docusign.

Tip: This procedure is explained in this [short how-to video](https://docusign.gallery.video/watch?videoId=6250567558001).

Use this procedure to add the Docusign Envelope Status component to a Salesforce layout:

1. As a Salesforce administrator, open a record, select the gear icon ![A screenshot displays the a gear icon with a lightning bolt inside.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU0MjIsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.c26VXFODx6Cd5R6ZtctohUEUqOkP4W2O0NXTBFlRbJg&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the a gear icon with a lightning bolt inside.") and choose Edit Page from the dropdown menu.
2. Drag the Docusign Envelope Status component to the page layout on the right.

   ![A screen capture displays a cursor dragging the Docusign Envelope Status component to an Opportunity layout and saving the change.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Add-the-DocuSign-Envelope-Status-component.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU0MjIsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.c26VXFODx6Cd5R6ZtctohUEUqOkP4W2O0NXTBFlRbJg&_LANG=enus "A screen capture displays a cursor dragging the Docusign Envelope Status component to an Opportunity layout and saving the change.")
3. Activate the change to your layout:
   1. Select Activation.
   2. Select a tab to set your default options: Org Default, App Default, or App, Record Type, and Profile.
   3. Select Assign and choose options from the dialog box.
   4. Select Close.
4. Select Save.
5. Select the back arrow ![A screenshot displays the Salesforce Edit Page Back arrow icon.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/A-screenshot-of-the-Back-arrow-on-the-Salesforce-Edit-Page-settings-bar.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU0MjIsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.c26VXFODx6Cd5R6ZtctohUEUqOkP4W2O0NXTBFlRbJg&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Salesforce Edit Page Back arrow icon.") to return to the record.

You have completed the process. You have added the Docusign Envelope Status component to the layout. Users with Docusign access can view and act on Docusign envelopes.

title: Configure a Start Docusign Workflow Step in Salesforce Flow
heading_path: Configure a Start Docusign Workflow Step in Salesforce Flow
text:
Learn to configure a Salesforce Flow to start a Docusign workflow.

Before you begin
:   - Purchase a Docusign Intelligent Agreement
      Management (IAM) plan.
    - Install Docusign Apps Launcher version 7.4 or
      higher.
    - [Prepare Your Salesforce Users to Start Docusign Workflows](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=bmk1744912007661.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to prepare your Salesforce users so that they can start Docusign workflows from Salesforce.").
    - [Build a workflow to be
      started by your Salesforce flow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=ovh1741282680990.html&_LANG=enus).

Important: Assign [Workflow Builder permissions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gbb1696973048215.html&_LANG=enus) to all Docusign
users (not administrators or senders) who must start workflows.

You can link Salesforce flows to Docusign
workflows by adding a Start Docusign
workflow step to your flow.

When a flow starts, Salesforce starts your linked workflow. Linking flows with
workflows builds an automation chain that creates agreements from Salesforce and Docusign data.

Use this procedure to add a **Start Docusign workflow** step to your Salesforce
flow:

1. Open a Salesforce flow and add a **Start Docusign workflow** step:
   1. Locate the point in your flow where you want to add your action.
   2. Hover over the circle icon and select the plus +
      icon that displays.![A screen capture displays the Add Element plus icon in a Salesforce Flow.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/A-screen-capture-displays-the-Add-Element-plus-icon-in-a-Salesforce-Flow.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU2MDQsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.14V1AnEWpQFKEUzLK9ifIHQq3btIpAEB_aQ826kxE0Q&_LANG=enus "A screen capture displays the Add Element plus icon in a Salesforce Flow.")
   3. Choose Action from the Add
      Element dialog box.
   4. Search: Enter and select `Start Docusign Workflow`.
2. Configure a Start Docusign workflow action:
   1. Label: Enter `Start Docusign
      workflow`.
   2. API Name: Keep or change the default name.
   3. Instance Name: Enter a string value for the name
      of the workflow instance.
3. Workflow ID: Add the workflow ID.
   1. As a Docusign administrator, go to Agreements > Workflows and select your workflow name.
   2. Copy the alpha-numeric workflow ID at the end of the URL. Do not copy
      the slash mark. ![A screenshot displays the end of a Docusign workflow URL highlighted in a green box.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-end-of-a-Workflow-Builder-workflow-URL-highlighted-in-a-green-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU2MDQsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.14V1AnEWpQFKEUzLK9ifIHQq3btIpAEB_aQ826kxE0Q&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the end of a Docusign workflow URL highlighted in a green box.")
   3. Return to your flow action and paste the ID (without slash mark) in the
      Workflow ID field.
4. Add workflow variables to your action:
   1. Select the switch next to a workflow variable that you want to include
      in your action.

      The switch displays as blue and says
      included.
   2. Enter and select a variable name in the field.
   3. Repeat this process for up to ten variables. Each variable name must
      exactly match the variable name in your workflow.
5. Select Save >  Activate.

   A message displays: Your flow was
   activated.

title: Configure a Start Docusign Workflow Step in Salesforce Flow
heading_path: Configure a Start Docusign Workflow Step in Salesforce Flow
text:
at this process for up to ten variables. Each variable name must
      exactly match the variable name in your workflow.
5. Select Save >  Activate.

   A message displays: Your flow was
   activated.

You have completed the process. You added a Start Docusign workflow
action to your Salesforce Flow. When your flow starts, it starts your linked
workflow.

Note: Users can only see whether the workflow
instance has failed in the Docusign logs.

Users will
not be able to [monitor the workflow instance process](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=vcp1698707326210.html&_LANG=enus). To do so, the Docusign administrator can go to Docusign’s platform to see the progress of the workflow
instance.

title: Learn About Using Agreement Manager in Salesforce
heading_path: Learn About Using Agreement Manager in Salesforce
text:
Learn about using Docusign Agreement Manager to manage your Docusign agreements in Salesforce.

Important: This feature is only available to IAM for Sales
customers. Contact [Docusign support](https://support.docusign.com/s/?language=en_US&_gl=1*74elk4*_gcl_au*MTY5ODQyMjA2MC4xNzY5NjM1OTA1LjE5ODM3MTQzNjguMTc2OTYzNjIwNC4xNzY5NjM2MjA0) or [Docusign sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales)
for more information.

Agreement Manager is a smart repository for your Docusign agreements. You can use Agreement Manager to
store, manage, and analyze your agreements.

More than just document storage, Agreement Manager is powered by [Docusign Iris](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fzd1707173174972&topicId=usy1712258503642.html&_LANG=enus), an AI tool that identifies and stores key attributes of your agreements. Agreement Manager makes it easy for users to find agreements, access vital information quickly, and gain valuable insights. Administrators can configure Agreement Manager to limit user access to agreements, ensuring that users can only access the agreements they need.

title: Learn About Using Agreement Manager in Salesforce
heading_path: Learn About Using Agreement Manager in Salesforce
Using Agreement Manager in Salesforce
text:
To use Agreement Manager in Salesforce, [add the
Docusign Completed Agreements component to your layouts](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pwq1747797355782&topicId=cmv1746652068556.html&_LANG=enus). This component displays your Docusign agreements and the controls to filter, search, and act on
them:

![A Salesforce opportunity displays an Agreements tab with a component titled Completed, with a search field and file names.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/A-Salesforce-opportunity-displays-a--Agreements-tab-with-a-component-titled-completed-with-a-search-field-and-file-names.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU3MjUsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.ppz295XDe-PvxgMlyY5X6sW-zdP8SP4kkH4XsXB6yTM&_LANG=enus "A Salesforce opportunity displays an Agreements tab with a component titled Completed, with a search field and file names.")

title: Learn About Using Agreement Manager in Salesforce
heading_path: Learn About Using Agreement Manager in Salesforce
Key features and benefits
text:
You can use the Docusign Agreements component to perform the following actions:

- [Search](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=zkc1748382358300.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to search for agreements in Agreement Manager for Salesforce."): Use natural language,
  attributes, or keywords to find agreements in your connected Docusign account.
- [Filter](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=wxi1748885548784.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to filter the agreements that display in Agreement Manager for Salesforce."): Use data points from your
  agreements like parties and expiration dates to narrow your search.
- [Customize Column View](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=rhn1748890283751.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to configure the agreement columns displayed in Agreement Manager for Salesforce."): Select the
  columns displayed for Agreement Manager, like Parties, Agreement
  Type, Effective Date, and so on.
- [Open](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=zkc1748382358300_1.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to open agreements from Agreement Manager for Salesforce."): Open a Docusign agreement from a Salesforce record.
- [Notify](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=tskb7ce9229-f39d-41f2-8d02-114ac23d92aa.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to set notifications for agreements displayed in the Docusign Completed Agreements component."): Turn on notifications
  for the agreements displayed in the ​Docusign​ Completed
  Agreements component and control the email notifications that you receive.

You can accomplish these tasks directly from Salesforce records, without logging on to your Docusign account.

Tip: Not all Agreement Manager functions are available in Salesforce yet. [Select this link to see the full list of Agreement Manager's features](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=ycv1702944444424.html&_LANG=enus).

title: Learn About Using Agreement Manager in Salesforce
heading_path: Learn About Using Agreement Manager in Salesforce
Prerequisites for using Agreement Manager in Salesforce
text:
Before you can use Agreement Manager in your Salesforce organization, you must complete the following tasks:

- [Purchase an Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) for Sales application](https://www.docusign.com/intelligent-agreement-management?utm_source=bing&utm_medium=cpc&utm_campaign=GBL_XX_PRF_UPS_2410_SEMIAM&utm_term=docusign%20iam&campaignid=569731866&adgroupid=1178678316174006&location=84406&device=c&matchtype=e&extensionid=&creative=73667602015411&keyword=docusign%20iam&placement=&network=o&msclkid=3287ee64f2c01f69bcd686665f40e7da).
- [Install Apps
  Launcher version 8.4 or higher](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=ogj1586134254571.html&_LANG=enus).

  Important: If you are already using Agreement Manager for Salesforce,
  upgrading to Docusign Apps Launcher v8.4 breaks your
  connection. After upgrading v8.4 to get the latest features, you must use
  the following controls to re-establish your connection to Docusign.
- [Grant Docusign for Salesforce access to your Salesforce users](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=dzl1586134206658.html&_LANG=enus).
- [Grant access to your Docusign for Salesforce users](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=eut1712157553977.html&_LANG=enus).

title: Learn About Using Agreement Manager in Salesforce
heading_path: Learn About Using Agreement Manager in Salesforce
What Agreement Manager displays
text:
Note: Because Docusign uses AI to extract key information from your agreements, completed envelopes take some time to display in Agreement Manager.

- The Docusign Completed Agreements component displays agreements sent from the
  following applications:
  - eSignature for Salesforce
  - CLM for Salesforce
  - Agreement Desk for Salesforce
  - Workflow builder for Salesforce
- The component displays agreements sent both before and after you add the component to your layouts.
- The component displays agreements sent from the source record and its child records. It does not display agreements from parent objects.
- Account records display agreements from the account and all of its child objects.

title: Learn About Using Agreement Manager in Salesforce
heading_path: Learn About Using Agreement Manager in Salesforce
Agreement Manager file security
text:
Docusign security settings control who can view agreements in the
Docusign Completed Agreements component.

Example: A Salesforce user has no access to an agreement named
Fontara NDA in the connected Docusign
account. The user opens a Fontara record. The Docusign Completed
Agreements component does not display the Fontara NDA
agreement.

title: Learn About Using Agreement Manager in Salesforce
heading_path: Learn About Using Agreement Manager in Salesforce
Examples of what Agreement Manager displays
text:
Example 1: A user sends agreements from an account named
Fontara. The user also sends agreements from the Fontara account's opportunity
record. On the Fontara account record, the Docusign Completed
Agreements component displays agreements from the account and opportunity.

Example 2: A user sends agreements from a Fontara contact. They
also send agreements from the related Fontara account. On the Fontara opportunity
record, the Docusign Completed Agreements component displays
agreements from the opportunity, but not the account.

title: Prepare Your Salesforce Users to Start Docusign Workflows
heading_path: Prepare Your Salesforce Users to Start Docusign Workflows
text:
Learn to prepare your Salesforce users so that they can start Docusign workflows from Salesforce.

Important: Do not follow these steps if you already have
Docusign Apps Launcher v7.4 or higher installed. Only follow
these steps to add a Start
Docusign Workflow  action to an organization with v7.3 or
lower installed.

Before you begin
:   - Purchase a Docusign Intelligent Agreement
      Management (IAM) plan.
    - Install Docusign Apps Launcher version 7.4 or
      higher.
    - [Build a workflow to be
      started by your Salesforce flow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=ovh1741282680990.html&_LANG=enus).

Before standard Salesforce users can start Docusign workflows, you
must assign the necessary permissions. Use this procedure to assign the necessary
Docusign and Salesforce user permissions:

1. Log on to your Docusign account as an administrator and go to Admin >  Permission Profiles.
2. Copy the DS Sender profile:
   1. DS Sender: Select Actions and
      choose Copy from the dropdown menu.

      The Copy Permission Profile dialog box opens.
   2. Name: Enter Workflow Builder DS
      Sender.
   3. Select the User Permissions tab.
   4. Scroll to Workflows and choose
      Use from the dropdown menu.
   5. Select Add.

   The Copy Permission Profile dialog box closes.
3. Add users to the new Workflow Builder DS Sender profile. Only add
   users that must start Workflow Builder workflows from Salesforce:
   1. Go to Admin >  Users.

      The Users page opens.
   2. Select Actions next to the user that you want to
      add and choose Edit from the dropdown menu.
   3. Permission Profile: Choose Workflow Builder DS
      Sender from the dropdown menu.
   4. Select Save.

   The user's page closes.
4. Assign Flow User permissions to Salesforce users who must
   start Docusign workflows:
   1. Log on to your Salesforce organization as an administrator, select the
      gear icon ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU1OTgsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.joOXXzzsq9oyEWOZ_Bl-XACbB3o9ntfdqCHn0x529Zw&_LANG=enus), and
      choose Setup from the dropdown menu.
   2. Enter and select Users in the Quick Find
      field.
   3. Select Edit next to a user.

      The User Edit page opens.
   4. Select the Flow User check box and select
      Save.

      ![A screenshot displays the Flow User check box selected and the Save button highlighted on the User Edit page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-Flow-User-check-box-selected-and-the-Save-button-highlighted-on-the-User-Edit-page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU1OTgsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.joOXXzzsq9oyEWOZ_Bl-XACbB3o9ntfdqCHn0x529Zw&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Flow User check box selected and the Save button highlighted on the User Edit page.")

   The User Edit page closes. Users must [re-activate their Docusign access in Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=vbw1648698462634&topicId=ymi1586134213877.html&_LANG=enus). After re-activating, users
   can start Docusign workflows.

You have completed the process. You have assigned the necessary Docusign and Salesforce permissions. After re-authorizing their Docusign users, these users can start Docusign
workflows from Salesforce.

title: Use Workflow Builder with Salesforce
heading_path: Use Workflow Builder with Salesforce
text:
Learn to build Docusign workflows that update records in
your Salesforce organization.

title: Use Workflow Builder with Salesforce
heading_path: Use Workflow Builder with Salesforce
Introduction
text:
With [Workflow Builder](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=dnx1696972415150.html&_LANG=enus), you can connect Docusign workflow steps like Web Forms and eSignature templates to
third-party extensions. You can create workflows to automate your agreement process,
including reading data from and writing data to Salesforce records.

A typical agreement process consists of numerous steps to complete before and after
signing. Some of these steps can be addressed today by integrating multiple Docusign products, usually with expert assistance and developer
resources.

Workflow Builder offers a platform that integrates Docusign products with third-party apps like Salesforce. By
building workflows, you can digitize your
business processes without writing code.

You can configure the following workflow steps to integrate Salesforce and Docusign in your agreement process:

- Writeback to Salesforce: Update Salesforce records with
  data from your workflow.
- Read from Salesforce: Import data from Salesforce
  records to your workflow.
- Store Files in Salesforce: Use variables to name
  completed agreements and route them to the appropriate records.
- Use Salesforce Files: Add files from Salesforce records
  to workflows.

title: Use Workflow Builder with Salesforce
heading_path: Use Workflow Builder with Salesforce
Developer resources
text:
The following resources are available for developers who are part of the API beta
program to build workflows and extension apps. If you'd like to join the beta
program, visit the [Docusign Developer beta trial page](https://www.docusign.com/lp/developer-betas) to apply.

[Workflow Builder API](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/maestro-api/)
:   Create and manage workflows using the Workflow Builder
    API.

[Extension Apps](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/)
:   Build custom third-party functionality into Docusign and
    publish your solutions in the App Center.

title: A Broken Page Icon Displays on Salesforce Records instead of the Docusign Completed Agreements Component
heading_path: A Broken Page Icon Displays on Salesforce Records instead of the Docusign Completed Agreements Component
text:
Learn to fix the issue of the Docusign Completed Agreements
component displaying a broken page icon instead of
Agreement Manager agreements.

Important: This feature is only available to IAM for
Sales customers. Contact [Docusign support](https://support.docusign.com/s/?language=en_US&_gl=1*74elk4*_gcl_au*MTY5ODQyMjA2MC4xNzY5NjM1OTA1LjE5ODM3MTQzNjguMTc2OTYzNjIwNC4xNzY5NjM2MjA0) or [Docusign sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales)
for more information.

A section with a broken page icon ![A page with a frowning face icon displays.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/A-page-with-a-frowning-face-displays.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU3NTksInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.159kz8YqOfCdrikdBDQNS-IAJOtqnkYmF-G12ANfQDE&_LANG=enus "A page with a frowning face icon displays.") displays on Salesforce records instead of the Docusign
Completed Agreements component. I cannot see my
Agreement Manager agreements on the record.

title: A Broken Page Icon Displays on Salesforce Records instead of the Docusign Completed Agreements Component
heading_path: A Broken Page Icon Displays on Salesforce Records instead of the Docusign Completed Agreements Component
Resolution: Refresh Your Browser Page
text:
Cause: This issue occurs intermittently, often when users go to another
window and return to the Salesforce page.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

Choose an option to refresh the Docusign Agreements
component on your Salesforce record:

- Refresh your browser page.
- Right-click the component and select Reload
  Frame.

You have completed the process. The broken page icon disappears and
the Docusign Completed Agreements component displays,
providing access to
Agreement Manager. You can use the Agreement Manager component to view and act on your Docusign agreements in without leaving Salesforce.

title: Add the Docusign Completed Agreements Component to a Salesforce Layout
heading_path: Add the Docusign Completed Agreements Component to a Salesforce Layout
Before you begin
text:
Important: This feature is only available to IAM for
Sales customers. Contact [Docusign support](https://support.docusign.com/s/?language=en_US&_gl=1*74elk4*_gcl_au*MTY5ODQyMjA2MC4xNzY5NjM1OTA1LjE5ODM3MTQzNjguMTc2OTYzNjIwNC4xNzY5NjM2MjA0) or [Docusign
sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales) for more information.

- [Purchase an Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) for Sales application](https://www.docusign.com/intelligent-agreement-management?utm_source=bing&utm_medium=cpc&utm_campaign=GBL_XX_PRF_UPS_2410_SEMIAM&utm_term=docusign%20iam&campaignid=569731866&adgroupid=1178678316174006&location=84406&device=c&matchtype=e&extensionid=&creative=73667602015411&keyword=docusign%20iam&placement=&network=o&msclkid=3287ee64f2c01f69bcd686665f40e7da).
- [Install Apps
  Launcher version 8.4 or higher](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=ogj1586134254571.html&_LANG=enus).
- [Prepare Your Salesforce Organization to Use Agreement Manager](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=vry1747859095666.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to prepare your Salesforce organization to use Docusign Agreement Manager.").

[Agreement Manager](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=adf1702945446135.html&_LANG=enus) is part of the Docusign IAM platform, powered by [Docusign
Iris](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fzd1707173174972&topicId=usy1712258503642.html). You can use Agreement Manager to store, manage, and analyze your agreements.

In Salesforce, you access Agreement Manager by adding the Docusign Agreements component to your layouts. Approved users can interact with Docusign agreements on your records without leaving Salesforce.

Use this procedure to add the Docusign Agreements component to a layout:

1. As a Salesforce administrator, open a record, select the gear icon, ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU0MjYsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.Ksb2y_--5z8Xori6-D7tWy_-UP1mJO1pyiBf3y4sS4I&_LANG=enus) and choose Edit Page from the dropdown menu.

   Note: You can add the Docusign Completed
   Agreements component to any part of your layout. The following instructions
   are recommendations, not requirements.
2. (Optional) Add a Docusign Agreements tab to your layout.
   1. Select Add Tab on the right navigation panel.

      A new Details tab displays.
   2. Select Details.

      The Tab Label dialog box opens.
   3. Tab Label: Select the Details dropdown menu and choose Custom.
   4. Custom Label: Enter a descriptive name, like Docusign Agreements.
   5. Select Done.

   ![A Salesforce layout displays a new tab with Custom as the type and Docusign Agreements as the custom label.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/A-Salesforce-layout-displays-a-new-tab-with-Custom-as-the-type-and-Docusign-Agreements-as-the-custom-label.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU0MjYsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.Ksb2y_--5z8Xori6-D7tWy_-UP1mJO1pyiBf3y4sS4I&_LANG=enus "A Salesforce layout displays a new tab with Custom as the type and Docusign Agreements as the custom label.")

   Your new tab displays in the right navigation panel.
3. Drag the Docusign Agreements component to the tab that you created and select Save.

5. Select the back arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/A-screenshot-of-the-Back-arrow-on-the-Salesforce-Edit-Page-settings-bar.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU0MjYsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.Ksb2y_--5z8Xori6-D7tWy_-UP1mJO1pyiBf3y4sS4I&_LANG=enus) to return to the record.

You have completed the process. You have added the Docusign Agreements component to a Salesforce layout. Users can interact with Agreement Manager content on Salesforce records.

title: Learn About Using Docusign Agreement Desk in Salesforce
heading_path: Learn About Using Docusign Agreement Desk in Salesforce
text:
Learn about using Docusign Agreement Desk in Salesforce to streamline your agreement process.

Important: This feature is only available to IAM for Sales
customers. Contact [Docusign support](https://support.docusign.com/s/?language=en_US&_gl=1*74elk4*_gcl_au*MTY5ODQyMjA2MC4xNzY5NjM1OTA1LjE5ODM3MTQzNjguMTc2OTYzNjIwNC4xNzY5NjM2MjA0) or [Docusign sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales)
for more information.

title: Learn About Using Docusign Agreement Desk in Salesforce
heading_path: Learn About Using Docusign Agreement Desk in Salesforce
What is Agreement Desk?
text:
Agreement Desk is a centralized workspace designed to simplify and
accelerate the pre-signature phase of the agreement lifecycle. It provides a single
location for all requests related to your agreements. Part of the unified
Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) platform, it centralizes request submission,
helps with negotiation, and streamlines approvals.

Through AI-Assisted Review, Workflow Builder workflow automation, and reusable fields that can be used
across products, Agreement Desk speeds up routine tasks.

Agreement Desk creates a central place to manage your agreement requests. You can use this space
to manage and collaborate on requests. When reviewers complete requests, Agreement
Desk helps send agreements for signature and even trigger other business
processes.

You can use Agreement Desk to accomplish the following tasks:

- Submit and manage requests.
- Negotiate and edit in-process agreements.
- Use AI to automate sales, procurement, and business contracts.

title: Learn About Using Docusign Agreement Desk in Salesforce
heading_path: Learn About Using Docusign Agreement Desk in Salesforce
Why use Agreement Desk in Salesforce?
text:
Many Salesforce users prefer to act on their agreements entirely from Salesforce.
When you add the Docusign Agreement Requests component to your
layouts, users can search for, sort, filter, and open requests without logging on to
Docusign.

Agreement Desk only displays requests created from the record that you are viewing and its
related objects. For example, when viewing the Acme record, Agreement Desk only
displays requests sent from Acme and its related records.

Agreement Desk is most helpful for the following use cases:

title: Learn About Using Docusign Agreement Desk in Salesforce
heading_path: Learn About Using Docusign Agreement Desk in Salesforce
Using Agreement Desk in Salesforce
text:
To use Agreement Desk in Salesforce, add the Docusign Agreement
Desk component to your layouts. The component displays your requests and the
controls to filter, search, and act on them:
![A Salesforce account displays an Agreement Desk tab with a component titled Requests with a search field and file names.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Agreement_Desk_for_Salesforce_component_on_record.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU2NjEsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.tjmDVd1cPBlLxQiWkcKTi7rmmc2p1HkFHKDmI2W_nQI&_LANG=enus "A Salesforce account displays an Agreement Desk tab with a component titled Requests with a search field and file names.")

title: Learn About Using Docusign Agreement Desk in Salesforce
heading_path: Learn About Using Docusign Agreement Desk in Salesforce
Key features and benefits
text:
You can use the Docusign Agreement Desk component to perform the following
actions:

- [Search and
  filter](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=tsk79fb118f-2fa3-4e60-965a-efb9edd7c2c8.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to search and filter the requests that display in the Docusign Agreement Requests component."): Find request titles from your connected Docusign account. Use data points from your requests like
  *submitter* and *request type* to narrow your search.
- [Open
  requests](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=tsk151c1542-bdf4-4c8e-ba20-37affb7adc7e.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to open a request from the Agreement Requests component to view or act on."): Select requests in the Docusign
  Agreement Requests component to open and act on them in your Docusign account.
- Monitor request progress: Track the progress of your requests as they move
  from initiation to completion.
- Column sort: Arrange your requests by categories like
  Title, Request Type,
  Owner, and so on.

You can accomplish these tasks directly from Salesforce records, without logging on to your Docusign account.

Tip: Not all Agreement Desk functions are available in Salesforce yet. Select this link to [see the full list of Agreement Desk features](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=msb1736279079453&topicId=oan1736279133237.html&_LANG=enus).

title: Getting Started With Docusign IAM for Sales: Salesforce
heading_path: Getting Started With Docusign IAM for Sales: Salesforce
text:
Learn to prepare your Salesforce organization for a Docusign IAM
for Sales implementation.

Important: This feature is only available to IAM for
Sales customers. Contact [Docusign support](https://support.docusign.com/s/?language=en_US&_gl=1*74elk4*_gcl_au*MTY5ODQyMjA2MC4xNzY5NjM1OTA1LjE5ODM3MTQzNjguMTc2OTYzNjIwNC4xNzY5NjM2MjA0) or [Docusign
sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales) for more information.

Note:

Docusign has a number of products that you can use to create an
IAM for Sales experience:

Apps Launcher for Salesforce
:   Users can generate and send contracts for review, approval, and
    signature.

Workflow Builder for Salesforce
:   Users can select the action to start Workflow Builder workflows from records.

Agreement Manager for Salesforce
:   Users can view and manage Docusign requests from
    the component.

Agreement Desk for Salesforce
:   Users can view and act on Docusign agreements from
    the component.

You must prepare your Salesforce organization before using these products
together:

- [Install Docusign Apps Launcher for
  Salesforce v8.4 or higher](https://login.salesforce.com/packaging/installPackage.apexp?p0=04tKg000000D3Vk).
- [Install the Salesforce
  application from the Docusign Setup Page](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=tsk91549a3b-f7f3-4b74-8e87-33ff33d198c0.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to install the Salesforce app in your Docusign account and connect it to your Salesforce organization.").
- [Install pre-built
  workflows, templates, and fields with the IAM for Sales Solution](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=tska61755e7-f8ba-4cfe-aad8-f02478b02dab.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to use the IAM for Sales Solution to install pre-built Docusign workflows that you can use in Salesforce.").
- [Add a Start with Docusign action to a layout](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=tsk32e505bf-436a-47b1-b7f6-55e6ad614ec5.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to build a new Lightning web component action to start a Docusign workflow from a record.").
- [Add the Envelope
  Status component to a Salesforce layout](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=&qwr1706590641805&topicId=all1586134265721.html&_LANG=enus)
- [Add the Docusign Completed Agreements component to your
  layouts](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=cmv1746652068556.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to add the Docusign Completed Agreements component to a Salesforce layout.").
- [Add the Docusign Agreement Requests component to your
  layouts](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=tsk7c6f3058-1654-462b-b850-8d0dc79ba199.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to add the Docusign Agreement Requests component to a Salesforce page layout.").
- [Create a Connect configuration to add Salesforce merge fields to a workflow
  step](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=xza1742958339120.html&_LANG=enus).

title: Workflow Builder for Salesforce Advanced Features

text:

With [Workflow Builder](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=dnx1696972415150.html&_LANG=enus), you can connect Docusign workflow steps like Web Forms and eSignature templates to
third-party extensions. You can create workflows to automate your agreement process,
including reading data from and writing data to Salesforce records.

After installing and configuring the IAM for Salesforce app, you can use advanced features to

  app
- Reconnect a Salesforce organization to the Salesforce app
- Refresh the connection to your Salesforce app
- Duplicate a workflow
- Export and import a workflow
- Delete the connection to a Salesforce organization from the Salesforce
  app

heading_path: Learn About the Docusign IAM for Sales Solution

Learn about using the IAM for Sales Solution to install pre-built Docusign workflows and templates in your
Salesforce organization.

You can install the Docusign IAM for Sales Solution in your Salesforce
organization. The solution installs a series of
pre-built Docusign resources in
your Salesforce organization. These resources
quickly create the building blocks for common IAM
for Sales use cases, like creating and sending an
NDA or MSA.

title: Docusign Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) for Sales: Salesforce
heading_path: Docusign Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) for Sales: Salesforce
What is Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) for Sales?
text:
You can use Docusign IAM for Sales to run the entire contract process for your sales and legal teams. With Docusign IAM
for Sales, you can generate, send, manage, track, and store agreements, all without
leaving Salesforce.

IAM for Sales can manage everything from simple
template-based NDA generation to complex negotiated contract workflows.

title: Docusign Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) for Sales: Salesforce
heading_path: Docusign Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) for Sales: Salesforce
IAM for Sales prerequisites
text:
To create IAM solutions for Salesforce, complete the following tasks:

- Purchase a [Docusign Intelligent Agreement
  Management (IAM) for Sales](https://www.docusign.com/intelligent-agreement-management?utm_source=bing&utm_medium=cpc&utm_campaign=GBL_XX_PRF_UPS_2410_SEMIAM&utm_term=docusign%20iam&campaignid=569731866&adgroupid=1178678316174006&location=84406&device=c&matchtype=e&extensionid=&creative=73667602015411&keyword=docusign%20iam&placement=&network=o&msclkid=3287ee64f2c01f69bcd686665f40e7da) license. This license includes Docusign eSignature, Workflow Builder, Agreement Prep,
  Agreement Desk, Agreement Manager, and the Docusign for
  Salesforce connector.
- [Install the Salesforce app from the Docusign App
  Center](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=jxo1706295810553.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to install the Salesforce app in your Docusign account and connect it to your Salesforce organization.").
- Create a workflow containing Salesforce steps.
- Install Docusign Apps Launcher for Salesforce v8.4
  or higher in your [Salesforce production organization](https://login.salesforce.com/packaging/installPackage.apexp?p0=04tKg000000D3Vk) or [Salesforce sandbox organization](https://test.salesforce.com/packaging/installPackage.apexp?p0=04tKg000000D3Vk).
- [Add a Start with Docusign action to a
  layout](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=tsk32e505bf-436a-47b1-b7f6-55e6ad614ec5.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to build a new Lightning web component action to start a Docusign workflow from a record.").

title: Docusign Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) for Sales: Salesforce
heading_path: Docusign Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) for Sales: Salesforce
How do I use IAM in Salesforce?
text:
Docusign has a number of products that you can use to create an IAM
for Sales experience in Salesforce:

- [Apps Launcher for Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=jgh1637077647417.html&_LANG=enus)
- [Workflow Builder for Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=bqi1705961132077.html&_LANG=enus)
- [Agreement Manager for Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pwq1747797355782&topicId=bky1747764447701.html&_LANG=enus)
- [Agreement Desk for Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=con007504df-80b7-41f7-aa5f-1daa72bcc257.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about using Docusign Agreement Desk in Salesforce to streamline your agreement process.")

You can use these products separately, or you can use them together to provide an
integrated system that manages agreements from creation to completion.

Table 1. IAM for Sales Features and Benefits

| Feature | Benefit | IAM for Sales Exclusive? |
| --- | --- | --- |
| CRM-Embedded User Experience | Agreement tools for all phases of the agreement process. Embedded or accessible in CRM (Generation, Agreement Desk, eSignature, Agreement Manager.) | YES |
| CRM AI Agent Integration | Interactive Agreement status, search, and Q&A from Salesforce, Agentforce, or Microsoft 365 Copilot. | YES |
| Agreement Desk | Communication, context, transparency, and status for sales, legal, and other teams negotiating and approving B2B Sales Agreement. | NO |
| Document Generation & Agreement Template Builder | Customized contract generation using CRM data for sales agreements, made accessible through an intuitive, drag-and-drop template setup. | NO |
| eSignature | Legally binding agreements that are automated using CRM user, and customer data. | NO |
| Workflow Builder | Automation and acceleration of otherwise manual agreement processes. | NO |
| Agreement Manager | When embedded in CRM pages, contextually accurate access to all agreements relevant to an account, opportunity, quote, or contact. | Partial (Not available in CX) |
| CRM Data Connector (App Center Extension App) | Data and file read and write supporting all IAM products and services. | Partial (Also available in CX) |

title: Docusign Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) for Sales: Salesforce
heading_path: Docusign Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) for Sales: Salesforce
IAM for Sales key use cases
text:
General Legal Request
:   Create requests that require legal team action. These are often procurement contracts sent from the buyer, contracting
    requests, or questions that require legal work.

Negotiated Interactive Sales Order
:   Create sales contract templates that include an MSA or terms and conditions, along with a price quote. This scenario uses pre-built contract templates, combined with stakeholder approvals and buyer negotiation. It can all be started by a sales request from a CRM system.

Interactive Onboarding/Consent form
:   Create onboarding and consent forms containing standardized contract language. These are often used in both B2B and B2C sales scenarios. These forms are not negotiated, and are often PDFs pre-filled with customer data.

One-Click NDA
:   Create simple, standard contracts that only require legal approval if a buyer
    decides to negotiate terms. This is often the first
    agreement that is sent to a customer.

title: Docusign Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) for Sales: Salesforce
heading_path: Docusign Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) for Sales: Salesforce
Sample setup
text:
You install Docusign Apps Launcher 8.4 in your Salesforce
organization.

You add a Start with Docusign action to your layouts. Users can select the action to start workflows from records.

You add the Docusign Agreement Desk component your layouts.
Users can view and act on workflow requests from the component.

You add the Docusign Agreements component to view Docusign Agreement Manager on your layouts. Users can view and act on
completed Docusign agreements from the component.

title: Docusign Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) for Sales: Salesforce
heading_path: Docusign Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) for Sales: Salesforce
Sample IAM for Sales flow
text:
Sam Sales selects the Start with Docusign button on the Acme,
Inc. opportunity. The list of available Docusign workflows
displays. Sam selects the Start button for the
MSA workflow. The MSA web form opens, containing pre-filled
data imported from the Acme record. The pre-filled data saves Sam time and the risk
of error. Sam adds a supplemental document to the form and selects
Submit. The MSA workflow starts, sending tasks to all
workflow participants.

Sam sees the MSA request displayed in the Acme record's Agreement Desk component, with a status of Ready for Review/Signature. Sam selects the request to open it. Sam selects the Documents tab and chooses the Send via Email option. Sam enters recipient email addresses, adds a quick message, and selects Send.

Recipients complete their workflow tasks, and the next day, Sam receives a
notification that the Acme MSA is ready for review. He selects the Acme MSA request
in the Agreement Requests component and selects the Send for
signature button. The agreement opens, displaying all changes that
the recipients made. After a quick review, Sam selects the
Send button.

A representative at Acme completes the request by reviewing and signing the MSA. Docusign Agreement Manager's AI feature extracts terms from the agreement
and writes the data back to Acme's Salesforce record. The completed agreement
displays in the Acme record's Agreement Manager component. Sam can see details like the
agreement name, party, effective date, and expiration date.

title: Adding Docusign Workflow Actions to Salesforce Layouts
heading_path: Adding Docusign Workflow Actions to Salesforce Layouts
text:
Learn about adding Docusign workflow actions to Salesforce layouts
either manually, or with the IAM Launcher controls.

If you want to start Docusign workflows from Salesforce records, you
can add a Start with Docusign action to your layouts. The Start
with Docusign action displays Docusign workflows
that you can start from your records without logging on to Docusign.

You can use the following methods to add Start with Docusign actions
to your layout:

(Recommended) Add actions with IAM Launcher Setup controls
:   Choose layouts, select workflows, and add actions directly from the Docusign Setup page.
:   This is the simplest method and it works for almost all Salesforce objects.
:   Access the interface by going to Docusign Setup >  Manage Connections and the IAM Launcher >  Manage. ![A screenshot displays the Docusign Setup page with Manage Connections and the IAM Launcher highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/IAM_Launcher_Manage_Connections_and_the_IAM_Launcher_manage_button.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU0MDEsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.UOwvLG2mhVwmdTwGndSFxZ7dBxGiWBBh0Zb23CrF5sw&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Docusign Setup page with Manage Connections and the IAM Launcher highlighted.")

[Add actions with Salesforce Object Manager](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=rrf1583359212854&topicId=fji1583359148678.html&_LANG=enus)
:   Currently, you can't use IAM Launcher controls for some Salesforce objects,
    like Case and Campaign.
:   In these situations, use Object Manager to add actions to layouts.

title: Configure Salesforce Workflow Steps
heading_path: Configure Salesforce Workflow Steps
text:
Learn about the Workflow Builder steps that you can configure to update
records in your Salesforce organization.

You can configure and use the following Salesforce steps in a workflow:

title: Configure Salesforce Workflow Steps
heading_path: Configure Salesforce Workflow Steps
Writeback to Salesforce
text:
Writeback to Salesforce updates Salesforce records with data from your Docusign workflow. The Writeback to Salesforce step updates
Salesforce fields, creates new records, and more. Administrators link workflow
fields to Salesforce fields in the Writeback to Salesforce step. When recipients
enter data in workflow fields, Docusign updates the information in
the linked Salesforce record fields. Writeback improves efficiency and reduces
errors by eliminating manual data entry.

Writeback to Salesforce examples

In a typical
Writeback to Salesforce scenario, you create a Web Forms step to gather
participant information. Add a Docusign template with custom
fields linked to Salesforce, followed by a writeback
step.

Example 1: Update existing record: You
build a workflow that includes a Collect Data with Web Forms step and a
Writeback to Salesforce step. In the writeback step, you link the Salesforce
opportunity's Amount field to the web form's
Amount field. A workflow recipient updates and signs an
agreement for an existing opportunity named Fontara. The recipient updates the
Fontara agreement's Amount field from $20,000 to $25,000. When the recipient
completes the assignment, Docusign updates the Fontara
record's Amount field to $25,000.

Example 2: Create new
record: A workflow recipient enters data for a prospective
customer named Fontara. When the recipient completes the assignment, the
writeback step creates a new Fontara record in the connected Salesforce
organization. The record contains all the data entered by the recipient, like
account name, billing address, amount, and so on.

title: Configure Salesforce Workflow Steps
heading_path: Configure Salesforce Workflow Steps
Read from Salesforce
text:
Read from Salesforce imports data from Salesforce records to your Docusign workflow. Administrators link workflow fields to
Salesforce fields in the Read from Salesforce step. When task recipients open an
envelope, they see Salesforce data imported from the record. Importing Salesforce
data improves efficiency and reduces errors by eliminating manual data entry.

Read from Salesforce example

You build a workflow
with these steps: Collect Data with Web Forms,
Read from Salesforce, and Writeback to
Salesforce. In the writeback step, you link the Salesforce
opportunity Amount field to the web form's
Amount field. A workflow recipient opens an agreement
and sees the Amount field pre-filled with the opportunity's amount. If the
recipient changes the amount, Docusign updates the Amount
field on the Salesforce record.

title: Configure Salesforce Workflow Steps
heading_path: Configure Salesforce Workflow Steps
Store Files in Salesforce
text:
The Store Files in Salesforce step uses variables to name completed agreements and
route them to the appropriate records.

Store Files in Salesforce example

You build a
workflow and want Docusign to store completed agreements in
Salesforce records. You configure Store Files in Salesforce
variables that set file naming conventions and route agreements to the correct
records. When all recipients complete the signing process, Docusign saves the finished agreement to the correct
Salesforce record. The participant is not required to name the agreement or
upload it to a record.

title: Configure Salesforce Workflow Steps
heading_path: Configure Salesforce Workflow Steps
Use Salesforce Files
text:
The Use Salesforce Files step adds files from Salesforce records
to workflows. Administrators open a record's Notes & Attachments and add the
record's ID to the Use Salesforce Files step. When workflows
start, Docusign adds selected files from that record's Notes &
Attachments. You can configure the following upload settings: All
Files, File Name Contains, and
Latest Document Only.

Note: The maximum import limit for this step is 10 files or
100 MB.

Use Salesforce Files
example

Example: You add a Use
Salesforce Files step to your workflow. You use the
File Name Contains option and enter the keywords
`Contract Renewal`. Your workflow sends an agreement to
recipients. When recipients open their workflow task, they see a file named
Contract Renewal 2025 included in the agreement.

title: Learn About Creating Agreement Desk for Salesforce Reports
heading_path: Learn About Creating Agreement Desk for Salesforce Reports
text:
Learn about creating custom Salesforce reports for your Docusign
Agreement Desk requests.

Important: This feature is only available to IAM for Sales
customers. Contact [Docusign support](https://support.docusign.com/s/?language=en_US&_gl=1*74elk4*_gcl_au*MTY5ODQyMjA2MC4xNzY5NjM1OTA1LjE5ODM3MTQzNjguMTc2OTYzNjIwNC4xNzY5NjM2MjA0) or [Docusign sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales)
for more information.

title: Learn About Creating Agreement Desk for Salesforce Reports
heading_path: Learn About Creating Agreement Desk for Salesforce Reports
Agreement Desk for Salesforce report requirements
text:
Before you can run Agreement Desk for Salesforce reports, you must meet the following
requirements:

- [Purchase a Docusign Intelligent
  Agreement Management (IAM) for Sales application](https://www.docusign.com/intelligent-agreement-management?utm_source=bing&utm_medium=cpc&utm_campaign=GBL_XX_PRF_UPS_2410_SEMIAM&utm_term=docusign%20iam&campaignid=569731866&adgroupid=1178678316174006&location=84406&device=c&matchtype=e&extensionid=&creative=73667602015411&keyword=docusign%20iam&placement=&network=o&msclkid=3287ee64f2c01f69bcd686665f40e7da).
- [Create an
  Agreement Desk for Salesforce external object](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=tsk2e317e21-7fca-4946-a4eb-c766238b2cb3.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to create an Agreement Desk for Salesforce external object to turn on reporting.").
- [Create an
  Agreement Desk Report](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=tsk850b23aa-f070-4f37-b82c-17ca872c951b.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to build a custom Salesforce report that displays your Docusign Agreement Desk requests.").

title: Learn About Creating Agreement Desk for Salesforce Reports
heading_path: Learn About Creating Agreement Desk for Salesforce Reports
Agreement Desk for Salesforce report filters
text:
[Learn about the filters that you can use to build Agreement Desk for Salesforce
reports](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=ref6aa90585-4765-48ab-a307-a088fed1e479.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about the filters that you can use to build Agreement Desk for Salesforce reports.").

title: Learn About Creating Agreement Desk for Salesforce Reports
heading_path: Learn About Creating Agreement Desk for Salesforce Reports
Agreement Desk for Salesforce reports overview
text:
Salesforce reports filter specific data sets from your organization to create structured, readable insights. You can build reports that highlight specific information, such as displaying all customers with expiring contracts. Salesforce reports eliminate manual searching and automate the delivery of business-critical metrics.

You can build custom Salesforce reports to filter and display your [Docusign
Agreement Desk for Salesforce requests](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=con007504df-80b7-41f7-aa5f-1daa72bcc257.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about using Docusign Agreement Desk in Salesforce to streamline your agreement process.").

title: Learn About Creating Agreement Desk for Salesforce Reports
heading_path: Learn About Creating Agreement Desk for Salesforce Reports
Example
text:
You create a report with columns for Docusign Agreement Desk Requests:
ID and Status. You add rows for
Created Date and Owner.

You add a filter with Show Me: All Docusign Agreement Desk
requests, Created Date: Current FY, and
Record Type > equals > Opportunity.

You run the report. Salesforce displays all Opportunity-based Agreement Desk requests
from the current fiscal year.

The report also displays the request's IDs, statuses, created dates, and owners. ![Salesforce report screen with a Filter By window setting RecordType to equal Opportunity.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/DAL_Agreement_Desk_Reporting_Report_Filters_and_Results.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU3MTEsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.DvJijlhlZ2drf6_Si3CLLZRst5dZEbftOa1MdjLKrtQ&_LANG=enus "Salesforce report screen with a Filter By window setting RecordType to equal Opportunity.")

title: Learn About Starting Docusign Workflows from Salesforce
heading_path: Learn About Starting Docusign Workflows from Salesforce
text:
Learn about using the Docusign IAM Launcher component or a custom
button to start Docusign
workflows from Salesforce records.

Important: This feature is only available to IAM for Sales
customers. Contact [Docusign support](https://support.docusign.com/s/?language=en_US&_gl=1*74elk4*_gcl_au*MTY5ODQyMjA2MC4xNzY5NjM1OTA1LjE5ODM3MTQzNjguMTc2OTYzNjIwNC4xNzY5NjM2MjA0) or [Docusign sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales) for more
information.

With [Workflow Builder](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=bqi1705961132077.html&_LANG=enus), you can
create workflows to automate your agreement
process.

You can start workflows from Salesforce records by adding a Start with Docusign action to your layouts. There are two ways to add Start with Docusign actions to layouts:

- (Recommended) [Use IAM Launcher Setup Controls](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=tska9d68585-f524-49a4-8898-496b6575d036.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to use the IAM Launcher Setup interface to add Docusign workflow actions to Salesforce layouts.")
- [Manually
  add actions with Salesforce Object Manager](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=rrf1583359212854&topicId=fji1583359148678.html&_LANG=enus)

Users can start workflows by selecting the Start with Docusign action on records:![A screenshot displays a Salesforce record with a Start with Docusign custom button highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Start_Workflow_Builder_Workflow_Custom_Button.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU1NjIsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.itWQ-Ty4_ihLM_wqU6gbx0-Gy4csiyDyYuFfwhOPsBA&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a Salesforce record with a Start with Docusign custom button highlighted.")

You can take the following actions from a Start with Docusign action menu:

[Start](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=tskcae26f13-f2e5-4afd-85a7-145a70b12977.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to start a Docusign workflow from a Start with Docusign action.")
:   Select the Start button to start any
    displayed workflow from a record. Data from the
    record is included in the generated agreement.
    The workflow can
    read data from the record, write changes back, add Salesforce files, and
    store new agreements.

[Add a Workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=tsk9d05e5e9-8ae1-4b02-96eb-1eb83729d6b5.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to add a workflow to the Start with Docusign action menu.")
:   Select this button to add a new workflow to a Start with Docusign action menu.
    Docusign only displays workflows
    related to the record's object. For example, on an Account
    record, Docusign only displays
    workflows built for accounts and related objects.

[Remove this workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=tskb2fdae68-780f-45b0-9b06-f869786823eb.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to remove a Docusign workflow from a Start with Docusign action menu.")
:   Select this option to remove a workflow from the Start with Docusign action
    menu. Removing a workflow from the component view does not delete the
    workflow from your Docusign account.

[Search workflows](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=tsk5491237f-0dec-4eed-9976-7b2124f9a7df.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to search for a workflow from the Start with Docusign action menu.")
:   Use the search field to find a specific workflow. Docusign only displays
    workflows related to the record's object.

title: Check Salesforce Logs for Failed Agreement Manager Actions
heading_path: Check Salesforce Logs for Failed Agreement Manager Actions
text:
Learn to check the status of failed Agreement Manager for Salesforce actions.

Important: This feature
is only available to IAM for Sales customers. Contact [Docusign support](https://support.docusign.com/s/?language=en_US&_gl=1*74elk4*_gcl_au*MTY5ODQyMjA2MC4xNzY5NjM1OTA1LjE5ODM3MTQzNjguMTc2OTYzNjIwNC4xNzY5NjM2MjA0) or [Docusign sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales) for more
information.

In Salesforce, you access
Agreement Manager by adding the Docusign
Agreements component to your layouts. Approved
users can interact with Docusign agreements on
your records without leaving Salesforce.

If errors occur with
Agreement Manager actions, you can view the logs in
Salesforce.

Use this procedure to check the status of failed Agreement Manager actions:

1. Select the Docusign Logs tab.

   The Docusign Logs page opens.
2. Select a log from the Docusign Log column.

   The log details page opens for that item.

You have completed the task. You viewed the details of a failed Agreement Manager action.

title: Complete a Salesforce Workflow Task
heading_path: Complete a Salesforce Workflow Task
text:
Learn to complete a task sent from a Docusign workflow.

Note: Standard users cannot view the progress of workflows in Salesforce. Salesforce administrators can [view failed workflows in the Docusign Logs page](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=vqr1744835015673.html&_LANG=enus). Only Docusign administrators can [monitor the progress of all workflows](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=vcp1698707326210.html&_LANG=enus).

Workflow Builder is a tool that your administrator uses to
automate your contract process. Docusign sends you an email when
you receive and complete workflow tasks. You complete tasks by entering contract
data, signing documents, and more.

Use this procedure to complete a workflow task:

1. Open the email from Docusign titled Review and
   complete workflow.
2. Select Review.
3. Complete all required workflow tasks and select Next for each completed stage.

You have completed the task. You have completed a workflow task. If
you are involved in later workflow steps, you receive additional messages from Docusign.

title: User and Group Management
heading_path: User and Group Management
text:
Read about user and group management tasks, such as managing agreement access and granting bulk upload permissions.

Administrators are responsible for safeguarding access to agreements in Agreement Manager. This includes making sure that the appropriate users and groups can view or manage agreements, and perform bulk uploads. Administrators also manage group membership for Agreement Manager groups.

 

Tip: In some cases, you might want to restrict the ability to delete agreements or update attributes to specific users and groups.

Note: See the  [Docusign eSignature Admin Guide](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390) for more information about using the  menu to manage users and groups.

title: User and Group Management
heading_path: User and Group Management
"View all agreements" versus "Organize and manage all agreements"
text:
There are two options when granting access to agreements in your account:

- View all agreements in this account: authorizes users or groups to view and download all agreements in the account.
- Organize and manage all agreements: authorizes users or groups to view, edit, download, and delete all agreements in the account. This permission level also grants access to manage agreement sets and attributes, like agreement types and fields.

Important:

When organizations purchase an IAM plan with Agreement Manager, by default, all users can access their own shared, received, sent, and uploaded agreements. The ability to view, organize, and manage agreements in the account requires an administrator to grant you this access.

Restriction:

*Unmanaged participants* can use Agreement Manager to view their own completed agreements. However, a full license and additional permissions are required to access the majority of Agreement Manager features. See the [License Management in Account Administration](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=bhs1740099648152.html) topic to learn more about IAM plans and license types.

These permissions control access to view Agreement Manager agreements and to perform various management tasks. This applies to administrators as well. An administrator does not automatically have access to Agreement Manager agreements and features, including bulk uploads.

Note: Agreements are permanently stored in Agreement Manager unless they are deleted by a user with the Organize and manage all agreements permission. Also, eSignature retention is not applicable to Agreement Manager.

title: User and Group Management
heading_path: User and Group Management
Access control with agreement sets
text:
Agreement sets are named groups of agreements that can be shared with users and groups. These sets give contract managers a more granular way to control access to agreements.

For example, a contract manager can organize agreements for productivity and data privacy by creating an agreement set. Once created, the contract manager now has an efficient way to grant access to the appropriate users and groups.

Consider this pain point:

- The contract manager oversees thousands of sales agreements and needs an efficient way to grant access to a subset of these agreements.
- The Renewals Team needs access to all MSAs that are expiring in a quarter without auto-renewal, while the U.S. Sales Team needs access to all MSAs under U.S. jurisdiction.
- The contract manager creates agreement sets to grant access to the relevant agreements for each team.

See the [Agreement Set Management](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=zed1730843285476.html&_LANG=enus "Read about agreement set management including how to use sets to control access to agreements. This topic answers the question: What are agreement sets and how can I use them?") topic to learn more about using this feature to control access to your agreements in Agreement Manager.

title: User and Group Management
heading_path: User and Group Management
Bulk upload permission
text:
All users can upload up to five third-party agreements to Agreement Manager. However, to upload more than five files to Agreement Manager, an additional permission is required. This permission is assigned from the  page. Once this permission is turned on, the user can upload up to 1,000 files at a time to Agreement Manager.

Note: The *bulk upload permission* is required to upload documents from third-party cloud storage locations.

title: User and Group Management
heading_path: User and Group Management
Group membership
text:
Administrators can manage group membership by adding and removing users from a group. Groups are managed from the  page, where the administrator can:

- Add a user to an existing group
- Remove a user from an existing group
- Create a new group
- Bulk add and update users

Note: The Bulk Add and Bulk Update functions are described in the Docusign eSignature Admin guide. See the [User Bulk Actions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=mxm1583277431469.html) topic for more information.

title: Natural Language Search Queries
heading_path: Natural Language Search Queries
text:
Read about how natural language search queries, including components of the
Search Results view.

Natural language (NL) queries offer a more intuitive and efficient way for you to
find relevant agreement information. Enter a question into the search field to and
receive a direct answer and a list of relevant agreements.

![A snippet of the view that shows an example search question to locate master service agreements with automatic renewal.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/NL-Search_Example-question-with-dropdown.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU3OTMsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.ArLpNODZVCwJOx2pA68KR4b76vxz5OcL2C5AjQUVE9E&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the view that shows an example search question to locate master service agreements with automatic renewal.")

This type of search is more robust than traditional keyword searches. With this search,
you can:

- **Search in your own words:** NL processing supports a more intuitive and
  human-like search experience.
- **Find answers faster:** NL queries return search results and direct answers.
- **Make more impactful decisions:** Gain insights as you search, to inform strategies and
  build stronger relationships with clients and vendors.

Tip: Ask more complex questions and get direct answers along
with the search results in one query. For example, if you asked "What is the value of my
purchase agreements with Company A"? The search results would include:

- A direct answer to your question (the total contract value for your purchase
  agreements with Company A)
- A list of purchase agreements with Company A

title: Natural Language Search Queries
heading_path: Natural Language Search Queries
Components of NL search results
text:
Unlike traditional searches, NL queries return both an answer to the question and a
list of relevant agreements.

![A snippet of the view that shows the Search Results for an NL query, including a direct answer and relevant agreements.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/NL-Search_Example-search-results.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU3OTMsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.ArLpNODZVCwJOx2pA68KR4b76vxz5OcL2C5AjQUVE9E&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the view that shows the Search Results for an NL query, including a direct answer and relevant agreements.")

The Search Results view has four primary components:

- **Search criteria and detected filters section:** Shows the question that you entered and
  any filters that were detected while generating the search results. In this
  example, the Agreement Type and
  Parties filters were detected:
  ![A snippet of the Search Results view that shows the search question and detected filters.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/NL-Search_Search-Results_top-section.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU3OTMsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.ArLpNODZVCwJOx2pA68KR4b76vxz5OcL2C5AjQUVE9E&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Search Results view that shows the search question and detected filters.")
- **Answer section:** Displays a direct answer to the entered question. In this example,
  the search located two purchase agreements with Acme that total 100,022 USD:
  ![A snippet of the Search Results that shows a generated answer.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/NL-Search_Search-Results_answer-section.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU3OTMsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.ArLpNODZVCwJOx2pA68KR4b76vxz5OcL2C5AjQUVE9E&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Search Results that shows a generated answer.")
- **Feedback mechanism:** Provides a way to rate the quality of the generated answer. Use
  the Good answer (thumbs up) and Bad
  answer (thumbs down) icons to rate the answer:
  ![A snippet of the Search Results that highlights the feedback buttons.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/NL-Search_Search-Results_Feedback-Mechanisms.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU3OTMsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.ArLpNODZVCwJOx2pA68KR4b76vxz5OcL2C5AjQUVE9E&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Search Results that highlights the feedback buttons.")

  Your feedback helps improve the accuracy and quality of the NL
  processing.
- **Agreements list:** Displays agreements that matched the detected filters. These filters
  were identified based on the search question and were used to locate relevant
  agreements. In this example, you have two purchase agreements with Acme:
  ![A snippet of the Search Results that shows the agreements list for the search query.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/NL-Search_Search-Results_agreements-list.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU3OTMsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.ArLpNODZVCwJOx2pA68KR4b76vxz5OcL2C5AjQUVE9E&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Search Results that shows the agreements list for the search query.")

title: Natural Language Search Queries
heading_path: Natural Language Search Queries
Components of NL search results
text:
