title: Natural Language Search Queries
heading_path: Natural Language Search Queries
Supported attributes and data points
text:
As you enter a question into the search field, a dropdown of "quick" answers displays. You can
select a quick answer or view the results for your question. The search supports
questions related to the following agreement attributes (fields) and data
points:

- Agreement Type
- Currency Code
- Effective Date
- Expiration Data
- Governing Law
- Party Name
- Renewal-related
  - Auto Renewal (Y/N)
  - Renewal Notice Period
  - Renewal Term
  - Renewal Type
- Termination for Cause - Notice Period
- Termination for Convenience - Notice Period
- Title
- Total Contract Value

title: "No Agreements to Show" Displays in the Docusign Agreements Component
heading_path: "No Agreements to Show" Displays in the Docusign Agreements Component
text:
Learn to fix the issue of the Docusign Completed Agreements
component displaying "No agreements to show" instead of Docusign
agreements.

Important: This feature is only available to IAM for
Sales customers. Contact [Docusign support](https://support.docusign.com/s/?language=en_US&_gl=1*74elk4*_gcl_au*MTY5ODQyMjA2MC4xNzY5NjM1OTA1LjE5ODM3MTQzNjguMTc2OTYzNjIwNC4xNzY5NjM2MjA0) or [Docusign sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales)
for more information.

A section with a spinning wheel icon ![A spinning purple wheel icon displays.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/A-spinning-purple-wheel-icon-displays.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU3NTYsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.11JDCNZGvqGqDV8ypaUTTAIod3pNu9Nw0IT2AkopgH4&_LANG=enus "A spinning purple wheel icon displays.") displays on Salesforce records instead of the Docusign
Completed Agreements component. The wheel spins for a while and then a No
agreements to show message displays. I cannot see my Docusign agreements on the record.

![A screenshot displays the Docusign Completed Agreements component with a no agreements to show message.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-Docusign-Agreements-component-with-a-no-agreements-to-show-message.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU3NTYsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.11JDCNZGvqGqDV8ypaUTTAIod3pNu9Nw0IT2AkopgH4&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Docusign Completed Agreements component with a no agreements to show message.")

title: "No Agreements to Show" Displays in the Docusign Agreements Component
heading_path: "No Agreements to Show" Displays in the Docusign Agreements Component
Resolution: Request Docusign Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) Access
text:
Cause: Your Salesforce user has Docusign
Apps Launcher access, but not Docusign IAM access.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. [Request Agreement Manager access from your
   Salesforce
   administrator](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=eut1712157553977.html&_LANG=enus "Read about user and group management tasks, such as managing agreement access and granting bulk upload permissions.").
2. Log off and log on to Salesforce.

   You have completed the process. When your administrator grants IAM
   access, the spinning wheel icon does not display. The Docusign Completed Agreements component displays, providing access to
   Agreement Manager. You can use Agreement Manager to view and act on your Docusign agreements without
   leaving Salesforce.

title: Configure a Read from Salesforce Step
heading_path: Configure a Read from Salesforce Step
text:
Learn to add and configure a Read from Salesforce step to import data from Salesforce records.

Before you begin
:   - Required: Installation of the Salesforce app from Docusign App Center. Review the [Installing Extension Applications for Workflow Builder](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=bgb1697668738892.html&_LANG=enus) topic for more information.
    - Required: [Workflows Create permissions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gbb1696973048215.html&_LANG=enus) or be an account administrator.
    - Required: Create a workflow as described in [Create a Basic Workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uhm1697826856678.html&_LANG=enus). Add steps like [invitation](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=kfx1698167344720.html&_LANG=enus), [web forms](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gua1698120920620.html&_LANG=enus), [eSignature](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uww1698167288211.html&_LANG=enus) and more to gather recipients, documents, and merge fields for your workflow.

You can use a Read from Salesforce step to import data from
Salesforce records to your workflow.
Administrators link workflow fields to Salesforce fields in the Read from Salesforce
step. When task recipients open an envelope, they see Salesforce data imported from
the record. Importing Salesforce data improves efficiency and reduces errors by
eliminating manual data entry.

Example: You build a workflow with a Read from Salesforce step. In the Read from Salesforce step, you link to a Salesforce record's Amount field. When a workflow recipient opens a contract, they see the record's Amount field pre-filled.

Use this procedure to create a Read from Salesforce step in a workflow:

1. Add a Read from Salesforce step as a Docusign administrator:
   1. Go to Agreements > Workflows and select Edit next to a workflow name.

      The workflow editor page displays.
   2. Select the Add a step ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Workflow_Builder_add_step_plus_sign.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU1MTQsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.STiVdSBl8Cl2DKJWOmQ1QVv7fkHZgFBgTLgvmxZXT34&_LANG=enus) icon.

      The Add New Step dialog box displays.
   3. Search for steps: Enter and select `Read from Salesforce`.

      The workflow editor page displays with the Read from Salesforce step added.
2. Name your Read from Salesforce step:
   1. Select Configure on the Read from Salesforce step.

      The configuration dialog box displays.
   2. Step Name: Keep or edit the name. It is helpful to add the name of the Salesforce object, like `Account - Read from Salesforce`.
3. Where will you read from?
   1. Connection: Select the field and choose a
      Salesforce organization that you connected to Docusign. Multiple options display if you connect more than one Salesforce
      organization.
   2. (Optional) Add New Connection: Select this button if you want to [connect more than one Salesforce organization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=ogk1719256147008.html&_LANG=enus).
   3. (Optional) Manage Connections: Select this
      button if you want to [add, edit, refresh, or delete a
      Salesforce connection](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=uaw1719259450391.html&_LANG=enus).
   4. Salesforce object: Search for an object or choose one from the dropdown menu.
   5. Select Next.

title: Configure a Read from Salesforce Step
heading_path: Configure a Read from Salesforce Step
text:
docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=uaw1719259450391.html&_LANG=enus).
   4. Salesforce object: Search for an object or choose one from the dropdown menu.
   5. Select Next.

      ![A screenshot displays a Read from Salesforce workflow step with the Connection and Salesforce object fields highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_Salesforce_Read_Where_will_you_read_from_object.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU1MTQsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.STiVdSBl8Cl2DKJWOmQ1QVv7fkHZgFBgTLgvmxZXT34&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a Read from Salesforce workflow step with the Connection and Salesforce object fields highlighted.")
4. Which fields would you like to read?: Select workflow
   fields to map to Salesforce fields. When workflows start, Docusign adds this data to agreements.
   1. Select Add or Remove Fields.

      The Add or Remove Fields page displays.
   2. Select the check box next to each Salesforce field that you'd like to
      use as a workflow variable. You'll be able to use these fields in later
      steps.

      Tip: When you select objects on the
      left, related objects open. The Source column
      displays each field's object path. ![A screen capture displays the Add or Remove Fields page with related objects highlighted and fields selected.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_Salesforce_Add_or_Remove_Fields.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU1MTQsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.STiVdSBl8Cl2DKJWOmQ1QVv7fkHZgFBgTLgvmxZXT34&_LANG=enus "A screen capture displays the Add or Remove Fields page with related objects highlighted and fields selected.")
   3. Select Save and Exit.

      The Add or Remove Fields dialog box closes. The fields that you
      selected display.
   4. Select Next.
5. Which records are you reading from?: Create a rule that matches Salesforce fields with equivalent workflow fields. Workflows use this rule to read Salesforce data. You can create as many rules as you want:
   1. Salesforce field: Select a Salesforce field to match with a Docusign field.

      Note: When you select a (reference) type field, a new field opens below it. Choose a child object from the new field.
   2. Operator: Choose an option like Equal
      to, Less than, and so on.
   3. Workflow field: Select a Docusign workflow field to match with your Salesforce field:

      - Manual entry: Add text for your new variable and select Save.

        Note: The name that you enter must exactly match an existing field. For example, the rule fails if you enter CompanyName for the Company\_Name variable.
      - Select a field from the start step or another step in this
        workflow:

title: Configure a Read from Salesforce Step
heading_path: Configure a Read from Salesforce Step
text:
actly match an existing field. For example, the rule fails if you enter CompanyName for the Company\_Name variable.
      - Select a field from the start step or another step in this
        workflow:

        Note: Steps like Read
        from HubSpot and Read from
        Salesforce display fields from up to five
        records. If more than five records are present, Salesforce
        logic decides which five to display. Selecting fields from
        multiple records helps you automate processes like mapping
        multiple Salesforce contacts to multiple Docusign participants.![A screenshot displays Read from Salesforce fields highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_Salesforce_Read_Workflow_field_selections.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU1MTQsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.STiVdSBl8Cl2DKJWOmQ1QVv7fkHZgFBgTLgvmxZXT34&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays Read from Salesforce fields highlighted.")

        You can use the fields that you select here in
        later steps like Collect Data with
        Webforms or Send Documents for
        Signature.
   4. Select Done to save your rule.

      ![A screenshot displays a Salesforce field, an operator value of Greater than or equal to, and a workflow field all highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_HubSpot_Read_Which_record_are_you_reading_from_Greater_than.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU1MTQsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.STiVdSBl8Cl2DKJWOmQ1QVv7fkHZgFBgTLgvmxZXT34&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a Salesforce field, an operator value of Greater than or equal to, and a workflow field all highlighted.")
6. (Optional) Add more rules:
   - +AND: Workflow Builder only imports Salesforce data that
     matches all of your rules.
   - +OR: Workflow Builder imports Salesforce data that matches
     any of your rules.
7. How many records should this step return?:
   - A single record: Returns the first record that
     matches your rule.
   - Multiple records: Returns up to five records that match your
     rule.

     Note: If more than five records are present, Salesforce logic
     decides which five to display.
8. (Optional) Preview this step: See examples of how this rule will work with up to ten Salesforce records:
   1. Select the Preview this step link.

      The Add Data to Preview Step page opens.
   2. Keep or edit the data provided in the field.
   3. Select Preview Salesforce Data.

      ![A screenshot displays the Read from Salesforce Add Data to Preview Step page of a Read from Salesforce workflow step.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Read_from_Salesforce_Add_Data_to_Preview_Step.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU1MTQsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.STiVdSBl8Cl2DKJWOmQ1QVv7fkHZgFBgTLgvmxZXT34&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Read from Salesforce Add Data to Preview Step page of a Read from Salesforce workflow step.")

      The page refreshes, displaying Salesforce data that your Read from Salesforce rule found.
   4. Select Close.

      The Add Data to Preview Step page closes.
   5. Select Apply.

   The Read from Salesforce dialog box closes. The workflow page displays your new step.

You have completed the process. You have built a Read from Salesforce step in your workflow.
When this step is triggered in a workflow, it imports field data from the Salesforce
records that you mapped.

title: Install the Salesforce Application ( Docusign App Center Instructions)
heading_path: Install the Salesforce Application (Docusign App Center Instructions)
text: Learn to install the Salesforce app in your Docusign account and connect it to your Salesforce organization.

title: Install the Salesforce Application ( Docusign App Center Instructions)
heading_path: Install the Salesforce Application (Docusign App Center Instructions)
Before you begin
text:
[Purchase a Docusign Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) for Sales application](https://www.docusign.com/intelligent-agreement-management?utm_source=bing&utm_medium=cpc&utm_campaign=GBL_XX_PRF_UPS_2410_SEMIAM&utm_term=docusign%20iam&campaignid=569731866&adgroupid=1178678316174006&location=84406&device=c&matchtype=e&extensionid=&creative=73667602015411&keyword=docusign%20iam&placement=&network=o&msclkid=3287ee64f2c01f69bcd686665f40e7da).

Important: Use these instructions to install the application from the Docusign App Center. Follow this link if you want to [install the app from your Salesforce organization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=&map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=tsk91549a3b-f7f3-4b74-8e87-33ff33d198c0.html&_LANG=enus).

Docusign administrators can get extension applications from the Docusign App Center.

You can install the Salesforce app to connect your Docusign account with your Salesforce organization. As an administrator, log on to the Docusign App Center and install the Salesforce app for your account. Workflow creators can add Salesforce steps to read and write data, store documents, add merge fields, and more.

For more information about viewing, installing, and managing partner extension apps, see the  [Docusign App Center Guide](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=ijy1698170251179.html&_LANG=enus).

Developers can review the [Extension apps 101 overview](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/extension-apps-101/) in the Docusign Developer Center.

Tip: Wait at least 30 minutes after installing before you configure a Salesforce workflow step. Docusign indexes all Salesforce fields to make them available for use.

Use this procedure to install and connect the Salesforce app through the Docusign App Center:

1. Log on to your Docusign account as an administrator and open the appropriate App Center site:
   - [Docusign Production](https://apps.docusign.com/app-center/extensionapps)
   - [Docusign Demo](https://apps-d.docusign.com/app-center/extensionapps)
2. Select the Salesforce tile and select Install App when the page refreshes.

   The Install and authorize "Salesforce" app page opens.
3. Select Continue and select Connect Account when the page refreshes.

   The Connect a Salesforce account page opens.
4. Connect to your Salesforce organization:

   Important: [Salesforce Government Cloud customers](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=000386092&type=1) must use the Salesforce Production option.

   1. Select a Salesforce organization to connect to Docusign:

      - Salesforce Production (login.salesforce.com): Connect a Salesforce production or developer organization.
      - Salesforce Sandbox (test.salesforce.com): Connect to a Salesforce sandbox organization.

      ![A screenshot displays the Connect a Salesforce account with Salesforce Production selected.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Workflow_Builder_Connect_a_Salesforce_Account.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU3NjQsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.C5uDT4-KnWKo5UpO_ij7cKSAB1t8f7_8E3TArn_YHb4&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Connect a Salesforce account with Salesforce Production selected.")
   2. Select Next.
   3. How would you like to connect this Salesforce account?

      - Private: Only accessible to you in Docusign.
      - Shared: Accessible by multiple users or the whole team in Docusign.
   4. Select Next.
   5. Name this Salesforce account: Keep or change the default name.
   6. Select Log In
5. Connect to your Salesforce organization. Salesforce does not ask for credentials if you are logged in to your organization.

   Important: Salesforce Government Cloud customers must use the Custom Domain option.

   Important: Salesforce Government Cloud customers must use the Custom Domain option.

title: Add Salesforce Merge Fields to a Send Documents for Signature Step
heading_path: Add Salesforce Merge Fields to a Send Documents for Signature Step
text:
Learn to add a Salesforce merge field to a Send Documents for Signature step.

Before you begin
:   - Required: Installation of the Salesforce app
      from Docusign App Center. Review the [Installing Extension Applications for Workflow Builder](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=bgb1697668738892.html&_LANG=enus) topic for more information.
    - Required: [Workflows Create permissions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gbb1696973048215.html&_LANG=enus) or be an account administrator.
    - Required: [Create a Connect configuration to use Workflow Builder with
      Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=xza1742958339120.html&_LANG=enus).
    - Required: [Build a Docusign envelope template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=gou1591745811909.html&_LANG=enus) containing [Salesforce merge fields from
      a single object](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=wnq1738608337037.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to fix the \"This template is not supported\" error in a Workflow Builder Send Documents for Signature step.").

You can access a Docusign envelope template in a workflow by adding a Send Documents for
Signature step. When your envelope template contains Salesforce merge
fields, you can add the Source Record ID to a workflow. Docusign uses these merge fields to update Salesforce records when
recipients sign agreements.

Example: You create a workflow that contains a Read from
Salesforce step. You connect an envelope template containing Salesforce fields to
your Send Documents for Signature step. Your envelope template contains fields for
the Contact object.

When you configure the Send Documents for Signature step, you add Contact ID
to the Source Record ID field. The Contact ID variable
contains the Title field.

A workflow recipient signs an agreement. The recipient updates the Title field from
HR Assistant to HR Manager when
signing. Docusign updates the recipient's title to HR
Manager on the contact record.

Use this procedure to add a Source Record ID to your Send
Documents for Signature step:

1. Build a [Docusign envelope
   template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=gou1591745811909.html&_LANG=enus) containing Salesforce merge fields [from a single object](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=wnq1738608337037.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to fix the \"This template is not supported\" error in a Workflow Builder Send Documents for Signature step.").

title: Add Salesforce Merge Fields to a Send Documents for Signature Step
heading_path: Add Salesforce Merge Fields to a Send Documents for Signature Step
text:
=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=wnq1738608337037.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to fix the \"This template is not supported\" error in a Workflow Builder Send Documents for Signature step.").

   Note: If your envelope template contains merge fields from more than one
   object (Account, Opportunity, and so on) [an error displays](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=wnq1738608337037.html&_LANG=enus).
2. [Create a basic
   workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uhm1697826856678.html&_LANG=enus) containing [Read from
   Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=idr1713985144297.html&_LANG=enus) and [Send Documents for Signature](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uww1698167288211.html&_LANG=enus) steps.
3. Add a Salesforce signer to your Send Documents for Signature step:
   1. Who is the signer?: Add a Salesforce Contact as a
      signer.
   2. Select Next.
4. Add a Salesforce merge field to your Send Documents for Signature step:
   1. Connect document fields (Merge Fields).
   2. Source Record ID: Select a field from your Read
      from Salesforce step. ![A screenshot displays the Connect document fields (Merge Fields) section with Contact ID highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-connect-document-fields-section-of-a-Workflow-Builder-for-Salesforce-Get-Signatures-step.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU0NzUsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.hQat9pzirZIiFMbOfhFqj6oN4bJFlHhJteQ-6sUO2Ps&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Connect document fields (Merge Fields) section with Contact ID highlighted.")
   3. Select Next.
   4. Select Apply.

   The Send Documents for Signature dialog box closes.

You have completed the process. You have added a Source Record ID to
your Send Documents for Signature step. When task recipients change field data, Docusign updates the corresponding Salesforce record fields.

title: Reconnect a Salesforce Organization to Docusign
heading_path: Reconnect a Salesforce Organization to Docusign
text:
Learn to fix connection problems by reconnecting your Salesforce organization toDocusign.

You can connect Salesforce organizations to your Docusign IAM account. When you connect to Docusign, you can build
workflows that read from and write to your Salesforce records. If you experience
problems with your connection, you can reconnect Docusign and
Salesforce.

You can access the reconnect controls through your Salesforce organization or your Docusign account.

Use this procedure to reconnect Salesforce with your Docusign IAM account:

1. Choose your login method:
   - Login from Salesforce:
     1. [Access the Docusign
        Setup page as a Salesforce administrator](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=etv1586134206174.html&_LANG=enus)
     2. Manage Connections and the IAM Launcher: Select
        Manage.
   - Login from Docusign:
     1. Open the [Production](https://apps.docusign.com/app-center/extensionapps) or [Demo](https://apps-d.docusign.com/app-center/extensionapps) App Center site.
     2. Select the Salesforce tile.
     3. Select Manage and choose Manage
        Connections.
2. Select the three-dot menu icon ![A screenshot displays three dots stacked vertically.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/A-screenshot-diplays-a-threedot-menu-icon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU1NTIsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.PoPMvp7OqIv68z_YtJQPK6abx75eOW54TOK2tYrFVr0&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays three dots stacked vertically.") next to a connection and choose Reconnect.

   The page refreshes. A message displays: Successfully
   reconnected.

You have completed the process. You have reconnected Salesforce to your Docusign IAM account.

title: Learn About the Overwrite If Field Value Exists Feature
heading_path: Learn About the Overwrite If Field Value Exists Feature
text:
Learn about the function of the Overwrite if field value exists feature in a
workflow.

You can use Workflow Builder to automate agreement actions from your Docusign account.

You can use a Writeback to Salesforce step to update Salesforce
records with information from your workflow. When task recipients enter data in
workflow fields, Docusign updates the information in the linked
Salesforce record fields.

The Writeback to Salesforce step contains an option: Overwrite if field value
exists.

Selecting this option replaces the Salesforce field's data with data entered by a workflow
participant.![A screenshot displays the Overwrite if field value exists option in a green highlighted box.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Workflow_Builder_Writeback_to_Salesforce_overwrite_if_field_value_exists.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU0ODEsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.02cORPnaahSY4BAfFR5RL0hy5fXvuElkXVozAEkx32M&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Overwrite if field value exists option in a green highlighted box.")

Example: You build a workflow with a Writeback to
Salesforce step. In the writeback step, you select Overwrite
if field value exists. for the Salesforce Amount
field.

A workflow recipient updates and signs a contract for an existing opportunity named Fontara.
The recipient updates the Fontara contract's Amount field from $20,000 to $25,000. When
the recipient completes the assignment, Docusign updates the Fontara
record's Amount field to $25,000.

title: Link Multiple Read from Salesforce Steps
heading_path: Link Multiple Read from Salesforce Steps
text:
Learn to link two or more Read from Salesforce workflow steps.

Before you begin
:   - Required: Installation of the Salesforce app
      from Docusign App Center. Review the [Installing Extension Applications for Workflow Builder](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=bgb1697668738892.html&_LANG=enus) topic for more information.
    - Required: [Workflows Create permissions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gbb1696973048215.html&_LANG=enus) or be an account administrator.
    - Required: Access to a suitable template to use for your workflow
      step. Review the [Template Requirements](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=xnw1709852160602.html&_LANG=enus) topic for more information.

You can use a Read from Salesforce step to import Salesforce data
to your workflow. Administrators link workflow
fields to Salesforce fields in the Read from Salesforce step. When task recipients
open an envelope, they see Salesforce data imported from the record. Importing
Salesforce data improves efficiency and reduces errors by eliminating manual data
entry.

You can configure more than one Read from Salesforce step in the same workflow.

Example: You want data from your workflow to read different fields from
Opportunity and Contact records. You build a read step linked to the Account object
and add one group of fields. You create a second step linked to the Opportunity
object and add fields there. A workflow participant opens a Docusign web form. The participant sees data imported from the linked Account and
Opportunity fields. The participant is not required to enter this information.

Use this procedure to add multiple Read from Salesforce steps to your workflow:

1. [Configure a Read from Salesforce
   step](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=idr1713985144297.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to add and configure a Read from Salesforce step to import data from Salesforce records.").
2. Add a Read from Salesforce step as a Docusign administrator:
   1. Go to Agreements > Workflows and select Edit next to a workflow
      name.

      The workflow editor page displays.
   2. Select the Add a step
      ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Workflow_Builder_add_step_plus_sign.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU1MTcsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.YHi2Y--39yB5cXbLXrWs_XEn3t_vnWpgj7AxfEsiOV8&_LANG=enus) icon.

      The Add New Step dialog box displays.
   3. Search for steps: Enter and select
      `Read from Salesforce`.

      The workflow editor page displays with the Read from Salesforce
      step added.
3. Name your Read from Salesforce step:
   1. Select Configure on the Read from Salesforce
      step.

title: Link Multiple Read from Salesforce Steps
heading_path: Link Multiple Read from Salesforce Steps
text:
sforce`.

      The workflow editor page displays with the Read from Salesforce
      step added.
3. Name your Read from Salesforce step:
   1. Select Configure on the Read from Salesforce
      step.

      The configuration dialog box displays.
   2. Step Name: Keep or edit the name. It is helpful
      to add the name of the Salesforce object, like `Account - Read
      from Salesforce`.
4. Where will you read from?
   1. Connection: Select the field and choose a
      Salesforce organization that you connected to Docusign. Multiple options display if you connect more than one Salesforce
      organization.
   2. (Optional) Add New Connection: Select this
      button if you want to [connect more than one Salesforce
      organization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=ogk1719256147008.html&_LANG=enus).
   3. (Optional) Manage Connections: Select this
      button if you want to [add, edit, refresh, or delete a
      Salesforce connection](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=uaw1719259450391.html&_LANG=enus).
   4. Salesforce object: Search for an object or
      choose one from the dropdown menu.
   5. Select Next.

      ![A screenshot displays a Read from Salesforce workflow step with the Connection and Salesforce object fields highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_Salesforce_Read_Where_will_you_read_from_object.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU1MTcsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.YHi2Y--39yB5cXbLXrWs_XEn3t_vnWpgj7AxfEsiOV8&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a Read from Salesforce workflow step with the Connection and Salesforce object fields highlighted.")
5. Which fields would you like to read?: Select workflow
   fields to map to Salesforce fields. When workflows start, Docusign adds this data to agreements.
   1. Select Add or Remove Fields.

      The Add or Remove Fields page displays.
   2. Select the check box next to each Salesforce field that you'd like to
      use as a workflow variable. You'll be able to use these fields in later
      steps.

      Tip: When you select objects on the
      left, related objects open. The Source column
      displays each field's object path. ![A screen capture displays the Add or Remove Fields page with related objects highlighted and fields selected.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_Salesforce_Add_or_Remove_Fields.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU1MTcsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.YHi2Y--39yB5cXbLXrWs_XEn3t_vnWpgj7AxfEsiOV8&_LANG=enus "A screen capture displays the Add or Remove Fields page with related objects highlighted and fields selected.")
   3. Select Save and Exit.

      The Add or Remove Fields dialog box closes. The fields that you
      selected display.
   4. Select Next.
6. Which records are you reading from?: Create a rule that
   matches Salesforce fields with equivalent workflow fields. Workflows use
   this rule to read Salesforce data. You can create as many rules as you want:
   1. Salesforce field: Select a Salesforce field to
      match with a Docusign workflow field.

      Note: When you select a
      (reference) type field, a new field opens
      below it. Choose a child object from the new field.
   2. Operator: Choose an option like Equal
      to, Less than, and so on.
   3. Workflow field: Select a Docusign field to match with your Salesforce field:

      - Manual entry: Add text for your new
        variable and select Save.

title: Link Multiple Read from Salesforce Steps
heading_path: Link Multiple Read from Salesforce Steps
text:
Equal
      to, Less than, and so on.
   3. Workflow field: Select a Docusign field to match with your Salesforce field:

      - Manual entry: Add text for your new
        variable and select Save.

        Note: The name that you enter must exactly
        match an existing field. For example, the rule fails if you
        enter CompanyName for the
        Company\_Name
        variable.
      - Select a field from the start step or another step in this
        workflow:

        Note: Steps like Read
        from HubSpot and Read from
        Salesforce display fields from up to five
        records. If more than five records are present, Salesforce
        logic decides which five to display. Selecting fields from
        multiple records helps you automate processes like mapping
        multiple Salesforce contacts to multiple Docusign participants.![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_Salesforce_Read_Workflow_field_selections.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU1MTcsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.YHi2Y--39yB5cXbLXrWs_XEn3t_vnWpgj7AxfEsiOV8&_LANG=enus)

        You can use the fields that you select here in
        later steps like Collect Data with
        Webforms or Send Documents for
        Signature.
   4. Select Done to save your rule.

      ![A screenshot displays a Salesforce field, an operator value of Greater than or equal to, and a workflow field all highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_HubSpot_Read_Which_record_are_you_reading_from_Greater_than.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU1MTcsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.YHi2Y--39yB5cXbLXrWs_XEn3t_vnWpgj7AxfEsiOV8&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a Salesforce field, an operator value of Greater than or equal to, and a workflow field all highlighted.")
7. (Optional) Add more rules:
   - +AND: Workflow Builder only imports Salesforce data that
     matches all of your rules.
   - +OR: Workflow Builder imports Salesforce data that matches
     any of your rules.
8. How many records should this step return?:

   Note: If more than five records are present, Salesforce
   logic decides which five display. Selecting fields from multiple records
   helps you automate processes like mapping multiple Salesforce contacts to
   multiple workflow participants.You can use the fields that
   you select here in later steps like Collect Data with
   Webforms or Send Documents for
   Signature.

   - A single record: Returns the first record that
     matches your rule.
   - Multiple records: Returns up to five records that
     match your rule.
9. (Optional) Preview this step: See examples of how this
   rule will work with up to ten Salesforce records:
   1. Select the Preview this step link.

      The Add Data to Preview Step page opens.
   2. Keep or edit the data provided in the field.
   3. Select Preview Salesforce Data.

      ![A screenshot displays the Read from Salesforce Add Data to Preview Step page of a Read from Salesforce workflow step.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Read_from_Salesforce_Add_Data_to_Preview_Step.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU1MTcsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.YHi2Y--39yB5cXbLXrWs_XEn3t_vnWpgj7AxfEsiOV8&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Read from Salesforce Add Data to Preview Step page of a Read from Salesforce workflow step.")

title: Link Multiple Read from Salesforce Steps
heading_path: Link Multiple Read from Salesforce Steps
text:
yZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.YHi2Y--39yB5cXbLXrWs_XEn3t_vnWpgj7AxfEsiOV8&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Read from Salesforce Add Data to Preview Step page of a Read from Salesforce workflow step.")

   4. Select Close.

You have completed the process. You created and linked two Read
from Salesforce steps for related objects. You can repeat this process to map other
objects like Contact and Case. When this step is triggered in a workflow, it imports
record data from all objects you mapped. Workflow participants are not required to enter
data for these fields.

title: Configure a Store Files in Salesforce Step
heading_path: Configure a Store Files in Salesforce Step
text:
Learn to add and configure a Store files in Salesforce step to add files to Salesforce
records.

title: Configure a Store Files in Salesforce Step
heading_path: Configure a Store Files in Salesforce Step
Before you begin
text:
- Required: Installation of the Salesforce app from Docusign App Center. Review the [Installing Extension Applications for
  Workflow Builder](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=bgb1697668738892.html&_LANG=enus)
  topic for more information.
- Required: [Workflows Create permissions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gbb1696973048215.html&_LANG=enus) or be an account
  administrator.
- Required: Create a workflow as described in [Create a Basic Workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uhm1697826856678.html&_LANG=enus). Add steps like
  [invitation](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=kfx1698167344720.html&_LANG=enus), [web forms](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gua1698120920620.html&_LANG=enus), [eSignature](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uww1698167288211.html&_LANG=enus) and more to gather recipients,
  documents, and merge fields for your workflow.

The Store files in Salesforce step adds signed agreements and
certificates of completion to Salesforce records. Administrators choose files to
store, a location to store them, and a naming convention for the files. The Store
files in Salesforce step decides the name and storage location of agreements sent
from workflows.

Example: You build a workflow with a Store files in Salesforce
step. Your workflow sends an agreement to three recipients. When all three
recipients sign the agreement, Docusign uses Store files in
Salesforce variables to name the agreement and add it to the correct record.

Note: Currently, the Store files in Salesforce step limits file sizes
to 10 MB.

Use the following procedure to configure a Store files in Salesforce step for your
workflow:

1. Add a Store files in Salesforce step as a Docusign administrator:
   1. Go to Agreements > Workflows and select Edit next to a workflow
      name.

      The workflow editor page displays.
   2. Select the Add a step
      ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Workflow_Builder_add_step_plus_sign.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU1MzEsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.AUSyu2E41GlSD44Uv_mVnQtvrpT-p0mKafBsZ0ejZHw&_LANG=enus) icon.

      The Add New Step dialog box displays.
   3. Search for steps: Enter `Store files
      in Salesforce` in the search field and select the
      Store files in Salesforce step that displays.

      The workflow editor page displays with the Store files in
      Salesforce step added.
2. Name your Store files in Salesforce step:
   1. Select Configure on the Store files in
      Salesforce step.

      The configuration dialog box displays.
   2. Step Name: Keep or edit the name. It is helpful
      to add the name of the Salesforce object, like `Account -
      Store files in Salesforce`.
3. What are you storing?:
   1. Select a file from the dropdown menu.
   2. Select Next

title: Configure a Store Files in Salesforce Step
heading_path: Configure a Store Files in Salesforce Step
Before you begin
text:
It is helpful
      to add the name of the Salesforce object, like `Account -
      Store files in Salesforce`.
3. What are you storing?:
   1. Select a file from the dropdown menu.
   2. Select Next

      ![A screenshot displays the What are you storing section of a Store files in Salesforce step with the file selection highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_Salesforce_Store_Files_What_are_you_storing.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU1MzEsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.AUSyu2E41GlSD44Uv_mVnQtvrpT-p0mKafBsZ0ejZHw&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the What are you storing section of a Store files in Salesforce step with the file selection highlighted.")
4. Where are you storing it?
   1. Connection: Select the field and choose a Salesforce organization that
      you connected to Docusign. Multiple options display if
      you connect more than one Salesforce organization.
   2. (Optional) Add New Connection: Select this button if you want to [connect more than one Salesforce
      organization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=ogk1719256147008.html&_LANG=enus).
   3. (Optional) Manage Connections: Select this
      button if you want to [add, edit, refresh, or delete a
      Salesforce connection](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=uaw1719259450391.html&_LANG=enus).
   4. Select Record: Select a field from the start
      step or another step in this workflow:

      - Manual entry: Add text for your new
        variable and select Save.

        Note: The name that you enter must exactly
        match an existing field. For example, the rule fails if you
        enter CompanyName for the
        Company\_Name
        variable.
      - Select a field from the start step or another step in this
        workflow:

        Note:
        - You can only select records from other workflow
          steps that are built on the same object as this step
          (Account, Contact, and so on.)
        - Steps like Read from HubSpot
          and Read from Salesforce
          display fields from up to five records. If more than
          five records are present, Salesforce logic decides
          which five to display. Selecting fields from
          multiple records helps you automate processes like
          mapping multiple Salesforce contacts to multiple Docusign participants.
        - You can use the fields that you select here in later
          steps like Collect Data with
          Webforms or Send Documents
          for Signature.![A screenshot displays Read from Salesforce fields highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_Salesforce_Read_Workflow_field_selections.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU1MzEsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.AUSyu2E41GlSD44Uv_mVnQtvrpT-p0mKafBsZ0ejZHw&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays Read from Salesforce fields highlighted.")

      The field that you selected displays.
   5. Select Next.

Before you begin

5. What will you call it?: Use variables and text to create a
   custom file name for your agreements:
   1. Add Variable: Use the search field or dropdown
      menu to select variables to add to the agreement name.
   2. Add Text: Enter text to add to the
      agreement name and select Add.

      Note: The File name field
      displays the naming convention. As you add variables and text, the
      name updates. Example: File name:
      [Account ID][Envelope ID]MSA.

      ![A screen capture displays the Store files in Salesforce step settings with the Add Variable and Add Text features used to create a file name.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_Salesforce_Store_files_What_will_you_call_it.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU1MzEsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.AUSyu2E41GlSD44Uv_mVnQtvrpT-p0mKafBsZ0ejZHw&_LANG=enus "A screen capture displays the Store files in Salesforce step settings with the Add Variable and Add Text features used to create a file name.")
   3. Select Apply.

   The Store file in Salesforce dialog box closes. The page displays your new step.

You have completed the process. You have added a Store files in
Salesforce step to your workflow. When signers complete an agreement, Docusign adds the agreement and certificate of completion to the
specified record.

title: Link Docusign Workflows to Salesforce Flows
heading_path: Link Docusign Workflows to Salesforce Flows
text:
Learn about linking Docusign workflows and Salesforce flows to create integrated
automation.

title: Link Docusign Workflows to Salesforce Flows
heading_path: Link Docusign Workflows to Salesforce Flows
Why link workflows?
text:
[Workflow Builder](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=dnx1696972415150.html&_LANG=enus) automates agreement actions from your Docusign
account.

Salesforce Flow is a workflow builder that you can use to automate agreements from
your Salesforce organization.

You can link Docusign workflows to Salesforce flows by adding a **Start Docusign
Workflow** step to your flow.

When a flow starts, Salesforce starts your linked Docusign workflow. This integration
automates the creation of agreements that contain Docusign and
Salesforce data.

title: Link Docusign Workflows to Salesforce Flows
heading_path: Link Docusign Workflows to Salesforce Flows
Workflow linking example
text:
You build a Record-Triggered Flow and link it to a Docusign workflow.
You configure your flow to start when an opportunity's stage changes to
Negotiation/Review. You add a Start Docusign Workflow step to your flow. When started, your workflow
generates an NDA with Docusign and Salesforce data.

A user changes the stage of the Burlington Textiles opportunity to Negotiation/Review.
Salesforce starts the workflow linked to your flow. Docusign
generates an NDA containing data from the record and sends it for signature.

title: Link Docusign Workflows to Salesforce Flows
heading_path: Link Docusign Workflows to Salesforce Flows
Workflow linking prerequisites
text:
- Purchase a Docusign
  Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) plan.
- Install Docusign Apps
  Launcher version 7.4 or higher.
- Install the Salesforce
  Application from the Docusign App Center.
- Assign Workflows Create permissions
  to your Docusign user, or be an account administrator.
- Build a workflow to be started by your Salesforce flow.
- Prepare your Salesforce users for linking
  flows to Docusign workflows.
- Configure a Start Docusign
  Workflow step in a Salesforce flow.

title: Link Docusign Workflows to Salesforce Flows
heading_path: Link Docusign Workflows to Salesforce Flows
Monitor workflows
text:
Standard Salesforce users
:   Standard users cannot view the progress of Docusign workflows in Salesforce.

Salesforce administrators
:   Administrators can only [view
    failed workflows in the Docusign Logs page](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=vqr1744835015673.html&_LANG=enus).

Docusign administrators
:   Docusign administrators can [monitor the progress of all workflows](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=vcp1698707326210.html&_LANG=enus).

title: Link Docusign Workflows to Salesforce Flows
heading_path: Link Docusign Workflows to Salesforce Flows
Additional resources
text:
The following topics demonstrate how to prepare for and configure flows that start
Docusign workflows:

[Access Required to Start Docusign Workflows from Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=nlu1742250066565.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about the permissions that you must add for users to start Docusign workflows in Salesforce.")

[Check the Status of a Flow That Failed to Start a Docusign Workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=vqr1744835015673.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to check the status of a flow that failed to start a workflow.")

title: Learn About Agreement Manager for Salesforce User Access
heading_path: Learn About Agreement Manager for Salesforce User Access
text:
Learn about granting user access to the Docusign Completed Agreements component on Salesforce layouts.

Important: This feature is only available to IAM for Sales customers. Contact [Docusign support](https://support.docusign.com/s/?language=en_US&_gl=1*74elk4*_gcl_au*MTY5ODQyMjA2MC4xNzY5NjM1OTA1LjE5ODM3MTQzNjguMTc2OTYzNjIwNC4xNzY5NjM2MjA0) or [Docusign sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales) for more information.

To use Agreement Manager, Salesforce users need the following access:

- [An Apps Launcher user](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=dzl1586134206658.html&_LANG=enus).
- [An Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) user with Agreement Manager access](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=eut1712157553977.html&_LANG=enus "Read about user and group management tasks, such as managing agreement access and granting bulk upload permissions.").

Docusign administrators grant access to these user types. Approved users activate their access by selecting a Docusign button or component and entering their Docusign and Salesforce credentials.

title: Learn About Agreement Manager for Salesforce User Access
heading_path: Learn About Agreement Manager for Salesforce User Access
How the Docusign Completed Agreements component displays for different user types
text:
User access dictates how the Docusign Completed Agreements component displays on a layout. Depending on the access granted, Docusign displays or hides the component on Salesforce layouts.

| Salesforce user access | How Docusign Agreements displays |
| --- | --- |
| Docusign Apps Launcher and Docusign IAM access | Users can see the Docusign Completed Agreements component and act on the agreements displayed. |
| Docusign Apps Launcher access only | Users see a blank component with a spinning wheel icon instead of the Docusign Completed Agreements component. |
| No Docusign user access | The Docusign Completed Agreements component does not display on records. |

title: Learn About Searching for Agreements with Agreement Manager for Salesforce
heading_path: Learn About Searching for Agreements with Agreement Manager for Salesforce
text:
Learn to search for agreements in Agreement Manager for Salesforce.

Important: This feature is only available to IAM for Sales customers. Contact [Docusign support](https://support.docusign.com/s/?language=en_US&_gl=1*74elk4*_gcl_au*MTY5ODQyMjA2MC4xNzY5NjM1OTA1LjE5ODM3MTQzNjguMTc2OTYzNjIwNC4xNzY5NjM2MjA0) or [Docusign sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales) for more information.

The Docusign Completed Agreements component displays Docusign agreements on Salesforce records. You can use Agreement Manager to [search for your agreements in Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=qzl1712244340883.html&_LANG=enus).

With Agreement Manager, you have a single place to go to search across all of your completed agreements within your account. The search experience shows contextual help to craft search queries using synonyms, concepts, and everyday language. These search capabilities lead to a more accurate, flexible, and efficient retrieval of information.

title: Learn About Searching for Agreements with Agreement Manager for Salesforce
heading_path: Learn About Searching for Agreements with Agreement Manager for Salesforce
Finding the Agreement Manager search field in Salesforce
text:
Access the Agreement Manager search feature in Salesforce by opening a record that contains the [Docusign Completed Agreements component](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pwq1747797355782&topicId=cmv1746652068556.html&_LANG=enus). A Salesforce administrator may have placed the component on a tab labeled Docusign Agreements or something similar. The component features the word Completed at the top.

- The Docusign Completed Agreements component displays agreements sent from the following applications:
  - eSignature for Salesforce
  - CLM for Salesforce
  - Agreement Desk for Salesforce
  - Workflow builder for Salesforce
- The component displays agreements sent both before and after you add the component to your layouts.
- The component displays agreements sent from the source record and its child records. It does not display agreements from parent objects.
- Account records display agreements from the account and all of its child objects.

![The Completed section of a Salesforce record displays a search field with Fontara and MSA entered.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/The-Completed-section-of-a-Salesforce-record-displays-a-search-field-with-Fontara-and-MSA-entered-2.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU3MzQsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.ppCmVOau4faYUHprIzhuuPn1pfqr6L5L3WVhECOKz4U&_LANG=enus)

title: Learn About Searching for Agreements with Agreement Manager for Salesforce
heading_path: Learn About Searching for Agreements with Agreement Manager for Salesforce
Agreement Manager for Salesforce search capabilities
text:
You can use the following capabilities in the Agreement Manager for Salesforce search field:

| **Capability** | **Description** |
| --- | --- |
| **Boolean search** | Users can combine search terms using Boolean operators to refine their search results. Agreement Manager currently supports the *AND* operator. |
| **Fuzzy search** | The search results contain agreements that closely match a user's query, even if it's not an exact match. Users can find relevant agreements based on approximate search terms. |
| **Keyword search** | When users enter a keyword or phrase, the system searches the full agreement content to find exact keywords. |
| **Natural language (NL) search queries** | Users can enter a natural language question into the search field and receive both search results and direct answers. See the [Natural Language Search Queries](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=fas1739987568042.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how natural language search queries, including components of the Search Results view.") topic to learn more about NL queries. |
| **Natural language search suggestions** | When users enter a keyword or phrase, personalized suggestions are displayed. These suggestions are designed to enhance discoverability and help users find relevant information more easily. |
| **Recent searches** | The search remembers and displays recent searches for easy future reference. Users can also clear recent searches. |
| **Related concepts search** | The search automatically includes synonyms and related concepts in the search results. Users can capture all relevant documents even if the exact search term is not used. |
| **Semantic search** | Semantic search improves the accuracy of search results by understanding:  - the intent of the search query - the contextual meaning of search terms |
| **Shareable searches** | A shareable link is generated when a search is performed, including applying one or more filters. Receivers of the link have access to agreements in the search results list depending on their permissions. |
| **Spelling assist** | The search automatically corrects spelling errors in queries. Users can find relevant information even if mistakes exist. |
| **Text normalization** | The search finds the most relevant agreements or clauses regardless of the specific word form used in the query. |
| **Type-ahead suggestions** | The search bar provides type-ahead suggestions as the user enters a search query. |
| **Wildcard search** | Users can use wildcard characters in their search to find agreements that contain variations of a word or phrase. Agreement Manager currently supports the use of asterisks and quotation marks. |

title: Export and Import a Workflow Builder Workflow
heading_path: Export and Import a Workflow Builder Workflow
text:
Learn to export a Workflow Builder workflow and import it to either the same or another Docusign account.

You can export Docusign workflows and import them to either
the same account or another Docusign account.

Example 1: You build a workflow in your Docusign demo account. You want to add this workflow to your Docusign production account. You download the workflow from your
demo account and upload the .ZIP file to your production account. You validate that
all steps will work in production and publish the workflow.

Example 2: You build a workflow in your Docusign production account. You want to build a new workflow that
is similar to this workflow, but with a few differences. You download the workflow
and upload the .ZIP file back to your production account. You rename the imported
workflow, make some changes, and publish the workflow.

Use this procedure to export and import a workflow:

1. As a Docusign administrator, go to Agreements >  Workflow Builder Workflows.
2. Export your workflow:
   1. Select the three-dot menu icon ![A screenshot displays three dots stacked vertically.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/A-screenshot-diplays-a-threedot-menu-icon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU1ODQsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.dDjBzbTUlt-AXQrQdesXXWqFUqstFPggLyizzL7VMhw&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays three dots stacked vertically.") next to a workflow and choose
      Export.

      A dialog box displays: Export this
      workflow?
   2. Select Export.

   The page refreshes. The workflow configuration downloads to your device
   as a .ZIP file.
3. Import your workflow to the same Docusign account or another
   account:
   1. As a Docusign administrator, go to Agreements > Workflows.
   2. Select Import Workflow.

      The Creating your workflow dialog box displays.
   3. Select Import Workflow and select
      Upload
   4. Follow the prompts to upload the .ZIP file from your device.

   The page refreshes. The imported workflow configuration
   displays.
4. Select Review & Publish if you are ready to use the
   workflow. Select Save Draft if you are not ready to
   publish.

You have completed the process. You have exported and imported a
workflow. If you imported it into a different Docusign account, review
each step to ensure that the workflow functions.

   2. Select Export.

   The page refreshes. The workflow configuration downloads to your device
   as a .ZIP file.
3. Import your workflow to the same Docusign account or another
   account:
   1. As a Docusign administrator, go to Agreements > Workflows.
   2. Select Import Workflow.

      The Creating your workflow dialog box displays.
   3. Select Import Workflow and select
      Upload
   4. Follow the prompts to upload the .ZIP file from your device.

   The page refreshes. The imported workflow configuration
   displays.
4. Select Review & Publish if you are ready to use the
   workflow. Select Save Draft if you are not ready to
   publish.

You have completed the process. You have exported and imported a
workflow. If you imported it into a different Docusign account, review
each step to ensure that the workflow functions.

title: Edit the Name of a Salesforce Connection
heading_path: Edit the Name of a Salesforce Connection
text:
Learn to edit the name of a Salesforce organization that's connected to your Docusign IAM account.

You can edit the name of a Salesforce organization that's connected to your Docusign IAM account. Editing the name of an
organization is useful when you have connected more than one Salesforce organization
to Docusign. Editing a name does not change the name of your
Salesforce organization; only how it displays in the app.

You can access the controls from your Salesforce organization or your Docusign account.

Use this procedure to edit the name of a Salesforce connection:

1. Choose your login method:
   - Login from Salesforce:
     1. [Access the Docusign Setup page as a Salesforce
        administrator](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=etv1586134206174.html&_LANG=enus)
     2. Manage Connections and the IAM Launcher: Select
        Manage.
   - Login from Docusign:
     1. Open the [Production](https://apps.docusign.com/app-center/extensionapps) or [Demo](https://apps-d.docusign.com/app-center/extensionapps) App Center site.
     2. Select the Salesforce tile.
     3. Select Manage and choose Manage
        Connections.
2. Select the three-dot menu icon ![A screenshot displays three dots stacked vertically.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/A-screenshot-diplays-a-threedot-menu-icon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU1NDcsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.ZuThDNCR-HqOxQQTRHw8Uk-TBGq8qW1mR7AAiqsggO4&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays three dots stacked vertically.") next to a connection and choose Edit Connection
   Name.
3. Edit the name in the Connection Name field and select
   Save.

   The page refreshes. A message displays: Successfully edited
   connection.

You have completed the process. You edited the name of a Salesforce
connection. This edit does not change the name of
your Salesforce organization; only the name of the connection.
