title: Access Required to Start Docusign Workflows from Salesforce
heading_path: Access Required to Start Docusign Workflows from Salesforce
text:
Learn about the permissions that you must add for users to start Docusign workflows in
Salesforce.

You can use [Workflow Builder](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=dnx1696972415150.html&_LANG=enus) to automate agreement
actions from your Docusign account.

Salesforce Flow is a workflow builder that you can use to automate agreements from
your Salesforce organization.

You can link Salesforce flows to Docusign workflows by adding a **Start Docusign
Workflow Builder** workflow step to your flow.

All users who want to start Docusign workflows from Salesforce must have proper
permissions.

Follow this link to learn how to [assign the proper
Docusign and Salesforce user permissions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=bmk1744912007661.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to prepare your Salesforce users so that they can start Docusign workflows from Salesforce.").

title: Access Required to Start Docusign Workflows from Salesforce
heading_path: Access Required to Start Docusign Workflows from Salesforce
Required Docusign permissions
text:
You must have one of the following permissions in your Docusign
account to use Docusign workflows:

| Docusign permission | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Docusign administrator** | Authorizes applications for Workflow Builder and enables users to create and run workflows by granting required user permissions. They are granted full workflow permissions by default. |
| Workflow creator | An eSignature user who creates, edits, and publishes their own workflows. They can also use any published workflow to start an agreement process. |
| Preparer | Runs published workflows to start the agreement process and monitors the progress of running instances. They have access to use any published workflow on the account to start an agreement process. |
| Participant | Users who participate in agreement workflows. They do not require Docusign credentials. |

title: Access Required to Start Docusign Workflows from Salesforce
heading_path: Access Required to Start Docusign Workflows from Salesforce
Required Salesforce permissions
text:
Users belonging to any of the following permission sets can start workflows from
Salesforce:

| Permissions | Description |
| --- | --- |
| User setting: Flow User | Provides access to Salesforce flows. |
| Permission set: **Docusign Administrator** | Provides administrative access to Docusign Apps Launcher. |
| Permission set: **Docusign User** | Provides access to eSignature in Docusign Apps Launcher. |

title: Install Docusign Apps Launcher in Your Salesforce Organization
heading_path: Install Docusign Apps Launcher in Your Salesforce Organization
text:
Learn how to install the Docusign Apps Launcher package of apps in your Salesforce
organization.

Docusign Apps Launcher for
Salesforce is a package that includes the following Docusign apps:

- [Docusign eSignature for Salesforce](https://appexchange.salesforce.com/appxListingDetail?listingId=a0N30000001taX4EAI&tab=e)
- [Docusign CLM for Salesforce](https://appexchange.salesforce.com/appxListingDetail?listingId=a0N30000004e3VUEAY&tab=e)
- [Docusign Gen for Salesforce](https://appexchange.salesforce.com/appxListingDetail?listingId=a0N3A00000FeGX6UAN&tab=e)

When you install any of these apps, Docusign installs the complete Docusign Apps Launcher package.

Use this procedure to install the Docusign Apps Launcher package of apps in your Salesforce
organization:

1. As a Salesforce administrator, choose a Docusign app from the
   AppExchange:
   1. Select the App Launcher grid ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/app-launcher-grid.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTUzNjcsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.7EWB9_f2I220IaxKnqR47saW0ecF99765W6q1GkrNA0&_LANG=enus) and choose View
      All.
   2. Select Visit AppExchange >  Go to AppExchange.
   3. Enter `Docusign eSignature`, `Gen`, or
      `CLM` in the search field.
   4. Select an app and choose Get it Now when the app page
      displays.
2. Log in to the AppExchange with Salesforce administrator credentials:

   Note: Enter production credentials even if installing in a sandbox
   organization. These credentials are used to access the AppExchange.

   1. Select Log In on the LET'S GET STARTED
      page.
   2. Select Salesforce on the Hello
      Trailblazer! dialog box that displays.
   3. Enter Salesforce production administrator credentials on the page that displays and
      select Log In.

      The Where do you want to install this package? dialog
      box displays.
3. Choose where to install the package:
   1. Select Install in Production or Install in
      Sandbox.
   2. Select I have read and agree to the terms and
      conditions.
   3. Select Confirm and Install.

      The page refreshes. The Salesforce login page displays.
4. Install the Apps Launcher package:
   1. Enter production or sandbox administrator credentials and select Log
      In.
   2. Select Install for All
      Users.

      Note: If you want to choose ​Install for Admins Only, or ​Install for Specific Profiles​, see this topic about [how Salesforce applies app permissions](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=sf.distribution_installing_packages.htm&type=5).
   3. Select Install or Upgrade.
   4. Select Done when the page refreshes.

You have completed the process. You have installed the
Docusign Apps Launcher package of apps. This package includes Docusign eSignature, CLM, and Gen. You receive an email notification when
Salesforce completes the installation.

title: Create Multiple Salesforce Connections
heading_path: Create Multiple Salesforce Connections
text:
Learn to connect multiple Salesforce organizations to your Docusign IAM account.

You can connect multiple Salesforce organizations to your Docusign
IAM account. When you connect multiple organizations, you can
build workflows for more than one Salesforce organization. Docusign does not limit the number of Salesforce organizations you
can connect to Docusign.

Example: You connect two production organizations and one
sandbox to your Docusign account. You build workflows
for each organization. Each Salesforce organization has access to fields from the
same Docusign account.

Use this procedure to connect multiple Salesforce organizations to your Docusign account:

1. Choose your login method:
   - Login from Salesforce:
     1. [Access the Docusign Setup page as a Salesforce
        administrator](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=etv1586134206174.html&_LANG=enus)
     2. Manage Connections and the IAM Launcher: Select
        Manage.
   - Login from Docusign:
     1. Open the [Production](https://apps.docusign.com/app-center/extensionapps) or [Demo](https://apps-d.docusign.com/app-center/extensionapps) App Center site.
     2. Select the Salesforce tile.
     3. Select Manage and choose Manage
        Connections.
2. Select + New Connection.
3. Configure your new connection:
   1. Connect a Salesforce account: Choose
      Salesforce Production or
      Salesforce Sandbox and select
      Next.

      ![A screenshot displays the Connect a Salesforce account with Salesforce Production selected.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Workflow_Builder_Connect_a_Salesforce_Account.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU1NDUsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.Ht6tuUGyzD3EW2sdZWXmsb_fkMIz1RlQXu99BuBPeZQ&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Connect a Salesforce account with Salesforce Production selected.")
   2. How would you like to connect this Salesforce
      account?: Choose Private or
      Shared and select
      Next.

      Important: If you log on from
      Salesforce, you must choose the Shared
      option.
   3. Salesforce Connection Name: Enter a descriptive
      name in the field and select Log In.
   4. Connect to your Salesforce organization. Salesforce does not ask for
      credentials if you are logged in to your organization:
   - Standard domain: Enter your Salesforce credentials
     and select Log In.
   - Custom domain
     1. Select Use Custom Domain.
     2. Custom Domain: Enter your domain address and
        select Continue.
     3. Enter your Salesforce credentials and select Log
        In.

   The page refreshes. A message displays: Logged in to your
   Salesforce account.

You have completed the task. You connected an additional Salesforce
organization in your Salesforce app. You can build workflows for the additional
organization.

title: Build a Docusign Workflow to Be Started By a Salesforce Flow
heading_path: Build a Docusign Workflow to Be Started By a Salesforce Flow
text:
Learn to build a Docusign workflow that can be started by a Salesforce
flow.

Before you begin
:   - Purchase a Docusign Intelligent Agreement
      Management (IAM) plan.
    - Required: Installation of the Salesforce app
      from Docusign App Center. Review the [Installing Extension Applications for Workflow Builder](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=bgb1697668738892.html&_LANG=enus) topic for more information.
    - Required: [Workflows Create permissions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gbb1696973048215.html&_LANG=enus) or be an account administrator.
    - Required: Access to a suitable template to use for your workflow
      step. Review the [Template Requirements](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=xnw1709852160602.html&_LANG=enus) topic for more information.

Workflow Builder automates agreement actions from your Docusign account.

Salesforce Flow is a workflow builder that you can use to automate agreements from your
Salesforce organization.

You can link Salesforce flows to Docusign workflows by adding a **Start Docusign
Workflow Builder** workflow step to your flow.

When a flow starts, Salesforce starts your linked Docusign workflow. Linking flows with
workflows builds an automation chain that creates agreements from Salesforce and Docusign data.

To use a Start Docusign workflow builder workflow step, create a
Docusign workflow that can be started by a
Salesforce flow.

Use this procedure to create a workflow that can be started by a flow:

1. As a Docusign administrator, create your workflow:
   1. Log on to your Docusign account and go to Agreements > Workflows.
   2. Select Create Workflow.

      The Create a workflow page displays.
   3. Select + Blank Workflow.![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_Salesforce_workflow_build_Blank_workflow_.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU2MDEsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.jeYGuZHgUNpOs9GFGDQGpwQnlKqz4IzkuYVDr92YNsQ&_LANG=enus)

      The Untitled workflow page displays.
   4. Select + Add workflow start.

      The Add workflow start page displays.
2. What starts this workflow?: Choose one of the two
   supported options from the dropdown menu:

   ![A screenshot displays the What starts this workflow step with From a Link and From an API Call highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/What-starts-this-workflow-with-From-a-link-and-From-an-API-Call-highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU2MDEsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.jeYGuZHgUNpOs9GFGDQGpwQnlKqz4IzkuYVDr92YNsQ&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the What starts this workflow step with From a Link and From an API Call highlighted.")

   - (Option 1: Recommended) From an API Call: This
     workflow starts when an API call starts the workflow. Use the following
     procedure:
     1. Select From an API Call from the dropdown
        menu.
     2. Select Next.
     3. Set up variables for dynamic information: Add
        up to ten variables, including the Record ID
        variable:

        Important: Make each
        variable a Text type. Name one variable
        Record ID (case sensitive) and the
        others Variable 1 through
        Variable 9. This is the only variable
        format that you can use in a Salesforce flow.

title: Build a Docusign Workflow to Be Started By a Salesforce Flow
heading_path: Build a Docusign Workflow to Be Started By a Salesforce Flow
text:
type. Name one variable
        Record ID (case sensitive) and the
        others Variable 1 through
        Variable 9. This is the only variable
        format that you can use in a Salesforce flow.

        1. Select New Variable and choose
           Text from the dropdown menu.
        2. Enter Variable 1 in the field.
     4. Repeat this process for any additional variables and select
        Next.![A screenshot displays the Set Up Variables step with Variable 1 highlighted in green.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Workflow_Builder_start_step_for_Salesforce_Flow.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU2MDEsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.jeYGuZHgUNpOs9GFGDQGpwQnlKqz4IzkuYVDr92YNsQ&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Set Up Variables step with Variable 1 highlighted in green.")
     5. Will this API call be started by a human or an automated
        process?:
        1. Select Automated process.
        2. Select Apply.
   - (Option 2) From a Link: This workflow starts when a participant
     opens a shared link or a link embedded on an external website. Only use this
     option for non-agreement request flows, or when you do not need to see
     status updates.
     1. Select From a Link from the dropdown
        menu.
     2. Select Apply.

   The page refreshes. Your start step displays.
3. Complete your workflow:
   1. Add more steps to your workflow. These are the steps that are started
      from the [Configure a Start Docusign Workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=azl1741206795047.html&_LANG=enus) step in your
      Salesforce flow.
   2. Select Review & Publish when you have
      finished building your workflow.

You have completed the process. You have built a workflow that can
be started by a Salesforce flow. When your flow starts, Salesforce starts your
workflow.

title: Configure a Writeback to Salesforce Step
heading_path: Configure a Writeback to Salesforce Step
text:
Learn to add and configure a Writeback to Salesforce step to update Salesforce records.

Before you begin
:   - Required: Installation of the Salesforce app
      from Docusign App Center. Review the [Installing Extension
      Applications for Workflow Builder](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=bgb1697668738892.html&_LANG=enus) article for more
      information.
    - Required: [Workflows Create
      permissions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gbb1696973048215.html&_LANG=enus) or be an account
      administrator.
    - Required: Create a workflow as described in [Create a Basic Workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uhm1697826856678.html&_LANG=enus). Add
      steps like [invitation](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=kfx1698167344720.html&_LANG=enus),
      [web forms](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gua1698120920620.html&_LANG=enus), [eSignature](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uww1698167288211.html&_LANG=enus) and
      more to gather recipients, documents, and merge fields for your
      workflow.

You can use a Writeback to Salesforce step to update Salesforce records
with information from your workflow. The Writeback to Salesforce step
updates Salesforce fields, saves documents to records, creates new records, and
more.

Administrators link workflow fields to Salesforce fields in the Writeback to
Salesforce step. When task recipients enter data in workflow fields, Docusign updates the information in the linked Salesforce record
fields.

Writeback improves efficiency and reduces errors caused by manually updating
records.

Example: You can build a workflow that includes a Collect Data
with Web Forms step and a Writeback to
Salesforce step. In the writeback step, you link the Salesforce
opportunity's Amount field to the web form's
Amount field. A workflow recipient updates and signs a
contract for an existing opportunity named Fontara. The recipient updates the
Fontara contract's Amount field from $20,000 to $25,000. When the recipient
completes the assignment, Docusign updates the Fontara record's
Amount field to $25,000.

Note: Follow the required formatting for all fields. See the
[Supported Salesforce Field Types](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=ref769a389d-135f-4fb1-8076-9b01358ffe4d.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about the field types that you can use in Salesforce workflow steps.")
topic for more information.

Use the following procedure to create a Writeback to Salesforce
step in a workflow:

1. Add a Writeback to Salesforce step as a Docusign administrator:
   1. Go to Agreements > Workflows and select Edit next to a workflow
      name.

      The workflow editor page displays.
   2. Select the Add a step
      ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Workflow_Builder_add_step_plus_sign.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU1MjIsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.tCIZsewbT5fvVwkzvologFURuHHZ2ikhLBE3mApP8Oc&_LANG=enus) icon.

      The Add New Step dialog box displays.
   3. Search for steps: Enter `Writeback to
      Salesforce` in the search field and select the
      Writeback to Salesforce step that displays.

      The workflow editor page displays with the Writeback to
      Salesforce step added.
2. Name your Writeback to Salesforce step:
   1. Select Configure on the Writeback to Salesforce
      step.

title: Configure a Writeback to Salesforce Step
heading_path: Configure a Writeback to Salesforce Step
text:
The workflow editor page displays with the Writeback to
      Salesforce step added.
2. Name your Writeback to Salesforce step:
   1. Select Configure on the Writeback to Salesforce
      step.

      The configuration dialog box displays.
   2. Step Name: Keep or edit the name. It is helpful
      to add the name of the Salesforce object, like `Account -
      Writeback to Salesforce`.
3. Where will you write to?
   1. Select connection: Select the field and choose a
      Salesforce organization that you connected to Docusign. Multiple options display if you connected more than one Salesforce
      organization.
   2. (Optional) Add New Connection: Select this
      button if you want to [connect more than one Salesforce
      organization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=ogk1719256147008.html&_LANG=enus) .
   3. (Optional) Manage Connections: Select this
      button if you want to [add, edit, refresh, or delete a
      Salesforce connection](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=uaw1719259450391.html&_LANG=enus).
   4. Salesforce object: Search for an object or
      choose one from the dropdown menu.
   5. Write settings: Select an option from the list
      that opens:

      - Update or Create: Update a record or
        create a new record if none exists.
      - Update: Update data on an existing
        record.
      - Create: Create a new record.
   6. Select Next.

      ![A screenshot displays a Writeback to Salesforce workflow step with the Connection, Salesforce object, and Write settings highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_Salesforce_Writeback_Where_will_you_write_to_Salesforce_object.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU1MjIsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.tCIZsewbT5fvVwkzvologFURuHHZ2ikhLBE3mApP8Oc&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a Writeback to Salesforce workflow step with the Connection, Salesforce object, and Write settings highlighted.")
4. Which fields are you writing to?: Select workflow fields to
   map to Salesforce fields. When workflow recipients enter data, Docusign updates Salesforce records.
   1. Select Add or Remove Fields.

      The Add or Remove Fields page displays.
   2. Select the check box next to each Salesforce field that you'd like to
      use as a workflow variable. You'll be able to use these fields in later
      steps.

      ![A screenshot displays the Add or Remove Fields page with two Salesforce fields selected and highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Workflow_Builder_Writeback_to_Salesforce_Add_or_Remove_Fields.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU1MjIsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.tCIZsewbT5fvVwkzvologFURuHHZ2ikhLBE3mApP8Oc&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Add or Remove Fields page with two Salesforce fields selected and highlighted.")
   3. Select Save and Exit.

      The Add or Remove Fields dialog box closes. The fields that you
      selected display.
   4. Fields: Add a variable or step for each
      field:

      - Manual entry: Add text for your new
        variable and select Save.

        Note: The name that you enter must exactly
        match an existing field. For example, the rule fails if you
        enter CompanyName for the
        Company\_Name
        variable.
      - Select a field from the start step or another step in this
        workflow:

title: Configure a Writeback to Salesforce Step
heading_path: Configure a Writeback to Salesforce Step
text:
field. For example, the rule fails if you
        enter CompanyName for the
        Company\_Name
        variable.
      - Select a field from the start step or another step in this
        workflow:

        Note: Steps like Read
        from HubSpot and Read from
        Salesforce display fields from up to five
        records. If more than five records are present, Salesforce
        logic decides which five to display. Selecting fields from
        multiple records helps you automate processes like mapping
        multiple Salesforce contacts to multiple Docusign participants.![A screenshot displays Read from Salesforce fields highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_Salesforce_Read_Workflow_field_selections.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU1MjIsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.tCIZsewbT5fvVwkzvologFURuHHZ2ikhLBE3mApP8Oc&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays Read from Salesforce fields highlighted.")

        You can use the fields that you select here in
        later steps like Collect Data with
        Webforms or Send Documents for
        Signature.
   5. (Optional) Overwrite if field value exists:
      Select this check box if you want the data entered by a workflow
      participant to overwrite existing Salesforce data.
   6. Repeat this process for each variable displayed.
   7. Select Next.

      ![A screenshot displays the Account ID Salesforce field and the Overwrite if field value exists checkbox highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Workflow_Builder_Writeback_to_Salesforce_Which_fields_are_you_writing_to.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU1MjIsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.tCIZsewbT5fvVwkzvologFURuHHZ2ikhLBE3mApP8Oc&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Account ID Salesforce field and the Overwrite if field value exists checkbox highlighted.")
5. Which record are you writing to?: Create a rule that
   matches a Salesforce field with an equivalent workflow field. This allows
   Workflow Builder to interact with Salesforce data.
   1. Salesforce field: Select a Salesforce field to
      match with a Docusign workflow field.

      Note: When you select a
      (reference) type field, a new field opens
      below it. Choose a child object from the new field.
   2. Operator: Choose an option like Equal
      to, Less than, and so on.
   3. Workflow field: Select a Docusign workflow field to match with your Salesforce field. The following
      options display:

      - Manual entry: Add text for your new
        variable and select Save.
      - Select a field from the start step or another workflow
        step.
   4. Select Done to save your rule.

      ![A screenshot displays a Salesforce field, an operator value of Greater than or equal to, and a workflow field all highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_Salesforce_Writeback_step_Which_record_are_you_writing_to_Rule.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU1MjIsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.tCIZsewbT5fvVwkzvologFURuHHZ2ikhLBE3mApP8Oc&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a Salesforce field, an operator value of Greater than or equal to, and a workflow field all highlighted.")
6. (Optional) Add more rules:
   - +AND: Workflow Builder only imports Salesforce data that
     matches all of your rules.
   - +OR: Workflow Builder imports Salesforce data that matches
     any of your rules.
7. Select Apply.

title: Configure a Writeback to Salesforce Step
heading_path: Configure a Writeback to Salesforce Step
text:
:
   - +AND: Workflow Builder only imports Salesforce data that
     matches all of your rules.
   - +OR: Workflow Builder imports Salesforce data that matches
     any of your rules.
7. Select Apply.

   The Writeback to Salesforce dialog box closes. The page displays your new step.

You have completed the process. You have built a Writeback to
Salesforce step in your workflow. When this step is triggered in a workflow, Docusign updates field data on Salesforce records that you mapped.

title: Delete a Salesforce Connection to Your Docusign IAM Account
heading_path: Delete a Salesforce Connection to Your Docusign IAM Account
text:
Learn to delete the Salesforce connection to your Docusign IAM account.

You can connect Salesforce organizations to your Docusign IAM account. When you connect to Docusign, you can
build workflows that read from and write to your Salesforce records. You can delete
a Salesforce connection if you no longer want to access that organization.

You can access the delete controls through your Salesforce organization or your Docusign account.

Use this procedure to delete a Salesforce connection:

1. Choose your login method:
   - Login from Salesforce:
     1. [Access the Docusign Setup page as a Salesforce
        administrator](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=etv1586134206174.html&_LANG=enus)
     2. Manage Connections and the IAM Launcher: Select
        Manage.
   - Login from Docusign:
     1. Open the [Production](https://apps.docusign.com/app-center/extensionapps) or [Demo](https://apps-d.docusign.com/app-center/extensionapps) App Center site.
     2. Select the Salesforce tile.
     3. Select Manage and choose Manage
        Connections.
2. Select the three-dot menu icon ![A screenshot displays three dots stacked vertically.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/A-screenshot-diplays-a-threedot-menu-icon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU1NTksInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.LxRsze5vdAFzIizVNjS9cFSY9m79jQCOFxePyv_Vakc&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays three dots stacked vertically.") next to a connection and choose Delete
   Connection.

   A dialog box displays: Delete this connection?
3. Delete this connection?: Enter `DELETE` in the field and select Delete Connection.![A screenshot displays the Refresh this connection dialog box with the word Refresh and the Refresh Connection button highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Workflow_Builder_Delete_Connection_dialog_box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU1NTksInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.LxRsze5vdAFzIizVNjS9cFSY9m79jQCOFxePyv_Vakc&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Refresh this connection dialog box with the word Refresh and the Refresh Connection button highlighted.")

   The dialog box closes. The Connections page displays with a message: Successfully deleted
   connection.

You have completed the task. You deleted a connection to a
Salesforce organization.

heading_path: Update the Salesforce Application
text: Learn to update the Salesforce app to access new features.

title: Update the Salesforce Application
heading_path: Update the Salesforce Application
Before you begin
text:
- [Purchase a Docusign
  Intelligent Agreement
  Management (IAM) for Sales application](https://www.docusign.com/intelligent-agreement-management?utm_source=bing&utm_medium=cpc&utm_campaign=GBL_XX_PRF_UPS_2410_SEMIAM&utm_term=docusign%20iam&campaignid=569731866&adgroupid=1178678316174006&location=84406&device=c&matchtype=e&extensionid=&creative=73667602015411&keyword=docusign%20iam&placement=&network=o&msclkid=3287ee64f2c01f69bcd686665f40e7da).
- [Install the Salesforce Application from the Docusign App Center](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=jxo1706295810553.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to install the Salesforce app in your Docusign account and connect it to your Salesforce organization.").

Docusign continues to release new versions of the Salesforce
application. To access new features, update your version of the app.

Tip: Wait at least 30 minutes after installing before
you configure a workflow step. Docusign must index all Salesforce
fields to make them available for use.

You can access the upgrade controls through your Salesforce organization or your Docusign account.

Use this procedure to update your version of the Docusign Salesforce app:

1. Choose your login method:
   - Login from Salesforce:
     1. [Access the Docusign
        Setup page as a Salesforce administrator](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=etv1586134206174.html&_LANG=enus)
     2. Manage Connections and the IAM Launcher: Select
        Manage.
   - Login from Docusign:
     1. Open the [Production](https://apps.docusign.com/app-center/extensionapps) or [Demo](https://apps-d.docusign.com/app-center/extensionapps) App Center site.
     2. Select the Salesforce tile.
     3. Select Manage and choose Manage
        Connections.
2. Select the three-dot menu icon ![A screenshot displays three dots stacked vertically.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/A-screenshot-diplays-a-threedot-menu-icon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU1NDAsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.t_PuJKqG9HRT-aROk7K1SmpFHgomK5vPXhPX8z7h0y4&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays three dots stacked vertically.") next to a connection and choose Reload Objects.

   A dialog box displays: Refresh this
   connection?
3. Refresh this connection?: Enter `REFRESH`
   in the field and select Refresh Connection. ![A screenshot displays the Refresh this connection dialog box with the word Refresh and the Refresh Connection button highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Workflow_Builder_Refresh_Connection_dialog_box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU1NDAsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.t_PuJKqG9HRT-aROk7K1SmpFHgomK5vPXhPX8z7h0y4&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Refresh this connection dialog box with the word Refresh and the Refresh Connection button highlighted.")

   The dialog box closes. The Connections page displays with a message:
   Successfully refreshed.

You have completed the process. You have updated the Salesforce app. You can access the latest workflow builder features.

title: Pre-Built Document Templates Included in the Docusign IAM for Sales Solution
heading_path: Pre-Built Document Templates Included in the Docusign IAM for Sales Solution
text:
Learn about the pre-built document templates that install in your Salesforce organization
from the Docusign IAM for Sales Solution.

You can install the Docusign IAM for Sales Solution in your
Salesforce organization. This solution installs a series of pre-built Docusign document templates in your Salesforce organization. Each
template represents a common use case, like creating and sending an NDA or MSA.

These templates are functional and do not require additional
configuration.

Installing these templates gives you a quick way to implement Docusign workflows in your Salesforce organization. You can
customize these templates to fit your exact use cases.

The following images display the contents of the pre-built templates that install
with the Docusign IAM for Sales Solution:

title: Pre-Built Document Templates Included in the Docusign IAM for Sales Solution
heading_path: Pre-Built Document Templates Included in the Docusign IAM for Sales Solution
NDA template
text: ![A screenshot displays a non-disclosure agreement template with signature fields added.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/IAM_for_Sales_Solutions_Package_NDA_template.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTUzODYsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.L-NY7bP-X4fawz0deook3qIuuxu5iO5xYnO8vmDML88&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a non-disclosure agreement template with signature fields added.")

title: Pre-Built Document Templates Included in the Docusign IAM for Sales Solution
heading_path: Pre-Built Document Templates Included in the Docusign IAM for Sales Solution
MSA template

title: Pre-Built Agreement Desk Statuses Included in the Docusign IAM for Sales Solution
heading_path: Pre-Built Agreement Desk Statuses Included in the Docusign IAM for Sales Solution
text:
Learn about the pre-built Agreement Desk statuses that install in your Salesforce
organization from the Docusign IAM for Sales Solution.

You can install the Docusign IAM for Sales Solution in your
Salesforce organization. This solution installs a series of pre-built Docusign Agreement Desk statuses in your Salesforce organization.
Each status represents a common action, like Legal Review or Ready for Signature.

These statuses are functional and do not require additional configuration.
Installing these statuses gives you a quick way to implement Docusign workflows in your Salesforce organization.

The following table displays the pre-built Agreement Desk statuses that install with
the Docusign IAM for Sales Solution:

title: Pre-Built Agreement Desk Statuses Included in the Docusign IAM for Sales Solution
heading_path: Pre-Built Agreement Desk Statuses Included in the Docusign IAM for Sales Solution
Agreement Desk status list
text:
| Status name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| New (default) | A new request has been created. |
| Agreement Generated; Ready for Business Review | An agreement has been generated and needs sending for customer review. |
| New Agreement Uploaded | A new document has been uploaded. |
| External Customer Review | An agreement has been sent for customer review. |
| Legal Review | An agreement must be reviewed by the legal team. |
| Ready for Signature | An agreement has been approved and can be sent to the customer for signing. |
| Waiting for Signature | An agreement has been sent but not signed. |
| Closed | An agreement has been reviewed and signed. |

title: Agreement Desk for Salesforce Report Filters
heading_path: Agreement Desk for Salesforce Report Filters
text:
Learn about the filters that you can use to build Agreement Desk for Salesforce reports.

Important: This feature is only available to IAM for
Sales customers. Contact [Docusign support](https://support.docusign.com/s/?language=en_US&_gl=1*74elk4*_gcl_au*MTY5ODQyMjA2MC4xNzY5NjM1OTA1LjE5ODM3MTQzNjguMTc2OTYzNjIwNC4xNzY5NjM2MjA0) or [Docusign
sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales) for more information.

You can build custom Salesforce reports to filter and display your [Docusign
Agreement Desk for Salesforce requests](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=con007504df-80b7-41f7-aa5f-1daa72bcc257.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about using Docusign Agreement Desk in Salesforce to streamline your agreement process."). To build your report, you add
columns, rows, and filters. These data points determine the Agreement Desk requests
that Salesforce delivers in your report results.

You can sort and filter your Docusign for Salesforce data based on
the following data points:

Table 1. Agreement Desk for Salesforce report filters

| Filter type | Purpose |
| --- | --- |
| Docusign Agreement Desk Requests: ID | The unique system ID for the Agreement Desk entry itself. Use this option to find one specific record or prevent duplicates in a list. |
| CreatedDate | The date the agreement request was first started. Use this option for measuring time to signature or how many new requests were created in a time span. |
| LastActivityDate | The last time someone interacted with the request, like editing or sending an email. Use this option to identify information like stalled agreements that haven't been updated recently. |
| Owner | The person currently responsible for the request, like a legal or sales representative. Use this option to view who is managing your agreements. |
| RecordID | The ID of the Salesforce record. Use this option to view the record that this agreement is linked to. |
| RecordName | The name of the linked Salesforce record, like Burlington Textiles. Use this option to display business context for the agreement. |
| RecordType | The type of record displayed. Use this option to filter different types of Salesforce records, like sales opportunities or partner accounts. |
| RequestID | The unique identifier specific to a Docusign request. Use this option to track a request across the Docusign IAM platform. |
| RequestType | The agreement's categorization, like MSA, NDA, or SOW. Use this option to view the types of agreements that your team is working on. |
| Status | The stage of an agreement. Use this option to view the progress of your agreements, like Draft, In Review, Signed, or Canceled. |
| Submitter | The person who originally completed the intake form or sent the email to start the process. Use this option to view the people responsible for initiating agreements. |
| Title | The subject line or name given to the specific agreement request. Use this option to identify a deal without opening a request. |

title: Supported Salesforce Field Types
heading_path: Supported Salesforce Field Types
text:
Learn about the field types that you can use in Salesforce workflow
steps.

Note: Docusign continues to add support for new field
types. If you can't access new field types or configurations, open your Salesforce step and save it again.

When you build
Salesforce workflow steps, you match
Salesforce fields with other workflow fields. Workflow Builder formats data from Salesforce fields in specific ways.

You can add the following field types to Salesforce steps:

| Field type | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Boolean | Add true/false options. |
| Checkbox | Add a checkbox to select true/false or yes/no options. |
| Currency | Add a unified currency field to capture, map, and manage monetary amounts and currency codes throughout workflows. |
| Date | Add a date with no time. Unlike adding dates into string values, workflows can use date fields to schedule actions. |
| Datetime | Add a date and time. Unlike adding dates and times into string values, workflows can use datetime fields to schedule actions or timestamp events. |
| Double | Add a number with decimal points. |
| Dropdown | Add list of allowed values in a dropdown menu. |
| Email | Add an email address. You must use valid formatting, like user@company.com. |
| Encryptedstring | Add a field for encrypted text that can be used for secure data handling, such as storing sensitive information like passwords or API keys. Workflows can use this data securely, without showing it in plain text. |
| ID | Add the unique identifier of a Salesforce record, like a company or contact. |
| Integer | Add a whole number that can be positive, negative, or zero, and does not include fractions or decimals. |
| List | Add a list of items. |
| Phone | Add a phone number. You must use a valid country code, like `+61 xxx xxx xxxx` |
| Picklist | Add a list from which users can make selections. |
| String | Add human-readable text, like words and sentences. |
| Text | Add fields into which users can enter text. |
| Textarea | Add text entry fields with attributes like minimum and maximum character limits, placeholder text, and validation rules. |

title: Supported Salesforce Field Types
heading_path: Supported Salesforce Field Types
Date field formatting
text:
Due to Salesforce date restrictions, you can only use the following date formats in a
Writeback to Salesforce workflow step:

- YYYY-MM-DD
- YYYY-MM-DDThh:ssZ
- YYYY-MM-DDThh:ss.sssZ

- YYYY/MM/DD
- YYYY/MM/DDThh:ssZ
- YYYY/MM/DDThh:ss.sssZ

title: Supported Salesforce Field Types
heading_path: Supported Salesforce Field Types
Phone number field formatting
text:
Phone number format restrictions

Due to Salesforce restrictions, you can only use the following formats in
Writeback to Salesforce or Read from Salesforce steps:

- PhoneNumberFormat.E164
- PhoneNumberFormat.INTERNATIONAL
- PhoneNumberFormat.NATIONAL

Country code formatting

Add a country code if you
are using a phonenumber field in the following Workflow Builder
for Salesforce options:

- Read from Salesforce
- Writeback to Salesforce:
  - Update or Create
  - Update

Add the country code in front of the contact number. For example,
for a phone number with a code of +61, use the following format: `+61
xxx xxx xxxx`.

If your Writeback to Salesforce step has the
Create option selected, you are not required to use a
country code. However, using a country code is still recommended.

![A screenshot displays the Where will you write to stage of a Workflow Builder Writeback to Salesforce step, with the Write settings section highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_Salesforce_Country_Code_rules.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU0NDgsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.Cv060P5yIT6BFBb5tyDszjylL2JCQ03MKKi-LjHfUws&_LANG=enus)

Ensure that you also add a country code to the Salesforce record's phone
field:![A screenshot displays the phone field with a country code highlighted on a Salesforce contact.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Workflow_Builder_phone_number_country_code_on_Salesforce_record.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU0NDgsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.Cv060P5yIT6BFBb5tyDszjylL2JCQ03MKKi-LjHfUws&_LANG=enus)

Correcting phone numbers

- Docusign adds the United States country code of
  `+1` to Salesforce records lacking a country
  code.
- Workflow task recipients can change incorrect country codes.
- Workflow task recipients can also change incorrect phone numbers.

Example 1:: A workflow sends a task from a Salesforce
record with no country code. Rather than refusing to advance the workflow task,
Docusign automatically adds the United States country code
of `+1` to the phone number. The organization is in the
United States, so the workflow recipient keeps the `+1`
country code and completes the task. The Writeback to Salesforce step adds the
`+1` country code to the
record.

Example 2: A workflow sends a task from a
Salesforce record with no country code. Docusign adds the
United States country code of `+1` to the task. The
recipient knows that the organization is from Australia and changes the country
code to `+61`. The recipient completes the task. The
Writeback to Salesforce step changes the record's country code to
`+61`.

Example 3: A workflow
sends a task from a Salesforce record with an incorrect phone number of
`1234`. Rather than refusing to advance the workflow
task, Docusign sends the task with an editable phone number.
The recipient enters the correct phone number of `+1
555-666-7777` and completes the task. The Writeback to Salesforce
step changes the record's phone number from `1234` to
`+1 555-666-7777`.

title: Supported Salesforce Field Types
heading_path: Supported Salesforce Field Types
Checkbox field formatting
text:
You can connect Salesforce checkbox fields to workflow checkbox fields. This is
useful when you want your workflow to read from and writeback to Salesforce yes/no
fields.

Example: Your Salesforce organization has an Express
Shipping checkbox field. The checkbox has values of
Checked = Yes (true) and Unchecked = No
(false).

You want workflow task recipients to add express shipping to their orders by
selecting the checkbox. You configure the following steps in your workflow:

- Read from Salesforce: You add the Salesforce
  Express Shipping (checkbox) field.
- Collect Data with Web Forms: You add an
  Express Shipping checkbox field and map it to the
  Salesforce checkbox.
- Writeback to Salesforce: You map the Salesforce Express
  Shipping field to the web form field.

A workflow task recipient selects the Express Shipping
checkbox on the web form and completes the task. The Writeback to Salesforce step
adds a check to the record's Express Shipping checkbox.

title: Supported Salesforce Field Types
heading_path: Supported Salesforce Field Types
Dropdown field formatting
text:
You can connect Salesforce dropdown fields to workflow dropdown fields. This is
useful when you want your workflow to read from and writeback to Salesforce dropdown
fields.

Example: Your Salesforce organization has a Product
Size dropdown field. The dropdown field has values of
Large, Medium, and
Small.

You want workflow task recipients to select a size when ordering shirts. You
configure the following steps in your workflow:

- Read from Salesforce: You add the Salesforce
  Product Size (picklist) field.
- Collect Data with Web Forms: You add a
  Product Size (picklist) dropdown field and map it
  to the Salesforce checkbox.
- Writeback to Salesforce: You map the Salesforce
  Product Size field to the web form field.

A workflow task recipient selects the Medium option from the
web form's Product Size dropdown menu and completes the task.
The Writeback to Salesforce step changes the record's Product
Size value to Medium.

title: Pre-Built Workflows Included in the Docusign IAM for Sales Solution
heading_path: Pre-Built Workflows Included in the Docusign IAM for Sales Solution
text:
Learn about the pre-built workflows that install in your Salesforce organization from the
Docusign IAM for Sales Solution.

You can install the Docusign IAM for Sales Solution in your
Salesforce organization. This solution installs a series of pre-built Docusign workflows in your Salesforce organization. Each workflow
represents a common workflow use case, like creating and sending an NDA or MSA.
These workflows are functional and do not require step connection or
field mapping. Installing these workflows gives you a quick way to implement Docusign workflows in your Salesforce organization.

You can customize these workflows to fit your exact use cases. You can also duplicate
them to make new workflows that are similar to the original. And you can
export these workflows to quickly add a workflow to a different organization. For
example, you can build and test a workflow in your sandbox before exporting it to
production.

The following tables display the contents of the pre-built workflows that install
with the Docusign IAM for Sales Solution:

title: Pre-Built Workflows Included in the Docusign IAM for Sales Solution
heading_path: Pre-Built Workflows Included in the Docusign IAM for Sales Solution
Workflows included:
text:
- Agreement Desk Request: MSA Submitted
- Gen and Send NDA (Account Object)
- Embedded Signing NDA (Account Object)
- Agreement Desk Request: MSA Ready for Signature
- Agreement Desk - MSA: Request Closed with Data writeback
- Agreement Desk Request: MSA New Agreement Uploaded
- MSA with Legal Request (Opportunity Object)
- General Legal Request (Opportunity Object)
- General Legal Request (Account Object)
- All Agreement Desk - Envelope Completed (ALL) > Request Closed
- All Agreement Desk: Envelope Sent - Status Change to Waiting for Signature
- All Agreement Desk - Add Owner and Groups

title: Pre-Built Workflows Included in the Docusign IAM for Sales Solution
heading_path: Pre-Built Workflows Included in the Docusign IAM for Sales Solution

|  | Path End |  |

title: Agreement Desk for Salesforce User Access
heading_path: Agreement Desk for Salesforce User Access
text:
Learn about granting Salesforce user access to the Docusign Agreement
Requests component.

Important: This feature is only available to IAM for
Sales customers. Contact [Docusign support](https://support.docusign.com/s/?language=en_US&_gl=1*74elk4*_gcl_au*MTY5ODQyMjA2MC4xNzY5NjM1OTA1LjE5ODM3MTQzNjguMTc2OTYzNjIwNC4xNzY5NjM2MjA0) or [Docusign
sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales) for more information.

Salesforce users require the following access to use Agreement Desk:

- [A Docusign Apps
  Launcher user](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=uwf1586134275195.html&_LANG=enus).
- [A Docusign Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) user with Agreement Desk access](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=msb1736279079453&topicId=yao1749836146880.html&_LANG=enus).

Docusign administrators grant access to these user types. Approved users activate their access by selecting a Docusign button or component and entering their Docusign and Salesforce credentials.

title: Agreement Desk for Salesforce User Access
heading_path: Agreement Desk for Salesforce User Access
How does the​ component display for different users?
text:
User access dictates how the Docusign Agreement Requests
component displays on a layout. Depending on the access granted, Docusign displays or hides the component on Salesforce
layouts.

| Salesforce user access | How Docusign Agreement Desk displays |
| --- | --- |
| Docusign Apps Launcher and Docusign IAM access | Users can see the Docusign Agreement Desk​ component and act on the agreements displayed. |
| Docusign Apps Launcher access only | Users see a blank component with a spinning wheel icon instead of the Docusign Agreement Desk​ component. |
| No Docusign user access | The Docusign Agreement Requests component does not display on records. |

title: Format String Values in Salesforce Steps
heading_path: Format String Values in Salesforce Steps
text:
Learn to create concatenated values in Salesforce step string fields.

You can create Salesforce workflow steps that read and update Salesforce data. You
can add string fields to Salesforce steps.

String fields are human-readable text like words, IDs, or simple labels. Workflow Builder uses
string fields as text, not as number or date values used in calculations or
scheduling.

Because Workflow Builder formats string value fields as rich text inputs, you can enter text and add variables in the same field. This is useful when you want Salesforce to store a single, human-readable value built from multiple pieces of data.

Important: The following guidelines apply to formatting Workflow Builder for Salesforce string fields:

- Add text and variables in Writeback to Salesforce fields and rules.
- Add text and variables in Read from Salesforce rules.
- Add multiple variables by selecting the string field's + icon.
- Add up to three string variables in one string field.
- Enter a space after text if you want to add variables after it.
- Add only string variables. You cannot add Boolean, array, or other field types.

title: Format String Values in Salesforce Steps
heading_path: Format String Values in Salesforce Steps
Writeback to Salesforce field formatting
text:
You can add text and variables when formatting Writeback to Salesforce fields. Select a Salesforce field and link it to variables from other workflow steps. You can enter text and up to three string values to a single string field.

Example 1: You create a workflow that includes a
Writeback to Salesforce step. You build the step to create a
new Account record if one doesn't exist. You want each new record's Account Name field
to contain the following content:

- The words `New Customer`
- The `First Name` and `Last Name` variables of a
  Salesforce contact

In the Which fields are you writing to? section, you add
the Account Name string field. You enter the words
`New Customer:`  followed by a space. You select the
field's + icon and add the contact's First
Name and Last Name variables.

A
workflow recipient completes a task for a new customer named Alex Smith. The
Writeback to Salesforce step in your workflow creates a new record. The Account Name
field of the new record displays this text: New Customer: Alex
Smith.![A screen capture displays the process of entering text and variables into the Account Name field of a Salesforce step.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_Salesforce_and_HubSpot_concatenated_string_fields.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU0NTEsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.Qx0kfhE9vMik_eeDOfJNTm8zAU-LT--sFqkpnUpUwLE&_LANG=enus "A screen capture displays the process of entering text and variables into the Account Name field of a Salesforce step.")

Example 2: You create a workflow that includes a Writeback
to Salesforce step. Your organization has a custom string field named
Products Purchasing. When customers purchase multiple products,
you want all products combined in a single field, rather than in separate fields.

In
the Which fields are you writing to? section, you add the
Products Purchasing string field. You add Opportunity string variables for
`Product 1`
`Product 2`, and `Product 3`.

A workflow
recipient completes a task for a customer that is purchasing cups, straws, and lids.
The Writeback to Salesforce step adds the three products to the customer's
Salesforce record. The record's Products Purchasing field displays this text:
Cups, Straws, Lids.

title: Format String Values in Salesforce Steps
heading_path: Format String Values in Salesforce Steps
Read from Salesforce and Writeback to Salesforce rule formatting
text:
You can add text and variables when formatting Read from and Writeback to Salesforce rules. When you create a rule, you can select a Salesforce string field and link it to workflow fields from other steps. You can format the matching workflow field with text and up to three string values.

Writeback to Salesforce example: You create a workflow that includes a Writeback
to Salesforce step.

In the Which record are you writing
to? section, you add the Owner ID string
field. You select the Equal to operator. For
Workflow field, you select the field's
+ icon and add the Owner ID,
First Name, and Last Name
variables.

A workflow recipient completes a task for a customer with an owner
named Terry Jones. The Writeback to Salesforce step only updates Salesforce records
that match the Owner ID, First Name, and Last Name of Terry Jones.![A screen capture displays the process of adding three variables into the workflow field of a Salesforce step rule.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_Salesforce_Rule_concatenated_string_fields.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU0NTEsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.Qx0kfhE9vMik_eeDOfJNTm8zAU-LT--sFqkpnUpUwLE&_LANG=enus "A screen capture displays the process of adding three variables into the workflow field of a Salesforce step rule.")

Read from Salesforce example: You create a workflow that includes a Read from
Salesforce step.

In the Which records are you reading
from? section, you add the Owner string field.
You select the Equal to operator. For Workflow
field, you enter the words `Owner name and email:` followed by a space. You select the field's +
icon and add the Signer: Name and Signer:
Email variables from a web form.

A workflow recipient
completes a task for a customer named Fontara with an owner named Terry Jones. The
Read from Salesforce step adds records with an owner name of Terry
Jones and an email of terry.jones@fontara.com.
Your workflow can use fields from these records as variables in later
steps.

title: Pre-Built Agreement Desk Web Forms Included in the Docusign IAM for Sales Solution
heading_path: Pre-Built Agreement Desk Web Forms Included in the Docusign IAM for Sales Solution
text:
Learn about the pre-built web form that installs in your Salesforce organization
from the Docusign IAM for Sales Solution.

You can install the Docusign IAM for Sales Solution in your
Salesforce organization. This solution installs a series of pre-built Docusign Agreement Desk web form templates in your Salesforce
organization. Each template represents a common use case, like creating and sending
an NDA or MSA.

These templates are functional and do not require additional
configuration.

Installing these templates gives you a quick way to implement Docusign workflows in your Salesforce organization. You can
customize these templates to fit your exact use cases.

The following images display the contents of the pre-built templates that install
with the Docusign IAM for Sales Solution:

title: Customize Agreement Manager for Salesforce Columns
heading_path: Customize Agreement Manager for Salesforce Columns
text:
Learn to configure the agreement columns displayed in Agreement Manager for Salesforce.

Important: This feature is only available to IAM for
Sales customers. Contact [Docusign support](https://support.docusign.com/s/?language=en_US&_gl=1*74elk4*_gcl_au*MTY5ODQyMjA2MC4xNzY5NjM1OTA1LjE5ODM3MTQzNjguMTc2OTYzNjIwNC4xNzY5NjM2MjA0) or [Docusign
sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales) for more information.

The Docusign Completed Agreements component displays Agreement Manager on Salesforce records. You can [customize the agreement filters column view](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=xnz1717535025265&topicId=qrh1702945502942.html&_LANG=enus) displayed in the Docusign Completed Agreements component.

When you customize your column view, the following rules apply:

- Your changes do not affect other users.
- Your custom settings remain when you log off and on.
- Your settings apply to all records and objects that contain the Docusign Completed Agreements component.

Note: Your settings *do not* remain if you empty your browser cache, use a
new browser, or open an incognito session.

1. Select the Customize columns gear ![A screenshot displays the Customize Columns gear for Agreement Manager for Salesforce agreement columns.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-Customize-Columns-gear-for-Navigator-for-Salesforce-agreement-columns.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU3NDcsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.2jb2HZs5KB-TQxtjfJf2mctEmBlt8cKFLlG0sGCwNhA&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Customize Columns gear for Agreement Manager for Salesforce agreement columns.") icon next to the filters.

   The Customize columns dialog box opens. A number of
   fields are turned on (to the right) by default. These fields represent the
   columns in the agreement list table. Columns display in the order that the
   fields are represented in the dialog box.

   ![A screenshot of the Customize columns dialog box that shows the default selections.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Completed-documents-view_Customize-columns-dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU3NDcsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.2jb2HZs5KB-TQxtjfJf2mctEmBlt8cKFLlG0sGCwNhA&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Customize columns dialog box that shows the default selections.")
2. Search for columns in the Find columns field or scroll
   through the list.
3. Select the toggles to turn the associated field on (to the right) or off (to
   the left).
4. (Optional) Re-order the columns: Select the dots next to a field name and drag
   it to a new location:

   ![A snippet of the Customize columns dialog box that highlights the 'drag to' feature for reordering column headers.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Completed-documents_Customize-columns-dialog-box_drag-and-drop.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU3NDcsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.2jb2HZs5KB-TQxtjfJf2mctEmBlt8cKFLlG0sGCwNhA&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Customize columns dialog box that highlights the 'drag to' feature for reordering column headers.")
5. Select Save after configuring the columns.

   The dialog box closes.

You have completed the process. Docusign
displays your new column configuration in the Docusign Agreements
component.

title: Prepare Your Salesforce Organization to Start Docusign Workflows
heading_path: Prepare Your Salesforce Organization to Start Docusign Workflows
text:
Learn to prepare your Salesforce organization so that administrators can link Salesforce
flows to Docusign workflows.

Important: Do not follow these steps if you already have
Docusign Apps Launcher v7.4 or higher installed. Only follow these
steps to add a Start Docusign Workflow Builder workflow action to
an organization with v7.3 or lower installed.

Before you begin
:   - [Purchase a Docusign
      Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) plan](https://www.docusign.com/intelligent-agreement-management?utm_source=bing&utm_medium=cpc&utm_campaign=GBL_XX_PRF_UPS_2410_SEMIAM&utm_term=docusign%20iam&campaignid=569731866&adgroupid=1178678316174006&location=84406&device=c&matchtype=e&extensionid=&creative=73667602015411&keyword=docusign%20iam&placement=&network=o&msclkid=3287ee64f2c01f69bcd686665f40e7da).
    - [Install Docusign Apps Launcher version 7.4 or
      higher](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=ogj1586134254571.html&_LANG=enus).
    - [Install the Salesforce Application from the Docusign App Center](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=bgb1697668738892.html&_LANG=enus).
    - [Assign Workflows Create permissions to your Docusign user](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gbb1696973048215.html&_LANG=enus), or be an account administrator.
    - [Build a workflow to be started by your Salesforce
      flow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=ovh1741282680990.html&_LANG=enus).

You can link Salesforce flows to Docusign workflows by adding a Start Docusign
Workflow Builder workflow action to your Salesforce flow.

When a flow starts, Salesforce starts your linked Docusign workflow. Linking flows with
workflows builds an automation chain that creates agreements from Salesforce and Docusign data.

Before you can link flows and Docusign workflows, you must prepare your Salesforce
organization.

Use this procedure to prepare your organization:

1. As a Salesforce and Docusign administrator, [Disconnect and reconnect Docusign and Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=ntr1586134210309.html&_LANG=enus).

   Important: Disconnecting removes Docusign access from all Salesforce users. Disconnecting
   does not remove users from your connected Docusign
   account.
2. [Open Docusign Apps
   Launcher](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=etv1586134206174.html&_LANG=enus).
3. Go to Docusign Setup >  User Management.
4. [Re-add your Docusign
   users](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=uwf1586134275195.html&_LANG=enus).

   Important: Users must [re-activate their Docusign access in Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=vbw1648698462634&topicId=ymi1586134213877.html&_LANG=enus). After re-activating, users can start Docusign workflows.

You have completed the task. You have prepared your Salesforce
organization. You can add a Start Docusign Workflow Builder workflow
action to a Salesforce flow. [Prepare your users for
starting workflows from Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=bmk1744912007661.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to prepare your Salesforce users so that they can start Docusign workflows from Salesforce.").

title: Open a Request from the Agreement Requests Component
heading_path: Open a Request from the Agreement Requests Component
text:
Learn to open a request from the Agreement Requests component to view or act
on.

title: Open a Request from the Agreement Requests Component
heading_path: Open a Request from the Agreement Requests Component
Before you begin
text:
Important: This feature is only available to IAM for Sales
customers. Contact [Docusign support](https://support.docusign.com/s/?language=en_US&_gl=1*74elk4*_gcl_au*MTY5ODQyMjA2MC4xNzY5NjM1OTA1LjE5ODM3MTQzNjguMTc2OTYzNjIwNC4xNzY5NjM2MjA0) or [Docusign
sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales) for more information.

[Agreement Desk for Salesforce User Access](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=ref9c5cd52b-33e2-4b25-a821-a666d4bd222e.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about granting Salesforce user access to the Docusign Agreement Requests component.")

Agreement Desk is a central platform for streamlining your entire agreement process
within Docusign. You can use Agreement Desk to submit and manage
requests, negotiate and edit agreements, and use AI to automate contracts. When you
add the Agreement Requests component to your layouts, users can review, manage,
sign, and send agreements without logging on to Docusign.

You can also open and act on requests from the Agreement Requests component.

Use this procedure to open a request:

1. Open a record and look for the Agreement Requests component.

   Note: A Salesforce administrator may have placed the
   component on a tab labeled Agreement Requests or
   something similar. The component features the word
   Requests at the top.
2. Search Request Titles: Enter search terms in the
   field.

   ![The Requests section of a Salesforce record displays a highlighted search field with Legal Request entered.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Agreement_Desk_for_Salesforce_search_results.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU3MDMsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.unSDda2GD6lpMLzm70zcgxs5bbTpqLtpLCMlf5iDcMc&_LANG=enus "The Requests section of a Salesforce record displays a highlighted search field with Legal Request entered.")
3. Press [Enter] to see all results, or select a result
   from the dropdown menu that opens beneath.

   The component displays requests sent from this record and its child
   objects. The component does not display results for parent objects, or Agreement
   Desk requests sent from your Docusign web
   account.
4. Select a request from the displayed list.

   ![A screenshot displays the Requests section of a Salesforce record with an Agreement Desk request highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Agreement_Desk_for_Salesforce_request_selected.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU3MDMsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.unSDda2GD6lpMLzm70zcgxs5bbTpqLtpLCMlf5iDcMc&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Requests section of a Salesforce record with an Agreement Desk request highlighted.")

   The request opens in a new browser tab.
5. Use any control to view or act on the request:
   - Overview: View the request's activity feed. You can
     also send an email about the request.
   - Details: View the request's details, like request
     type, requestor name, owner, Salesforce account, and so on.
   - Documents: Preview, download, edit, upload a new
     version, and see the version history of all documents submitted with the
     request. You can also add content to the request by uploading or generating
     documents.
   - Approvals: View all approvals for this
     request.
   - Envelopes: View all envelopes sent for signature from
     this request.
   - Follow: Receive notifications about this request as
     it moves through the review and approval process.
   - Share: Send an email about this request to a user or
     group. You can also copy a link to share this request through another
     messaging platform.

heading_path: Open a Request from the Agreement Requests Component
Before you begin
text:
s through the review and approval process.
   - Share: Send an email about this request to a user or
     group. You can also copy a link to share this request through another

   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Agreement_Desk_for_Salesforce_open_AD_request.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU3MDMsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.unSDda2GD6lpMLzm70zcgxs5bbTpqLtpLCMlf5iDcMc&_LANG=enus)

title: Prepare Your Salesforce Organization to Use a Start with Docusign Action
heading_path: Prepare Your Salesforce Organization to Use a Start with Docusign Action
text: Learn to prepare your Salesforce organization to start Docusign Workflow Builder workflows from Salesforce.

title: Prepare Your Salesforce Organization to Use a Start with Docusign Action
heading_path: Prepare Your Salesforce Organization to Use a Start with Docusign Action
Before you begin
text:
[Purchase a Docusign Intelligent Agreement
Management (IAM) for Sales application](https://www.docusign.com/intelligent-agreement-management?utm_source=bing&utm_medium=cpc&utm_campaign=GBL_XX_PRF_UPS_2410_SEMIAM&utm_term=docusign%20iam&campaignid=569731866&adgroupid=1178678316174006&location=84406&device=c&matchtype=e&extensionid=&creative=73667602015411&keyword=docusign%20iam&placement=&network=o&msclkid=3287ee64f2c01f69bcd686665f40e7da)

Important:

- This feature is only available to IAM for Sales customers. Contact [Docusign support](https://support.docusign.com/s/?language=en_US&_gl=1*74elk4*_gcl_au*MTY5ODQyMjA2MC4xNzY5NjM1OTA1LjE5ODM3MTQzNjguMTc2OTYzNjIwNC4xNzY5NjM2MjA0) or [Docusign
  sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales) for more information.
- Do not follow these steps if you already have Docusign
  Apps Launcher v8.4 or higher installed. Only follow these steps to add a
  Start with Docusign component or action in an
  organization with v8.3 or lower installed.

.

With [Docusign
Workflow Builder](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=bqi1705961132077.html&_LANG=enus), you can create workflows to
automate your agreement process. If you want to start workflows from Salesforce, you
can add a Start with Docusign action or component to your
layouts. Users can start a workflow by selecting Start next
to a workflow name. To start workflows from Salesforce, you must prepare your
organization.

Use this procedure to prepare your Salesforce organization to start workflows from
Salesforce:

1. As a Salesforce and Docusign administrator, [Install Docusign Apps
   Launcher version 8.4 or higher](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=ogj1586134254571.html&_LANG=enus).
   - [Install for Salesforce production](https://login.salesforce.com/packaging/installPackage.apexp?p0=04tKg000000D3Vk).
   - [Install for Salesforce sandbox](https://test.salesforce.com/packaging/installPackage.apexp?p0=04tKg000000D3Vk).
2. [Disconnect and reconnect Docusign and Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=ntr1586134210309.html&_LANG=enus).

   Important: Disconnecting removes Docusign access from all Salesforce users. Disconnecting does not remove users from your connected Docusign account.
3. [Open Docusign Apps Launcher](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=etv1586134206174.html&_LANG=enus).
4. Go to Docusign Setup >  User Management.
5. [Re-add your Docusign users](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=uwf1586134275195.html&_LANG=enus).

   Important: Users must [re-activate their Docusign access in Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=vbw1648698462634&topicId=ymi1586134213877.html&_LANG=enus). After re-activating,
   users can access Agreement Desk in the Docusign Agreement
   Requests component.

You have completed the task. You have prepared your Salesforce organization to start
workflows from Salesforce.

title: Prepare Your Salesforce Organization to Use a Start with Docusign Action
heading_path: Prepare Your Salesforce Organization to Use a Start with Docusign Action
Next steps
text:
Complete the following tasks to finish your integration setup:

- [Build a workflow to be triggered by a
  Start with Docusign action](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=tskccafc250-9431-4f0c-9518-d4526d9128b1.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to build a workflow that can be triggered by a Start with Docusign action.").
- [Build a Start with Docusign action](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=tsk32e505bf-436a-47b1-b7f6-55e6ad614ec5.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to build a new Lightning web component action to start a Docusign workflow from a record.").
- [Add a Start with Docusign action to a layout](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=tskb637a1ca-b7ba-4604-97f7-f5f2d5f899f6.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to add a Start with Docusign action to a page layout to start workflows.") .
- [Grant user permissions to start
  workflows from Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=tsk3640d081-197c-4f31-990a-0309487e92a8.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to assign a custom Docusign permission profile so that standard users can execute Workflow Builder workflows from Salesforce.").

title: Step 1: Create an Agreement Desk for Salesforce External Object
heading_path: Step 1: Create an Agreement Desk for Salesforce External Object
