title: Step 1: Create an Agreement Desk for Salesforce External Object
heading_path: Step 1: Create an Agreement Desk for Salesforce External Object
Before you begin
text:
Important: This feature is only available to IAM for Sales
customers. Contact [Docusign support](https://support.docusign.com/s/?language=en_US&_gl=1*74elk4*_gcl_au*MTY5ODQyMjA2MC4xNzY5NjM1OTA1LjE5ODM3MTQzNjguMTc2OTYzNjIwNC4xNzY5NjM2MjA0) or [Docusign
sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales) for more information.

[Purchase a ​ Docusign ​ Intelligent Agreement
Management (IAM) for Sales application](https://www.docusign.com/intelligent-agreement-management?utm_source=bing&utm_medium=cpc&utm_campaign=GBL_XX_PRF_UPS_2410_SEMIAM&utm_term=docusign%20iam&campaignid=569731866&adgroupid=1178678316174006&location=84406&device=c&matchtype=e&extensionid=&creative=73667602015411&keyword=docusign%20iam&placement=&network=o&msclkid=3287ee64f2c01f69bcd686665f40e7da).

Before you can view Agreement Desk reports in Salesforce, you must create an Agreement Desk
for Salesforce external object. With an external object, you can create custom
Salesforce reports that display Docusign Agreement Desk requests.

Use this procedure to create an Agreement Desk for Salesforce external object in
your Salesforce organization:

1. As a Salesforce administrator, open the New External Data
   Source page:
   1. Select the gear icon ![A screenshot displays the Salesforce Setup gear icon.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU3MTgsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.Tj-rc07vmhCKQgyPkzXoqdKIMwAaoS-8qsBA6NOlrGs&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Salesforce Setup gear icon.") and choose Setup.
   2. Enter and select External Data Sources in the
      Quick Find field.

      The External Data Sources page displays.
   3. Select New External Data Source.

      The New External Data Source page displays.
2. Create a Docusign Agreements External Data Source:
   1. External Data Source: Enter `Docusign
      Agreements External Data Source`.
   2. Name: Enter
      `ADTickets_External_Data_Source`.
   3. Type: Select Salesforce Connect:
      Custom –dfsle.ADDataSourceProvider from the dropdown
      menu.
   4. Identity Type: Keep
      Anonymous.
   5. Authentication Protocol: Keep No
      Authentication.
   6. Select Save.

      ![A screenshot displays the External Data Sources page with settings turned on for a Docusign Agreements External Data Source.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/DAL_Agreement_Desk_Reporting_External_Data_Source_settings.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU3MTgsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.Tj-rc07vmhCKQgyPkzXoqdKIMwAaoS-8qsBA6NOlrGs&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the External Data Sources page with settings turned on for a Docusign Agreements External Data Source.")

   The External Data Sources page displays your new data
   source.
3. Validate and sync your data source:
   1. Select Validate and Sync.

      The Validate External Data Source page displays.
   2. Select the check box next to Docusign Agreement Desk
      Requests.
   3. Select Sync.

      ![A screenshot displays the External Data Sources page with Docusign Agreement Desk Requests and the Sync button highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/DAL_Agreement_Desk_Reporting_External_Data_Source_Validate_and_Sync.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU3MTgsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.Tj-rc07vmhCKQgyPkzXoqdKIMwAaoS-8qsBA6NOlrGs&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the External Data Sources page with Docusign Agreement Desk Requests and the Sync button highlighted.")

title: Step 1: Create an Agreement Desk for Salesforce External Object
heading_path: Step 1: Create an Agreement Desk for Salesforce External Object
Before you begin
text:
GQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.Tj-rc07vmhCKQgyPkzXoqdKIMwAaoS-8qsBA6NOlrGs&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the External Data Sources page with Docusign Agreement Desk Requests and the Sync button highlighted.")

      The page refreshes.
4. Open the Docusign Agreement Desk Requests object:
   1. Enter and select External Objects in the Quick
      Find field.

      The External Objects page displays.
   2. Select Edit next to Docusign Agreement
      Desk Requests.

      The Docusign Agreement Desk Requests page
      displays.
   3. Optional Features: Select the check box next to
      Allow Reports.

      ![A screenshot displays the External Connection Detail section with the Allow Reports checkbox selected.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/DAL_Agreement_Desk_Reporting_External_Data_Source_Allow_Reports.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU3MTgsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.Tj-rc07vmhCKQgyPkzXoqdKIMwAaoS-8qsBA6NOlrGs&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the External Connection Detail section with the Allow Reports checkbox selected.")
   4. Select Save.

      The External Objects page displays.

You have completed the process. You have created an Agreement Desk for Salesforce external
object with reports enabled.

title: Step 1: Create an Agreement Desk for Salesforce External Object
heading_path: Step 1: Create an Agreement Desk for Salesforce External Object
Next steps
text:
[Step 2:
Create an Agreement Desk for Salesforce Report](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=tsk850b23aa-f070-4f37-b82c-17ca872c951b.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to build a custom Salesforce report that displays your Docusign Agreement Desk requests.").

title: Build a Start with Docusign Action (Manual Controls)
heading_path: Build a Start with Docusign Action (Manual Controls)
text:
Learn to build a new Lightning web component action to start a Docusign
workflow from a record.

title: Build a Start with Docusign Action (Manual Controls)
heading_path: Build a Start with Docusign Action (Manual Controls)
Before you begin
text:
Important:

- This feature is only available to IAM for Sales customers. Contact [Docusign support](https://support.docusign.com/s/?language=en_US&_gl=1*74elk4*_gcl_au*MTY5ODQyMjA2MC4xNzY5NjM1OTA1LjE5ODM3MTQzNjguMTc2OTYzNjIwNC4xNzY5NjM2MjA0) or [Docusign
  sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales) for more information.
- Docusign recommends using the [IAM Launcher Setup Controls](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=tska9d68585-f524-49a4-8898-496b6575d036.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to use the IAM Launcher Setup interface to add Docusign workflow actions to Salesforce layouts.") to add workflow actions. Use these
  instructions if the IAM Launcher Setup controls fail.

[Prepare your
organization to start workflows from Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=tsk2a2ae3f7-99c2-4deb-ab15-61fc26ade4d1.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to prepare your Salesforce organization to start Docusign Workflow Builder workflows from Salesforce.").

If you want to start workflows from Salesforce records, you can add a Start
with Docusign action to your layouts. The Start with Docusign action displays workflows that you can start from your
records. You can take the following actions from the Start with Docusign action menu:

- Start: Select the Start button to start any
  displayed workflow from a record.
- Add a Workflow: Add a new workflow to the Start with
  Docusign action.
- Remove this workflow: Remove a workflow from the
  Start with Docusign action.
- Search workflows: Find a specific workflow from the
  list.

Use this procedure to create a Start with Docusign action:

1. Access the Buttons, Links, and Actions page as a Salesforce administrator:
   1. Select the gear icon ![A screenshot displays a gear icon with a lightning bolt inside.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU0MTIsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.UUHXwcumcWRJ8WS2ejYhJILIcxOfgInNrp0w66qpR_8&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a gear icon with a lightning bolt inside.") and choose Setup from the dropdown menu.
   2. Enter and select Object Manager in the Quick
      Find field.
   3. Select a Salesforce object in the left column and select
      Buttons, Links, and Actions.

   The Buttons, Links, and Actions page displays.
2. Select New Action in the upper right of the page.

   The New Action page displays.
3. Create your Start with Docusign action:
   1. Action Type: Select Lightning Web
      Component from the dropdown menu.
   2. Lightning Web Component: Select
      dfsle:embeddedWorkflowLauncher from the
      dropdown menu.
   3. Standard Label Type: Keep the default value of
      None.
   4. Label: Enter `Start with
      Docusign`
   5. Name: Keep the default value of `Start_with_Docusign`
   6. Select Save.![A screenshot displays the Action page with Start with Docusign fields and the Save button highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Create_Start_with_Docusign_Lightning_action.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU0MTIsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.UUHXwcumcWRJ8WS2ejYhJILIcxOfgInNrp0w66qpR_8&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Action page with Start with Docusign fields and the Save button highlighted.")

   The page closes. The Buttons, Links, and Actions page
   displays.

You have completed the process. You have created a new Start with
Docusign Lightning web component action.

title: Build a Start with Docusign Action (Manual Controls)
heading_path: Build a Start with Docusign Action (Manual Controls)
Next steps
text:
[Add the
Start with Docusign action](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=tskb637a1ca-b7ba-4604-97f7-f5f2d5f899f6.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to add a Start with Docusign action to a page layout to start workflows.") to a layout so that your users can start workflows
from records.

title: Grant User Permissions to Start Workflows from Salesforce
heading_path: Grant User Permissions to Start Workflows from Salesforce
text: Learn to assign a custom Docusign permission profile so that standard users can execute Workflow Builder workflows from Salesforce.

title: Grant User Permissions to Start Workflows from Salesforce
heading_path: Grant User Permissions to Start Workflows from Salesforce
Before you begin
text:
Important: This feature is only available to IAM for
Sales customers. Contact [Docusign support](https://support.docusign.com/s/?language=en_US&_gl=1*74elk4*_gcl_au*MTY5ODQyMjA2MC4xNzY5NjM1OTA1LjE5ODM3MTQzNjguMTc2OTYzNjIwNC4xNzY5NjM2MjA0) or [Docusign
sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales) for more information.

- [Purchase a Docusign Intelligent Agreement
  Management (IAM) for Sales application](https://www.docusign.com/intelligent-agreement-management?utm_source=bing&utm_medium=cpc&utm_campaign=GBL_XX_PRF_UPS_2410_SEMIAM&utm_term=docusign%20iam&campaignid=569731866&adgroupid=1178678316174006&location=84406&device=c&matchtype=e&extensionid=&creative=73667602015411&keyword=docusign%20iam&placement=&network=o&msclkid=3287ee64f2c01f69bcd686665f40e7da).
- [Install Docusign Apps
  Launcher version 8.4 or higher](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=ogj1586134254571.html&_LANG=enus).
- Add a [Start with
  Docusign action](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=tskb637a1ca-b7ba-4604-97f7-f5f2d5f899f6.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to add a Start with Docusign action to a page layout to start workflows.") to a layout.

Docusign administrator users can start workflows from Salesforce. Standard Docusign users need custom permissions to send workflows from
Salesforce. Create a custom Docusign permission profile and grant
standard users Use access to the workflows.

Use this procedure to create a custom permission profile and grant access to users:

1. As a Docusign administrator, go to Admin >  Permission Profiles and select Add Permission Profile.

   The Add Permission Profiles page displays.
2. Create a new Workflow Builder permission profile for standard Salesforce users:
   1. Name: Enter a descriptive name. For example,
      `Start Docusign Workflow`.
   2. Select the User Permissions tab.
   3. Select the Allow users to send envelopes check box.
   4. Scroll down to Start Docusign Workflow and
      select Use from the dropdown menu.
   5. Select Add.

   The dialog box closes. The Permission Profiles page displays a message: You
   successfully created the permission profile (name of
   profile.)
3. Grant users access to your new permission profile:
   1. Go to USERS AND GROUPS and select
      Users.

      The Users page displays.
   2. Select Actions next to a user name and select
      Edit from the dropdown menu.

      The user's page displays.
   3. Select Permission Profile and choose the name of
      your new permission profile from the dropdown menu.
   4. Select Save Changes at the bottom of the
      page.

      The user's page closes. The Users page displays a message: You successfully
      update the user (user name.)

You have completed the process. You have created a Workflow Builder
permission profile and granted access to a user. The user can [start workflows from Salesforce
records](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=tskcae26f13-f2e5-4afd-85a7-145a70b12977.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to start a Docusign workflow from a Start with Docusign action.").

title: Duplicate a Workflow
heading_path: Duplicate a Workflow
text: Learn to quickly build new workflows by duplicating existing workflows.

title: Duplicate a Workflow
heading_path: Duplicate a Workflow
Before you begin
text:
You can duplicate workflows to quickly build similar workflows.

Example: You have a workflow called Salesforce
MSA. The workflow is designed to generate and send MSA agreements
from Salesforce records. You want to build a similar workflow to create and send NDA
agreements from Salesforce records. Rather than building a new workflow, you select
the Duplicate option from your Salesforce
MSA workflow menu. Docusign instantly creates a
duplicate workflow named COPY Salesforce MSA. All steps,
fields, and settings from the original workflow are copied to the duplicate
workflow. You rename the imported workflow Salesforce NDA, make
a few changes, and publish it.

Note: Steps, settings, and field
mappings from the original function in the duplicated workflow. You are not
required to re-map fields or rebuild steps.

Use this procedure to duplicate a for Salesforce workflow:

1. As a Docusign administrator, go to Agreements > Workflows.
2. Select the more actions menu icon ![A screenshot displays three dots stacked vertically.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/A-screenshot-diplays-a-threedot-menu-icon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU1NTcsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0._WZ3ss8SglnrTnTSiezfHbl8bJX_mXBpgQnEFFoAhu8&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays three dots stacked vertically.") next to a workflow and choose Duplicate.

   The page refreshes. A message displays: Workflow
   duplicated. The workflow editor page opens, displaying the
   duplicated workflow with the word COPY in the
   title.
3. Make changes to your duplicated workflow.
4. Select Review & Publish if you are ready to use the
   workflow. Select Save Draft if you are not ready to
   publish.

You have completed the process. You have duplicated a workflow and created a new workflow
from it.

Select the more actions menu icon ![A screenshot displays three dots stacked vertically.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/A-screenshot-diplays-a-threedot-menu-icon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU2MzcsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.nVID8tujOAnYX-MKw2BpPy6iReriXXEzbdGojBrx6gM&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays three dots stacked vertically.") next to a workflow and choose Duplicate.

   duplicated. The workflow editor page opens, displaying the
