title: Check Salesforce Logs for Failed Agreement Desk Actions
heading_path: Check Salesforce Logs for Failed Agreement Desk Actions
text:
Learn to check the status of failed Agreement Desk for Salesforce
actions.

title: Check Salesforce Logs for Failed Agreement Desk Actions
heading_path: Check Salesforce Logs for Failed Agreement Desk Actions
Before you begin
text:
Important: This feature is only
available to IAM for Sales customers. Contact [Docusign support](https://support.docusign.com/s/?language=en_US&_gl=1*74elk4*_gcl_au*MTY5ODQyMjA2MC4xNzY5NjM1OTA1LjE5ODM3MTQzNjguMTc2OTYzNjIwNC4xNzY5NjM2MjA0) or [Docusign
sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales) for more information.

[Agreement Desk for Salesforce User Access](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=ref9c5cd52b-33e2-4b25-a821-a666d4bd222e.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about granting Salesforce user access to the Docusign Agreement Requests component.")

In Salesforce, you access Docusign Agreement Desk by adding the Docusign Embedded
Agreement Desk component to your layouts. Approved users can interact
with Docusign agreements on your records without leaving
Salesforce.

If errors occur with Agreement Desk actions, you can view the logs in
Salesforce.

Use this procedure to check the status of failed Agreement Desk actions:

1. Select the Docusign Logs tab.

   The Docusign Logs page opens.
2. Select a log from the Docusign Log column.

   The log details page opens for that item.

You have completed the task. You viewed the details of a
failed Agreement Desk action.

title: Search for a Workflow in the Start with Docusign Action Menu
heading_path: Search for a Workflow in the Start with Docusign Action Menu
text: Learn to search for a workflow from the Start with Docusign action menu.

title: Search for a Workflow in the Start with Docusign Action Menu
heading_path: Search for a Workflow in the Start with Docusign Action Menu
Before you begin
text:
Important: This feature is only available to IAM for
Sales customers. Contact [Docusign support](https://support.docusign.com/s/?language=en_US&_gl=1*74elk4*_gcl_au*MTY5ODQyMjA2MC4xNzY5NjM1OTA1LjE5ODM3MTQzNjguMTc2OTYzNjIwNC4xNzY5NjM2MjA0) or [Docusign
sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales) for more information.

- [Add a Start with
  Docusign action to a layout](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=tskb637a1ca-b7ba-4604-97f7-f5f2d5f899f6.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to add a Start with Docusign action to a page layout to start workflows.").
- Add users to a workflow permission profile.

If you want to start Docusign workflows from Salesforce
records, you can add a Start with Docusign action to your
layouts. The action displays workflows that you can start from your records. You can
add and remove the workflows that display in the action menu. You can also search
for workflows added to the action menu.

Both administrators and standard users can search for workflows in the action menu.

Use this procedure to search for a workflow in a Start with Docusign action menu:

Note: Docusign only displays
workflows related to the record's object.

1. Choose your access method:
   - From the Docusign Setup page:
     1. [Access the Docusign Setup
        page as a Salesforce administrator](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=etv1586134206174.html&_LANG=enus)
     2. Manage Connections and the IAM Launcher: Select
        Manage.
     3. Select the IAM Launcher Setup tab.
     4. Select the Manage button next to a layout
        (Account, Opportunity, and so on.)

        The Start with Docusign menu displays.
   - From a Salesforce record: Select the Start with
     Docusign action in the upper right of the record.

     The Start
     with Docusign menu displays.
2. Search workflows: Enter a full or partial workflow name
   in the field.

   ![A screenshot displays the Search workflows field highlighted in the Start with Docusign action menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Start_Workflow_Builder_Workflow_Button_Menu_Search_Workflows.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU1NjcsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.ojlxSFLZPZZWqK3kaOD2B6mEpw-RjItxrr180waaYr8&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Search workflows field highlighted in the Start with Docusign action menu.")

   Workflows matching your search query display.
3. Select the X icon to close the action menu dialog
   box.

You have completed the process. You have searched for a workflow in
the Start with Docusign action menu.

title: Configure a Use Salesforce Files Step
heading_path: Configure a Use Salesforce Files Step
text:
Learn to add and configure a Use Salesforce Files step to add Salesforce files to
workflows.

title: Configure a Use Salesforce Files Step
heading_path: Configure a Use Salesforce Files Step
Before you begin
text:
Important: This feature is only available to IAM for
Sales customers. Contact [Docusign support](https://support.docusign.com/s/?language=en_US&_gl=1*74elk4*_gcl_au*MTY5ODQyMjA2MC4xNzY5NjM1OTA1LjE5ODM3MTQzNjguMTc2OTYzNjIwNC4xNzY5NjM2MjA0) or [Docusign
sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales) for more information.

Before you begin
:   - Required: Installation of the Salesforce app
      from Docusign App Center. Review the [Installing Extension
      Applications for Workflow Builder](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=bgb1697668738892.html&_LANG=enus) topic for more
      information.
    - Required: [Workflows Create
      permissions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gbb1696973048215.html&_LANG=enus) or be an account
      administrator.
    - Required: Create a workflow as described in [Create a Basic Workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uhm1697826856678.html&_LANG=enus). Add
      steps like [invitation](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=kfx1698167344720.html&_LANG=enus),
      [web forms](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gua1698120920620.html&_LANG=enus), [eSignature](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uww1698167288211.html&_LANG=enus) and
      more to gather recipients, documents, and merge fields for your
      workflow.

The Use Salesforce Files step adds Salesforce files
to workflows. Administrators open a record's Notes & Attachments and add
the record's ID to the Use Salesforce Files step. When
workflows start, Docusign adds selected files from that
record's Notes & Attachments. You can configure the following upload settings: All Files, File Name Contains, and Latest Document Only.

Example: You add a Use Salesforce Files
step to your workflow. You use the File Name Contains option
and enter the keywords `Contract Renewal`. Your workflow sends
an agreement to recipients. When recipients open their workflow task, they see a
file named Contract Renewal 2025 included in the agreement.

Use this procedure to configure a Use Salesforce Files step for
your workflow:

1. Add a Use Salesforce Files step as a Docusign administrator:
   1. Go to Agreements > Workflows and select Edit next to a workflow
      name.

      The workflow editor page displays.
   2. Select the Add a step
      ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Workflow_Builder_add_step_plus_sign.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU1MzMsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.LpPAD6khQ1QKXEOv5Ki05MVzvTqnYVQCm0SsmWvav_s&_LANG=enus) icon.

      The Add New Step dialog box displays.
   3. Search for steps: Enter and select `Use
      Salesforce Files`.

      The workflow editor page displays with the Use Salesforce Files
      step added.
2. Name your Use Salesforce Files step:
   1. Select Configure on the Use Salesforce Files
      step.

title: Configure a Use Salesforce Files Step
heading_path: Configure a Use Salesforce Files Step
Before you begin
text:
Files`.

      The workflow editor page displays with the Use Salesforce Files
      step added.
2. Name your Use Salesforce Files step:
   1. Select Configure on the Use Salesforce Files
      step.

      The configuration dialog box displays.
   2. Step Name: Keep or edit the name. It is helpful
      to add the name of the Salesforce object, like `Account - Use
      Salesforce Files`.
3. Where are these files located?
   1. Select connection: Select the field and choose a
      Salesforce organization that you connected to Docusign. Multiple options display if you connect more than one Salesforce
      organization.
   2. (Optional) Add New Connection: Select this
      button if you want to [connect more than one Salesforce
      organization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=ogk1719256147008.html&_LANG=enus).
   3. (Optional) Manage Connections: Select this
      button if you want to [add, edit, refresh, or delete a
      Salesforce connection](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=uaw1719259450391.html&_LANG=enus).
   4. Select Record ID: Select a field from the start
      step or another step in this workflow:

      - Manual entry: Add text for your new
        variable and select Save.

        Note: The name that you enter must exactly
        match an existing field. For example, the rule fails if you
        enter CompanyName for the
        Company\_Name
        variable.
      - Select a field from the start step or another step in this
        workflow:

        Note: Steps like Read
        from HubSpot and Read from
        Salesforce display fields from up to five
        records. If more than five records are present, Salesforce
        logic decides which five to display. Selecting fields from
        multiple records helps you automate processes like mapping
        multiple Salesforce contacts to multiple Docusign participants.![A screenshot displays Read from Salesforce fields highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_Salesforce_Read_Workflow_field_selections.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU1MzMsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.LpPAD6khQ1QKXEOv5Ki05MVzvTqnYVQCm0SsmWvav_s&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays Read from Salesforce fields highlighted.")

        You can use the fields that you select here in
        later steps like Collect Data with
        Webforms or Send Documents for
        Signature.
   5. Select Next.

      ![Screenshot showing a connection highlighted for a Salesforce connection and a record ID path.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_Salesforce_Use_Salesforce_Files_Where_are_these_files_located.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU1MzMsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.LpPAD6khQ1QKXEOv5Ki05MVzvTqnYVQCm0SsmWvav_s&_LANG=enus "Screenshot showing a connection highlighted for a Salesforce connection and a record ID path.")
4. Which files do you want to import?

   Note: You can import a maximum of ten files or 100 MB.

   Filter files by: Select an option:
   - All Files: Upload all documents from the
     record.
   - File Name Contains: Upload documents that match
     file name keywords.
   - Latest Document Only: Upload the most recently
     added document from the record.
5. Select Apply.

title: Configure a Use Salesforce Files Step
heading_path: Configure a Use Salesforce Files Step
Before you begin
text:
the
     record.
   - File Name Contains: Upload documents that match
     file name keywords.
   - Latest Document Only: Upload the most recently
     added document from the record.
5. Select Apply.

   ![A screenshot displays file import filters for name and a notice about the ten file import limit.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_Salesforce_Use_Salesforce_Files_Which_files_do_you_want_to_import.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU1MzMsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.LpPAD6khQ1QKXEOv5Ki05MVzvTqnYVQCm0SsmWvav_s&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays file import filters for name and a notice about the ten file import limit.")

   The Use Salesforce Files dialog box closes. The page displays your new step.

You have completed the process. You have built a Use Salesforce
Files step in your workflow. When a workflow starts this step, Docusign adds the most recent file from the specified record.

title: Delete a Saved Search in the Docusign Agreement Desk Component
heading_path: Delete a Saved Search in the Docusign Agreement Desk Component
text:
Learn to delete a saved search from the Docusign Agreement Desk
component.

title: Delete a Saved Search in the Docusign Agreement Desk Component
heading_path: Delete a Saved Search in the Docusign Agreement Desk Component
Before you begin
text:
Important: This feature is only
available to IAM for Sales customers. Contact [Docusign support](https://support.docusign.com/s/?language=en_US&_gl=1*74elk4*_gcl_au*MTY5ODQyMjA2MC4xNzY5NjM1OTA1LjE5ODM3MTQzNjguMTc2OTYzNjIwNC4xNzY5NjM2MjA0) or [Docusign
sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales) for more information.

[Create a saved search in the
Docusign Agreement Requests component](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=tsk96d6f83e-3826-4c3f-9bba-6ac03bea79bb.html&_LANG=enus)

Agreement Desk is a central platform for streamlining your entire agreement process
within Docusign. You can use Agreement Desk to submit and manage
requests, negotiate and edit agreements, and use AI to automate contracts.

You can search and use filters to locate the requests that you want to see. You can
also save your searches to run again later.

Use this procedure to delete a saved search in the Docusign
Agreement Requests component:

1. Open a record to the Agreement Requests component and select the
   Manage saved searches
   ![A screenshot displays a bookmark icon.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Screenshot_2026-05-11_at_5.55.40AM.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU2OTgsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.noqRr2DZM5-KzOfShNBDowmlem-oXWFVW3NidBb58Rs&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a bookmark icon.") button.

   The Manage Saved Searches dialog box displays.
2. Select Manage saved searches.

   The Manage Saved searches dialog box displays.
3. Delete the saved search:
   1. Select the three-dot menu icon ![A screenshot displays three dots stacked vertically.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/A-screenshot-diplays-a-threedot-menu-icon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU2OTgsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.noqRr2DZM5-KzOfShNBDowmlem-oXWFVW3NidBb58Rs&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays three dots stacked vertically.") next to a saved search and choose
      Delete.

      ![A screenshot displays the Manage Saved Searches dialog box with the three-dot menu displaying options to edit or delete a saved search.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Agreement_Desk_for_Salesforce_Edit_and_delete_saved_searches_menu.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU2OTgsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.noqRr2DZM5-KzOfShNBDowmlem-oXWFVW3NidBb58Rs&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Manage Saved Searches dialog box with the three-dot menu displaying options to edit or delete a saved search.")

      The Delete this saved search? dialog box
      displays.
   2. Select Delete.

      The dialog box closes. A message displays: Saved
      search deleted.
   3. Select X to close the Manage Saved Searches
      dialog box.

      The dialog box closes. The Docusign Agreement
      Desk component displays.

You have completed the process. You have deleted a saved search in
the Docusign Agreement Requests component.

title: Search and Filter in the Agreement Requests Component
heading_path: Search and Filter in the Agreement Requests Component
text:
Learn to search and filter the requests that display in the Docusign
Agreement Requests component.

title: Search and Filter in the Agreement Requests Component
heading_path: Search and Filter in the Agreement Requests Component
Before you begin
text:
Important: This feature is only available to IAM for
Sales customers. Contact [Docusign support](https://support.docusign.com/s/?language=en_US&_gl=1*74elk4*_gcl_au*MTY5ODQyMjA2MC4xNzY5NjM1OTA1LjE5ODM3MTQzNjguMTc2OTYzNjIwNC4xNzY5NjM2MjA0) or [Docusign
sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales) for more information.

Agreement Desk is a central platform for streamlining your entire agreement process
within Docusign. You can use Agreement Desk to submit and manage
requests, negotiate and edit agreements, and use AI to automate contracts.

You can also search and use filters to locate the requests that you want to see.

Use this procedure to search for and filter the requests that display in the Docusign Agreement Requests component:

1. Open a record and look for the Docusign Agreement
   Requests component.

   Note: A Salesforce administrator may have placed the
   component on a tab labeled Agreement Desk Requests or
   something similar. The component features the word
   Requests at the top.
2. Search Request Titles: Enter search terms in the
   field.

   ![The Requests section of a Salesforce record displays a highlighted search field with Legal Request entered.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Agreement_Desk_for_Salesforce_search_results.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU2ODMsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.VeGYDXgKgtYqR2zwvjyW67biB53lI_W0pm4a8RsmHaQ&_LANG=enus "The Requests section of a Salesforce record displays a highlighted search field with Legal Request entered.")
3. Press [Enter] to see all results, or select a result
   from the dropdown menu that opens beneath.

   The component displays requests sent from this record and its child
   objects. The component does not display results for parent objects, or Agreement
   Desk requests sent from your Docusign web
   account.
4. (Optional) Use the filters to refine the list of search results. Select a
   filter, choose a value, and select Apply.

   For example, use the Request Type filter to locate a
   New Customer Intake request:

   ![A screenshot displays the Request Type filter highlighted with the New Customer Intake check box selected.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Agreement_Desk_for_Salesforce_Filter_Request_Titles.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU2ODMsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.VeGYDXgKgtYqR2zwvjyW67biB53lI_W0pm4a8RsmHaQ&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Request Type filter highlighted with the New Customer Intake check box selected.")

   New Customer Intake requests for this object and its child objects display in the
   Agreement Requests component.
5. (Optional) Continue to apply filters to refine the list of requests that are displayed.
   Select All Filters to access additional filters:
   - Created At: Select from pre-defined date ranges or
     enter a custom date range.
   - Due Date: Select from requests containing a
     specific due date.
   - Last Activity At: Select from a pre-defined date
     range or enter a custom date range.
   - Owner: Select a name from the list of request owners.
     The owner is assigned by the request submitter.
   - Status: Select from specific statuses for open and
     closed requests. The options listed include custom statuses.
   - Status Type: Select Open or
     Closed.
   - Submitter: Select a name from the list of users who
     created the requests shown in the dashboard.
   - Request Type: Select from the request types listed to
     narrow the results to only those types.

You have completed the process. You have used filters with your
search to display only the requests that you want to see.

title: Add the Docusign Agreement Requests Component to a Salesforce Layout
heading_path: Add the Docusign Agreement Requests Component to a Salesforce Layout
text:
Learn to add the Docusign Agreement Requests component to a Salesforce
page layout.

title: Add the Docusign Agreement Requests Component to a Salesforce Layout
heading_path: Add the Docusign Agreement Requests Component to a Salesforce Layout
Before you begin
text:
Important: This feature is only available to IAM for
Sales customers. Contact [Docusign support](https://support.docusign.com/s/?language=en_US&_gl=1*74elk4*_gcl_au*MTY5ODQyMjA2MC4xNzY5NjM1OTA1LjE5ODM3MTQzNjguMTc2OTYzNjIwNC4xNzY5NjM2MjA0) or [Docusign
sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales) for more information.

- [Purchase a Docusign Intelligent Agreement
  Management (IAM) for Sales application](https://www.docusign.com/intelligent-agreement-management?utm_source=bing&utm_medium=cpc&utm_campaign=GBL_XX_PRF_UPS_2410_SEMIAM&utm_term=docusign%20iam&campaignid=569731866&adgroupid=1178678316174006&location=84406&device=c&matchtype=e&extensionid=&creative=73667602015411&keyword=docusign%20iam&placement=&network=o&msclkid=3287ee64f2c01f69bcd686665f40e7da).
- [Prepare Your Salesforce Organization to Use Agreement Desk](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=tsk87e9ead6-6ace-475f-b10f-0b41ecc882e1.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to prepare your Salesforce organization to use Docusign Agreement Desk.").

Docusign Agreement Desk is part of the Docusign IAM platform, powered by [Docusign Iris](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fzd1707173174972&topicId=usy1712258503642.html).

Agreement Desk is a
central platform for streamlining your entire agreement process within Docusign. You can use Agreement Desk to submit and manage
requests, negotiate and edit agreements, and use AI to automate
contracts.

Many Salesforce users prefer to act on their agreements entirely
from Salesforce. When you add the Agreement Requests component to your layouts,
users can review, manage, sign, and send agreements without logging on to Docusign.

Use this procedure to add the component to a
layout:

1. As a Salesforce administrator, open a record, select the gear icon, ![A screenshot displays a gear icon with a lightning bolt inside.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU0MzEsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.BmnMzOnruxrsfK_28WW1ViT2eDYRa5XFoJyj6RSA3d0&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a gear icon with a lightning bolt inside.") and choose
   Edit Page from the dropdown menu.

   Note: You can add the Docusign Agreement
   Requests component to any part of your layout. The following
   instructions are recommendations, not requirements.
2. (Optional) Add an Agreement Requests tab to your layout.
   1. Select Add Tab on the right navigation
      panel.

      A new Details tab displays.
   2. Select Details.

      The Tab Label dialog box opens.
   3. Tab Label: Select the
      Details dropdown menu and choose
      Custom.
   4. Custom Label: Enter a descriptive name, like
      Docusign Agreement Desk.
   5. Select Done.

   ![A screenshot displays a custom tab with Docusign Agreement Desk in the label field.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Agreement_Desk_Tab_Salesforce.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU0MzEsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.BmnMzOnruxrsfK_28WW1ViT2eDYRa5XFoJyj6RSA3d0&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a custom tab with Docusign Agreement Desk in the label field.")

   Your new tab displays in the right navigation panel.
3. Drag the Docusign Agreement Requests component to the
   tab that you created and select Save.

title: Add the Docusign Agreement Requests Component to a Salesforce Layout
heading_path: Add the Docusign Agreement Requests Component to a Salesforce Layout
Before you begin
text:
Docusign Agreement Desk in the label field.")

   Your new tab displays in the right navigation panel.
3. Drag the Docusign Agreement Requests component to the
   tab that you created and select Save.

   ![A screen capture displays dragging the Docusign Agreement Requests component to a Salesforce layout.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Agreement_Desk_for_Salesforce_add_Agreement_Requests_component_to_layout.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU0MzEsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.BmnMzOnruxrsfK_28WW1ViT2eDYRa5XFoJyj6RSA3d0&_LANG=enus "A screen capture displays dragging the Docusign Agreement Requests component to a Salesforce layout.")
4. Activate the change to your layout:
   1. Select Activate.
   2. Select a tab to set your default options: Org
      Default, App Default, or
      App, Record Type, and Profile.
   3. Select Assign and choose options from the dialog
      box.
   4. Select Close.
5. Select the back arrow ![A screenshot displays a blue box with a left-facing arrow icon.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/A-screenshot-of-the-Back-arrow-on-the-Salesforce-Edit-Page-settings-bar.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU0MzEsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.BmnMzOnruxrsfK_28WW1ViT2eDYRa5XFoJyj6RSA3d0&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a blue box with a left-facing arrow icon.") to return to the record.

You have completed the process. You have added the
Docusign Agreement Requests component to a Salesforce layout.
Users can interact with Docusign Agreement Desk content on Salesforce
records.

title: Step 2: Create an Agreement Desk for Salesforce Report
heading_path: Step 2: Create an Agreement Desk for Salesforce Report
text:
Learn to build a custom Salesforce report that displays your Docusign
Agreement Desk requests.

title: Step 2: Create an Agreement Desk for Salesforce Report
heading_path: Step 2: Create an Agreement Desk for Salesforce Report
Before you begin
text:
Important: This feature is only available to IAM for Sales
customers. Contact [Docusign support](https://support.docusign.com/s/?language=en_US&_gl=1*74elk4*_gcl_au*MTY5ODQyMjA2MC4xNzY5NjM1OTA1LjE5ODM3MTQzNjguMTc2OTYzNjIwNC4xNzY5NjM2MjA0) or [Docusign
sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales) for more information.

[Create an Agreement Desk for
Salesforce external object](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=tsk2e317e21-7fca-4946-a4eb-c766238b2cb3.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to create an Agreement Desk for Salesforce external object to turn on reporting.").

Now that you have created an Agreement Desk external object, you can build a report to
display Agreement Desk requests.

Use this procedure to build an Agreement Desk for
Salesforce report:

1. Open the Salesforce Create Report page:
   1. Select the App Launcher grid ![A screenshot displays a square icon made of nine colored dots.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/app-launcher-grid.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU3MjIsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.Q3c4SyDlHuALNr80jO6YXGNNu1GPw4rXST_8gK-s8do&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a square icon made of nine colored dots.").
   2. Enter and select `Reports` in the *Search apps
      and items* field.
   3. Select New Report.

      The Create Report dialog box displays.
2. Search Report Types: Enter `Docusign Agreement
   Desk Requests` in the field.
3. Select the Docusign Agreement Desk Requests link that
   displays.
4. Select Start Report.

   The Reports page displays.
5. Create an outline for your new Agreement Desk Requests report:
   1. Select the Outline tab.
   2. GROUP ROWS: Select options from the dropdown
      menu.
   3. GROUP COLUMNS: Select options from the dropdown
      menu.
   4. Columns: Select options from the dropdown
      menu.
6. Create filters for your new Agreement Desk Requests report:
   1. Select the Filters tab.
   2. Filters: Select an option from the dropdown
      menu.

      The Filter By dialog box displays.
   3. Operator: Select options from the dropdown
      menu.
   4. Complete your filter and select Apply.

      ![A screenshot displays a Salesforce report with outline and filter tabs highlighted and filters displayed.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/DAL_Agreement_Desk_Reporting_Outline_and_Filters.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU3MjIsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.Q3c4SyDlHuALNr80jO6YXGNNu1GPw4rXST_8gK-s8do&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a Salesforce report with outline and filter tabs highlighted and filters displayed.")
   5. Select Save and Run.

   The Save Report dialog box displays.
7. Enter details for your new report:
   1. Report Name: Enter a descriptive name, like
      `Docusign Agreement Desk Requests`.
   2. Report Unique Name: Keep or edit the name.
   3. (Optional) Report Description: Enter a
      description of the report's function.
   4. Folder: Keep the folder or select
      Select Folder to change locations.
   5. Select Save.

      A message displays: Report "Docusign Agreement Desk
      Requests" was saved. The page refreshes with report
      results displayed.

You have completed the process. You have completed, saved, and run a new Agreement Desk
report. You can change filters to display different data sets.

title: Prepare Your Salesforce Organization to Use Agreement Desk
heading_path: Prepare Your Salesforce Organization to Use Agreement Desk
text: Learn to prepare your Salesforce organization to use Docusign Agreement Desk.

title: Prepare Your Salesforce Organization to Use Agreement Desk
heading_path: Prepare Your Salesforce Organization to Use Agreement Desk
Before you begin
text:
Important:

- This feature is only available to IAM for Sales customers. Contact [Docusign support](https://support.docusign.com/s/?language=en_US&_gl=1*74elk4*_gcl_au*MTY5ODQyMjA2MC4xNzY5NjM1OTA1LjE5ODM3MTQzNjguMTc2OTYzNjIwNC4xNzY5NjM2MjA0) or [Docusign
  sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales) for more information..
- Do not follow these steps if you already have Docusign
  Apps Launcher v8.4 or higher installed. Only follow these steps to add the
  Docusign Agreement Desk component in an
  organization with v8.3 or lower installed.

Agreement Desk is a central platform for streamlining your entire agreement process
within Docusign.

You can use Agreement Desk in your Salesforce organization by adding the
Docusign Agreements component to your layouts. This
component displays your Docusign agreements and the controls to
filter, search, and act on them. Before you can use Agreement Desk in Salesforce,
you must prepare your organization.

Use this procedure to prepare your Salesforce organization to use Agreement Desk:

1. Log on to Salesforce as an administrator.
2. [Install Docusign Apps
   Launcher version 8.4 or higher](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=ogj1586134254571.html&_LANG=enus).
   - [Install for Salesforce
     production](https://login.salesforce.com/packaging/installPackage.apexp?p0=04tKg000000D3Vk)
   - [Install for Salesforce
     sandbox](https://test.salesforce.com/packaging/installPackage.apexp?p0=04tKg000000D3Vk)

You have completed the task. You have prepared your Salesforce
organization to use Docusign Agreement Desk.

title: Prepare Your Salesforce Organization to Use Agreement Desk
heading_path: Prepare Your Salesforce Organization to Use Agreement Desk
Next steps
text:
- [Grant Agreement Desk
  access to your Docusign for Salesforce users](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=ref9c5cd52b-33e2-4b25-a821-a666d4bd222e.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about granting Salesforce user access to the Docusign Agreement Requests component.").
- [Add the Docusign Agreement Requests
  component to your Salesforce layouts](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=tsk7c6f3058-1654-462b-b850-8d0dc79ba199.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to add the Docusign Agreement Requests component to a Salesforce page layout.").

title: Configure an Agreement Desk Workflow Step
heading_path: Configure an Agreement Desk Workflow Step
text:
Important: This feature is only available to IAM for
Sales customers. Contact [Docusign support](https://support.docusign.com/s/?language=en_US&_gl=1*74elk4*_gcl_au*MTY5ODQyMjA2MC4xNzY5NjM1OTA1LjE5ODM3MTQzNjguMTc2OTYzNjIwNC4xNzY5NjM2MjA0) or [Docusign
sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales) for more information.

Before you begin
:   - Purchase a Docusign Intelligent Agreement
      Management (IAM) plan.
    - Required: Installation of the Salesforce app
      from Docusign App Center. Review the [Installing Extension
      Applications for Workflow Builder](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=bgb1697668738892.html&_LANG=enus) topic for more
      information.
    - Required: [Workflows Create
      permissions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gbb1696973048215.html&_LANG=enus) or be an account
      administrator.

Workflow Builder is a workflow builder that you can use to automate agreement actions from
your Docusign account.

Agreement Desk is a centralized workspace designed to simplify and accelerate the
pre-signature phase of the agreement lifecycle. It provides a single location for
all requests related to your agreements.

You can use Agreement Desk steps in Workflow Builder workflows by adding a Create a
Request step.

Use this procedure to configure a Create a Request step:

1. Build a Workflow to Be Triggered By Agreement Desk Requests.
2. Add an Agreement Desk step:
   1. Select + Add a step from the workflow builder
      canvas.

      The Add New Step panel displays.
   2. Agreement Desk: Select Create a
      Request.

      The Create a Request panel displays.
   3. Step Name: Keep or change the name.
3. Select a request type:
   1. Which request type would you like to use?:
      Choose a request type from the dropdown menu.
   2. How would you like to fill out the request
      form?: Select an option:

      - Automatic: Form fields will be
        auto-mapped from the documents within the request.
      - Manual Entry: Form fields will be filled
        out manually.
   3. Select Next.

   The Request title section displays.
4. Request title:
   1. Select the plus + to add variables. You can add
      up to five variables. You can also enter text. The text and variables
      combine to create unique workflow request titles.
   2. Select Next.

   The Documents section displays.
5. Documents:
   1. Choose which documents you would like to include within the
      request:

      - Include all documents
      - Manually select documents to include:
        Select Add Document >  Select Document and choose a document from the dropdown
        menu.
      - Do not include any documents
   2. Select Next.
6. Field mapping: Docusign auto-maps fields from your
   documents. Review the mappings and complete any required fields that are
   missing.
   1. For each variable, enter text manually or choose a variable from the
      dropdown menu.
   2. Select Finish.
7. Select Apply.

You have completed the process. You have configured a Create a Request
step in your workflow. Your workflow can use Agreement Desk requests.

title: Install the IAM for Salesforce Application (Salesforce Instructions)
heading_path: Install the IAM for Salesforce Application (Salesforce Instructions)
text: Learn to install the Salesforce app in your Docusign account and connect it to your Salesforce organization.

title: Install the IAM for Salesforce Application (Salesforce Instructions)
heading_path: Install the IAM for Salesforce Application (Salesforce Instructions)
Before you begin
text:
[Purchase a Docusign Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) for Sales application](https://www.docusign.com/intelligent-agreement-management?utm_source=bing&utm_medium=cpc&utm_campaign=GBL_XX_PRF_UPS_2410_SEMIAM&utm_term=docusign%20iam&campaignid=569731866&adgroupid=1178678316174006&location=84406&device=c&matchtype=e&extensionid=&creative=73667602015411&keyword=docusign%20iam&placement=&network=o&msclkid=3287ee64f2c01f69bcd686665f40e7da).

Important: Use these instructions to install the application from your Salesforce organization. Follow this link if you want to [install the app from the Docusign App Center](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=&qwr1706590641805&topicId=jxo1706295810553.html&_LANG=enus).

You can install the Salesforce application to connect your Docusign account with your Salesforce organization.

You can use that integration to build workflows that use Salesforce steps. These steps can read and write data, store documents, add merge fields, and more.

You can also add Docusign workflow actions to Salesforce layouts. Users can start Docusign workflows directly from Salesforce records.

Tip: Wait at least 30 minutes after installing the app before you configure a Workflow Builder for Salesforce step. Docusign indexes all of your Salesforce fields to make them available.

Use this procedure to install and connect the Salesforce app from the Docusign Setup page:

1. Manage Connections and the IAM Launcher: Select Manage.

   The docusign app center dialog box displays.
2. Install the Salesforce app:
   1. Select Install App.

      The Install and authorize "Salesforce" app dialog box displays.
   2. Select Install and Authorize.
   3. Select Continue

   The dialog box closes. A message displays: App successfully installed.
3. Connect your Docusign account to your Salesforce organization:

   Important: [Salesforce Government Cloud customers](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=000386092&type=1) must use the Salesforce Production option.

   1. Select Connect Account.

      The Connect a Salesforce account dialog box displays.
   2. Select your Salesforce connection: Choose an option:

      - Salesforce Production (login.salesforce.com): Connect a Salesforce Production or Developer organization.
      - Salesforce Sandbox (test.salesforce.com): Connect to a Salesforce Sandbox organization.

      ![A screenshot displays the Connect a Salesforce account with Salesforce Production selected.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Workflow_Builder_Connect_a_Salesforce_Account.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTUzNjksInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.feA9ouqj50Z8-C9X2gMoEuU0ikJRHtiPLW7jQgMp2-w&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Connect a Salesforce account with Salesforce Production selected.")
   3. Select Next.
   4. Salesforce Connection Name: Keep or change the connection name.
   5. Select Log In.

      The Salesforce login page displays.
4. Choose a Username: Select your Salesforce username from the list.

   The page refreshes. The docusign app center dialog box displays. Your connection displays with a Status of Processing.
5. Close the dialog box.

You have completed the process. You have installed the Salesforce app and connected it to Docusign. In five to ten minutes, the connection displays an Active status.

title: Create a Saved Search in the Docusign Agreement Requests Component
heading_path: Create a Saved Search in the Docusign Agreement Requests Component
text:
Learn how to create customized searches to retrieve an updated list of requests that
match the search criteria.

title: Create a Saved Search in the Docusign Agreement Requests Component
heading_path: Create a Saved Search in the Docusign Agreement Requests Component
Before you begin
text:
Important: This feature is only available to IAM for
Sales customers. Contact [Docusign support](https://support.docusign.com/s/?language=en_US&_gl=1*74elk4*_gcl_au*MTY5ODQyMjA2MC4xNzY5NjM1OTA1LjE5ODM3MTQzNjguMTc2OTYzNjIwNC4xNzY5NjM2MjA0) or [Docusign
sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales) for more information.

Agreement Desk is a central platform for streamlining your entire agreement process
within Docusign. You can use Agreement Desk to submit and manage
requests, negotiate and edit agreements, and use AI to automate contracts.

You can search and use filters to locate the requests that you want to see. You can
also save your searches to run again later.

Use this procedure to create a saved search in the Docusign
Agreement Requests component:

1. Open a record and [search for content in the Agreement Requests component](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=tsk79fb118f-2fa3-4e60-965a-efb9edd7c2c8.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to search and filter the requests that display in the Docusign Agreement Requests component.").

   The component displays requests sent from this record and its child
   objects. The component does not display requests sent from parent objects or
   requests sent from your Docusign web account.
2. Select the Save search link.

   The New saved search dialog box displays.
3. Create a new saved search:
   1. New saved search: Enter a title for your saved search. You can enter up to 120 characters.
   2. Description: Enter text that describes the purpose of the search. You can enter up to 200 characters.
   3. Select Save.

      ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Agreement_Desk_for_Salesforce_Saved_Search_dialog_box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU2ODgsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.Czs-CLnSRKP_Wi2awEcgIsk0Th4F4OPkBVzgTtjnIRs&_LANG=enus)

      The dialog box closes. A message displays: Saved search created.

You have completed the process. You have created a saved search in the Agreement Requests
component. When you run the saved search, the component displays filtered results of all
requests sent from this record and its child objects. The results do not display
requests sent from parent objects or requests sent from your Docusign
web account.

title: Build a Workflow to Be Triggered By Agreement Desk Requests
heading_path: Build a Workflow to Be Triggered By Agreement Desk Requests
text:
Learn to build a workflow that can be triggered by Agreement Desk
requests.

Important: This feature is only available to IAM for
Sales customers. Contact [Docusign support](https://support.docusign.com/s/?language=en_US&_gl=1*74elk4*_gcl_au*MTY5ODQyMjA2MC4xNzY5NjM1OTA1LjE5ODM3MTQzNjguMTc2OTYzNjIwNC4xNzY5NjM2MjA0) or [Docusign
sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales) for more information.

Before you begin
:   - Purchase a Docusign Intelligent Agreement
      Management (IAM) plan.
    - Required: Installation of the Salesforce app
      from Docusign App Center. Review the [Installing Extension
      Applications for Workflow Builder](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=bgb1697668738892.html&_LANG=enus) topic for more
      information.
    - Required: [Workflows Create
      permissions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gbb1696973048215.html&_LANG=enus) or be an account
      administrator.

Workflow Builder is a workflow builder that you can use to automate agreement actions from
your Docusign account.

Agreement Desk is a centralized workspace designed to simplify and accelerate the
pre-signature phase of the agreement lifecycle. It provides a single location for
all requests related to your agreements.

You can use Agreement Desk steps in Workflow Builder workflows by adding a Create a
Request step.

Before you can add a Create a Request step, you must create a
Workflow Builder workflow that can interact with Agreement Desk requests.

Use this procedure to create a Workflow Builder workflow that can be triggered by Agreement
Desk requests:

1. As a Docusign administrator, create your workflow:
   1. Log on to your Docusign account and go to Agreements >  Workflow Builder Workflows.
   2. Select Create Workflow.

      The Create a workflow page
      displays.
   3. Select + Blank Workflow.![A screenshot displays the Create a workflow page with the Blank Workflow button highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_Salesforce_workflow_build_Blank_workflow_.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU2NzAsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.0BjppYISrpRoTX4ycEtYwKX6Notblo7Pv72fvlwah0w&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Create a workflow page with the Blank Workflow button highlighted.")

      The Untitled workflow page displays.
   4. Select + Add workflow start.

      The Add workflow start page displays.
2. What starts this workflow?:
   1. Select From an API Call.
   2. Select Next.

   The Set up variables for dynamic information
   section displays.
3. Set up variables for dynamic information:
   1. Select New Variable and choose
      Text from the dropdown menu.
   2. Enter recordId in the field.
   3. Create another text variable named
      objectName
   4. Create another text variable named
      applicationName
   5. Select Next.

      ![A screenshot displays the Set up variables for dynamic information section with three text variables highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Agreement_Desk_for_Salesforce_create_workflow_start_step_-_Variables.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU2NzAsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.0BjppYISrpRoTX4ycEtYwKX6Notblo7Pv72fvlwah0w&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Set up variables for dynamic information section with three text variables highlighted.")

   The Will this API call be started by a human or an automated
   process? section displays.
4. Will this API call be started by a human or an automated
   process?:
   1. Select Automated process.
   2. Select Apply.

title: Build a Workflow to Be Triggered By Agreement Desk Requests
heading_path: Build a Workflow to Be Triggered By Agreement Desk Requests
text:
PI call be started by a human or an automated
   process? section displays.
4. Will this API call be started by a human or an automated
   process?:
   1. Select Automated process.
   2. Select Apply.

      ![A screenshot displays the Add Workflow Start step with the Automated process radio button and Apply button highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Agreement_Desk_for_Salesforce_create_workflow_start_step_-_Automated_Process.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU2NzAsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.0BjppYISrpRoTX4ycEtYwKX6Notblo7Pv72fvlwah0w&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Add Workflow Start step with the Automated process radio button and Apply button highlighted.")
5. Complete your workflow:
   1. Add more steps to your workflow.
   2. Select Review & Publish when you have
      finished building your workflow.

You have completed the process. You have built a Workflow Builder workflow
that can be triggered by Agreement Desk requests.

title: Build a Workflow to Be Triggered By Agreement Desk Requests
heading_path: Build a Workflow to Be Triggered By Agreement Desk Requests
Next steps
text: Configure an Agreement Desk workflow step.

title: Add a Workflow to the Start with Docusign Action Menu
heading_path: Add a Workflow to the Start with Docusign Action Menu
text: Learn to add a workflow to the Start with Docusign action menu.

title: Add a Workflow to the Start with Docusign Action Menu
heading_path: Add a Workflow to the Start with Docusign Action Menu
Before you begin
text:
Important: This feature is only available to IAM for
Sales customers. Contact [Docusign support](https://support.docusign.com/s/?language=en_US&_gl=1*74elk4*_gcl_au*MTY5ODQyMjA2MC4xNzY5NjM1OTA1LjE5ODM3MTQzNjguMTc2OTYzNjIwNC4xNzY5NjM2MjA0) or [Docusign
sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales) for more information.

[Add a Start with Docusign action to a layout](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=tskb637a1ca-b7ba-4604-97f7-f5f2d5f899f6.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to add a Start with Docusign action to a page layout to start workflows.").

If you want to start workflows from Salesforce records, you can add a Start
with Docusign action to your layouts. The action displays workflows
that you can start from your records. You can add and remove the workflows that
display in the action menu. You can also search for workflows in the action
menu.

Only administrators can add workflows to the action menu.

Use this procedure to add a workflow tile to the Start with Docusign action menu:

1. Choose your access method:
   - From the Docusign Setup page:
     1. [Access the Docusign Setup
        page as a Salesforce administrator](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=etv1586134206174.html&_LANG=enus)
     2. Manage Connections and the IAM Launcher: Select
        Manage.
     3. Select the IAM Launcher Setup tab.
     4. Select the Manage button next to a layout
        (Account, Opportunity, and so on.)

        The Start with Docusign menu displays.
   - From a Salesforce record: Select the Start with
     Docusign action in the upper right of the record.

     The Start
     with Docusign menu displays.
2. Select Add Workflow.

   ![A screenshot displays a list of Docusign workflows displayed in the Start with Docusign action menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Start_Workflow_Builder_Workflow_Button_Add_a_Workflow.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU1NjksInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.fdw0MeDXAOETiaX7g-XUIJPSKcy3b0127GrzFI2qdso&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a list of Docusign workflows displayed in the Start with Docusign action menu.")

   The Add workflows dialog box displays.
3. Add a workflow to the action menu:
   1. Select the check box next to each workflow that you want to add.
   2. (Optional) Use the search field to find more workflows.
   3. (Optional) Filter which workflows display:

      1. Select Created by.
      2. Select Me or Not
         Me.
      3. Select Apply.
   4. Select Add Selected.

      The Add workflows dialog box closes. A message displays:
      (number of workflows) added. The workflow that
      you added displays in the Start with Docusign action
      menu.
4. Select the X icon to close the action menu dialog
   box.

You have completed the process. You have added a workflow to the Start with Docusign action menu. Users can start workflows from Salesforce
records.

title: Use the IAM for Sales Solution to Install Pre-Built Docusign Workflows and Templates
heading_path: Use the IAM for Sales Solution to Install Pre-Built Docusign Workflows and Templates
text: Learn to use the IAM for Sales Solution to install pre-built Docusign workflows that you can use in Salesforce.

title: Use the IAM for Sales Solution to Install Pre-Built Docusign Workflows and Templates
heading_path: Use the IAM for Sales Solution to Install Pre-Built Docusign Workflows and Templates
Before you begin
text:
Install Docusign Apps Launcher in your Salesforce organization.

You can use the Docusign IAM for Sales Solution to install pre-built Docusign resources in your Salesforce organization. These
resources quickly create the building blocks for common IAM for Sales use cases,
like creating and sending an NDA or MSA.

The solution includes the following resources:

- Document templates
- Agreement Desk statuses
- Docusign workflows
- Custom fields

These resources are functional and do not require additional
configuration.

Installing these resources gives you a quick way to implement IAM for Sales features
in your Salesforce organization. You can customize these resources to fit your exact
use cases.

Use this procedure to install the IAM for Sales Solution to your Salesforce
organization:

1. Docusign IAM for Sales Solution:
   Select Install.![A screenshot displays the Docusign Setup page with the IAM for Sales Solution Install button highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/IAM_Solutions_package_Install_button.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTUzNzYsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.p4y-PRXqR_Vy5qMtsqPIbE7yl9vmt3q8p-TlH1CMo0A&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Docusign Setup page with the IAM for Sales Solution Install button highlighted.")

   The Docusign app center dialog box opens to the
   Details tab.
2. Configure settings on the Details tab:
   1. Salesforce connection: Choose the Salesforce
      connection that you set up.
   2. Select Install.![A screenshot displays the Docusign App Center Details tab with the Salesforce connection field highlighted and the Install button visible.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/IAM_Solutions_Package_Salesforce_Connection_and_Install.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTUzNzYsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.p4y-PRXqR_Vy5qMtsqPIbE7yl9vmt3q8p-TlH1CMo0A&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Docusign App Center Details tab with the Salesforce connection field highlighted and the Install button visible.")

      The Install tab displays with an Installing
      progress bar. When the installation reaches 100%, the
      Publish tab displays.
3. Configure settings on the Publish tab:
   1. Select Publish All.![A screenshot displays the Docusign App Center Publish tab with the Publish All button visible.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/IAM_Solutions_Package_Publish_All_button.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTUzNzYsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.p4y-PRXqR_Vy5qMtsqPIbE7yl9vmt3q8p-TlH1CMo0A&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Docusign App Center Publish tab with the Publish All button visible.")

      The Publish Workflows page displays.
   2. Select Authorize My Account.

      The Docusign Workflow Builder is requesting
      access dialog box displays.
   3. Select Allow Access.

      The dialog box closes. The Publish Workflows page
      displays.
   4. Select Publish.

      The Publish workflows page displays a spinning wheel icon. The
      Done tab displays.
4. Select Done.

   The Docusign app center dialog box
   closes.

You have completed the process. You have installed IAM Solutions
resources in your Docusign account and connected it to your Salesforce
organization. Salesforce users can start Docusign workflows from
Salesforce records.

title: Add Docusign Workflow Actions to Layouts (IAM Launcher Setup Controls)
heading_path: Add Docusign Workflow Actions to Layouts (IAM Launcher Setup Controls)
text:
Learn to use the IAM Launcher Setup interface to add Docusign workflow
actions to Salesforce layouts.

title: Add Docusign Workflow Actions to Layouts (IAM Launcher Setup Controls)
heading_path: Add Docusign Workflow Actions to Layouts (IAM Launcher Setup Controls)
Before you begin
text:
Important: You cannot use the IAM Launcher Setup
controls to add actions to some Salesforce objects, like Case. If you can't add an action
with the IAM Launcher Setup controls, [add actions
with Salesforce Object Manager](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=rrf1583359212854&topicId=fji1583359148678.html&_LANG=enus).

If you want to start
Docusign workflows from Salesforce records, you can add a
Start with Docusign action to your layouts. The Start with
Docusign action displays Docusign workflows
that users can start from records without logging on to Docusign.

Use this procedure to add a Start with Docusign action to a
layout with the IAM Launcher Setup controls:

1. Manage Connections and the IAM Launcher: Select Manage.

   The docusign app center dialog box displays.
2. Add an IAM Launcher action to a Salesforce layout:
   1. Select the IAM Launcher Setup tab.
   2. Select a connection: Choose a connection from the dropdown menu. You can only choose shared connections.

      ![A screenshot displays the IAM Launcher Setup tab and Connection menu highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/IAM_for_Sales_IAM_Launcher_Setup_tab.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU0MDgsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.hrGDavqRIs8ApXNWxMcwMjLMLS6WY32-0qZ3NzfWZ1M&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the IAM Launcher Setup tab and Connection menu highlighted.")

      The Create IAM Launcher section displays.
   3. Select Create.

      The Button Options section displays.
   4. Object: Enter an object name or select one from the dropdown menu.
   5. Account Layouts (optional): Select check boxes to add the action to specific layouts.
   6. Select Create.

      ![A screenshot displays the Button Options page with the Account object selected.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/IAM_for_Sales_IAM_Launcher_Button_Options_object.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU0MDgsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.hrGDavqRIs8ApXNWxMcwMjLMLS6WY32-0qZ3NzfWZ1M&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Button Options page with the Account object selected.")
3. Repeat this process for each layout to which you want to add Docusign workflow actions.

You have completed the process. You have created a Start with Docusign action and added it to a layout. Users can start workflows without logging on to Docusign.

title: Remove a Workflow from the Start with Docusign Action Menu
heading_path: Remove a Workflow from the Start with Docusign Action Menu
text:
Learn to remove a Docusign workflow from a Start with Docusign action
menu.

title: Remove a Workflow from the Start with Docusign Action Menu
heading_path: Remove a Workflow from the Start with Docusign Action Menu
Before you begin
text:
Important: This feature is only available to IAM for
Sales customers. Contact [Docusign support](https://support.docusign.com/s/?language=en_US&_gl=1*74elk4*_gcl_au*MTY5ODQyMjA2MC4xNzY5NjM1OTA1LjE5ODM3MTQzNjguMTc2OTYzNjIwNC4xNzY5NjM2MjA0) or [Docusign
sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales) for more information.

[Add a Start with Docusign action to a layout](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=tskb637a1ca-b7ba-4604-97f7-f5f2d5f899f6.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to add a Start with Docusign action to a page layout to start workflows.").

If you want to start Docusign workflows from Salesforce records,
you can add a Start with Docusign action to your layouts. The
action displays Docusign workflows that you can start from your
records. You can add and remove the workflows that display in the action menu. You
can also search for workflows to add to the action menu.

Only administrators can remove workflows from the action menu.

Use this procedure to remove a workflow from a Start with Docusign
action menu:

1. Choose your access method:
   - From the Docusign Setup page:
     1. [Access the Docusign Setup
        page as a Salesforce administrator](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=etv1586134206174.html&_LANG=enus)
     2. Manage Connections and the IAM Launcher: Select
        Manage.
     3. Select the IAM Launcher Setup tab.
     4. Select the Manage button next to a layout
        (Account, Opportunity, and so on.)

        The Start with Docusign menu displays.
   - From a Salesforce record: Select the Start with
     Docusign action in the upper right of the record.

     The Start
     with Docusign menu displays.
2. Select the three-dot menu ![A screenshot displays three dots placed in a horizontal line.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/IAM_Launcher_component_three-dot_menu.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU1NzIsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.I5EdJ7msCd2aSbR8euYdC841vrrFm0bZQ7_-oDsPt08&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays three dots placed in a horizontal line.") next to a workflow and choose Remove this
   workflow from the dropdown menu.

   ![A screenshot displays the three-dot menu and the Remove this workflow option highlighted in the Start with Docusign action menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_Salesforce_action_menu_Remove_this_workflow.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU1NzIsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.I5EdJ7msCd2aSbR8euYdC841vrrFm0bZQ7_-oDsPt08&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the three-dot menu and the Remove this workflow option highlighted in the Start with Docusign action menu.")The Remove workflow dialog box displays.
3. Select Remove

   Note: Docusign removes the workflow tile
   from the menu, but does not delete the workflow. You can re-add the workflow
   tile to the menu later.

   The Remove workflow dialog box closes. A message displays:
   (name of workflow) removed.
4. Select the X icon to close the action menu dialog
   box.

You have completed the process. You have removed a workflow from the
Start with Docusign action menu.

title: Add Docusign Workflow Actions to Layouts (Manual Controls)
heading_path: Add Docusign Workflow Actions to Layouts (Manual Controls)
text:
Learn to add a Start with Docusign action to a page layout to start
workflows.

title: Add Docusign Workflow Actions to Layouts (Manual Controls)
heading_path: Add Docusign Workflow Actions to Layouts (Manual Controls)
Before you begin
text:
Important:

- This feature is only available to IAM for Sales customers. Contact [Docusign support](https://support.docusign.com/s/?language=en_US&_gl=1*74elk4*_gcl_au*MTY5ODQyMjA2MC4xNzY5NjM1OTA1LjE5ODM3MTQzNjguMTc2OTYzNjIwNC4xNzY5NjM2MjA0) or [Docusign
  sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales) for more information.
- Docusign recommends using the [IAM Launcher Setup Controls](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=tska9d68585-f524-49a4-8898-496b6575d036.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to use the IAM Launcher Setup interface to add Docusign workflow actions to Salesforce layouts.") to add workflow actions. Use these
  instructions if the IAM Launcher Setup controls fail.

[Build a Start with Docusign
action](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=tsk32e505bf-436a-47b1-b7f6-55e6ad614ec5.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to build a new Lightning web component action to start a Docusign workflow from a record.").

If you want to start Docusign workflows from Salesforce records,
you can add a Start with Docusign action to your layouts. The
Start with Docusign action displays workflows that you can start
from your records. Users can start workflows from a record without logging on to
your Docusign account.

Use this procedure to add a Start with Docusign action to a
layout:

1. Access an object's page layout as a Salesforce administrator:
   1. Select the gear icon ![A screenshot displays a six-point gear with a lightning bolt inside it.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/salesforce-gear_.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU0MTYsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.GmTEyRox3CkbE0WCgfKgM7etFAQVamp4T4rgnsMt70k&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a six-point gear with a lightning bolt inside it.") and choose Setup.
   2. Enter and select Object Manager in the Quick Find
      field.
   3. Select a Salesforce object in the left column and select
      Page Layouts on the Details page.
   4. Select a layout from the Page Layouts
      column.

   The object's page layout displays.
2. Find the Start with Docusign action:
   1. Select Mobile & Lightning Actions in the
      upper left of the layout menu.
   2. Salesforce Mobile and Lightning Experience
      Actions: Select the override the predefined
      actions link. If the link does not display, you do not
      need to take this action.

      ![A screenshot displays the Salesforce Mobile and Lightning actions section with the override the predefined actions link highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Mobile_and_Lightning_actions_override_the_predefined_actions_link.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU0MTYsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.GmTEyRox3CkbE0WCgfKgM7etFAQVamp4T4rgnsMt70k&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Salesforce Mobile and Lightning actions section with the override the predefined actions link highlighted.")
   3. Enter Start with Docusign in the Quick
      Find field.
3. Add the Start with Docusign action to the page layout:
   1. Drag the Start with Docusign action to the
      Salesforce Mobile and Lightning Experience
      Actions section.
   2. Select Save.

Before you begin

d.
3. Add the Start with Docusign action to the page layout:
   1. Drag the Start with Docusign action to the
      Salesforce Mobile and Lightning Experience

   2. Select Save.

   ![A screen capture displays the process of dragging the Start with Docusign action to the Salesforce Mobile and Lightning Experience Actions section.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Add_Start_with_Docusign_button_to_layout.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU0MTYsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.GmTEyRox3CkbE0WCgfKgM7etFAQVamp4T4rgnsMt70k&_LANG=enus "A screen capture displays the process of dragging the Start with Docusign action to the Salesforce Mobile and Lightning Experience Actions section.")

   The Saving Layout message displays. The Page

title: Turn on Agreement Manager for Salesforce Notifications
heading_path: Turn on Agreement Manager for Salesforce Notifications
text:
Learn to set notifications for agreements displayed in the Docusign
Completed Agreements component.

title: Turn on Agreement Manager for Salesforce Notifications
heading_path: Turn on Agreement Manager for Salesforce Notifications
Before you begin
text:
Important: This feature is only available to IAM for
Sales customers. Contact [Docusign support](https://support.docusign.com/s/?language=en_US&_gl=1*74elk4*_gcl_au*MTY5ODQyMjA2MC4xNzY5NjM1OTA1LjE5ODM3MTQzNjguMTc2OTYzNjIwNC4xNzY5NjM2MjA0) or [Docusign
sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales) for more information.

You can turn on notifications for the agreements displayed in the Docusign Completed Agreements component. Notifications help you
stay informed about upcoming milestones for individual agreements. You can manage
notifications by interacting with agreements on the Docusign
Completed Agreements component interface. Notification settings give you control
over the email notifications that you receive.

Use this procedure to manage agreement notifications in the Docusign Completed Agreements component:

1. Open a record and look for the Docusign Agreements
   component.

   Note: A Salesforce administrator may have placed the
   component on a tab labeled Docusign Agreements or
   something similar. The component features the word
   Completed at the top.
2. Hover your cursor over the agreement that you want to track and select the
   Set a notification (bell) icon:

   Note: If a notification already exists, the
   Manage notifications (bell) icon displays. Select the bell icon to update existing notifications for the
   agreement.

   ![A screenshot of the Completed view that highlights the Set a notification option in the agreement list table.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Navigator_for_Salesforce_notification_bell_icon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU3ODYsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.CYvcZ9ZKcOwnwbrgv3NXyU10w5woosaw8TJ5_CqVkdA&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Completed view that highlights the Set a notification option in the agreement list table.")

   The Notifications function opens. The Renewal Notice
   Date and Expiration Date notifications
   are turned on by default.

   Note: When you
   enable the notification, you are designated as the recipient and all
   frequency options are selected.
3. (Optional) + Add: Add notifications for this agreement:
   1. Select the bell icon and select + Add from the
      Notifications dialog box that displays.

      ![A screenshot of the Completed view that highlights the Add notification option in the agreement list table.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Navigator_for_Salesforce_notification__Add_icon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU3ODYsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.CYvcZ9ZKcOwnwbrgv3NXyU10w5woosaw8TJ5_CqVkdA&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Completed view that highlights the Add notification option in the agreement list table.")

      The Add notification dialog box displays.
   2. Notification type: Select an option from the dropdown menu.
   3. Select Save.
4. (Optional) Edit: Select the link to customize the
   frequency and recipients for the notifications:
   1. Select the bell icon and select Edit next to an
      entry in the Notifications dialog box.

      The Edit notification dialog box displays.
   2. Expand the frequency dropdown, like Remind me
      prior to expiration date, and choose when you
      want to receive notifications.
   3. Add a user: Specify the users that should also receive these notifications. Type a
      name or email address in the field and
      select a name from the dropdown list.

      Note: The Self
      option is selected by default and cannot be removed.
   4. Select Save.

title: Turn on Agreement Manager for Salesforce Notifications
heading_path: Turn on Agreement Manager for Salesforce Notifications
Before you begin
text:
ons. Type a
      name or email address in the field and
      select a name from the dropdown list.

      Note: The Self
      option is selected by default and cannot be removed.
   4. Select Save.

      ![A screenshot of the Edit notification dialog box that shows configuration options for the selected notification.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Document-level-notifications_Edit-notification-dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU3ODYsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.CYvcZ9ZKcOwnwbrgv3NXyU10w5woosaw8TJ5_CqVkdA&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Edit notification dialog box that shows configuration options for the selected notification.")

   The Edit notification dialog box
   closes.
5. Use the Manage notifications (bell) icon to update the
   document's notifications at any time, including:

   - Adding new notifications
   - Updating existing notification settings
   - Managing the distribution list
   - Deleting the notification

You have completed the process. You have added or edited agreement notifications in the Docusign Completed Agreements component. You receive notifications
when agreement milestones occur.

title: Add a List of Participants to a Salesforce Workflow Step
heading_path: Add a List of Participants to a Salesforce Workflow Step
text: Learn to add a list of participants to a Salesforce workflow step.

title: Add a List of Participants to a Salesforce Workflow Step
heading_path: Add a List of Participants to a Salesforce Workflow Step
Before you begin
text:
- Required: Installation of the Salesforce app from Docusign App Center. Review the [Installing Extension Applications for
  Workflow Builder](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=bgb1697668738892.html&_LANG=enus)
  topic for more information.
- Required: [Workflows Create permissions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gbb1696973048215.html&_LANG=enus) or be an account
  administrator.
- Required: Create a workflow as described in [Create a Basic Workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uhm1697826856678.html&_LANG=enus). Add steps like
  [invitation](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=kfx1698167344720.html&_LANG=enus), [web forms](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gua1698120920620.html&_LANG=enus), [eSignature](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uww1698167288211.html&_LANG=enus) and more to gather recipients,
  documents, and merge fields for your workflow.

You can dynamically map recipients from a Read from Salesforce
step to a Send Documents for Signature step. Docusign can import a list of contacts related to a Salesforce
record and add them as envelope recipients. Senders are not required to enter
recipient names and contact information manually.

You can use the following related list objects to add agreement recipients:

| Parent Object | Related List (Child) | Common use Case |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Account | Contacts | Map multiple contacts as agreement signers |
| Opportunity | Opportunity Contact Role >  Contact | Map contact roles (for example, buyer, approver) as participants |
| Opportunity | Opportunity Line Items | Read multiple product lines |

The following logic applies to creating Salesforce participant lists:

- The Read from Salesforce step adds up to five related records per parent object.
  If more than five records exist, Salesforce logic decides which five records to
  add.
- You can only map participant lists in the Read from Salesforce step. You cannot
  map participant lists in Writeback to Salesforce or other Salesforce steps.
- You can map up to 10 participant fields per related object, like Full Name,
  Email, and so on.
- When you select a related list object, Docusign reads fields across multiple
  objects, like Opportunity >  Opportunity Contact Role >  Contact.

Example: You want to create a workflow that
automatically sends an NDA agreement to multiple Salesforce contacts. You add a Read
from Salesforce step to your workflow. You create a Salesforce Contacts participant
list by mapping the following Salesforce and Docusign fields:

| Salesforce field | Docusign field |
| --- | --- |
| Account / Contacts / Department | participantRoleName |
| Account / Contacts / Full Name | participantName |
| Account / Contacts / Email | participantEmail |
| Account / Contacts / Business Phone | participantPhone |

You add a Send Documents for Signature step to your workflow.
You choose the Add dynamic list of participants option and
add the Salesforce Contacts list. You assign a recipient role of
`Signer` to the list.

A user starts a workflow from a Salesforce account with five contacts.

Result: Docusign creates an agreement for all
five contacts, with signature fields for each contact's department, full name,
email, and business phone. Docusign sends the NDA to all contacts
automatically, with no manual entry needed.

Use this procedure to add a list of participants to a workflow:

title: Add a List of Participants to a Salesforce Workflow Step
heading_path: Add a List of Participants to a Salesforce Workflow Step
Before you begin
text:
