's department, full name,
email, and business phone. Docusign sends the NDA to all contacts
automatically, with no manual entry needed.

Use this procedure to add a list of participants to a workflow:

1. [Create a basic workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uhm1697826856678.html&_LANG=enus) containing [Read from Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=idr1713985144297.html&_LANG=enus) and [Send Documents for Signature](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uww1698167288211.html&_LANG=enus) steps.
2. Configure your Read from Salesforce step:
   1. Open the step to the Which fields would you like to
      read? section.
   2. Select Add or Remove Fields.

      The Add or Remove Fields page displays.
   3. Select the Related Lists tab on the left.
   4. Select the check box next to each Salesforce related list field that
      you'd like to use.
   5. Select Save and Exit.

      ![A screenshot displays the Add or remove fields section with Salesforce field check boxes highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Read_from_Salesforce_Related_Lists_Contacts_fields_selected.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU0ODYsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.GcaTz-r4hWbbf5m67zItKZXorfbnz38pcCeRVpbImPw&_LANG=enus)

      The Add or Remove Fields dialog box closes.
      The related list object that you selected displays.
   6. Select the Map button next to the related
      list.

      ![A screenshot displays the Which fields would you like to read section with a Map button highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Read_from_Salesforce_Related_Lists_Map_Contacts_button.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU0ODYsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.GcaTz-r4hWbbf5m67zItKZXorfbnz38pcCeRVpbImPw&_LANG=enus)

      The Map to Docusign Object dialog box
      displays.
3. Map your Salesforce and Docusign objects and fields:
   1. Docusign object: Select a Docusign object from the dropdown menu.

      The Map Salesforce fields to your Participant
      object section expands.
   2. Salesforce fields: Select a Salesforce field to
      match with each Docusign field.
   3. Repeat this process for each displayed field.
   4. Select Save Mapping.

      ![A screenshot displays the Map to Docusign object dialog box with a Docusign object and fields highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Read_from_Salesforce_Map_to_Docusign_Object_dialog_box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU0ODYsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.GcaTz-r4hWbbf5m67zItKZXorfbnz38pcCeRVpbImPw&_LANG=enus)

      The Map to Docusign Object dialog box closes.
   5. Select Next.
   6. Complete the rest of your Read from Salesforce step and select
      Apply.
4. Configure your Send Documents for Signature step:
   1. Open the step to the Confirm recipient details
      section.
   2. Match the template's [name] recipient to one of this
      workflow's participants: Select Add
      Participant from the dropdown menu.
   3. Select Add participant.

title: Add a List of Participants to a Salesforce Workflow Step
heading_path: Add a List of Participants to a Salesforce Workflow Step
Before you begin
text:
ient details
      section.
   2. Match the template's [name] recipient to one of this
      workflow's participants: Select Add
      Participant from the dropdown menu.
   3. Select Add participant.

      ![A screenshot displays the Confirm recipient details section with the Select and Add Participant fields highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Read_from_Salesforce_Related_Lists_Add_Participant.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU0ODYsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.GcaTz-r4hWbbf5m67zItKZXorfbnz38pcCeRVpbImPw&_LANG=enus)

      The Add Participant dialog box displays.

      Note: A participant is a placeholder for someone who
      will take action at specific steps in your workflow. Docusign uses the participant information to
      assign that role to a person when the workflow
      runs.
   4. Add a dynamic list of participants: Select the
      switch icon to turn it on.

      The switch moves to the right.
   5. Dynamic list: Choose the dynamic recipients list
      from your Read from Salesforce step.

      ![A screenshot displays the Add Participant dialog box with a Salesforce Dynamic Recipients list highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Read_from_Salesforce_Dynamic_Recipients_Add_Participant_dialog_box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU0ODYsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.GcaTz-r4hWbbf5m67zItKZXorfbnz38pcCeRVpbImPw&_LANG=enus)
   6. Role: Enter a role name for your recipients
      list, like `Approver`.

      Tip:
      - Use unique role names: Make sure the role names that you’re bringing in
        from Salesforce are unique. If there are multiple roles with
        the same name in your incoming list, Docusign only imports the first one.
      - Use the same role name in your source system and
        eSignature template: Make sure the role names
        from Salesforce match the recipient roles in your eSignature
        template. If they don’t, the workflow will likely fail.
   7. Select Confirm Role.

      ![A screenshot displays the Add Participant dialog box with a Salesforce Dynamic Recipients list and a Role highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Send_Documents_for_Signature_step_add_dynamic_Read_from_Salesforce_list.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU0ODYsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.GcaTz-r4hWbbf5m67zItKZXorfbnz38pcCeRVpbImPw&_LANG=enus)

      The Additional guidance dialog box displays. Select
      OK to close the dialog box.
   8. Select Add.

      The Add Participant dialog box closes.
   9. Select Next.
5. Complete the rest of your Send Documents for Signature step and select
   Apply.

You have completed the process. You have added a list of participants to your workflow. When
the workflow starts, Docusign creates an agreement and adds all mapped
contacts and fields.

title: Start a Docusign Workflow from a Salesforce Record
heading_path: Start a Docusign Workflow from a Salesforce Record
text: Learn to start a Docusign workflow from a Start with Docusign action.

title: Start a Docusign Workflow from a Salesforce Record
heading_path: Start a Docusign Workflow from a Salesforce Record
Before you begin
text:
Important: This feature is only available to IAM for
Sales customers. Contact [Docusign support](https://support.docusign.com/s/?language=en_US&_gl=1*74elk4*_gcl_au*MTY5ODQyMjA2MC4xNzY5NjM1OTA1LjE5ODM3MTQzNjguMTc2OTYzNjIwNC4xNzY5NjM2MjA0) or [Docusign
sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales) for more information.

You can use the Start with Docusign action to start workflows
from Salesforce records.

Both administrators and standard users can start workflows from the action menu.

Use this procedure to start a workflow from a Start with Docusign
action:

Note: The action menu only displays workflows that
you can use on that record.

1. Open a record containing a Start with Docusign action.
2. Select the Start with Docusign action in the upper
   right of the record.

   The Start with Docusign action menu displays.
3. Use the action menu to search for a workflow:
   1. Enter a full or partial workflow name in the Search
      workflows field.

      ![A screenshot displays the Search workflows field highlighted in the Start with Docusign action menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Start_Workflow_Builder_Workflow_Button_Menu_Search_Workflows.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU1NzUsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.Cbc6dIq3T9tM0dSTyJpJbBvIfA6kA-IkxrzTK3f6KNg&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Search workflows field highlighted in the Start with Docusign action menu.")

      Workflows matching your search query display.
   2. Select Start next to a workflow name.

      A message displays: Your workflow was
      initiated.
   3. Select the X button in the upper right to close
      the Docusign dialog box.

   The Docusign dialog box closes. The Salesforce record
   displays.

You have completed the process. You have started a workflow from a
Start with Docusign action. Docusign sends tasks to
all workflow recipients.

title: Edit a Saved Search in the Docusign Agreement Requests Component
heading_path: Edit a Saved Search in the Docusign Agreement Requests Component
text:
Learn how to edit a saved search from the Docusign Agreement Requests
component.

title: Edit a Saved Search in the Docusign Agreement Requests Component
heading_path: Edit a Saved Search in the Docusign Agreement Requests Component
Before you begin
text:
Important: This feature is only available to IAM for Sales
customers. Contact [Docusign support](https://support.docusign.com/s/?language=en_US&_gl=1*74elk4*_gcl_au*MTY5ODQyMjA2MC4xNzY5NjM1OTA1LjE5ODM3MTQzNjguMTc2OTYzNjIwNC4xNzY5NjM2MjA0) or [Docusign
sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales) for more information.

[Create a saved search in the
Docusign Agreement Requests component](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=tsk96d6f83e-3826-4c3f-9bba-6ac03bea79bb.html&_LANG=enus).

Agreement Desk is a central platform for streamlining your entire agreement process within Docusign. You can use Agreement Desk to submit and manage requests, negotiate and edit agreements, and use AI to automate contracts.

You can search and use filters to locate the requests that you want to see. You can also save your searches to run again later.

Use this procedure to edit a saved search in the Docusign Agreement
Requests component:

1. Open a record to the Agreement Requests component and select the
   Manage saved searches
   ![A screenshot displays a bookmark icon.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Screenshot_2026-05-11_at_5.55.40AM.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU2OTMsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.zu3WfuOqMwF-cMyxjjRC1MCKsLZKBcDb-07i4euEASQ&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a bookmark icon.") button.

   The Manage Saved Searches dialog box displays.
2. Select Manage saved searches.

   The Manage Saved searches dialog box displays.
3. Edit the saved search:
   1. Select the three-dot menu icon ![A screenshot displays three dots stacked vertically.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/A-screenshot-diplays-a-threedot-menu-icon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU2OTMsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.zu3WfuOqMwF-cMyxjjRC1MCKsLZKBcDb-07i4euEASQ&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays three dots stacked vertically.") next to a saved search and choose Edit name and
      description.

      ![A screenshot displays the Manage Saved Searches dialog box with the three-dot menu displaying options to edit or delete a saved search.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Agreement_Desk_for_Salesforce_Edit_and_delete_saved_searches_menu.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU2OTMsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.zu3WfuOqMwF-cMyxjjRC1MCKsLZKBcDb-07i4euEASQ&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Manage Saved Searches dialog box with the three-dot menu displaying options to edit or delete a saved search.")

      The Edit Saved Search dialog box displays.
   2. (Optional) New saved search: Edit the name of the saved search. You can enter up to 120 characters.
   3. (Optional) Description: Add or edit the saved search description. You can enter up to 200 characters.
   4. Select Update.

      The dialog box closes. A message displays: Search updated successfully.
   5. Select the X to close the Manage Saved Searches dialog box.

   The dialog box closes. The Docusign Agreement Requests component
   displays.

You have completed the process. You have edited a saved search from the Docusign Agreement Requests component.

title: Build a Workflow to Be Triggered By a Start with Docusign Action
heading_path: Build a Workflow to Be Triggered By a Start with Docusign Action
text: Learn to build a workflow that can be triggered by a Start with Docusign action.

title: Build a Workflow to Be Triggered By a Start with Docusign Action
heading_path: Build a Workflow to Be Triggered By a Start with Docusign Action
Before you begin
text:
Important: This feature is only available to IAM for
Sales customers. Contact [Docusign support](https://support.docusign.com/s/?language=en_US&_gl=1*74elk4*_gcl_au*MTY5ODQyMjA2MC4xNzY5NjM1OTA1LjE5ODM3MTQzNjguMTc2OTYzNjIwNC4xNzY5NjM2MjA0) or [Docusign
sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales) for more information.

Required: [Workflows Create permissions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gbb1696973048215.html&_LANG=enus) or be an account
administrator.

You can use Workflow Builder to automate agreement actions from
your Docusign account.

Docusign Apps Launcher is a package of Docusign
apps that you can use in your Salesforce organization. These apps include Docusign eSignature for Salesforce, Docusign Gen
for Salesforce, and Docusign CLM for Salesforce.

You cannot build Workflow Builder workflows in the Apps Launcher user interface. However, you
can start Workflow Builder workflows from Salesforce records to create agreements with
Salesforce and Docusign data.

Before you can start workflows from Salesforce, you must create a compatible
workflow.

Use this procedure to build a Workflow Builder workflow that can be started from
Salesforce:

1. As a Docusign administrator, create your workflow:
   1. Log on to your Docusign account and go to Agreements >  Workflows.
   2. Select Create Workflow.

      The Creating your workflow dialog box
      displays.
   3. Select New Canvas and select
      Continue.

      The Creating your workflow dialog box
      closes. The workflow builder page displays.
   4. Select Add workflow start.

      The Add workflow start dialog box displays.
2. Select a method: Select either From a Link or From an API
   Call from the dropdown menu and select
   Next.

   Note: You cannot select other methods when building workflows that start in Salesforce.

   ![A screenshot displays the What starts this workflow step with From a Link and From an API Call highlighted in green.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/What-starts-this-workflow-with-From-a-link-and-From-an-API-Call-highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU1NjQsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.LSy4WW9GiF-cBH8I1yfEQhwJPYwIhv5vkrmZLvO2Qsw&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the What starts this workflow step with From a Link and From an API Call highlighted in green.")
3. Set up variables for dynamic information: Add the following variables:
   1. Select New Variable and choose
      Text from the dropdown menu.
   2. Enter `recordId` in the field.
   3. Create another text variable and enter `objectName` in the field.
   4. Create another text variable and enter `applicationName` in the field.
   5. Create another text variable and enter `orgId` in the field.

      ![A screenshot displays the Set Up Variables step with recordId, objectName, applicationName, and orgId variables highlighted in green.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Start_Workflow_Builder_workflows_from_Salesforce_Set_up_variables_for_dynamic_information.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU1NjQsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.LSy4WW9GiF-cBH8I1yfEQhwJPYwIhv5vkrmZLvO2Qsw&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Set Up Variables step with recordId, objectName, applicationName, and orgId variables highlighted in green.")
4. Will this API call be started by a human or an automated
   process?:
   1. Select Human.
   2. Select Apply.

   The page refreshes. Your From an API call start
   step displays.
5. Complete your workflow:
   1. Add more steps to your workflow.
   2. Select Review & Publish when you have
      finished building your workflow.

title: Build a Workflow to Be Triggered By a Start with Docusign Action
heading_path: Build a Workflow to Be Triggered By a Start with Docusign Action
Before you begin
text:
shes. Your From an API call start
   step displays.
5. Complete your workflow:
   1. Add more steps to your workflow.
   2. Select Review & Publish when you have
      finished building your workflow.

You have completed the process. You have built a workflow that can
be triggered by a Send with Docusign action. When a user selects a
workflow's Start button, the workflow starts.

title: Update Salesforce Settings to Add Docusign IAM Workflow Actions
heading_path: Update Salesforce Settings to Add Docusign IAM Workflow Actions
text:
Learn to update your Salesforce settings to add Docusign IAM
workflow actions on your layouts.

title: Update Salesforce Settings to Add Docusign IAM Workflow Actions
heading_path: Update Salesforce Settings to Add Docusign IAM Workflow Actions
Before you begin
text:
If you want to start Docusign workflows from Salesforce records,
you can add Start with Docusign actions to your layouts. The
Start with Docusign action displays workflows that you can start
from your records. Users can start Workflow Builder workflows from a record without logging
on to Docusign.

You can use the IAM Launcher Setup interface to add a ​Start with Docusign​ action to your layouts. Access the interface by going to Docusign Setup >  Manage Connections and the IAM Launcher >  Manage​​.

Before you can add workflow actions to your layout, you must update your Salesforce
settings.

Use this procedure to update your Salesforce settings to add Docusign IAM workflow actions on your layouts:

1. Access a page layout as a Salesforce administrator:
   1. Select the gear icon ![A screenshot displays a six-point gear with a lightning bolt inside it.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/salesforce-gear_.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU0MDYsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.MVVnutmSiW917VGaP79TacXwlB3BiSCxSAquMeI8V7Q&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a six-point gear with a lightning bolt inside it.") and choose Setup.
   2. Enter and select Object Manager in the Quick Find
      field.

      The Object Manager page displays.
   3. Select a layout from the LABEL column.

      The object's Details page displays.
   4. Select Page Layouts.

      The Page Layouts page displays.
   5. PAGE LAYOUT NAME: Select the page layout that you
      want to update.

      The selected page layout displays.
2. Override the default global publisher layout for this object:
   1. Select Fields in the object layout menu.
   2. Salesforce Mobile and Lightning Experience Actions:
      Select the override the predefined actions link.

      Note: If you do not see the link, your layout does not need updating. You can close the page without taking action.

      The bar displays a list of actions.
   3. Select Save on the object layout menu.

      ![A screen capture displays selecting the Quick Actions in the Salesforce Classic Publisher wrench icon and saving the Account layout changes.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/IAM_Launcher_override_the_global_publisher_layout.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU0MDYsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.MVVnutmSiW917VGaP79TacXwlB3BiSCxSAquMeI8V7Q&_LANG=enus "A screen capture displays selecting the Quick Actions in the Salesforce Classic Publisher wrench icon and saving the Account layout changes.")

      A Saving layout message displays. The Page Layouts page opens.
3. Repeat the process for each layout to which you want to add Docusign workflow actions.
4. Access the Trusted URLs page:
   1. Enter and select Trusted URLs in the Quick Find
      field.

      The Trusted URLs page displays.
   2. Select New Trusted URL.

      The Trusted URLs page displays.
   3. API Name: Enter
      `Contentful_CDN`.
   4. URL: Enter
      `https://cdn.contentful.com`.
   5. Select Save.

      ![A screenshot displays the Trusted URLs page with Contentful CDN entered in the API Name and contentful dot-com in the URL field.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/IAM_for_Salesforce_Trusted_URLs_for_Contentful.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU0MDYsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.MVVnutmSiW917VGaP79TacXwlB3BiSCxSAquMeI8V7Q&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Trusted URLs page with Contentful CDN entered in the API Name and contentful dot-com in the URL field.")

title: Update Salesforce Settings to Add Docusign IAM Workflow Actions
heading_path: Update Salesforce Settings to Add Docusign IAM Workflow Actions
Before you begin
text:
M2ZDlmODM1In0.MVVnutmSiW917VGaP79TacXwlB3BiSCxSAquMeI8V7Q&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Trusted URLs page with Contentful CDN entered in the API Name and contentful dot-com in the URL field.")

      The page refreshes, displaying your new trusted
      URL.
5. Access the Session Settings page:
   1. Enter and select Session Settings in the Quick
      Find field.
   1. Uncheck Lock sessions to the IP address from which they
      originated.
   2. Select Save.

You have completed the process. You have updated the necessary Salesforce settings. You can
add Docusign workflow actions to this layout.

title: Customize Agreement Desk for Salesforce Columns
heading_path: Customize Agreement Desk for Salesforce Columns
Before you begin
text:
Important: This feature is only available to IAM for
Sales customers. Contact [Docusign support](https://support.docusign.com/s/?language=en_US&_gl=1*74elk4*_gcl_au*MTY5ODQyMjA2MC4xNzY5NjM1OTA1LjE5ODM3MTQzNjguMTc2OTYzNjIwNC4xNzY5NjM2MjA0) or [Docusign
sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales) for more information.

Agreement Desk is a central platform for streamlining your entire agreement process
within Docusign. You can use Agreement Desk to submit and manage
requests, negotiate and edit agreements, and use AI to automate contracts. When you
add the Agreement Requests component to your layouts, users can review, manage,
sign, and send agreements without logging on to Docusign.

You can customize component columns to view and act on request details like Title,
Owner, Source Record, ID, and more. When you customize your column view, the
following rules apply:

- Your changes do not affect other users.
- Your custom settings remain when you log off and on.
- Your settings apply to all records and objects that contain the Embedded Agreement Requests
  component.

Note: Your settings *do not* remain if you empty
your browser cache, use a new browser, or open an incognito session.

Use this procedure to customize component columns to view and act on details of
displayed requests:

1. Open a record and look for the Embedded Agreements Desk component.

   Note: A Salesforce administrator may have placed the
   component on a tab labeled Agreement Desk or
   something similar. The component features the word
   Requests at the top.
2. Select the Customize Columns ![A screenshot displays a pair of switches with one to the right and one to the left.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Agreement_Desk_for_Salesforce_Customize_Columns_icon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU2NzksInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.QUrBQQM4dGLa5plcWlQ8P0g2RZ-haD3xm1iow9DLbsw&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a pair of switches with one to the right and one to the left.") icon.

   The Customize columns dialog box displays.
3. Select the switch next to a column name to display or hide a column. You can
   search for columns by entering column names in the Find
   columns field.
   - Owner: View the name of the request owner. The owner
     is assigned by the request submitter.
   - Source Record: View the Salesforce object that the
     request was created from. You can select the link to open the record.
   - ID: View the unique system ID for the Agreement Desk
     request record in Salesforce.
   - Status: View the current stage of an Agreement Desk
     request, like In Review, Signed, Canceled, and so on.
   - Last Activity At: View the date and time of the most
     recent interaction with a request, like the date it was edited or sent for
     signature.
   - Due Date: View the date by which the owner must
     take action or complete the request.
   - Request Type: View the category of the Agreement Desk
     request, like MSA, NDA, SOW, and so on.
   - Created At: View the date and time that the Agreement
     Desk request was first created.
   - Submitter: View the name of the person who created
     the Agreement Desk request.

   The switches move to the right when you turn them on. They
   move to the left when you turn them off.
4. Select Save.

title: Customize Agreement Desk for Salesforce Columns
heading_path: Customize Agreement Desk for Salesforce Columns
Before you begin
text:
ter: View the name of the person who created
     the Agreement Desk request.

   The switches move to the right when you turn them on. They
   move to the left when you turn them off.
4. Select Save.

   ![A screenshot displays the Customize Columns dialog box with the Owner and Source Record switches turned on.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Agreement_Desk_for_Salesforce_Customize_Columns_dialog_box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU2NzksInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.QUrBQQM4dGLa5plcWlQ8P0g2RZ-haD3xm1iow9DLbsw&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Customize Columns dialog box with the Owner and Source Record switches turned on.")The dialog box closes. The columns that you turned on display in the
   Embedded Agreement Requests component.

You have completed the process. You have changed the columns that display in the Embedded
Agreement Requests component.

title: Refresh the Salesforce Connection to Your Docusign Account
heading_path: Refresh the Salesforce Connection to Your Docusign Account
text:
Learn to refresh the connection between Salesforce and Docusign after
you add fields or objects to your Salesforce organization.

You can connect Salesforce organizations to your Docusign IAM account. When you create a connection, you can build workflows that
read from and write to your Salesforce records. If you add Salesforce fields or
objects, perform a refresh so that the Salesforce app can access the new
content.

You can access the refresh controls through your Salesforce organization or your Docusign account.

Use this procedure to refresh the Salesforce content available to the app:

1. Choose your login method:
   - Login from Salesforce:
     1. [Access the Docusign Setup
        page as a Salesforce administrator](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=etv1586134206174.html&_LANG=enus)
     2. Manage Connections and the IAM Launcher: Select
        Manage.
   - Login from Docusign:
     1. Open the [Production](https://apps.docusign.com/app-center/extensionapps) or [Demo](https://apps-d.docusign.com/app-center/extensionapps) App Center site.
     2. Select the Salesforce tile.
     3. Select Manage and choose Manage
        Connections.
2. Select the three-dot menu icon ![A screenshot displays three dots stacked vertically.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/A-screenshot-diplays-a-threedot-menu-icon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU1NTQsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.ojiDoD1pZMQ81kwh0okVy2wutVmFnAYPpK_ZpiqcXTE&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays three dots stacked vertically.") next to a connection and choose Reload
   Objects.

   A dialog box displays: Refresh this
   connection?
3. Refresh this connection?: Enter `REFRESH` in the field and select Refresh Connection. ![A screenshot displays the Refresh this connection dialog box with the word Refresh and the Refresh Connection button highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Workflow_Builder_Refresh_Connection_dialog_box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU1NTQsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.ojiDoD1pZMQ81kwh0okVy2wutVmFnAYPpK_ZpiqcXTE&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Refresh this connection dialog box with the word Refresh and the Refresh Connection button highlighted.")

   The dialog box closes. The Connections page displays with a message: Successfully refreshed.

You have completed the process. You refreshed the connection to your
Docusign IAM account. New Salesforce fields or objects are now available for use in
Docusign workflows.

title: Refresh the Salesforce Connection to Your Docusign Account
heading_path: Refresh the Salesforce Connection to Your Docusign Account
text:
Learn to refresh the connection between Salesforce and Docusign after
you add fields or objects to your Salesforce organization.

You can connect Salesforce organizations to your Docusign IAM account. When you create a connection, you can build workflows that
read from and write to your Salesforce records. If you add Salesforce fields or
objects, perform a refresh so that the Salesforce app can access the new
content.

You can access the refresh controls through your Salesforce organization or your Docusign account.

Use this procedure to refresh the Salesforce content available to the app:

1. Choose your login method:
   - Login from Salesforce:
     1. [Access the Docusign Setup
        page as a Salesforce administrator](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=etv1586134206174.html&_LANG=enus)
     2. Manage Connections and the IAM Launcher: Select
        Manage.
   - Login from Docusign:
     1. Open the [Production](https://apps.docusign.com/app-center/extensionapps) or [Demo](https://apps-d.docusign.com/app-center/extensionapps) App Center site.
     2. Select the Salesforce tile.
     3. Select Manage and choose Manage
        Connections.
2. Select the three-dot menu icon ![A screenshot displays three dots stacked vertically.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/A-screenshot-diplays-a-threedot-menu-icon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU2MzMsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.12Kijgiewsunx8-PXgetElI1TU3IOPXbRGIlQtdUhEM&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays three dots stacked vertically.") next to a connection and choose Reload
   Objects.

   A dialog box displays: Refresh this
   connection?
3. Refresh this connection?: Enter `REFRESH` in the field and select Refresh Connection. ![A screenshot displays the Refresh this connection dialog box with the word Refresh and the Refresh Connection button highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Workflow_Builder_Refresh_Connection_dialog_box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU2MzMsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.12Kijgiewsunx8-PXgetElI1TU3IOPXbRGIlQtdUhEM&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Refresh this connection dialog box with the word Refresh and the Refresh Connection button highlighted.")

   The dialog box closes. The Connections page displays with a message: Successfully refreshed.

You have completed the process. You refreshed the connection to your
Docusign IAM account. New Salesforce fields or objects are now available for use in
Docusign workflows.

title: Use Docusign Connect to Link Salesforce Fields to Docusign Fields
heading_path: Use Docusign Connect to Link Salesforce Fields to Docusign Fields
text: Learn to map Salesforce objects to Docusign fields with Docusign Connect.

title: Use Docusign Connect to Link Salesforce Fields to Docusign Fields
heading_path: Use Docusign Connect to Link Salesforce Fields to Docusign Fields
Before you begin
text:
[Create a Connect Configuration to Add Salesforce Merge Fields to a Workflow Step](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=xza1742958339120.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create a Connect configuration to add Salesforce merge fields to your workflow's Send Documents for Signature step.")

You can use Docusign Connect to link Salesforce fields to Docusign fields. When recipients complete and sign agreements, Docusign updates Salesforce fields you linked with Docusign fields.

Use this procedure to link Salesforce fields to Docusign fields
with Connect:

1. Log in to your Docusign account as an administrator and go to Admin >  INTEGRATIONS >  Connect.
2. Select the Actions button next to your Salesforce
   configuration and choose Edit from the dropdown
   menu.
3. Connect Objects: Select New Object
   and choose Salesforce Object from the dropdown
   menu.

   The Salesforce Object Connect page opens.
4. Map a Salesforce object to your Docusign account:
   1. Inactive Object: Select Activate
      Object from the dropdown menu.
   2. Object Name: Enter a name for your
      connection.
   3. (Optional) Object Description: Enter a
      description that explains details of the connection.
   4. On Completed Envelope Only: Only update
      Salesforce records when all recipients complete the signing process.
   5. Add if No Match: Add data to Salesforce if it
      does not exist.
   6. Select Salesforce Object: Choose a Salesforce
      object from the dropdown menu.

      ![A screenshot displays the General Settings section, with Inactive Object and Select Salesforce Object highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-General-Settings-section-with-Inactive-Object-and-Select-Salesforce-Object-highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU0MzYsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.jPw0LGybABpG7V8oaBdEigiNplcCHkt1svjkgcqeaas&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the General Settings section, with Inactive Object and Select Salesforce Object highlighted.")
5. Select Where: Select the fields that are used to match a
   Salesforce object with Docusign information:
   1. Select Docusign Field: Select a field from your
      Docusign account to match with a Salesforce
      field.
   2. Select Salesforce Field: Select a Salesforce
      field to match with a field from your Docusign
      account.
   3. (Optional) + Add Item: Connect more Docusign and Salesforce fields.

      ![A screenshot displays the Docusign Recipient ID field and the Salesforce Owner ID field highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-Docusign-Recipient-ID-field-and-the-Salesforce-Owner-ID-field-highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU0MzYsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.jPw0LGybABpG7V8oaBdEigiNplcCHkt1svjkgcqeaas&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Docusign Recipient ID field and the Salesforce Owner ID field highlighted.")
6. Update Fields: Choose the Docusign
   fields that write back information to Salesforce fields:
   1. Select Docusign Field: Select a field from your
      Docusign account to update with a Salesforce
      field.
   2. Select Salesforce Field: Select a Salesforce field to be
      updated by your Docusign field.
   3. (Optional) + Add Item: Connect more Docusign and Salesforce fields.

title: Use Docusign Connect to Link Salesforce Fields to Docusign Fields
heading_path: Use Docusign Connect to Link Salesforce Fields to Docusign Fields
Before you begin
text:
a Salesforce
      field.
   2. Select Salesforce Field: Select a Salesforce field to be
      updated by your Docusign field.
   3. (Optional) + Add Item: Connect more Docusign and Salesforce fields.

      ![A screenshot displays the Update Fields section with the Docusign Recipient ID field and the Salesforce Owner ID field highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-Update-Fields-section-with-Docusign-Recipient-ID-field-and-the-Salesforce-Owner-ID-field-highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU0MzYsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.jPw0LGybABpG7V8oaBdEigiNplcCHkt1svjkgcqeaas&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Update Fields section with the Docusign Recipient ID field and the Salesforce Owner ID field highlighted.")
7. (Optional) Attachments: Choose how completed agreements
   save to Salesforce records:
   1. Attach Docusign Envelopes: Choose this option to
      save completed agreements to Salesforce records.
   2. Attach Docusign Certificate: Choose this option
      to save Docusign certificates of completion to
      Salesforce records.
8. Select Save.

You have completed the process. You have linked Salesforce fields
to Docusign fields. When recipients complete and sign agreements, Docusign updates Salesforce fields you linked with Docusign fields.

title: Check the Status of a Flow That Failed to Start a Docusign Workflow
heading_path: Check the Status of a Flow That Failed to Start a Docusign Workflow
text:
Learn to check the status of a flow that failed to start a workflow.

Salesforce administrators can build a Start Docusign Workflow Builder
workflow step to link Salesforce flows with workflows. When a flow starts, Salesforce starts the
linked workflow. Linking flows with workflows builds an automation chain that
creates agreements from Salesforce and Docusign data.

If you want to view the status of a workflow, you can [check the workflow process in your Docusign account](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=vcp1698707326210.html&_LANG=enus).

If a Flow fails to start your workflow, it does not display in your Docusign account. You can only view the logs in Salesforce.

Use this procedure to check the status of a flow that failed to start a workflow:

1. Select the Docusign Logs tab.

   The Docusign Logs page opens.
2. Select a log from the Docusign Log column.

   The log details page opens for that item.

You have completed the task. You viewed the details of a flow that
failed to start a workflow.

title: Prepare Your Salesforce Organization to Use Agreement Manager
heading_path: Prepare Your Salesforce Organization to Use Agreement Manager
text:
Learn to prepare your Salesforce organization to use Docusign Agreement Manager.

Important: This feature is only available to IAM for
Sales customers. Contact [Docusign support](https://support.docusign.com/s/?language=en_US&_gl=1*74elk4*_gcl_au*MTY5ODQyMjA2MC4xNzY5NjM1OTA1LjE5ODM3MTQzNjguMTc2OTYzNjIwNC4xNzY5NjM2MjA0) or [Docusign
sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales) for more information.

Important: Do not follow these steps if you already
have Docusign Apps Launcher v8.4 or higher installed. Only follow
these steps to add the Docusign Agreements component in an
organization with v8.3 or lower installed.

Before you begin
:   - [Purchase an Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) for Sales
      application](https://www.docusign.com/intelligent-agreement-management?utm_source=bing&utm_medium=cpc&utm_campaign=GBL_XX_PRF_UPS_2410_SEMIAM&utm_term=docusign%20iam&campaignid=569731866&adgroupid=1178678316174006&location=84406&device=c&matchtype=e&extensionid=&creative=73667602015411&keyword=docusign%20iam&placement=&network=o&msclkid=3287ee64f2c01f69bcd686665f40e7da).
    - [Install Apps Launcher version 8.4 or
      higher](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=ogj1586134254571.html&_LANG=enus).

      Important: If you are already
      using Agreement Manager for Salesforce, upgrading to Docusign Apps Launcher v8.4 breaks your
      connection. After upgrading v8.4 to get the latest features,
      you must use the following controls to re-establish your
      connection to Docusign.

[Agreement Manager](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=adf1702945446135.html&_LANG=enus) is a smart repository for your Docusign agreements. You can use Agreement Manager
to store, manage, and analyze all of your agreements.

You can use Agreement Manager in your Salesforce organization by adding the Docusign Agreements component to your layouts.

To use Agreement Manager in Salesforce, add the Docusign Completed Agreements component to your layouts. This component displays your Docusign agreements and the controls to filter, search, and take action on them.

Before you can use Agreement Manager in Salesforce, you must prepare your organization.

Use this procedure to prepare your Salesforce organization to use Agreement Manager:

1. [Open Docusign Apps Launcher](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=etv1586134206174.html&_LANG=enus).
2. Go to Docusign Setup >  User Management.
3. [Grant Agreement Manager access to your Docusign for Salesforce users](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=eut1712157553977.html&_LANG=enus "Read about user and group management tasks, such as managing agreement access and granting bulk upload permissions.").

You have completed the task. You have prepared your Salesforce
organization to use Agreement Manager. You can add the Docusign Completed
Agreements component to your Salesforce layouts.

title: "This Template Is Not Supported" Displays When I Configure a Send Documents for Signature Step
heading_path: "This Template Is Not Supported" Displays When I Configure a Send Documents for Signature Step
text:
Learn to fix the "This template is not supported" error in a Workflow Builder Send Documents for Signature
step.

I added a Salesforce field to my workflow's Send Documents for Signature
step. When I selected Next, an error displayed:
This template is not supported. I cannot save my Send
Documents for Signature step and finish building my template.

title: "This Template Is Not Supported" Displays When I Configure a Send Documents for Signature Step
heading_path: "This Template Is Not Supported" Displays When I Configure a Send Documents for Signature Step
Resolution: Delete Salesforce Fields That Are Not from Your Source Object
text:
Cause: Your workflow
connects to a Docusign template containing fields from
multiple Salesforce objects. Workflow Builder can only use
templates containing fields from a single Salesforce source object (Opportunity,
Account, and so on.)

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. Log on to your Docusign account as an administrator
   and go to Templates >  My Templates.
2. Open your template's Place Fields page:
   1. Select the more actions menu next to your template ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-use-button-with-a-three-dot-menu-next-to-it.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU2NTYsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.TZPemy7h3EkwzcAXuHWtHbEODJ9u645wXvZRn3LOj-w&_LANG=enus) and choose Edit from the
      dropdown menu.
   2. Select Next.

   The template's Place Fields page opens, displaying your
   template's merge fields.
3. Remove fields that are not from your Salesforce source object. For
   example, if you choose Opportunity as your source, remove fields from
   all other objects:
   1. Select a merge field.
   2. Select Salesforce in the right
      column.

      The field details display the Salesforce
      object.
   3. Select DELETE if the field is not from
      your Salesforce source object. ![A screenshot displays the Contact Full Name field with the Delete button highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-Contact-Full-Name-field-with-the-Delete-button-highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU2NTYsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.TZPemy7h3EkwzcAXuHWtHbEODJ9u645wXvZRn3LOj-w&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Contact Full Name field with the Delete button highlighted.")
4. Repeat this process for all non-source fields and select
   SAVE AND CLOSE.

   You have completed the process. You have removed all non-source Salesforce fields from
   your template. Return to your workflow and complete your Send
   Documents for Signature step. Docusign
   does not display the This template is not supported
   error.

title: Use Filters to Display Agreements in Agreement Manager for Salesforce
heading_path: Use Filters to Display Agreements in Agreement Manager for Salesforce
text:
Learn to filter the agreements that display in Agreement Manager for Salesforce.

Important: This feature is only available to IAM for
Sales customers. Contact [Docusign support](https://support.docusign.com/s/?language=en_US&_gl=1*74elk4*_gcl_au*MTY5ODQyMjA2MC4xNzY5NjM1OTA1LjE5ODM3MTQzNjguMTc2OTYzNjIwNC4xNzY5NjM2MjA0) or [Docusign
sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales) for more information.

The Docusign Completed Agreements component displays Agreement Manager on Salesforce records. You can use
Agreement Manager to search for your Docusign
agreements in Salesforce. You can also use [filters to narrow search results](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=fjb1712255517076.html&_LANG=enus), making it easier to locate the
agreements you want to see.

Use this procedure to filter the agreements that display in Agreement Manager for Salesforce:

1. Open a record and look for the Docusign Agreements
   component.

   Note: A Salesforce administrator may have placed the
   component on a tab labeled Docusign Agreements or
   something similar. The component features the word
   Completed at the top.
2. Try keywords, phrases, or a question: Enter search terms
   in the field.

   ![The Completed section of a Salesforce record displays a search field with Fontara and MSA entered.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/The-Completed-section-of-a-Salesforce-record-displays-a-search-field-with-Fontara-and-MSA-entered-2.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU3NDIsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.-nUU2EetfwMT0RtWrxvN9Id0USTAZbQuL-1nA5Y_KX4&_LANG=enus "The Completed section of a Salesforce record displays a search field with Fontara and MSA entered.")
3. Press [Enter] to see all results, or select a result
   from the dropdown menu that opens beneath.

   Agreement Manager displays completed agreements sent from this record and its child objects.
   Agreement Manager does not display results for parent objects, or agreements sent from
   Docusign CLM or your Docusign web
   account.
4. Use the filters to refine the list of search results. Expand a filter, select a
   value, and select Apply.

   For example, use the Agreement Type filter to locate services
   agreements:

   ![A screenshot of the Agreement Type filter that shows Services Agreement is selected.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Filter-search-results-by-Type-filter_MSA-example.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU3NDIsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.-nUU2EetfwMT0RtWrxvN9Id0USTAZbQuL-1nA5Y_KX4&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Agreement Type filter that shows Services Agreement is selected.")

   Services agreements display in the Docusign Agreements
   component.
5. Continue to apply filters to refine the agreement list table as needed. Select
   All Filters to access additional filters.

You have completed the process. You have used filters with
your search to display the agreements you want to see in Agreement Manager for Salesforce.

title: Uninstall the Salesforce Application
heading_path: Uninstall the Salesforce Application
text:
Learn to uninstall the Salesforce app.

The Salesforce app connects your Salesforce organization with your Docusign IAM account. You install and
authorize the Salesforce app from the Docusign App Center. You can
uninstall the Salesforce app if you no longer want to use it with your Docusign account.

Use this procedure to uninstall the Salesforce app:

1. Choose your login method:
   - Login from Salesforce:
     1. [Access the Docusign
        Setup page as a Salesforce administrator](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=etv1586134206174.html&_LANG=enus)
     2. Manage Connections and the IAM Launcher: Select
        Manage.
   - Login from Docusign:
     1. Open the [Production](https://apps.docusign.com/app-center) or [Demo](https://apps-d.docusign.com/app-center) App Center site.
     2. Select the Salesforce tile.
     3. Select Manage and choose Manage
        Connections.
2. Select Manage and choose Uninstall
   App.
3. Uninstall this app?: Enter UNINSTALL in
   the field and select Uninstall.![A screenshot displays the Uninstall this app dialog box with the word UNINSTALL in the field and the Uninstall button highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-Uninstall-this-app-dialog-box-with-the-word-UNINSTALL-in-the-field-and-the-Uninstall-button-highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU2NTEsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.4FKMIz-0ShDu_n5WGoXFCppdDfqDnT42gFrPxl2WbRw&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Uninstall this app dialog box with the word UNINSTALL in the field and the Uninstall button highlighted.")

   Docusign removes the Salesforce app. The page
   refreshes.

You have completed the process. You have uninstalled the Salesforce app. Your workflows cannot read from or write to your Salesforce
organization.

title: Create a Connect Configuration
heading_path: Create a Connect Configuration
text:
Connect notifies other applications when a Docusign event you want to know about
occurs. Learn how to create a Connect configuration.

title: Create a Connect Configuration
heading_path: Create a Connect Configuration
Before you begin
text:
- Set up a webhook listener. Review URL to Publish in [Connect Configuration Settings](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=zjq1665170940873.html&_LANG=enus "Docusign Connect sends updates to other applications. Look up the settings that you use to set up Docusign Connect.") for more information.
- Connect must be enabled for the account.
- If you want to follow only selected groups of users, create the groups first.
  See [Add Groups](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=mbg1583277362677.html&_LANG=enus) for details.

Connect sends updates (event messages
or webhooks) about your Docusign activity to other
applications. This topic shows you how to create a Connect configuration so you can kick
off downstream processes in your own systems.

1. From the Accounts view, in the sidebar under INTEGRATIONS,
   select Connect.

   The Connect page displays.
2. At the top left, select Add Configuration > Custom.

   The Add Custom Configuration window
   displays.
3. Enter the settings that you want to use. For more information, see [Connect Configuration Settings](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=zjq1665170940873.html&_LANG=enus "Docusign Connect sends updates to other applications. Look up the settings that you use to set up Docusign Connect.").
4. Select Add Configuration.

The configuration saves. Consider testing the configuration
as a next step. Send Docusign transactions and observe your application's
behavior.

title: Create a Connect Configuration to Add Salesforce Merge Fields to a Workflow Step
heading_path: Create a Connect Configuration to Add Salesforce Merge Fields to a Workflow Step
text: Learn how to create a Connect configuration to add Salesforce merge fields to your workflow's Send Documents for Signature step.

title: Create a Connect Configuration to Add Salesforce Merge Fields to a Workflow Step
heading_path: Create a Connect Configuration to Add Salesforce Merge Fields to a Workflow Step
Before you begin
text:
- Set up a webhook listener. Review URL to Publish in [Connect Configuration Settings](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=zjq1665170940873.html&_LANG=enus "Docusign Connect sends updates to other applications. Look up the settings that you use to set up Docusign Connect.") for more information.
- Enable Connect for your Docusign account.
- Enable Workflow Builder for your Docusign account.

Note: If you want to follow only selected groups of users, create the groups first. See [Add Groups](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=mbg1583277362677.html&_LANG=enus) for details.

Docusign Connect sends updates (event messages or
webhooks) about your Docusign activity to other
applications. Create a Connect configuration to add a Salesforce merge
field in your workflow's Send Documents for Signature step.

The Send Documents for Signature step uses your Connect configuration to access
Salesforce fields from a Docusign envelope template. If you do not
create a Connect configuration, you cannot add a Salesforce field to a Send
Documents for Signature step.

After creating a Connect configuration, you can add a Salesforce merge field to your
workflow's Send Documents for Signature step.

Use this procedure to create a Salesforce Connect configuration in your Docusign account:

1. Log in to your Docusign account as an administrator and go to Admin >  INTEGRATIONS >  Connect.
2. Select Add Configuration and choose Salesforce from the dropdown menu. ![A screenshot displays the Add Configuration dropdown menu with Salesforce highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-Add-Configuration-dropdown-menu-with-Salesforce-highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU0MzMsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.-Qxx9n7SiuId6jZiJsA17ko0Xq0n3QlhOvNVwcdL0e4&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Add Configuration dropdown menu with Salesforce highlighted.")

   The Connect with Salesforce dialog box opens.
3. Salesforce Environment: Choose an environment to connect to Docusign:

   - Production: Connect to a Salesforce production environment.
   - Test: Connect to a Salesforce sandbox environment.
   - URL: Enter your Salesforce environment's URL.
     - Sample URL for OAuth: https://[your\_salesforce\_instance\_url\_here]/services/oauth2/authorize
     - Sample URL for other:
       https://[your\_salesforce\_instance\_url\_here]/services/Soap/u/39.0
4. Salesforce SOAP Web Services API Version: Select the appropriate API version from the dropdown menu.
5. Salesforce Login Method: Choose a method to connect Docusign and Salesforce:

   Important: When creating your Connect configuration, enter the same Salesforce administrator credentials you used to [install the Salesforce app](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=jxo1706295810553.html&_LANG=enus).

title: Create a Connect Configuration to Add Salesforce Merge Fields to a Workflow Step
heading_path: Create a Connect Configuration to Add Salesforce Merge Fields to a Workflow Step
Before you begin
text:
the same Salesforce administrator credentials you used to [install the Salesforce app](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=jxo1706295810553.html&_LANG=enus).

   - OAuth (Open Authorization) - Recommended:
     1. Select Connect with OAuth. The Salesforce login page displays.
     2. Enter Salesforce administrator credentials and select Log In.
     3. Allow Access?: Select Allow.
   - Salesforce username and password - Less secure:
     1. Salesforce Username: Enter your Salesforce administrator username.
     2. Salesforce Password and Security Token: Enter your Salesforce administrator password and security token. Append your security token to your password. For example, if your password is MyPassword and your security token is XXXXXX, enter MyPasswordXXXXXX.
     3. Select Connect.![A screenshot displays the Connect with Salesforce dialog box with Production, API 54, and OAuth login method selected.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-Connect-with-Salesforce-dialog-box-with-Production-API-54-and-OAuth-login-method-selected.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU0MzMsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.-Qxx9n7SiuId6jZiJsA17ko0Xq0n3QlhOvNVwcdL0e4&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Connect with Salesforce dialog box with Production, API 54, and OAuth login method selected.")

   The Connect for Salesforce Configuration page displays.
6. Complete the setup of your connect configuration:
   1. System Settings: Choose Active from the Connection dropdown menu.
   2. Sender Selectable Items: Select the checkbox for each Salesforce object that you want senders to access.
   3. [Choose other Connect options like Associated Users, Envelope Events, Recipient Events, and so on](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=vob1727899215236&topicId=ctv1583277395112.html&_LANG=enus)

      ![A screenshot displays the Connection field set to Active and the Sender Selectable Items section highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-Connection-field-set-to-Active-and-the-Sender-Selectable-Items-section-highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU0MzMsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.-Qxx9n7SiuId6jZiJsA17ko0Xq0n3QlhOvNVwcdL0e4&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Connection field set to Active and the Sender Selectable Items section highlighted.")
7. Select Save.

You have completed the process. You have connected your Salesforce
organization to your Docusign account. You can add Salesforce merge
fields to your workflow's Send Documents for Signature step.

title: Link Multiple Writeback to Salesforce Steps
heading_path: Link Multiple Writeback to Salesforce Steps
text:
Learn to link two or more Writeback to Salesforce workflow steps.

Before you begin
:   - Required: Installation of the Salesforce app
      from the Docusign App Center. Review the [Installing Extension Applications for Workflow Builder](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=bgb1697668738892.html&_LANG=enus) topic for more information.
    - Required: [Workflows Create permissions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gbb1696973048215.html&_LANG=enus) or be an account administrator.
    - Required: Access to a suitable template to use for your workflow
      step. Review the [Template Requirements](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=xnw1709852160602.html&_LANG=enus) topic for more information.

You can use a Writeback to Salesforce step to update Salesforce records
with information from your Docusign workflow. The Writeback to
Salesforce step updates Salesforce fields, creates new records, and more.
Administrators link workflow fields to Salesforce fields in the Writeback to
Salesforce step. When task recipients enter data in workflow fields, Docusign updates the information in the linked Salesforce
fields.

You can configure more than one Writeback to Salesforce step in the same
workflow.

Example: You want data from your workflow to write back to
different fields from Account and Opportunity records. You build a writeback step
linked to the Account object and add one group of fields. You create a second step
linked to the Opportunity object and add fields there. A workflow participant enters
information into a Docusign web form. The participant enters an
Account Number and an Opportunity Amount. Docusign updates the
linked Account and Opportunity fields.

Use this procedure to add multiple Writeback to Salesforce steps to your workflow:

1. [Configure a Writeback to Salesforce
   step](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=pen1706132774147.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to add and configure a Writeback to Salesforce step to update Salesforce records.").
2. Add a Writeback to Salesforce step as a Docusign administrator:
   1. Go to Agreements > Workflows and select Edit next to a workflow
      name.

      The workflow editor page displays.
   2. Select the Add a step
      ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Workflow_Builder_add_step_plus_sign.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU1MjYsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.W_N_mVwsBXL9Q7g2rTvxVuuA2KBYLwZScuz7oNnCxyY&_LANG=enus) icon.

      The Add New Step dialog box displays.
   3. Search for steps: Enter `Writeback to
      Salesforce` in the search field and select the
      Writeback to Salesforce step that displays.

      The workflow editor page displays with the Writeback to
      Salesforce step added.
3. Name your Writeback to Salesforce step:
   1. Select Configure on the Writeback to Salesforce
      step.

title: Link Multiple Writeback to Salesforce Steps
heading_path: Link Multiple Writeback to Salesforce Steps
text:
The workflow editor page displays with the Writeback to
      Salesforce step added.
3. Name your Writeback to Salesforce step:
   1. Select Configure on the Writeback to Salesforce
      step.

      The configuration dialog box displays.
   2. Step Name: Keep or edit the name. It is helpful
      to add the name of the Salesforce object, like `Account -
      Writeback to Salesforce`.
4. Where will you write to?
   1. Select connection: Select the field and choose a
      Salesforce organization that you connected to Docusign. Multiple options display if you connected more than one Salesforce
      organization.
   2. (Optional) Add New Connection: Select this
      button if you want to [connect more than one Salesforce
      organization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=ogk1719256147008.html&_LANG=enus).
   3. (Optional) Manage Connections: Select this
      button if you want to [add, edit, refresh, or delete a
      Salesforce connection](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=uaw1719259450391.html&_LANG=enus).
   4. Salesforce object: Search for an object or
      choose one from the dropdown menu.
   5. Write settings: Select an option from the list
      that opens:

      - Update or Create: Update a record or
        create a new record if none exists.
      - Update: Update data on an existing
        record.
      - Create: Create a new record.
   6. Select Next.

      ![A screenshot displays a Writeback to Salesforce workflow step with the Connection, Salesforce object, and Write settings highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_Salesforce_Writeback_Where_will_you_write_to_Salesforce_object.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU1MjYsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.W_N_mVwsBXL9Q7g2rTvxVuuA2KBYLwZScuz7oNnCxyY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a Writeback to Salesforce workflow step with the Connection, Salesforce object, and Write settings highlighted.")
5. Which fields are you writing to?: Select workflow fields to
   map to Salesforce fields. When workflow recipients enter data, Docusign updates Salesforce records.
   1. Select Add or Remove Fields.

      The Add or Remove Fields page displays.
   2. Select the check box next to each Salesforce field that you'd like to
      use as a workflow variable. You'll be able to use these fields in later
      steps.

      ![A screenshot displays the Add or Remove Fields page with two Salesforce fields selected and highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Workflow_Builder_Writeback_to_Salesforce_Add_or_Remove_Fields.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU1MjYsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.W_N_mVwsBXL9Q7g2rTvxVuuA2KBYLwZScuz7oNnCxyY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Add or Remove Fields page with two Salesforce fields selected and highlighted.")
   3. Select Save and Exit.

      The Add or Remove Fields dialog box closes. The fields that you
      selected display.
   4. Fields: Add a variable or step for each
      field:

      - Manual entry: Add text for your new
        variable and select Save.

        Note: The name that you enter must exactly
        match an existing field. For example, the rule fails if you
        enter CompanyName for the
        Company\_Name
        variable.
      - Select a field from the start step or another step in this
        workflow:

title: Link Multiple Writeback to Salesforce Steps
heading_path: Link Multiple Writeback to Salesforce Steps
text:
field. For example, the rule fails if you
        enter CompanyName for the
        Company\_Name
        variable.
      - Select a field from the start step or another step in this
        workflow:

        Note: Steps like Read
        from HubSpot and Read from
        Salesforce display fields from up to five
        records. If more than five records are present, Salesforce
        logic decides which five to display. Selecting fields from
        multiple records helps you automate processes like mapping
        multiple Salesforce contacts to multiple Docusign participants.![A screenshot displays Read from Salesforce fields highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_Salesforce_Read_Workflow_field_selections.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU1MjYsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.W_N_mVwsBXL9Q7g2rTvxVuuA2KBYLwZScuz7oNnCxyY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays Read from Salesforce fields highlighted.")

        You can use the fields that you select here in
        later steps like Collect Data with
        Webforms or Send Documents for
        Signature.
   5. (Optional) Overwrite if field value exists:
      Select this check box if you want the data entered by a workflow
      participant to overwrite existing Salesforce data.
   6. Repeat this process for each variable displayed.
   7. Select Next.

      ![A screenshot displays the Account ID Salesforce field and the Overwrite if field value exists checkbox highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Workflow_Builder_Writeback_to_Salesforce_Which_fields_are_you_writing_to.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU1MjYsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.W_N_mVwsBXL9Q7g2rTvxVuuA2KBYLwZScuz7oNnCxyY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Account ID Salesforce field and the Overwrite if field value exists checkbox highlighted.")
6. Which record are you writing to?: Create a rule that
   matches a Salesforce field with an equivalent workflow field. This allows
   Workflow Builder to interact with Salesforce data.
   1. Salesforce field: Select a Salesforce field to
      match with a Docusign field.

      Note: When you select a
      (reference) type field, a new field opens
      below it. Choose a child object from the new field.
   2. Operator: Choose an option like Equal
      to, Less than, and so on.
   3. Workflow field: Select a Docusign field to match with your Salesforce field. The following
      options display:

      - Manual entry: Add text for your new
        variable and select Save.
      - Select a field from the start step or another workflow
        step.
   4. Select Done to save your rule.

      ![A screenshot displays a Salesforce field, an operator value of Greater than or equal to, and a workflow field all highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_Salesforce_Writeback_step_Which_record_are_you_writing_to_Rule.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU1MjYsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.W_N_mVwsBXL9Q7g2rTvxVuuA2KBYLwZScuz7oNnCxyY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a Salesforce field, an operator value of Greater than or equal to, and a workflow field all highlighted.")
7. (Optional) Add more rules:
   - +AND: Workflow Builder only imports Salesforce data that
     matches all of your rules.
   - +OR: Workflow Builder imports Salesforce data that matches
     any of your rules.
8. Select Apply.

title: Link Multiple Writeback to Salesforce Steps
heading_path: Link Multiple Writeback to Salesforce Steps
text:
:
   - +AND: Workflow Builder only imports Salesforce data that
     matches all of your rules.
   - +OR: Workflow Builder imports Salesforce data that matches
     any of your rules.
8. Select Apply.

   The Writeback to Salesforce dialog box closes. The page displays your new step.

You have completed the process. You created and linked two
Writeback to Salesforce steps for related objects. You can repeat this process to map
other objects like Contact and Case. When this step is triggered in a workflow, it
updates records for all objects you mapped. Workflow participants are not required to
enter data for these fields.

title: Agreement Set Management
heading_path: Agreement Set Management
text:
Read about agreement set management including how to use sets to control access to
agreements. This topic answers the question: What are agreement sets and how can I use
them?

title: Agreement Set Management
heading_path: Agreement Set Management
Definition and overview
text:
Agreement sets are named groups of agreements that can be shared with users and groups. These
sets are task- and action-oriented. Contract managers use agreement sets to organize
agreements for productivity and data privacy, including managing access at
scale.

Note: Contract managers, or any users that are assigned the
Organize and manage all agreements permission, have
access to agreement set management. See the [User and Group Management](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=eut1712157553977.html&_LANG=enus "Read about user and group management tasks, such as managing agreement access and granting bulk upload permissions.")
topic to learn more about Agreement Manager permissions and using agreement sets for access
control.

![A diagram that illustrates how agreement sets can be used to organize and manage access to agreements.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Agreement-Set-Management_What-are-sets-diagram.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4MDIsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.cfDkX0CWXwDZe_EccH9OodkVLGLWC7KH8h0wemHg45c&_LANG=enus "A diagram that illustrates how agreement sets can be used to organize and manage access to agreements.")

Consider this use case:

- Contract managers oversee thousands of sales agreements and need an efficient way to grant
  access to a subset of these agreements. For example:
  - The Renewals team needs access to all master service agreements (MSAs)
    expiring in a quarter without auto-renewal.
  - The U.S. Sales Team needs access to all MSAs under U.S.
    jurisdiction.
- With agreement sets, contract managers have a way to grant access to both teams to the
  appropriate agreements.

Important: There is a 100 set limit per account. You
can create up to 100 agreement sets per account. If you have multiple accounts, each
account can have up to 100 sets.

title: Agreement Set Management
heading_path: Agreement Set Management
Smart filters
text:
You can set up smart filters that add agreements to your sets automatically. This feature
streamlines the agreement management process by updating sets based on agreement
attribute values.

You can apply Smart filters to sets during set creation:

![A snippet of the New agreement set view that shows the Smart filters step used to add and remove agreements based on attribute values.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Agreement-set-management_New-agreement-set_step-2-Smart-filters-snippet.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4MDIsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.cfDkX0CWXwDZe_EccH9OodkVLGLWC7KH8h0wemHg45c&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the New agreement set view that shows the Smart filters step used to add and remove agreements based on attribute values.")

After applying the smart filters and saving the set, a recurring rule dynamically adds and
removes agreements based on the fields specified in your filters. Agreements are
selected if they match conditions across filters. Consider these examples:

- You applied the Parties and Agreement
  Type filters to the set. The agreements must match for the
  selected party *AND* the selected agreement type to be added to the
  set.
- You applied the Agreement Type filter to the set and
  selected multiple values. The agreements that match *ANY* of the selected
  values are added to the set.

  Tip: Some filters, such as
  Parties and Agreement
  Type, support multiple values. When multiple values are
  selected, the agreements must match one of the values to be added to the
  set.

Important: The smart filters feature does *not* support
these types of filters:

- **Rolling date filters:** the filter value is based on a relative time
  period that updates as time passes, such as "last 7 days".
- **Duration-based filters:** the filter value is based on a length of
  time, such as: "within 30 days".

You can update the smart filters for an agreement set at any time. Expand the
 menu for the set that you want to
update and select Edit smart filters:

![A snippet of the All agreement sets view that shows the Edit smart filters option in the menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Agreement-set-management_Actions_Edit-smart-filters-highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4MDIsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.cfDkX0CWXwDZe_EccH9OodkVLGLWC7KH8h0wemHg45c&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the All agreement sets view that shows the Edit smart filters option in the menu.")

Tip: Smart filters can also be created from the filters
that you apply to the agreement list table on the  page. See the [Create Agreement Sets from the Agreement
List Table](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=dmx1750998922002.html&_LANG=enus) topic to learn more about this feature.
