title: Agreement Set Management
heading_path: Agreement Set Management
Agreement set usage by role
text:
Agreement set management is primarily used by contract managers, not administrators. Consider
this example:

![A diagram that illustrates how administrators, contract managers, and users or groups use agreement set management.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Agreement-Set-Management_Contract-mgradmin-diagram.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4MDIsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.cfDkX0CWXwDZe_EccH9OodkVLGLWC7KH8h0wemHg45c&_LANG=enus "A diagram that illustrates how administrators, contract managers, and users or groups use agreement set management.")

- *Administrators* manage users and groups in their organization, including granting
  access to software and features in the Admin portal.
- *Contract managers* create and manage sets to make sure the right people have access to
  relevant agreements. They also add agreements into sets and share sets with the
  appropriate users and groups.
- *Users and groups* view agreements in a set to focus on agreements that need their
  immediate attention.

Note: The separation between 'user and group management' and 'agreement set
management' is intentional to address access control concerns. For example, IT
administrators should not be exposed to sensitive agreement data. Contract managers
use agreement sets to ensure that only the appropriate users have access to
sensitive agreement data.

title: Agreement Set Management
heading_path: Agreement Set Management
Agreement set access points and visibility
text:
Access to the agreement set functionality is dependent on your Agreement Manager permissions. Contract
managers, or any users that are assigned the Organize and manage all
agreements permission, have access to agreement set management. See
the [User and Group Management](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=eut1712157553977.html&_LANG=enus) topic to learn more about Agreement Manager permissions and using agreement sets for access control.

Review these examples of where this functionality is accessed and used in Agreement Manager:

- Manage Sets action (located in the  menu): used by contract managers to create
  and manage agreement sets:![A snippet of the page that shows the Manage Sets option selected in the menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Agreement-sets_entry-point_Manage-sets-action-menu.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4MDIsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.cfDkX0CWXwDZe_EccH9OodkVLGLWC7KH8h0wemHg45c&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the page that shows the Manage Sets option selected in the menu.")
- Sets filter (located in the agreement list table): used to filter the
  agreement list table to show agreements for a selected set:![A snippet of the page that displays the expanded Sets filter.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Agreement-sets_entry-point_Sets-filter.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4MDIsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.cfDkX0CWXwDZe_EccH9OodkVLGLWC7KH8h0wemHg45c&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the page that displays the expanded Sets filter.")

  The dropdown contains sets that were either shared with a user or
  group, or created by the logged-in user.
- Sets toggle (located in the Customize
  columns dialog box): turn on (blue position) this toggle to
  display the Sets column in the agreement list table:![A screenshot of the Customize columns dialog box that shows the Sets toggle.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Agreement-set-management_Customize-columns_Sets-toggle.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4MDIsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.cfDkX0CWXwDZe_EccH9OodkVLGLWC7KH8h0wemHg45c&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Customize columns dialog box that shows the Sets toggle.")

  This column displays the set information for the agreements in the
  table. See the [Display
  Set Information in the Agreement List Table](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=bti1750999198357.html&_LANG=enus) topic to learn more about this feature.
- Agreement sets pane (located on the Agreement
  preview page): displays the agreement sets to which the agreement
  was added: ![A snippet of the Agreement preview page that shows the Agreement sets pane.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Agreement-sets_entry-point_Agreement-sets-pane.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4MDIsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.cfDkX0CWXwDZe_EccH9OodkVLGLWC7KH8h0wemHg45c&_LANG=enus "A snippet of the Agreement preview page that shows the Agreement sets pane.")

  Contract managers can add agreements to existing sets or create new
  agreement sets from this pane.

title: Connect Configuration Settings
heading_path: Connect Configuration Settings
text:
Docusign Connect sends updates to other applications. Look up the
settings that you use to set up Docusign Connect.

Docusign Connect notifies other applications when a Docusign event you want to know about occurs. You can use Connect to kick off downstream
workflows. This topic describes the settings that are available for custom Connect
configurations. To learn more, see [Create a Connect Configuration](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=xwi1583277389681.html&_LANG=enus "Connect notifies other applications when a Docusign event you want to know about occurs. Learn how to create a Connect configuration.").

title: Connect Configuration Settings
heading_path: Connect Configuration Settings
text:
950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=xwi1583277389681.html&_LANG=enus "Connect notifies other applications when a Docusign event you want to know about occurs. Learn how to create a Connect configuration.").

| Setting | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Name | A name for the Connect configuration. |
| URL to Publish | The web address of your Connect listener. The URL must use the HTTPS protocol and SSL/TLS connections. It may include query parameters. Connect uses port 443 for HTTPS by default. You can specify port 1443, 2443, 3443, 4443, 5443, 6443, 7443, 8443, or 9443 in the URL instead. Example: https://listener.example.com:1443/listener.aspx The SSL/TLS certificate for your web server must chain to a certificate authority on Microsoft's trust list. Self-signed certificates do not work. You may use free certificates from Let’s Encrypt. |
| Enable Log (Account-Level Connect only) | This option enables logging, which can help you troubleshoot any problems. This setting is on by default. It is automatically enabled for Organization-Level Connect configurations. |
| Require Acknowledgment (Account-Level Connect only) | This option is selected by default. Requires Connect to wait for an acknowledgement from your listener after sending a message. Connect records a successful message transfer when your listener returns an HTTP 200 status code. This functionality is automatically enabled for Organization-Level Connect configurations. |
| Data Format | The Data Format you want to receive. This setting is available only for custom Connect. Important: You cannot change this setting after you save the configuration. The options are:  - REST v2.1: JSON (default) - Legacy: XML |
| Event Message Delivery Mode | If the configuration is for a third-party integration or partner, verify the format to use. Important: You can't change this setting after you save the configuration.  The options are:   - Aggregate: Groups event messages for periodic sending. The   information about the event is from when the system sent the   message. - Send Individual Messages (SIM) (default): Sends individual   messages for each event. The information about the event is   from when the event occurred. This option is only available   for JSON configurations. |
| Trigger Events | The events you want to follow. |
| Include Data | The data you want Connect messages to include. The available options depend on the Data Format you chose. |
| Associated Users/Groups | The users or groups of users you want to follow or exclude.  - All Users (includes new users):   Connect sends updates for all account users.  - Select Users/Groups to include: Connect sends updates for the   account users or groups that you select. - Select Users/Groups to exclude:   Connect does not send updates for the account users or   groups you select. |
| Include HMAC Signature | Generates a Hash-based Message Authentication Code (HMAC) and includes it in the message header. |
| Include OAuth | Enables OAuth for Connect messages to your web server. Enable OAuth for the account first. |
| Include Basic Authentication Header | Enables Basic Authentication for Connect messages to your web server. |
| Enable Mutual TLS | Adds Mutual Transport Layer Security (TLS) authentication and authorization to the configuration. Mutual TLS only initiates when your listener sends a valid Certificate Request message during the TLS handshake protocol. Docusign cannot initiate Mutual TLS because it is the client. When you use it with OAuth, the access token request to your authorization server includes the X.509 certificate. |

title: Search for Agreements with Agreement Manager for Salesforce
heading_path: Search for Agreements with Agreement Manager for Salesforce
text:
Learn to search for agreements in Agreement Manager for Salesforce.

Important: This feature is only available to IAM for
Sales customers. Contact [Docusign support](https://support.docusign.com/s/?language=en_US&_gl=1*74elk4*_gcl_au*MTY5ODQyMjA2MC4xNzY5NjM1OTA1LjE5ODM3MTQzNjguMTc2OTYzNjIwNC4xNzY5NjM2MjA0) or [Docusign
sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales) for more information.

The Docusign Completed Agreements component displaysAgreement Manager on Salesforce records. You can use Agreement Manager to [Search for your agreements in
Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=qzl1712244340883.html&_LANG=enus).

Use this procedure to search for agreements in Agreement Manager for Salesforce:

1. Open a record and look for the Docusign Agreements
   component.

   Note: A Salesforce administrator may have placed the
   component on a tab labeled Docusign Agreements or
   something similar. The component features the word
   Completed at the top.
2. Try keywords, phrases, or a question: Enter search terms
   in the field.

![The Completed section of a Salesforce record displays a search field with Fontara and MSA entered.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/The-Completed-section-of-a-Salesforce-record-displays-a-search-field-with-Fontara-and-MSA-entered-2.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU3MzgsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.0EiOBATvTGXDBrTtQwFtUVgy2obFIpD-Don5vBnKd2A&_LANG=enus "The Completed section of a Salesforce record displays a search field with Fontara and MSA entered.")

3. Press [Enter] to see all results, or select a result
   from the dropdown menu that displays beneath.

   Agreement Manager displays completed agreements sent from this record and its
   child objects. Agreement Manager does not display results for parent objects, or
   agreements sent from Docusign CLM or your Docusign web account.

You have completed the process. You have searched for
completed agreements from this record and those of its child objects.

title: Open an Agreement with Agreement Manager for Salesforce
heading_path: Open an Agreement with Agreement Manager for Salesforce
text:
Learn to open agreements from Agreement Manager for Salesforce.

Important: This feature is only available to IAM for Sales customers. Contact [Docusign support](https://support.docusign.com/s/?language=en_US&_gl=1*74elk4*_gcl_au*MTY5ODQyMjA2MC4xNzY5NjM1OTA1LjE5ODM3MTQzNjguMTc2OTYzNjIwNC4xNzY5NjM2MjA0) or [Docusign sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales) for more information.

The Docusign Agreements component displays Agreement Manager on Salesforce records. You can use Agreement Manager to open an agreement from the Docusign Agreements component.

Use this procedure to open an agreement from the Docusign Agreements component:

1. Open a record and look for the Docusign Agreements component.

   Note: A Salesforce administrator may have placed the component on a tab labeled Docusign Agreements or something similar. The component features the word Completed at the top.
2. Select an agreement in the File Name column:

   ![A screenshot displays the Docusign Agreements component with an agreement highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835/page/images/Agreement_Manager_select_agreement.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU3ODMsInNoZWFmIjoibWFwOGMzMGFhOTQtN2U5Zi00OTUwLTkyZmMtZGQyMmM2ZDlmODM1In0.Qx9sog2ek59xs_7ctDOT4CRtMF34J9zinBeHHifr1Dg&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Docusign Agreements component with an agreement highlighted.")

   The agreement opens in another browser tab. If you are not logged in to your Docusign account, the Docusign Log In page
   opens in another browser tab. Enter your Docusign credentials
   and the agreement opens.

You have completed the process. You have opened an agreement from the Docusign Agreements component.

title: Agreement Manager for Salesforce Troubleshooting Guide
heading_path: Agreement Manager for Salesforce Troubleshooting Guide
text:
Learn to troubleshoot issues with Agreement Manager for
Salesforce.

Important: This feature is only available to IAM for
Sales customers. Contact [Docusign support](https://support.docusign.com/s/?language=en_US&_gl=1*74elk4*_gcl_au*MTY5ODQyMjA2MC4xNzY5NjM1OTA1LjE5ODM3MTQzNjguMTc2OTYzNjIwNC4xNzY5NjM2MjA0) or [Docusign sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales)
for more information.

This guide helps administrators troubleshoot issues with
Agreement Manager for Salesforce. You access Agreement Manager by adding the Docusign Completed
Agreements component to Salesforce Layouts.

Agreement Manager for Salesforce is part of [Intelligent Agreement Management
(IAM)](https://www.docusign.com/intelligent-agreement-management?utm_source=bing&utm_medium=cpc&utm_campaign=GBL_XX_PRF_UPS_2410_SEMIAM&utm_term=docusign%20iam&campaignid=569731866&adgroupid=1178678316174006&location=84406&device=c&matchtype=e&extensionid=&creative=73667602015411&keyword=docusign%20iam&placement=&network=o&msclkid=3287ee64f2c01f69bcd686665f40e7da).

title: Add the Envelope Status Component to a Salesforce Layout
heading_path: Add the Envelope Status Component to a Salesforce Layout
text:
Learn to add the Docusign Envelope Status component to a Salesforce layout.

Docusign  Envelope Status is a component that you can add to your Salesforce layouts. The component displays the progress of all Docusign envelopes sent from a record. The component displays envelopes sent using the following methods:

- A Send with Docusign action
- An Envelope Template action
- [A Start with Docusign workflow button](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=tskcae26f13-f2e5-4afd-85a7-145a70b12977.html&_LANG=enus)
- [An Agreement Desk for Salesforce request](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=tsk151c1542-bdf4-4c8e-ba20-37affb7adc7e.html&_LANG=enus)
- [eSignature actions in Salesforce Flows](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=dzi1705696883404.html&_LANG=enus)

Salesforce users with Docusign access can view and act on envelopes without logging on to Docusign.

Tip: This procedure is explained in this [short how-to video](https://docusign.gallery.video/watch?videoId=6250567558001).

Use this procedure to add the Docusign Envelope Status component to a Salesforce layout:

1. As a Salesforce administrator, open a record, select the gear icon ![A screenshot displays the a gear icon with a lightning bolt inside.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4MjksInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.6XLehdIQeCrEh7kzGFJMV6L-fjjbmgwAxt5fTgXcM54&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the a gear icon with a lightning bolt inside.") and choose Edit Page from the dropdown menu.
2. Drag the Docusign Envelope Status component to the page layout on the right.

   ![A screen capture displays a cursor dragging the Docusign Envelope Status component to an Opportunity layout and saving the change.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/Add-the-DocuSign-Envelope-Status-component.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4MjksInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.6XLehdIQeCrEh7kzGFJMV6L-fjjbmgwAxt5fTgXcM54&_LANG=enus "A screen capture displays a cursor dragging the Docusign Envelope Status component to an Opportunity layout and saving the change.")
3. Activate the change to your layout:
   1. Select Activation.
   2. Select a tab to set your default options: Org Default, App Default, or App, Record Type, and Profile.
   3. Select Assign and choose options from the dialog box.
   4. Select Close.
4. Select Save.
5. Select the back arrow ![A screenshot displays the Salesforce Edit Page Back arrow icon.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/A-screenshot-of-the-Back-arrow-on-the-Salesforce-Edit-Page-settings-bar.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4MjksInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.6XLehdIQeCrEh7kzGFJMV6L-fjjbmgwAxt5fTgXcM54&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Salesforce Edit Page Back arrow icon.") to return to the record.

You have completed the process. You have added the Docusign Envelope Status component to the layout. Users with Docusign access can view and act on Docusign envelopes.

title: Configure a Start Docusign Workflow Step in Salesforce Flow
heading_path: Configure a Start Docusign Workflow Step in Salesforce Flow
text:
Learn to configure a Salesforce Flow to start a Docusign workflow.

Before you begin
:   - Purchase a Docusign Intelligent Agreement
      Management (IAM) plan.
    - Install Docusign Apps Launcher version 7.4 or
      higher.
    - [Prepare Your Salesforce Users to Start Docusign Workflows](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=bmk1744912007661.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to prepare your Salesforce users so that they can start Docusign workflows from Salesforce.").
    - [Build a workflow to be
      started by your Salesforce flow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=ovh1741282680990.html&_LANG=enus).

Important: Assign [Workflow Builder permissions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gbb1696973048215.html&_LANG=enus) to all Docusign
users (not administrators or senders) who must start workflows.

You can link Salesforce flows to Docusign
workflows by adding a Start Docusign
workflow step to your flow.

When a flow starts, Salesforce starts your linked workflow. Linking flows with
workflows builds an automation chain that creates agreements from Salesforce and Docusign data.

Use this procedure to add a **Start Docusign workflow** step to your Salesforce
flow:

1. Open a Salesforce flow and add a **Start Docusign workflow** step:
   1. Locate the point in your flow where you want to add your action.
   2. Hover over the circle icon and select the plus +
      icon that displays.![A screen capture displays the Add Element plus icon in a Salesforce Flow.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/A-screen-capture-displays-the-Add-Element-plus-icon-in-a-Salesforce-Flow.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4OTEsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.hAghmhhHEpfG2wBoCO55zicJkmydHZBNakKaf05mNUg&_LANG=enus "A screen capture displays the Add Element plus icon in a Salesforce Flow.")
   3. Choose Action from the Add
      Element dialog box.
   4. Search: Enter and select `Start Docusign Workflow`.
2. Configure a Start Docusign workflow action:
   1. Label: Enter `Start Docusign
      workflow`.
   2. API Name: Keep or change the default name.
   3. Instance Name: Enter a string value for the name
      of the workflow instance.
3. Workflow ID: Add the workflow ID.
   1. As a Docusign administrator, go to Agreements > Workflows and select your workflow name.
   2. Copy the alpha-numeric workflow ID at the end of the URL. Do not copy
      the slash mark. ![A screenshot displays the end of a Docusign workflow URL highlighted in a green box.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-end-of-a-Workflow-Builder-workflow-URL-highlighted-in-a-green-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4OTEsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.hAghmhhHEpfG2wBoCO55zicJkmydHZBNakKaf05mNUg&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the end of a Docusign workflow URL highlighted in a green box.")
   3. Return to your flow action and paste the ID (without slash mark) in the
      Workflow ID field.
4. Add workflow variables to your action:
   1. Select the switch next to a workflow variable that you want to include
      in your action.

      The switch displays as blue and says
      included.
   2. Enter and select a variable name in the field.
   3. Repeat this process for up to ten variables. Each variable name must
      exactly match the variable name in your workflow.
5. Select Save >  Activate.

   A message displays: Your flow was
   activated.

You have completed the process. You added a Start Docusign workflow
action to your Salesforce Flow. When your flow starts, it starts your linked
workflow.

Note: Users can only see whether the workflow
instance has failed in the Docusign logs.

title: Configure a Start Docusign Workflow Step in Salesforce Flow
heading_path: Configure a Start Docusign Workflow Step in Salesforce Flow
text:
Start Docusign workflow
action to your Salesforce Flow. When your flow starts, it starts your linked
workflow.

Note: Users can only see whether the workflow
instance has failed in the Docusign logs.

Users will
not be able to [monitor the workflow instance process](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=vcp1698707326210.html&_LANG=enus). To do so, the Docusign administrator can go to Docusign’s platform to see the progress of the workflow
instance.

title: Prepare Your Salesforce Users to Start Docusign Workflows
heading_path: Prepare Your Salesforce Users to Start Docusign Workflows
text:
Learn to prepare your Salesforce users so that they can start Docusign workflows from Salesforce.

Important: Do not follow these steps if you already have
Docusign Apps Launcher v7.4 or higher installed. Only follow
these steps to add a Start
Docusign Workflow  action to an organization with v7.3 or
lower installed.

Before you begin
:   - Purchase a Docusign Intelligent Agreement
      Management (IAM) plan.
    - Install Docusign Apps Launcher version 7.4 or
      higher.
    - [Build a workflow to be
      started by your Salesforce flow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=ovh1741282680990.html&_LANG=enus).

Before standard Salesforce users can start Docusign workflows, you
must assign the necessary permissions. Use this procedure to assign the necessary
Docusign and Salesforce user permissions:

1. Log on to your Docusign account as an administrator and go to Admin >  Permission Profiles.
2. Copy the DS Sender profile:
   1. DS Sender: Select Actions and
      choose Copy from the dropdown menu.

      The Copy Permission Profile dialog box opens.
   2. Name: Enter Workflow Builder DS
      Sender.
   3. Select the User Permissions tab.
   4. Scroll to Workflows and choose
      Use from the dropdown menu.
   5. Select Add.

   The Copy Permission Profile dialog box closes.
3. Add users to the new Workflow Builder DS Sender profile. Only add
   users that must start Workflow Builder workflows from Salesforce:
   1. Go to Admin >  Users.

      The Users page opens.
   2. Select Actions next to the user that you want to
      add and choose Edit from the dropdown menu.
   3. Permission Profile: Choose Workflow Builder DS
      Sender from the dropdown menu.
   4. Select Save.

   The user's page closes.
4. Assign Flow User permissions to Salesforce users who must
   start Docusign workflows:
   1. Log on to your Salesforce organization as an administrator, select the
      gear icon ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4ODQsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.G8TMgHTkdEvg1Ftv3TVX3NntW6ImSPUgclUmQTY6PEM&_LANG=enus), and
      choose Setup from the dropdown menu.
   2. Enter and select Users in the Quick Find
      field.
   3. Select Edit next to a user.

      The User Edit page opens.
   4. Select the Flow User check box and select
      Save.

      ![A screenshot displays the Flow User check box selected and the Save button highlighted on the User Edit page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-Flow-User-check-box-selected-and-the-Save-button-highlighted-on-the-User-Edit-page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4ODQsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.G8TMgHTkdEvg1Ftv3TVX3NntW6ImSPUgclUmQTY6PEM&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Flow User check box selected and the Save button highlighted on the User Edit page.")

   The User Edit page closes. Users must [re-activate their Docusign access in Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=vbw1648698462634&topicId=ymi1586134213877.html&_LANG=enus). After re-activating, users
   can start Docusign workflows.

You have completed the process. You have assigned the necessary Docusign and Salesforce permissions. After re-authorizing their Docusign users, these users can start Docusign
workflows from Salesforce.

title: Use Workflow Builder with Salesforce
heading_path: Use Workflow Builder with Salesforce
text:
Learn to build Docusign workflows that update records in
your Salesforce organization.

title: Use Workflow Builder with Salesforce
heading_path: Use Workflow Builder with Salesforce
Introduction
text:
With [Workflow Builder](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=dnx1696972415150.html&_LANG=enus), you can connect Docusign workflow steps like Web Forms and eSignature templates to
third-party extensions. You can create workflows to automate your agreement process,
including reading data from and writing data to Salesforce records.

A typical agreement process consists of numerous steps to complete before and after
signing. Some of these steps can be addressed today by integrating multiple Docusign products, usually with expert assistance and developer
resources.

Workflow Builder offers a platform that integrates Docusign products with third-party apps like Salesforce. By
building workflows, you can digitize your
business processes without writing code.

You can configure the following workflow steps to integrate Salesforce and Docusign in your agreement process:

- Writeback to Salesforce: Update Salesforce records with
  data from your workflow.
- Read from Salesforce: Import data from Salesforce
  records to your workflow.
- Store Files in Salesforce: Use variables to name
  completed agreements and route them to the appropriate records.
- Use Salesforce Files: Add files from Salesforce records
  to workflows.

title: Use Workflow Builder with Salesforce
heading_path: Use Workflow Builder with Salesforce
Developer resources
text:
The following resources are available for developers who are part of the API beta
program to build workflows and extension apps. If you'd like to join the beta
program, visit the [Docusign Developer beta trial page](https://www.docusign.com/lp/developer-betas) to apply.

[Workflow Builder API](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/maestro-api/)
:   Create and manage workflows using the Workflow Builder
    API.

[Extension Apps](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/)
:   Build custom third-party functionality into Docusign and
    publish your solutions in the App Center.

title: Configure Workflow Builder for Salesforce Fields and Settings
heading_path: Configure Workflow Builder for Salesforce Fields and Settings
text:
Learn about configuring fields and settings when you build Salesforce workflow
steps.

You can configure many different types of fields and settings when you build Salesforce steps in
a workflow.

The following topics help you configure fields and settings in Salesforce
steps:

- Supported Salesforce field types
- Format string values in Salesforce steps
- Use the Record Count variable to see how many records a workflow finds
- Add Salesforce merge fields to a Send Documents for Signature step
- Learn about the Overwrite If Field Value Exists feature

title: Workflow Builder for Salesforce Advanced Features
heading_path: Workflow Builder for Salesforce Advanced Features
text:
Learn about how to use advanced features in Salesforce workflow steps.

With [Workflow Builder](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=dnx1696972415150.html&_LANG=enus), you can connect Docusign workflow steps like Web Forms and eSignature templates to
third-party extensions. You can create workflows to automate your agreement process,
including reading data from and writing data to Salesforce records.

After installing and configuring the IAM for Salesforce app, you can use advanced features to
customize how the Salesforce app functions.

You can use the following advanced features when using the app:

- Update the ​Salesforce application
- Edit the name of a Salesforce organization connected to the Salesforce
  app
- Reconnect a Salesforce organization to the Salesforce app
- Refresh the connection to your Salesforce app
- Duplicate a workflow
- Export and import a workflow
- Delete the connection to a Salesforce organization from the Salesforce
  app

title: Configure Salesforce Workflow Steps
heading_path: Configure Salesforce Workflow Steps
text:
Learn about the Workflow Builder steps that you can configure to update
records in your Salesforce organization.

You can configure and use the following Salesforce steps in a workflow:

title: Configure Salesforce Workflow Steps
heading_path: Configure Salesforce Workflow Steps
Writeback to Salesforce
text:
Writeback to Salesforce updates Salesforce records with data from your Docusign workflow. The Writeback to Salesforce step updates
Salesforce fields, creates new records, and more. Administrators link workflow
fields to Salesforce fields in the Writeback to Salesforce step. When recipients
enter data in workflow fields, Docusign updates the information in
the linked Salesforce record fields. Writeback improves efficiency and reduces
errors by eliminating manual data entry.

Writeback to Salesforce examples

In a typical
Writeback to Salesforce scenario, you create a Web Forms step to gather
participant information. Add a Docusign template with custom
fields linked to Salesforce, followed by a writeback
step.

Example 1: Update existing record: You
build a workflow that includes a Collect Data with Web Forms step and a
Writeback to Salesforce step. In the writeback step, you link the Salesforce
opportunity's Amount field to the web form's
Amount field. A workflow recipient updates and signs an
agreement for an existing opportunity named Fontara. The recipient updates the
Fontara agreement's Amount field from $20,000 to $25,000. When the recipient
completes the assignment, Docusign updates the Fontara
record's Amount field to $25,000.

Example 2: Create new
record: A workflow recipient enters data for a prospective
customer named Fontara. When the recipient completes the assignment, the
writeback step creates a new Fontara record in the connected Salesforce
organization. The record contains all the data entered by the recipient, like
account name, billing address, amount, and so on.

title: Configure Salesforce Workflow Steps
heading_path: Configure Salesforce Workflow Steps
Read from Salesforce
text:
Read from Salesforce imports data from Salesforce records to your Docusign workflow. Administrators link workflow fields to
Salesforce fields in the Read from Salesforce step. When task recipients open an
envelope, they see Salesforce data imported from the record. Importing Salesforce
data improves efficiency and reduces errors by eliminating manual data entry.

Read from Salesforce example

You build a workflow
with these steps: Collect Data with Web Forms,
Read from Salesforce, and Writeback to
Salesforce. In the writeback step, you link the Salesforce
opportunity Amount field to the web form's
Amount field. A workflow recipient opens an agreement
and sees the Amount field pre-filled with the opportunity's amount. If the
recipient changes the amount, Docusign updates the Amount
field on the Salesforce record.

title: Configure Salesforce Workflow Steps
heading_path: Configure Salesforce Workflow Steps
Store Files in Salesforce
text:
The Store Files in Salesforce step uses variables to name completed agreements and
route them to the appropriate records.

Store Files in Salesforce example

You build a
workflow and want Docusign to store completed agreements in
Salesforce records. You configure Store Files in Salesforce
variables that set file naming conventions and route agreements to the correct
records. When all recipients complete the signing process, Docusign saves the finished agreement to the correct
Salesforce record. The participant is not required to name the agreement or
upload it to a record.

title: Configure Salesforce Workflow Steps
heading_path: Configure Salesforce Workflow Steps
Use Salesforce Files
text:
The Use Salesforce Files step adds files from Salesforce records
to workflows. Administrators open a record's Notes & Attachments and add the
record's ID to the Use Salesforce Files step. When workflows
start, Docusign adds selected files from that record's Notes &
Attachments. You can configure the following upload settings: All
Files, File Name Contains, and
Latest Document Only.

Note: The maximum import limit for this step is 10 files or
100 MB.

Use Salesforce Files
example

Example: You add a Use
Salesforce Files step to your workflow. You use the
File Name Contains option and enter the keywords
`Contract Renewal`. Your workflow sends an agreement to
recipients. When recipients open their workflow task, they see a file named
Contract Renewal 2025 included in the agreement.

title: Learn About Starting Docusign Workflows from Salesforce
heading_path: Learn About Starting Docusign Workflows from Salesforce
text:
Learn about using the Docusign IAM Launcher component or a custom
button to start Docusign
workflows from Salesforce records.

Important: This feature is only available to IAM for Sales
customers. Contact [Docusign support](https://support.docusign.com/s/?language=en_US&_gl=1*74elk4*_gcl_au*MTY5ODQyMjA2MC4xNzY5NjM1OTA1LjE5ODM3MTQzNjguMTc2OTYzNjIwNC4xNzY5NjM2MjA0) or [Docusign sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales) for more
information.

With [Workflow Builder](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=bqi1705961132077.html&_LANG=enus), you can
create workflows to automate your agreement
process.

You can start workflows from Salesforce records by adding a Start with Docusign action to your layouts. There are two ways to add Start with Docusign actions to layouts:

- (Recommended) [Use IAM Launcher Setup Controls](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=tska9d68585-f524-49a4-8898-496b6575d036.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to use the IAM Launcher Setup interface to add Docusign workflow actions to Salesforce layouts.")
- [Manually
  add actions with Salesforce Object Manager](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=rrf1583359212854&topicId=fji1583359148678.html&_LANG=enus)

Users can start workflows by selecting the Start with Docusign action on records:![A screenshot displays a Salesforce record with a Start with Docusign custom button highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/Start_Workflow_Builder_Workflow_Custom_Button.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4OTgsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.n8l9eMK2S50ohf7WIM6UAqB-NCkvxZjYa5QNboNo80o&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a Salesforce record with a Start with Docusign custom button highlighted.")

You can take the following actions from a Start with Docusign action menu:

[Start](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=tskcae26f13-f2e5-4afd-85a7-145a70b12977.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to start a Docusign workflow from a Start with Docusign action.")
:   Select the Start button to start any
    displayed workflow from a record. Data from the
    record is included in the generated agreement.
    The workflow can
    read data from the record, write changes back, add Salesforce files, and
    store new agreements.

[Add a Workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=tsk9d05e5e9-8ae1-4b02-96eb-1eb83729d6b5.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to add a workflow to the Start with Docusign action menu.")
:   Select this button to add a new workflow to a Start with Docusign action menu.
    Docusign only displays workflows
    related to the record's object. For example, on an Account
    record, Docusign only displays
    workflows built for accounts and related objects.

[Remove this workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=tskb2fdae68-780f-45b0-9b06-f869786823eb.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to remove a Docusign workflow from a Start with Docusign action menu.")
:   Select this option to remove a workflow from the Start with Docusign action
    menu. Removing a workflow from the component view does not delete the
    workflow from your Docusign account.

[Search workflows](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=tsk5491237f-0dec-4eed-9976-7b2124f9a7df.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to search for a workflow from the Start with Docusign action menu.")
:   Use the search field to find a specific workflow. Docusign only displays
    workflows related to the record's object.

title: Getting Started with Workflow Builder for Salesforce
heading_path: Getting Started with Workflow Builder for Salesforce
text:
Learn about how to configure Salesforce steps in
workflows.

With [Workflow Builder](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=dnx1696972415150.html&_LANG=enus), you can connect
workflow steps like Web Forms and eSignature templates to third-party
extensions. You can create workflows to automate your agreement process,
including reading data from and writing data to Salesforce records.

Before using Salesforce features in workflows, first install, connect, and configure the Salesforce app.

Follow these steps to prepare your workflows to use the Salesforce app:

- Install the Salesforce application from the ​Docusign​ App Center
- Create a Connect configuration to add Salesforce merge fields to a Workflow Builder step
- Use ​Docusign​ Connect to link Salesforce fields to ​Docusign​ fields
- Add the Envelope Status component to a Salesforce layout

title: Complete a Salesforce Workflow Task
heading_path: Complete a Salesforce Workflow Task
text:
Learn to complete a task sent from a Docusign workflow.

Note: Standard users cannot view the progress of workflows in Salesforce. Salesforce administrators can [view failed workflows in the Docusign Logs page](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=vqr1744835015673.html&_LANG=enus). Only Docusign administrators can [monitor the progress of all workflows](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=vcp1698707326210.html&_LANG=enus).

Workflow Builder is a tool that your administrator uses to
automate your contract process. Docusign sends you an email when
you receive and complete workflow tasks. You complete tasks by entering contract
data, signing documents, and more.

Use this procedure to complete a workflow task:

1. Open the email from Docusign titled Review and
   complete workflow.
2. Select Review.
3. Complete all required workflow tasks and select Next for each completed stage.

You have completed the task. You have completed a workflow task. If
you are involved in later workflow steps, you receive additional messages from Docusign.

title: Configure a Read from Salesforce Step
heading_path: Configure a Read from Salesforce Step
text:
Learn to add and configure a Read from Salesforce step to import data from Salesforce records.

Before you begin
:   - Required: Installation of the Salesforce app from Docusign App Center. Review the [Installing Extension Applications for Workflow Builder](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=bgb1697668738892.html&_LANG=enus) topic for more information.
    - Required: [Workflows Create permissions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gbb1696973048215.html&_LANG=enus) or be an account administrator.
    - Required: Create a workflow as described in [Create a Basic Workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uhm1697826856678.html&_LANG=enus). Add steps like [invitation](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=kfx1698167344720.html&_LANG=enus), [web forms](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gua1698120920620.html&_LANG=enus), [eSignature](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uww1698167288211.html&_LANG=enus) and more to gather recipients, documents, and merge fields for your workflow.

You can use a Read from Salesforce step to import data from
Salesforce records to your workflow.
Administrators link workflow fields to Salesforce fields in the Read from Salesforce
step. When task recipients open an envelope, they see Salesforce data imported from
the record. Importing Salesforce data improves efficiency and reduces errors by
eliminating manual data entry.

Example: You build a workflow with a Read from Salesforce step. In the Read from Salesforce step, you link to a Salesforce record's Amount field. When a workflow recipient opens a contract, they see the record's Amount field pre-filled.

Use this procedure to create a Read from Salesforce step in a workflow:

1. Add a Read from Salesforce step as a Docusign administrator:
   1. Go to Agreements > Workflows and select Edit next to a workflow name.

      The workflow editor page displays.
   2. Select the Add a step ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/Workflow_Builder_add_step_plus_sign.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4NTksInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.XYTVCViE-U00R6SfAfB_FmwLN-9kVgkSrzZxIpP6-uI&_LANG=enus) icon.

      The Add New Step dialog box displays.
   3. Search for steps: Enter and select `Read from Salesforce`.

      The workflow editor page displays with the Read from Salesforce step added.
2. Name your Read from Salesforce step:
   1. Select Configure on the Read from Salesforce step.

      The configuration dialog box displays.
   2. Step Name: Keep or edit the name. It is helpful to add the name of the Salesforce object, like `Account - Read from Salesforce`.
3. Where will you read from?
   1. Connection: Select the field and choose a
      Salesforce organization that you connected to Docusign. Multiple options display if you connect more than one Salesforce
      organization.
   2. (Optional) Add New Connection: Select this button if you want to [connect more than one Salesforce organization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=ogk1719256147008.html&_LANG=enus).
   3. (Optional) Manage Connections: Select this
      button if you want to [add, edit, refresh, or delete a
      Salesforce connection](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=uaw1719259450391.html&_LANG=enus).
   4. Salesforce object: Search for an object or choose one from the dropdown menu.
   5. Select Next.

title: Configure a Read from Salesforce Step
heading_path: Configure a Read from Salesforce Step
text:
docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=uaw1719259450391.html&_LANG=enus).
   4. Salesforce object: Search for an object or choose one from the dropdown menu.
   5. Select Next.

      ![A screenshot displays a Read from Salesforce workflow step with the Connection and Salesforce object fields highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_Salesforce_Read_Where_will_you_read_from_object.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4NTksInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.XYTVCViE-U00R6SfAfB_FmwLN-9kVgkSrzZxIpP6-uI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a Read from Salesforce workflow step with the Connection and Salesforce object fields highlighted.")
4. Which fields would you like to read?: Select workflow
   fields to map to Salesforce fields. When workflows start, Docusign adds this data to agreements.
   1. Select Add or Remove Fields.

      The Add or Remove Fields page displays.
   2. Select the check box next to each Salesforce field that you'd like to
      use as a workflow variable. You'll be able to use these fields in later
      steps.

      Tip: When you select objects on the
      left, related objects open. The Source column
      displays each field's object path. ![A screen capture displays the Add or Remove Fields page with related objects highlighted and fields selected.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_Salesforce_Add_or_Remove_Fields.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4NTksInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.XYTVCViE-U00R6SfAfB_FmwLN-9kVgkSrzZxIpP6-uI&_LANG=enus "A screen capture displays the Add or Remove Fields page with related objects highlighted and fields selected.")
   3. Select Save and Exit.

      The Add or Remove Fields dialog box closes. The fields that you
      selected display.
   4. Select Next.
5. Which records are you reading from?: Create a rule that matches Salesforce fields with equivalent workflow fields. Workflows use this rule to read Salesforce data. You can create as many rules as you want:
   1. Salesforce field: Select a Salesforce field to match with a Docusign field.

      Note: When you select a (reference) type field, a new field opens below it. Choose a child object from the new field.
   2. Operator: Choose an option like Equal
      to, Less than, and so on.
   3. Workflow field: Select a Docusign workflow field to match with your Salesforce field:

      - Manual entry: Add text for your new variable and select Save.

        Note: The name that you enter must exactly match an existing field. For example, the rule fails if you enter CompanyName for the Company\_Name variable.
      - Select a field from the start step or another step in this
        workflow:

        Note: Steps like Read
        from HubSpot and Read from
        Salesforce display fields from up to five
        records. If more than five records are present, Salesforce
        logic decides which five to display. Selecting fields from
        multiple records helps you automate processes like mapping
        multiple Salesforce contacts to multiple Docusign participants.![A screenshot displays Read from Salesforce fields highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_Salesforce_Read_Workflow_field_selections.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4NTksInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.XYTVCViE-U00R6SfAfB_FmwLN-9kVgkSrzZxIpP6-uI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays Read from Salesforce fields highlighted.")

title: Configure a Read from Salesforce Step
heading_path: Configure a Read from Salesforce Step
text:
WduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4NTksInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.XYTVCViE-U00R6SfAfB_FmwLN-9kVgkSrzZxIpP6-uI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays Read from Salesforce fields highlighted.")

        You can use the fields that you select here in
        later steps like Collect Data with
        Webforms or Send Documents for
        Signature.
   4. Select Done to save your rule.

      ![A screenshot displays a Salesforce field, an operator value of Greater than or equal to, and a workflow field all highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_HubSpot_Read_Which_record_are_you_reading_from_Greater_than.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4NTksInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.XYTVCViE-U00R6SfAfB_FmwLN-9kVgkSrzZxIpP6-uI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a Salesforce field, an operator value of Greater than or equal to, and a workflow field all highlighted.")
6. (Optional) Add more rules:
   - +AND: Workflow Builder only imports Salesforce data that
     matches all of your rules.
   - +OR: Workflow Builder imports Salesforce data that matches
     any of your rules.
7. How many records should this step return?:
   - A single record: Returns the first record that
     matches your rule.
   - Multiple records: Returns up to five records that match your
     rule.

     Note: If more than five records are present, Salesforce logic
     decides which five to display.
8. (Optional) Preview this step: See examples of how this rule will work with up to ten Salesforce records:
   1. Select the Preview this step link.

      The Add Data to Preview Step page opens.
   2. Keep or edit the data provided in the field.
   3. Select Preview Salesforce Data.

      ![A screenshot displays the Read from Salesforce Add Data to Preview Step page of a Read from Salesforce workflow step.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/Read_from_Salesforce_Add_Data_to_Preview_Step.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4NTksInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.XYTVCViE-U00R6SfAfB_FmwLN-9kVgkSrzZxIpP6-uI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Read from Salesforce Add Data to Preview Step page of a Read from Salesforce workflow step.")

      The page refreshes, displaying Salesforce data that your Read from Salesforce rule found.
   4. Select Close.

      The Add Data to Preview Step page closes.
   5. Select Apply.

   The Read from Salesforce dialog box closes. The workflow page displays your new step.

You have completed the process. You have built a Read from Salesforce step in your workflow.
When this step is triggered in a workflow, it imports field data from the Salesforce
records that you mapped.

title: Configuration Details for Complex Branching Rules
heading_path: Configuration Details for Complex Branching Rules
text:
Read about nesting IF statements and using advanced operators in a branching rule workflow step.

This topic provides information on creating more complex branching rules and provides some illustrative examples.

title: Configuration Details for Complex Branching Rules
heading_path: Configuration Details for Complex Branching Rules
Nested IF statements
text:
You can design a workflow with multiple nested branching rules. You construct nested IFs by adding branching rules to the outcomes of another branching rule. Nested IFs support more complex conditional logic than just a single rule that provides a binary, TRUE/FALSE outcome. You can nest up to eight levels of rules.

If you delete a branching rule, you select whether to keep the True or False path steps in your workflow. You can also choose to delete all steps from both paths.

![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/delete_rule_steps_choice.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU5NzUsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.9t4D0esV1dwmWv_Dy7V--4F2dy6ka_bnS8wlPVidEjU&_LANG=enus)

title: Configuration Details for Complex Branching Rules
heading_path: Configuration Details for Complex Branching Rules
Multiple AND or OR conditions
text:
You can configure a single branching rule with up to five AND or OR conditions. You can mix AND and OR conditions to build multiple groups of conditions.

- A rule with multiple AND conditions evaluates to true if the conditions are all true, and false if any conditions are false.
- A rule with multiple OR conditions evaluates to true if any of the conditions are true, and false if all conditions are false.
- A rule with a mix of AND and OR conditions evaluates to true if any group of conditions evaluates to true, and false if all groups are false.

![Screenshot of a branching rule configuration with two groups of conditions.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/Branching-rule-example.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU5NzUsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.9t4D0esV1dwmWv_Dy7V--4F2dy6ka_bnS8wlPVidEjU&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of a branching rule configuration with two groups of conditions.")

title: Configuration Details for Complex Branching Rules
heading_path: Configuration Details for Complex Branching Rules
Advanced conditionality
text:
Branching rules support advanced conditionality logic. The available conditions depend on the data type you are using for your rule.

Booleans
:   For variables that are boolean and have only two possible values, the branching rule operators are `Is` and `Is not`. The results are `True` or `False`.
:   Examples:

    - Checkbox field is checked or unchecked.
    - Radio button is selected or unselected.
    - Identity verification check passed or failed.

Strings
:   For supported field types (text, note, email, name) that can contain string values, the standard comparison operators are provided. These can evaluate if the variable value equals, contains, starts with, or ends with a specified result.
:   Examples:

    - Text field contains the value `California`.
    - Email field ends with `.org`.
    - Text field does not equal the value `New Jersey`.![Screenshot of the rule operators for a string comparison.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/advancedruleoperators.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU5NzUsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.9t4D0esV1dwmWv_Dy7V--4F2dy6ka_bnS8wlPVidEjU&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of the rule operators for a string comparison.")

Dropdowns
:   For dropdown field types from a web form or eSignature step, standard comparison operators are provided to evaluate if the variable value equals a specified dropdown value. To select the result the rule will look for, the rule configuration presents the list of dropdown values from the mapped field to select from. ![Screenshot of configuration for a rule checking the value of a dropdown field from a web form step.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/dropdown_rule.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU5NzUsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.9t4D0esV1dwmWv_Dy7V--4F2dy6ka_bnS8wlPVidEjU&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of configuration for a rule checking the value of a dropdown field from a web form step.")

Dates
:   For date fields originating from a web form, eSignature step, instance date, or a starting variable, comparisons of equality, inequality, and dynamic comparison are supported. For web form date fields, use the (Normalized) version of the field. For other variables, you can select any variable of type = date for a comparison.![Screenshot of a rule evaluating a date field and the list of available operators.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/conditional_rule_date_operators.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU5NzUsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.9t4D0esV1dwmWv_Dy7V--4F2dy6ka_bnS8wlPVidEjU&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of a rule evaluating a date field and the list of available operators.")

Numbers
:   For number fields originating from a web form or a starting variable, the standard numerical comparisons are supported. For web form number fields, use the (Normalized) version of the field. For starting variables, you can select any variable of type = numbers for a numerical comparison. ![Screenshot of the configuration of a branching rule for a numerical comparison.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/branchingrulenumericalcomparison.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU5NzUsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.9t4D0esV1dwmWv_Dy7V--4F2dy6ka_bnS8wlPVidEjU&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of the configuration of a branching rule for a numerical comparison.")

title: Configuration Details for Complex Branching Rules
heading_path: Configuration Details for Complex Branching Rules
Merging branches
text:
You can configure a branching rule to merge the True and False branches. This is optional and useful if you want to combine the paths to use a single step outcome. You can add steps to each path to customize the workflow based on the initial outcome of the rule.

Note: You can also merge a multi branch rule. A multi branch rule can have up to 15 branches. You can merge all branches, or any two or more branches.

Important things to consider when opting to merge a branching rule:

- Variables and files generated from steps inside of a merged branching rule will not be available to use in steps occurring after the merge. Do not merge a rule if you are gathering data or generating files you want to use later in the workflow.
- You can nest another branching rule within the merged paths. The paths of a nested branching rule will also merge into a single outcome.
- You can edit the step to remove the merge setting. Any steps in the rule occurring before the merge point are retained on the branch where they exist. For any steps after the merge, you can delete them or select which path to move them to.

  Figure 1. Workflow example with a merged branching rule

  ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/workflow_example_merged_branching.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU5NzUsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.9t4D0esV1dwmWv_Dy7V--4F2dy6ka_bnS8wlPVidEjU&_LANG=enus)

title: Install the Salesforce Application ( Docusign App Center Instructions)
heading_path: Install the Salesforce Application (Docusign App Center Instructions)
text: Learn to install the Salesforce app in your Docusign account and connect it to your Salesforce organization.

title: Install the Salesforce Application ( Docusign App Center Instructions)
heading_path: Install the Salesforce Application (Docusign App Center Instructions)
Before you begin
text:
[Purchase a Docusign Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) for Sales application](https://www.docusign.com/intelligent-agreement-management?utm_source=bing&utm_medium=cpc&utm_campaign=GBL_XX_PRF_UPS_2410_SEMIAM&utm_term=docusign%20iam&campaignid=569731866&adgroupid=1178678316174006&location=84406&device=c&matchtype=e&extensionid=&creative=73667602015411&keyword=docusign%20iam&placement=&network=o&msclkid=3287ee64f2c01f69bcd686665f40e7da).

Important: Use these instructions to install the application from the Docusign App Center. Follow this link if you want to [install the app from your Salesforce organization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=&map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=tsk91549a3b-f7f3-4b74-8e87-33ff33d198c0.html&_LANG=enus).

Docusign administrators can get extension applications from the Docusign App Center.

You can install the Salesforce app to connect your Docusign account with your Salesforce organization. As an administrator, log on to the Docusign App Center and install the Salesforce app for your account. Workflow creators can add Salesforce steps to read and write data, store documents, add merge fields, and more.

For more information about viewing, installing, and managing partner extension apps, see the  [Docusign App Center Guide](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=ijy1698170251179.html&_LANG=enus).

Developers can review the [Extension apps 101 overview](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/extension-apps-101/) in the Docusign Developer Center.

Tip: Wait at least 30 minutes after installing before you configure a Salesforce workflow step. Docusign indexes all Salesforce fields to make them available for use.

Use this procedure to install and connect the Salesforce app through the Docusign App Center:

1. Log on to your Docusign account as an administrator and open the appropriate App Center site:
   - [Docusign Production](https://apps.docusign.com/app-center/extensionapps)
   - [Docusign Demo](https://apps-d.docusign.com/app-center/extensionapps)
2. Select the Salesforce tile and select Install App when the page refreshes.

   The Install and authorize "Salesforce" app page opens.
3. Select Continue and select Connect Account when the page refreshes.

   The Connect a Salesforce account page opens.
4. Connect to your Salesforce organization:

   Important: [Salesforce Government Cloud customers](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=000386092&type=1) must use the Salesforce Production option.

   1. Select a Salesforce organization to connect to Docusign:

      - Salesforce Production (login.salesforce.com): Connect a Salesforce production or developer organization.
      - Salesforce Sandbox (test.salesforce.com): Connect to a Salesforce sandbox organization.

      ![A screenshot displays the Connect a Salesforce account with Salesforce Production selected.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/Workflow_Builder_Connect_a_Salesforce_Account.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4MjAsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.BLXfUeLVFOArJWjrivh4LHj3X7eHlxnpF-xq23pDa1A&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Connect a Salesforce account with Salesforce Production selected.")
   2. Select Next.
   3. How would you like to connect this Salesforce account?

      - Private: Only accessible to you in Docusign.
      - Shared: Accessible by multiple users or the whole team in Docusign.
   4. Select Next.
   5. Name this Salesforce account: Keep or change the default name.
   6. Select Log In
5. Connect to your Salesforce organization. Salesforce does not ask for credentials if you are logged in to your organization.

   Important: Salesforce Government Cloud customers must use the Custom Domain option.

title: Install the Salesforce Application ( Docusign App Center Instructions)
heading_path: Install the Salesforce Application (Docusign App Center Instructions)
Before you begin
text:
your Salesforce organization. Salesforce does not ask for credentials if you are logged in to your organization.

   Important: Salesforce Government Cloud customers must use the Custom Domain option.

   - Standard domain: Enter your Salesforce credentials and select Log In.
   - Custom domain
     1. Select Use Custom Domain.
     2. Custom Domain: Enter your domain address and select Continue.
