heading_path: Configure a Store Files in Salesforce Step
Before you begin
text:
jU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.sPe-eWhrmfT4MuyYBkdMyUBhJ58IDd-IntyF96V3PzY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays Read from Salesforce fields highlighted.")

      The field that you selected displays.
   5. Select Next.

      ![A screenshot displays the Select Connection and Select Record fields with the Next button highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/Workflow_Builder_Store_files_in_Salesforce_Where_are_you_storing_it.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4NzIsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.sPe-eWhrmfT4MuyYBkdMyUBhJ58IDd-IntyF96V3PzY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Select Connection and Select Record fields with the Next button highlighted.")
5. What will you call it?: Use variables and text to create a
   custom file name for your agreements:
   1. Add Variable: Use the search field or dropdown
      menu to select variables to add to the agreement name.
   2. Add Text: Enter text to add to the
      agreement name and select Add.

      Note: The File name field
      displays the naming convention. As you add variables and text, the
      name updates. Example: File name:
      [Account ID][Envelope ID]MSA.

      ![A screen capture displays the Store files in Salesforce step settings with the Add Variable and Add Text features used to create a file name.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_Salesforce_Store_files_What_will_you_call_it.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4NzIsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.sPe-eWhrmfT4MuyYBkdMyUBhJ58IDd-IntyF96V3PzY&_LANG=enus "A screen capture displays the Store files in Salesforce step settings with the Add Variable and Add Text features used to create a file name.")
   3. Select Apply.

   The Store file in Salesforce dialog box closes. The page displays your new step.

You have completed the process. You have added a Store files in
Salesforce step to your workflow. When signers complete an agreement, Docusign adds the agreement and certificate of completion to the
specified record.

title: Link Docusign Workflows to Salesforce Flows
heading_path: Link Docusign Workflows to Salesforce Flows
text:
Learn about linking Docusign workflows and Salesforce flows to create integrated
automation.

title: Link Docusign Workflows to Salesforce Flows
heading_path: Link Docusign Workflows to Salesforce Flows
Why link workflows?
text:
[Workflow Builder](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=dnx1696972415150.html&_LANG=enus) automates agreement actions from your Docusign
account.

Salesforce Flow is a workflow builder that you can use to automate agreements from
your Salesforce organization.

You can link Docusign workflows to Salesforce flows by adding a **Start Docusign
Workflow** step to your flow.

When a flow starts, Salesforce starts your linked Docusign workflow. This integration
automates the creation of agreements that contain Docusign and
Salesforce data.

title: Link Docusign Workflows to Salesforce Flows
heading_path: Link Docusign Workflows to Salesforce Flows
Workflow linking example
text:
You build a Record-Triggered Flow and link it to a Docusign workflow.
You configure your flow to start when an opportunity's stage changes to
Negotiation/Review. You add a Start Docusign Workflow step to your flow. When started, your workflow
generates an NDA with Docusign and Salesforce data.

A user changes the stage of the Burlington Textiles opportunity to Negotiation/Review.
Salesforce starts the workflow linked to your flow. Docusign
generates an NDA containing data from the record and sends it for signature.

title: Link Docusign Workflows to Salesforce Flows
heading_path: Link Docusign Workflows to Salesforce Flows
Workflow linking prerequisites
text:
- Purchase a Docusign
  Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) plan.
- Install Docusign Apps
  Launcher version 7.4 or higher.
- Install the Salesforce
  Application from the Docusign App Center.
- Assign Workflows Create permissions
  to your Docusign user, or be an account administrator.
- Build a workflow to be started by your Salesforce flow.
- Prepare your Salesforce users for linking
  flows to Docusign workflows.
- Configure a Start Docusign
  Workflow step in a Salesforce flow.

title: Link Docusign Workflows to Salesforce Flows
heading_path: Link Docusign Workflows to Salesforce Flows
Monitor workflows
text:
Standard Salesforce users
:   Standard users cannot view the progress of Docusign workflows in Salesforce.

Salesforce administrators
:   Administrators can only [view
    failed workflows in the Docusign Logs page](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=vqr1744835015673.html&_LANG=enus).

Docusign administrators
:   Docusign administrators can [monitor the progress of all workflows](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=vcp1698707326210.html&_LANG=enus).

title: Link Docusign Workflows to Salesforce Flows
heading_path: Link Docusign Workflows to Salesforce Flows
Additional resources
text:
The following topics demonstrate how to prepare for and configure flows that start
Docusign workflows:

[Access Required to Start Docusign Workflows from Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=nlu1742250066565.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about the permissions that you must add for users to start Docusign workflows in Salesforce.")

[Check the Status of a Flow That Failed to Start a Docusign Workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=vqr1744835015673.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to check the status of a flow that failed to start a workflow.")

title: Export and Import a Workflow Builder Workflow
heading_path: Export and Import a Workflow Builder Workflow
text:
Learn to export a Workflow Builder workflow and import it to either the same or another Docusign account.

You can export Docusign workflows and import them to either
the same account or another Docusign account.

Example 1: You build a workflow in your Docusign demo account. You want to add this workflow to your Docusign production account. You download the workflow from your
demo account and upload the .ZIP file to your production account. You validate that
all steps will work in production and publish the workflow.

Example 2: You build a workflow in your Docusign production account. You want to build a new workflow that
is similar to this workflow, but with a few differences. You download the workflow
and upload the .ZIP file back to your production account. You rename the imported
workflow, make some changes, and publish the workflow.

Use this procedure to export and import a workflow:

1. As a Docusign administrator, go to Agreements >  Workflow Builder Workflows.
2. Export your workflow:
   1. Select the three-dot menu icon ![A screenshot displays three dots stacked vertically.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/A-screenshot-diplays-a-threedot-menu-icon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU5NTcsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.iRBsaZP4s904psiB5xyH943GuqLzmesP8eZatlrZms8&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays three dots stacked vertically.") next to a workflow and choose
      Export.

      A dialog box displays: Export this
      workflow?
   2. Select Export.

   The page refreshes. The workflow configuration downloads to your device
   as a .ZIP file.
3. Import your workflow to the same Docusign account or another
   account:
   1. As a Docusign administrator, go to Agreements > Workflows.
   2. Select Import Workflow.

      The Creating your workflow dialog box displays.
   3. Select Import Workflow and select
      Upload
   4. Follow the prompts to upload the .ZIP file from your device.

   The page refreshes. The imported workflow configuration
   displays.
4. Select Review & Publish if you are ready to use the
   workflow. Select Save Draft if you are not ready to
   publish.

You have completed the process. You have exported and imported a
workflow. If you imported it into a different Docusign account, review
each step to ensure that the workflow functions.

title: Edit the Name of a Salesforce Connection
heading_path: Edit the Name of a Salesforce Connection
text:
Learn to edit the name of a Salesforce organization that's connected to your Docusign IAM account.

You can edit the name of a Salesforce organization that's connected to your Docusign IAM account. Editing the name of an
organization is useful when you have connected more than one Salesforce organization
to Docusign. Editing a name does not change the name of your
Salesforce organization; only how it displays in the app.

You can access the controls from your Salesforce organization or your Docusign account.

Use this procedure to edit the name of a Salesforce connection:

1. Choose your login method:
   - Login from Salesforce:
     1. [Access the Docusign Setup page as a Salesforce
        administrator](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=etv1586134206174.html&_LANG=enus)
     2. Manage Connections and the IAM Launcher: Select
        Manage.
   - Login from Docusign:
     1. Open the [Production](https://apps.docusign.com/app-center/extensionapps) or [Demo](https://apps-d.docusign.com/app-center/extensionapps) App Center site.
     2. Select the Salesforce tile.
     3. Select Manage and choose Manage
        Connections.
2. Select the three-dot menu icon ![A screenshot displays three dots stacked vertically.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/A-screenshot-diplays-a-threedot-menu-icon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU5NDUsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.bzev8ms6uZVlQ2KNNb5NdDbiRWqhLcW3mU98QWqhsO4&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays three dots stacked vertically.") next to a connection and choose Edit Connection
   Name.
3. Edit the name in the Connection Name field and select
   Save.

   The page refreshes. A message displays: Successfully edited
   connection.

You have completed the process. You edited the name of a Salesforce
connection. This edit does not change the name of
your Salesforce organization; only the name of the connection.

title: Access Required to Start Docusign Workflows from Salesforce
heading_path: Access Required to Start Docusign Workflows from Salesforce
text:
Learn about the permissions that you must add for users to start Docusign workflows in
Salesforce.

You can use [Workflow Builder](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=dnx1696972415150.html&_LANG=enus) to automate agreement
actions from your Docusign account.

Salesforce Flow is a workflow builder that you can use to automate agreements from
your Salesforce organization.

You can link Salesforce flows to Docusign workflows by adding a **Start Docusign
Workflow Builder** workflow step to your flow.

All users who want to start Docusign workflows from Salesforce must have proper
permissions.

Follow this link to learn how to [assign the proper
Docusign and Salesforce user permissions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=bmk1744912007661.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to prepare your Salesforce users so that they can start Docusign workflows from Salesforce.").

title: Access Required to Start Docusign Workflows from Salesforce
heading_path: Access Required to Start Docusign Workflows from Salesforce
Required Docusign permissions
text:
You must have one of the following permissions in your Docusign
account to use Docusign workflows:

| Docusign permission | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Docusign administrator** | Authorizes applications for Workflow Builder and enables users to create and run workflows by granting required user permissions. They are granted full workflow permissions by default. |
| Workflow creator | An eSignature user who creates, edits, and publishes their own workflows. They can also use any published workflow to start an agreement process. |
| Preparer | Runs published workflows to start the agreement process and monitors the progress of running instances. They have access to use any published workflow on the account to start an agreement process. |
| Participant | Users who participate in agreement workflows. They do not require Docusign credentials. |

title: Access Required to Start Docusign Workflows from Salesforce
heading_path: Access Required to Start Docusign Workflows from Salesforce
Required Salesforce permissions
text:
Users belonging to any of the following permission sets can start workflows from
Salesforce:

| Permissions | Description |
| --- | --- |
| User setting: Flow User | Provides access to Salesforce flows. |
| Permission set: **Docusign Administrator** | Provides administrative access to Docusign Apps Launcher. |
| Permission set: **Docusign User** | Provides access to eSignature in Docusign Apps Launcher. |

title: Create Multiple Salesforce Connections
heading_path: Create Multiple Salesforce Connections
text:
Learn to connect multiple Salesforce organizations to your Docusign IAM account.

You can connect multiple Salesforce organizations to your Docusign
IAM account. When you connect multiple organizations, you can
build workflows for more than one Salesforce organization. Docusign does not limit the number of Salesforce organizations you
can connect to Docusign.

Example: You connect two production organizations and one
sandbox to your Docusign account. You build workflows
for each organization. Each Salesforce organization has access to fields from the
same Docusign account.

Use this procedure to connect multiple Salesforce organizations to your Docusign account:

1. Choose your login method:
   - Login from Salesforce:
     1. [Access the Docusign Setup page as a Salesforce
        administrator](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=etv1586134206174.html&_LANG=enus)
     2. Manage Connections and the IAM Launcher: Select
        Manage.
   - Login from Docusign:
     1. Open the [Production](https://apps.docusign.com/app-center/extensionapps) or [Demo](https://apps-d.docusign.com/app-center/extensionapps) App Center site.
     2. Select the Salesforce tile.
     3. Select Manage and choose Manage
        Connections.
2. Select + New Connection.
3. Configure your new connection:
   1. Connect a Salesforce account: Choose
      Salesforce Production or
      Salesforce Sandbox and select
      Next.

      ![A screenshot displays the Connect a Salesforce account with Salesforce Production selected.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/Workflow_Builder_Connect_a_Salesforce_Account.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU5NDEsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.flgxV9RfCKrMTUAi9AD1hIt1ZpO7WrowNbL0zWtZ6go&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Connect a Salesforce account with Salesforce Production selected.")
   2. How would you like to connect this Salesforce
      account?: Choose Private or
      Shared and select
      Next.

      Important: If you log on from
      Salesforce, you must choose the Shared
      option.
   3. Salesforce Connection Name: Enter a descriptive
      name in the field and select Log In.
   4. Connect to your Salesforce organization. Salesforce does not ask for
      credentials if you are logged in to your organization:
   - Standard domain: Enter your Salesforce credentials
     and select Log In.
   - Custom domain
     1. Select Use Custom Domain.
     2. Custom Domain: Enter your domain address and
        select Continue.
     3. Enter your Salesforce credentials and select Log
        In.

   The page refreshes. A message displays: Logged in to your
   Salesforce account.

You have completed the task. You connected an additional Salesforce
organization in your Salesforce app. You can build workflows for the additional
organization.

title: Build a Docusign Workflow to Be Started By a Salesforce Flow
heading_path: Build a Docusign Workflow to Be Started By a Salesforce Flow
text:
Learn to build a Docusign workflow that can be started by a Salesforce
flow.

Before you begin
:   - Purchase a Docusign Intelligent Agreement
      Management (IAM) plan.
    - Required: Installation of the Salesforce app
      from Docusign App Center. Review the [Installing Extension Applications for Workflow Builder](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=bgb1697668738892.html&_LANG=enus) topic for more information.
    - Required: [Workflows Create permissions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gbb1696973048215.html&_LANG=enus) or be an account administrator.
    - Required: Access to a suitable template to use for your workflow
      step. Review the [Template Requirements](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=xnw1709852160602.html&_LANG=enus) topic for more information.

Workflow Builder automates agreement actions from your Docusign account.

Salesforce Flow is a workflow builder that you can use to automate agreements from your
Salesforce organization.

You can link Salesforce flows to Docusign workflows by adding a **Start Docusign
Workflow Builder** workflow step to your flow.

When a flow starts, Salesforce starts your linked Docusign workflow. Linking flows with
workflows builds an automation chain that creates agreements from Salesforce and Docusign data.

To use a Start Docusign workflow builder workflow step, create a
Docusign workflow that can be started by a
Salesforce flow.

Use this procedure to create a workflow that can be started by a flow:

1. As a Docusign administrator, create your workflow:
   1. Log on to your Docusign account and go to Agreements > Workflows.
   2. Select Create Workflow.

      The Create a workflow page displays.
   3. Select + Blank Workflow.![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_Salesforce_workflow_build_Blank_workflow_.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4ODksInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.ZPO46lA-wsj_GNQSEFg15eUDk8PNASb_qlGDYv2U4bI&_LANG=enus)

      The Untitled workflow page displays.
   4. Select + Add workflow start.

      The Add workflow start page displays.
2. What starts this workflow?: Choose one of the two
   supported options from the dropdown menu:

   ![A screenshot displays the What starts this workflow step with From a Link and From an API Call highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/What-starts-this-workflow-with-From-a-link-and-From-an-API-Call-highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4ODksInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.ZPO46lA-wsj_GNQSEFg15eUDk8PNASb_qlGDYv2U4bI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the What starts this workflow step with From a Link and From an API Call highlighted.")

   - (Option 1: Recommended) From an API Call: This
     workflow starts when an API call starts the workflow. Use the following
     procedure:
     1. Select From an API Call from the dropdown
        menu.
     2. Select Next.
     3. Set up variables for dynamic information: Add
        up to ten variables, including the Record ID
        variable:

        Important: Make each
        variable a Text type. Name one variable
        Record ID (case sensitive) and the
        others Variable 1 through
        Variable 9. This is the only variable
        format that you can use in a Salesforce flow.

title: Build a Docusign Workflow to Be Started By a Salesforce Flow
heading_path: Build a Docusign Workflow to Be Started By a Salesforce Flow
text:
type. Name one variable
        Record ID (case sensitive) and the
        others Variable 1 through
        Variable 9. This is the only variable
        format that you can use in a Salesforce flow.

        1. Select New Variable and choose
           Text from the dropdown menu.
        2. Enter Variable 1 in the field.
     4. Repeat this process for any additional variables and select
        Next.![A screenshot displays the Set Up Variables step with Variable 1 highlighted in green.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/Workflow_Builder_start_step_for_Salesforce_Flow.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4ODksInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.ZPO46lA-wsj_GNQSEFg15eUDk8PNASb_qlGDYv2U4bI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Set Up Variables step with Variable 1 highlighted in green.")
     5. Will this API call be started by a human or an automated
        process?:
        1. Select Automated process.
        2. Select Apply.
   - (Option 2) From a Link: This workflow starts when a participant
     opens a shared link or a link embedded on an external website. Only use this
     option for non-agreement request flows, or when you do not need to see
     status updates.
     1. Select From a Link from the dropdown
        menu.
     2. Select Apply.

   The page refreshes. Your start step displays.
3. Complete your workflow:
   1. Add more steps to your workflow. These are the steps that are started
      from the [Configure a Start Docusign Workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=azl1741206795047.html&_LANG=enus) step in your
      Salesforce flow.
   2. Select Review & Publish when you have
      finished building your workflow.

You have completed the process. You have built a workflow that can
be started by a Salesforce flow. When your flow starts, Salesforce starts your
workflow.

title: Configure a Writeback to Salesforce Step
heading_path: Configure a Writeback to Salesforce Step
text:
Learn to add and configure a Writeback to Salesforce step to update Salesforce records.

Before you begin
:   - Required: Installation of the Salesforce app
      from Docusign App Center. Review the [Installing Extension
      Applications for Workflow Builder](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=bgb1697668738892.html&_LANG=enus) article for more
      information.
    - Required: [Workflows Create
      permissions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gbb1696973048215.html&_LANG=enus) or be an account
      administrator.
    - Required: Create a workflow as described in [Create a Basic Workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uhm1697826856678.html&_LANG=enus). Add
      steps like [invitation](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=kfx1698167344720.html&_LANG=enus),
      [web forms](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gua1698120920620.html&_LANG=enus), [eSignature](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uww1698167288211.html&_LANG=enus) and
      more to gather recipients, documents, and merge fields for your
      workflow.

You can use a Writeback to Salesforce step to update Salesforce records
with information from your workflow. The Writeback to Salesforce step
updates Salesforce fields, saves documents to records, creates new records, and
more.

Administrators link workflow fields to Salesforce fields in the Writeback to
Salesforce step. When task recipients enter data in workflow fields, Docusign updates the information in the linked Salesforce record
fields.

Writeback improves efficiency and reduces errors caused by manually updating
records.

Example: You can build a workflow that includes a Collect Data
with Web Forms step and a Writeback to
Salesforce step. In the writeback step, you link the Salesforce
opportunity's Amount field to the web form's
Amount field. A workflow recipient updates and signs a
contract for an existing opportunity named Fontara. The recipient updates the
Fontara contract's Amount field from $20,000 to $25,000. When the recipient
completes the assignment, Docusign updates the Fontara record's
Amount field to $25,000.

Note: Follow the required formatting for all fields. See the
[Supported Salesforce Field Types](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=ref769a389d-135f-4fb1-8076-9b01358ffe4d.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about the field types that you can use in Salesforce workflow steps.")
topic for more information.

Use the following procedure to create a Writeback to Salesforce
step in a workflow:

1. Add a Writeback to Salesforce step as a Docusign administrator:
   1. Go to Agreements > Workflows and select Edit next to a workflow
      name.

      The workflow editor page displays.
   2. Select the Add a step
      ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/Workflow_Builder_add_step_plus_sign.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4NjQsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.-pfFlbEjXPz8TCAVtV22qqWrWbC7_nZ6IIG2kYI2hdo&_LANG=enus) icon.

      The Add New Step dialog box displays.
   3. Search for steps: Enter `Writeback to
      Salesforce` in the search field and select the
      Writeback to Salesforce step that displays.

      The workflow editor page displays with the Writeback to
      Salesforce step added.
2. Name your Writeback to Salesforce step:
   1. Select Configure on the Writeback to Salesforce
      step.

title: Configure a Writeback to Salesforce Step
heading_path: Configure a Writeback to Salesforce Step
text:
The workflow editor page displays with the Writeback to
      Salesforce step added.
2. Name your Writeback to Salesforce step:
   1. Select Configure on the Writeback to Salesforce
      step.

      The configuration dialog box displays.
   2. Step Name: Keep or edit the name. It is helpful
      to add the name of the Salesforce object, like `Account -
      Writeback to Salesforce`.
3. Where will you write to?
   1. Select connection: Select the field and choose a
      Salesforce organization that you connected to Docusign. Multiple options display if you connected more than one Salesforce
      organization.
   2. (Optional) Add New Connection: Select this
      button if you want to [connect more than one Salesforce
      organization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=ogk1719256147008.html&_LANG=enus) .
   3. (Optional) Manage Connections: Select this
      button if you want to [add, edit, refresh, or delete a
      Salesforce connection](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=uaw1719259450391.html&_LANG=enus).
   4. Salesforce object: Search for an object or
      choose one from the dropdown menu.
   5. Write settings: Select an option from the list
      that opens:

      - Update or Create: Update a record or
        create a new record if none exists.
      - Update: Update data on an existing
        record.
      - Create: Create a new record.
   6. Select Next.

      ![A screenshot displays a Writeback to Salesforce workflow step with the Connection, Salesforce object, and Write settings highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_Salesforce_Writeback_Where_will_you_write_to_Salesforce_object.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4NjQsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.-pfFlbEjXPz8TCAVtV22qqWrWbC7_nZ6IIG2kYI2hdo&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a Writeback to Salesforce workflow step with the Connection, Salesforce object, and Write settings highlighted.")
4. Which fields are you writing to?: Select workflow fields to
   map to Salesforce fields. When workflow recipients enter data, Docusign updates Salesforce records.
   1. Select Add or Remove Fields.

      The Add or Remove Fields page displays.
   2. Select the check box next to each Salesforce field that you'd like to
      use as a workflow variable. You'll be able to use these fields in later
      steps.

      ![A screenshot displays the Add or Remove Fields page with two Salesforce fields selected and highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/Workflow_Builder_Writeback_to_Salesforce_Add_or_Remove_Fields.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4NjQsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.-pfFlbEjXPz8TCAVtV22qqWrWbC7_nZ6IIG2kYI2hdo&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Add or Remove Fields page with two Salesforce fields selected and highlighted.")
   3. Select Save and Exit.

      The Add or Remove Fields dialog box closes. The fields that you
      selected display.
   4. Fields: Add a variable or step for each
      field:

      - Manual entry: Add text for your new
        variable and select Save.

        Note: The name that you enter must exactly
        match an existing field. For example, the rule fails if you
        enter CompanyName for the
        Company\_Name
        variable.
      - Select a field from the start step or another step in this
        workflow:

title: Configure a Writeback to Salesforce Step
heading_path: Configure a Writeback to Salesforce Step
text:
field. For example, the rule fails if you
        enter CompanyName for the
        Company\_Name
        variable.
      - Select a field from the start step or another step in this
        workflow:

        Note: Steps like Read
        from HubSpot and Read from
        Salesforce display fields from up to five
        records. If more than five records are present, Salesforce
        logic decides which five to display. Selecting fields from
        multiple records helps you automate processes like mapping
        multiple Salesforce contacts to multiple Docusign participants.![A screenshot displays Read from Salesforce fields highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_Salesforce_Read_Workflow_field_selections.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4NjQsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.-pfFlbEjXPz8TCAVtV22qqWrWbC7_nZ6IIG2kYI2hdo&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays Read from Salesforce fields highlighted.")

        You can use the fields that you select here in
        later steps like Collect Data with
        Webforms or Send Documents for
        Signature.
   5. (Optional) Overwrite if field value exists:
      Select this check box if you want the data entered by a workflow
      participant to overwrite existing Salesforce data.
   6. Repeat this process for each variable displayed.
   7. Select Next.

      ![A screenshot displays the Account ID Salesforce field and the Overwrite if field value exists checkbox highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/Workflow_Builder_Writeback_to_Salesforce_Which_fields_are_you_writing_to.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4NjQsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.-pfFlbEjXPz8TCAVtV22qqWrWbC7_nZ6IIG2kYI2hdo&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Account ID Salesforce field and the Overwrite if field value exists checkbox highlighted.")
5. Which record are you writing to?: Create a rule that
   matches a Salesforce field with an equivalent workflow field. This allows
   Workflow Builder to interact with Salesforce data.
   1. Salesforce field: Select a Salesforce field to
      match with a Docusign workflow field.

      Note: When you select a
      (reference) type field, a new field opens
      below it. Choose a child object from the new field.
   2. Operator: Choose an option like Equal
      to, Less than, and so on.
   3. Workflow field: Select a Docusign workflow field to match with your Salesforce field. The following
      options display:

      - Manual entry: Add text for your new
        variable and select Save.
      - Select a field from the start step or another workflow
        step.
   4. Select Done to save your rule.

      ![A screenshot displays a Salesforce field, an operator value of Greater than or equal to, and a workflow field all highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_Salesforce_Writeback_step_Which_record_are_you_writing_to_Rule.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4NjQsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.-pfFlbEjXPz8TCAVtV22qqWrWbC7_nZ6IIG2kYI2hdo&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a Salesforce field, an operator value of Greater than or equal to, and a workflow field all highlighted.")
6. (Optional) Add more rules:
   - +AND: Workflow Builder only imports Salesforce data that
     matches all of your rules.
   - +OR: Workflow Builder imports Salesforce data that matches
     any of your rules.
7. Select Apply.

   The Writeback to Salesforce dialog box closes. The page displays your new step.

title: Configure a Writeback to Salesforce Step
heading_path: Configure a Writeback to Salesforce Step
text:
your rules.
   - +OR: Workflow Builder imports Salesforce data that matches
     any of your rules.
7. Select Apply.

   The Writeback to Salesforce dialog box closes. The page displays your new step.

You have completed the process. You have built a Writeback to
Salesforce step in your workflow. When this step is triggered in a workflow, Docusign updates field data on Salesforce records that you mapped.

title: Delete a Salesforce Connection to Your Docusign IAM Account
heading_path: Delete a Salesforce Connection to Your Docusign IAM Account
text:
Learn to delete the Salesforce connection to your Docusign IAM account.

You can connect Salesforce organizations to your Docusign IAM account. When you connect to Docusign, you can
build workflows that read from and write to your Salesforce records. You can delete
a Salesforce connection if you no longer want to access that organization.

You can access the delete controls through your Salesforce organization or your Docusign account.

Use this procedure to delete a Salesforce connection:

1. Choose your login method:
   - Login from Salesforce:
     1. [Access the Docusign Setup page as a Salesforce
        administrator](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=etv1586134206174.html&_LANG=enus)
     2. Manage Connections and the IAM Launcher: Select
        Manage.
   - Login from Docusign:
     1. Open the [Production](https://apps.docusign.com/app-center/extensionapps) or [Demo](https://apps-d.docusign.com/app-center/extensionapps) App Center site.
     2. Select the Salesforce tile.
     3. Select Manage and choose Manage
        Connections.
2. Select the three-dot menu icon ![A screenshot displays three dots stacked vertically.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/A-screenshot-diplays-a-threedot-menu-icon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU5NjIsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.oEa99v6J8WMP8k3TIhTZ7QlgMY4DpMRuHycYE0SgB_w&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays three dots stacked vertically.") next to a connection and choose Delete
   Connection.

   A dialog box displays: Delete this connection?
3. Delete this connection?: Enter `DELETE` in the field and select Delete Connection.![A screenshot displays the Refresh this connection dialog box with the word Refresh and the Refresh Connection button highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/Workflow_Builder_Delete_Connection_dialog_box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU5NjIsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.oEa99v6J8WMP8k3TIhTZ7QlgMY4DpMRuHycYE0SgB_w&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Refresh this connection dialog box with the word Refresh and the Refresh Connection button highlighted.")

   The dialog box closes. The Connections page displays with a message: Successfully deleted
   connection.

You have completed the task. You deleted a connection to a
Salesforce organization.

title: Update the Salesforce Application
heading_path: Update the Salesforce Application
text: Learn to update the Salesforce app to access new features.

title: Update the Salesforce Application
heading_path: Update the Salesforce Application
Before you begin
text:
- [Purchase a Docusign
  Intelligent Agreement
  Management (IAM) for Sales application](https://www.docusign.com/intelligent-agreement-management?utm_source=bing&utm_medium=cpc&utm_campaign=GBL_XX_PRF_UPS_2410_SEMIAM&utm_term=docusign%20iam&campaignid=569731866&adgroupid=1178678316174006&location=84406&device=c&matchtype=e&extensionid=&creative=73667602015411&keyword=docusign%20iam&placement=&network=o&msclkid=3287ee64f2c01f69bcd686665f40e7da).
- [Install the Salesforce Application from the Docusign App Center](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=jxo1706295810553.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to install the Salesforce app in your Docusign account and connect it to your Salesforce organization.").

Docusign continues to release new versions of the Salesforce
application. To access new features, update your version of the app.

Tip: Wait at least 30 minutes after installing before
you configure a workflow step. Docusign must index all Salesforce
fields to make them available for use.

You can access the upgrade controls through your Salesforce organization or your Docusign account.

Use this procedure to update your version of the Docusign Salesforce app:

1. Choose your login method:
   - Login from Salesforce:
     1. [Access the Docusign
        Setup page as a Salesforce administrator](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=etv1586134206174.html&_LANG=enus)
     2. Manage Connections and the IAM Launcher: Select
        Manage.
   - Login from Docusign:
     1. Open the [Production](https://apps.docusign.com/app-center/extensionapps) or [Demo](https://apps-d.docusign.com/app-center/extensionapps) App Center site.
     2. Select the Salesforce tile.
     3. Select Manage and choose Manage
        Connections.
2. Select the three-dot menu icon ![A screenshot displays three dots stacked vertically.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/A-screenshot-diplays-a-threedot-menu-icon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU5MzYsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.3bgvaSb_5TDIpaLBYy-FIUsejGuE2yBIcTqvmwkdJRQ&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays three dots stacked vertically.") next to a connection and choose Reload Objects.

   A dialog box displays: Refresh this
   connection?
3. Refresh this connection?: Enter `REFRESH`
   in the field and select Refresh Connection. ![A screenshot displays the Refresh this connection dialog box with the word Refresh and the Refresh Connection button highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/Workflow_Builder_Refresh_Connection_dialog_box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU5MzYsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.3bgvaSb_5TDIpaLBYy-FIUsejGuE2yBIcTqvmwkdJRQ&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Refresh this connection dialog box with the word Refresh and the Refresh Connection button highlighted.")

   The dialog box closes. The Connections page displays with a message:
   Successfully refreshed.

You have completed the process. You have updated the Salesforce app. You can access the latest workflow builder features.

title: Supported Salesforce Field Types
heading_path: Supported Salesforce Field Types
text:
Learn about the field types that you can use in Salesforce workflow
steps.

Note: Docusign continues to add support for new field
types. If you can't access new field types or configurations, open your Salesforce step and save it again.

When you build
Salesforce workflow steps, you match
Salesforce fields with other workflow fields. Workflow Builder formats data from Salesforce fields in specific ways.

You can add the following field types to Salesforce steps:

| Field type | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Boolean | Add true/false options. |
| Checkbox | Add a checkbox to select true/false or yes/no options. |
| Currency | Add a unified currency field to capture, map, and manage monetary amounts and currency codes throughout workflows. |
| Date | Add a date with no time. Unlike adding dates into string values, workflows can use date fields to schedule actions. |
| Datetime | Add a date and time. Unlike adding dates and times into string values, workflows can use datetime fields to schedule actions or timestamp events. |
| Double | Add a number with decimal points. |
| Dropdown | Add list of allowed values in a dropdown menu. |
| Email | Add an email address. You must use valid formatting, like user@company.com. |
| Encryptedstring | Add a field for encrypted text that can be used for secure data handling, such as storing sensitive information like passwords or API keys. Workflows can use this data securely, without showing it in plain text. |
| ID | Add the unique identifier of a Salesforce record, like a company or contact. |
| Integer | Add a whole number that can be positive, negative, or zero, and does not include fractions or decimals. |
| List | Add a list of items. |
| Phone | Add a phone number. You must use a valid country code, like `+61 xxx xxx xxxx` |
| Picklist | Add a list from which users can make selections. |
| String | Add human-readable text, like words and sentences. |
| Text | Add fields into which users can enter text. |
| Textarea | Add text entry fields with attributes like minimum and maximum character limits, placeholder text, and validation rules. |

title: Supported Salesforce Field Types
heading_path: Supported Salesforce Field Types
Date field formatting
text:
Due to Salesforce date restrictions, you can only use the following date formats in a
Writeback to Salesforce workflow step:

- YYYY-MM-DD
- YYYY-MM-DDThh:ssZ
- YYYY-MM-DDThh:ss.sssZ

- YYYY/MM/DD
- YYYY/MM/DDThh:ssZ
- YYYY/MM/DDThh:ss.sssZ

title: Supported Salesforce Field Types
heading_path: Supported Salesforce Field Types
Phone number field formatting
text:
Phone number format restrictions

Due to Salesforce restrictions, you can only use the following formats in
Writeback to Salesforce or Read from Salesforce steps:

- PhoneNumberFormat.E164
- PhoneNumberFormat.INTERNATIONAL
- PhoneNumberFormat.NATIONAL

Country code formatting

Add a country code if you
are using a phonenumber field in the following Workflow Builder
for Salesforce options:

- Read from Salesforce
- Writeback to Salesforce:
  - Update or Create
  - Update

Add the country code in front of the contact number. For example,
for a phone number with a code of +61, use the following format: `+61
xxx xxx xxxx`.

If your Writeback to Salesforce step has the
Create option selected, you are not required to use a
country code. However, using a country code is still recommended.

![A screenshot displays the Where will you write to stage of a Workflow Builder Writeback to Salesforce step, with the Write settings section highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_Salesforce_Country_Code_rules.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4MzYsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.ZmeeEPKlAOsas_aDsTGu-WTG0-2yVySa29aieLHB-8I&_LANG=enus)

Ensure that you also add a country code to the Salesforce record's phone
field:![A screenshot displays the phone field with a country code highlighted on a Salesforce contact.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/Workflow_Builder_phone_number_country_code_on_Salesforce_record.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4MzYsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.ZmeeEPKlAOsas_aDsTGu-WTG0-2yVySa29aieLHB-8I&_LANG=enus)

Correcting phone numbers

- Docusign adds the United States country code of
  `+1` to Salesforce records lacking a country
  code.
- Workflow task recipients can change incorrect country codes.
- Workflow task recipients can also change incorrect phone numbers.

Example 1:: A workflow sends a task from a Salesforce
record with no country code. Rather than refusing to advance the workflow task,
Docusign automatically adds the United States country code
of `+1` to the phone number. The organization is in the
United States, so the workflow recipient keeps the `+1`
country code and completes the task. The Writeback to Salesforce step adds the
`+1` country code to the
record.

Example 2: A workflow sends a task from a
Salesforce record with no country code. Docusign adds the
United States country code of `+1` to the task. The
recipient knows that the organization is from Australia and changes the country
code to `+61`. The recipient completes the task. The
Writeback to Salesforce step changes the record's country code to
`+61`.

Example 3: A workflow
sends a task from a Salesforce record with an incorrect phone number of
`1234`. Rather than refusing to advance the workflow
task, Docusign sends the task with an editable phone number.
The recipient enters the correct phone number of `+1
555-666-7777` and completes the task. The Writeback to Salesforce
step changes the record's phone number from `1234` to
`+1 555-666-7777`.

title: Supported Salesforce Field Types
heading_path: Supported Salesforce Field Types
Checkbox field formatting
text:
You can connect Salesforce checkbox fields to workflow checkbox fields. This is
useful when you want your workflow to read from and writeback to Salesforce yes/no
fields.

Example: Your Salesforce organization has an Express
Shipping checkbox field. The checkbox has values of
Checked = Yes (true) and Unchecked = No
(false).

You want workflow task recipients to add express shipping to their orders by
selecting the checkbox. You configure the following steps in your workflow:

- Read from Salesforce: You add the Salesforce
  Express Shipping (checkbox) field.
- Collect Data with Web Forms: You add an
  Express Shipping checkbox field and map it to the
  Salesforce checkbox.
- Writeback to Salesforce: You map the Salesforce Express
  Shipping field to the web form field.

A workflow task recipient selects the Express Shipping
checkbox on the web form and completes the task. The Writeback to Salesforce step
adds a check to the record's Express Shipping checkbox.

title: Supported Salesforce Field Types
heading_path: Supported Salesforce Field Types
Dropdown field formatting
text:
You can connect Salesforce dropdown fields to workflow dropdown fields. This is
useful when you want your workflow to read from and writeback to Salesforce dropdown
fields.

Example: Your Salesforce organization has a Product
Size dropdown field. The dropdown field has values of
Large, Medium, and
Small.

You want workflow task recipients to select a size when ordering shirts. You
configure the following steps in your workflow:

- Read from Salesforce: You add the Salesforce
  Product Size (picklist) field.
- Collect Data with Web Forms: You add a
  Product Size (picklist) dropdown field and map it
  to the Salesforce checkbox.
- Writeback to Salesforce: You map the Salesforce
  Product Size field to the web form field.

A workflow task recipient selects the Medium option from the
web form's Product Size dropdown menu and completes the task.
The Writeback to Salesforce step changes the record's Product
Size value to Medium.

title: Format String Values in Salesforce Steps
heading_path: Format String Values in Salesforce Steps
text:
Learn to create concatenated values in Salesforce step string fields.

You can create Salesforce workflow steps that read and update Salesforce data. You
can add string fields to Salesforce steps.

String fields are human-readable text like words, IDs, or simple labels. Workflow Builder uses
string fields as text, not as number or date values used in calculations or
scheduling.

Because Workflow Builder formats string value fields as rich text inputs, you can enter text and add variables in the same field. This is useful when you want Salesforce to store a single, human-readable value built from multiple pieces of data.

Important: The following guidelines apply to formatting Workflow Builder for Salesforce string fields:

- Add text and variables in Writeback to Salesforce fields and rules.
- Add text and variables in Read from Salesforce rules.
- Add multiple variables by selecting the string field's + icon.
- Add up to three string variables in one string field.
- Enter a space after text if you want to add variables after it.
- Add only string variables. You cannot add Boolean, array, or other field types.

title: Format String Values in Salesforce Steps
heading_path: Format String Values in Salesforce Steps
Writeback to Salesforce field formatting
text:
You can add text and variables when formatting Writeback to Salesforce fields. Select a Salesforce field and link it to variables from other workflow steps. You can enter text and up to three string values to a single string field.

Example 1: You create a workflow that includes a
Writeback to Salesforce step. You build the step to create a
new Account record if one doesn't exist. You want each new record's Account Name field
to contain the following content:

- The words `New Customer`
- The `First Name` and `Last Name` variables of a
  Salesforce contact

In the Which fields are you writing to? section, you add
the Account Name string field. You enter the words
`New Customer:`  followed by a space. You select the
field's + icon and add the contact's First
Name and Last Name variables.

A
workflow recipient completes a task for a new customer named Alex Smith. The
Writeback to Salesforce step in your workflow creates a new record. The Account Name
field of the new record displays this text: New Customer: Alex
Smith.![A screen capture displays the process of entering text and variables into the Account Name field of a Salesforce step.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_Salesforce_and_HubSpot_concatenated_string_fields.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4MzksInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.Rs3nVUPK0vwMGcL_Nb8d1LnW7KA0SN_7dUvYvgB-5U8&_LANG=enus "A screen capture displays the process of entering text and variables into the Account Name field of a Salesforce step.")

Example 2: You create a workflow that includes a Writeback
to Salesforce step. Your organization has a custom string field named
Products Purchasing. When customers purchase multiple products,
you want all products combined in a single field, rather than in separate fields.

In
the Which fields are you writing to? section, you add the
Products Purchasing string field. You add Opportunity string variables for
`Product 1`
`Product 2`, and `Product 3`.

A workflow
recipient completes a task for a customer that is purchasing cups, straws, and lids.
The Writeback to Salesforce step adds the three products to the customer's
Salesforce record. The record's Products Purchasing field displays this text:
Cups, Straws, Lids.

title: Format String Values in Salesforce Steps
heading_path: Format String Values in Salesforce Steps
Read from Salesforce and Writeback to Salesforce rule formatting
text:
You can add text and variables when formatting Read from and Writeback to Salesforce rules. When you create a rule, you can select a Salesforce string field and link it to workflow fields from other steps. You can format the matching workflow field with text and up to three string values.

Writeback to Salesforce example: You create a workflow that includes a Writeback
to Salesforce step.

In the Which record are you writing
to? section, you add the Owner ID string
field. You select the Equal to operator. For
Workflow field, you select the field's
+ icon and add the Owner ID,
First Name, and Last Name
variables.

A workflow recipient completes a task for a customer with an owner
named Terry Jones. The Writeback to Salesforce step only updates Salesforce records
that match the Owner ID, First Name, and Last Name of Terry Jones.![A screen capture displays the process of adding three variables into the workflow field of a Salesforce step rule.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_Salesforce_Rule_concatenated_string_fields.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4MzksInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.Rs3nVUPK0vwMGcL_Nb8d1LnW7KA0SN_7dUvYvgB-5U8&_LANG=enus "A screen capture displays the process of adding three variables into the workflow field of a Salesforce step rule.")

Read from Salesforce example: You create a workflow that includes a Read from
Salesforce step.

In the Which records are you reading
from? section, you add the Owner string field.
You select the Equal to operator. For Workflow
field, you enter the words `Owner name and email:` followed by a space. You select the field's +
icon and add the Signer: Name and Signer:
Email variables from a web form.

A workflow recipient
completes a task for a customer named Fontara with an owner named Terry Jones. The
Read from Salesforce step adds records with an owner name of Terry
Jones and an email of terry.jones@fontara.com.
Your workflow can use fields from these records as variables in later
steps.

title: Use the Record Count Variable to See How Many Records a Workflow Finds
heading_path: Use the Record Count Variable to See How Many Records a Workflow Finds
text:
Learn to use the Record Count field to find how many records are returned in a Read
from Salesforce step.

title: Use the Record Count Variable to See How Many Records a Workflow Finds
heading_path: Use the Record Count Variable to See How Many Records a Workflow Finds
Before you begin
text:
- Required: Installation of the Salesforce app from Docusign App Center. Review the [Installing Extension Applications for
  Workflow Builder](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=bgb1697668738892.html&_LANG=enus)
  topic for more information.
- Required: [Workflows Create permissions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gbb1696973048215.html&_LANG=enus) or be an account
  administrator.
- Required: Create a workflow as described in [Create a Basic Workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uhm1697826856678.html&_LANG=enus). Add steps like
  [invitation](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=kfx1698167344720.html&_LANG=enus), [web forms](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gua1698120920620.html&_LANG=enus), [eSignature](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uww1698167288211.html&_LANG=enus) and more to gather recipients,
  documents, and merge fields for your workflow.

You can use a Read from Salesforce step to import information
from your Salesforce records to use in workflow tasks.

The Read from Salesforce step displays fields from up to five
records. Selecting fields from multiple records is useful for processes involving
multiple people, like mapping several Salesforce contacts to several workflow participants.

Record Count is an Integer (number) variable that tracks how
many records your Read from Salesforce step finds. You can use Record Count in a
Branching Rule step to send your workflow down the
following paths:

- True Path: Proceed with the workflow if the record
  count is greater than zero.
- False Path: Take alternative action if the record count
  is zero.

The Record Count variable ensures that your workflows don't fail when no
records exist.

Example: You build a workflow to verify employee data. The
workflow contains a Read from Salesforce step with a Branching Rule below it. The
Branching Rule states that if the Read from Salesforce step returns zero records,
the workflow moves to the False path. A user starts the
workflow that finds zero records and triggers the False path. The workflow sends a
Docusign Web Form to the recipient to collect their details.
The recipient submits the form and the workflow uses that data to create a new
record in your Salesforce organization.

Note: If you don't see
`Record Count`, open your Read from Salesforce step in the
designer and save it.

Use this procedure to create a Branching Rule that works with your Read from
Salesforce step:

1. Add a Branching Rule step as a Docusign administrator:
   1. Go to Agreements > Workflows and select Edit next to a workflow
      name.

      The workflow editor page displays.
   2. Select the Add a step
      ![A screenshot displays a white plus sign in a purple square.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/Workflow_Builder_add_step_plus_sign.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4NDIsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.nirTdVX2HfKJkRZCF2sVjZ_tn2iwdUjAXHv_hJEKPsw&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a white plus sign in a purple square.") icon.

      The Add New Step dialog box displays.
   3. Search for steps: Enter and select
      `Add a Branching Rule`.

      - When adding the step between existing steps, select which path
        to move the steps that come after the new rule. Then select
        Configure.
      - If you are adding the step to the end of your workflow, select
        Configure.

title: Use the Record Count Variable to See How Many Records a Workflow Finds
heading_path: Use the Record Count Variable to See How Many Records a Workflow Finds
Before you begin
text:
teps, select which path
        to move the steps that come after the new rule. Then select
        Configure.
      - If you are adding the step to the end of your workflow, select
        Configure.

   The Add conditional rule dialog box displays.
2. Configure your Branching Rule:
   1. What will the rule check? Select the field and
      choose Record Count from the dropdown menu.
   2. What operator will the rule use? Choose an option like Equal
      to, Less than, and so on.
   3. What result will the rule look for? Enter a
      value between zero and five. Read from Salesforce steps cannot return
      more than five records.
   4. Select Done.

   ![Workflow Builder Branching Rule configuration showing Record Count selected as the field, an operator such as Equal to, and a numeric value between 0 and 5.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/Read_from_Salesforce_or_HubSpot_Record_Count_used_in_rule_.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4NDIsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.nirTdVX2HfKJkRZCF2sVjZ_tn2iwdUjAXHv_hJEKPsw&_LANG=enus "Workflow Builder Branching Rule configuration showing Record Count selected as the field, an operator such as Equal to, and a numeric value between 0 and 5.")
3. (Optional) Select + AND or + OR
   to add conditions to your rule. You can configure up to five conditions.
4. (Optional) Select the option Merge true and false
   branches? if you want to combine the branches to continue the
   workflow. This optional setting is useful if you want to combine the paths to
   use a single-step outcome.

   Important: Variables and files generated from
   steps inside of a merged Branching Rule will not be available to use in
   steps occurring after the merge. For more details, see [Merging branches](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=jaf1716918359288.html&anchor=jaf1716918359288__section-426&_LANG=enus).
5. Select Apply.

The workflow editor page displays with the Branching Rule step added.

title: Format Currency Fields in Salesforce Workflow Steps
heading_path: Format Currency Fields in Salesforce Workflow Steps
text: Learn to format currency fields in Read from Salesforce and Writeback to Salesforce steps.

title: Format Currency Fields in Salesforce Workflow Steps
heading_path: Format Currency Fields in Salesforce Workflow Steps
Before you begin
text:
Before you begin

- Required: Installation of the Salesforce app from Docusign App Center. Review the [Installing Extension Applications for Workflow Builder](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=bgb1697668738892.html&_LANG=enus) topic for more information.
- Required: [Workflows Create permissions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gbb1696973048215.html&_LANG=enus) or be an account administrator.
- Required: Create a workflow as described in [Create a Basic Workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uhm1697826856678.html&_LANG=enus). Add steps like [invitation](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=kfx1698167344720.html&_LANG=enus), [web forms](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gua1698120920620.html&_LANG=enus), [eSignature](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uww1698167288211.html&_LANG=enus) and more to gather recipients, documents, and merge fields for your workflow.

You can create Salesforce workflow steps that read and update Salesforce
data. You can use `currency` fields in Salesforce steps to import and update a record's monetary value.

A currency field is a unified `MonetaryAmount` variable that combines a number and
a currency code. For example: `1000000.00` and
`USD`.

When a workflow uses a Read from Salesforce step to import a Salesforce currency
field, Workflow Builder divides the field into the following elements:

- Value: A `doubleValue` numeric
  amount.
- Currency code: An ISO 4217
  `currencyCode`, often linked to a Salesforce
  `CurrencyIsoCode`.

Workflow Builder combines those elements into a `currency` variable that
contains both elements. Workflows can use each element in
the following ways:

- `Value`: Used in rules and later workflow steps that look for an
  amount.
- `Code`: Used to branch or map on a currency. For example: a workflow contains one branch for `USD` and another
  for `EUR`.

Note: In single-currency Salesforce organizations, Workflow Builder
reads the `Code` from organization-level settings. In
multi-currency organizations, Workflow Builder reads the `CurrencyIsoCode`
from records.

Read from Salesforce example: You want to import currency
amounts like `Annual Revenue` into workflows. You build a workflow
with a Read from Salesforce step and add the Salesforce Annual
Revenue field.

Result: When the workflow runs, Workflow Builder reads the Salesforce
Annual Revenue field and adds it to the agreement as a
currency variable. The variable includes both the numeric amount and the ISO 4217
currency code. The workflow uses this variable in rules, forms, and downstream
steps.

When using Writeback to Salesforce to update a Salesforce currency field, Workflow Builder
sends the following information:

- `doubleValue`: Sends a `doubleValue` numeric
  amount.
- `currencyCode`: Sends an ISO 4217 currency code to pair with
  the numeric amount.

Note:

- In single-currency Salesforce organizations, map the `Value` currency variable to a Salesforce
  `amount` field.
- In multi-currency organizations, map the
  `Code` currency variable to the record's
  `CurrencyIsoCode`.

Writeback to Salesforce example: You want workflows to update
Salesforce currency fields like `Annual Revenue`. You build a
workflow with a Read from Salesforce step and a Writeback to Salesforce step. In the
Read from Salesforce step, you add the Salesforce Annual
Revenue field.

In the Writeback to Salesforce step, you map the Annual
Revenue Salesforce field to the Annual
Revenue.Value workflow field.

title: Format Currency Fields in Salesforce Workflow Steps
heading_path: Format Currency Fields in Salesforce Workflow Steps
Before you begin
text:
he
Read from Salesforce step, you add the Salesforce Annual
Revenue field.

In the Writeback to Salesforce step, you map the Annual
Revenue Salesforce field to the Annual
Revenue.Value workflow field.

Result: When the workflow runs, Workflow Builder adds the Salesforce
`Annual Revenue` currency field to the workflow. A signer changes
an agreement's Annual Revenue value from
`$1,000,000 USD` to `$1,500,000 USD`.
When the workflow completes, Workflow Builder changes the record's Annual Revenue value from
$1,000,000 USD to $1,500,000 USD.

Use this procedure to add a currency field to Read from Salesforce and Writeback to Salesforce
steps:

1. [Create a basic workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uhm1697826856678.html&_LANG=enus) containing
   a [Read from Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=idr1713985144297.html&_LANG=enus) step.
2. Open the step to the Which fields would you like to read?
   section.
   1. Select Add or Remove Fields.

      The Add or Remove Fields page displays.
   2. Select the check box next to each Salesforce
      currency type field that you'd like to
      use.
   3. Select Save and Exit.

      The Add or Remove Fields dialog box closes.
      The fields that you selected display.
   4. Select Next.
3. Which records are you reading from?
   1. Salesforce field: Select a currency field from
      the previous step.
   2. Operator: Choose an option like Equal
      to, Less than, and so on.
   3. Workflow field: Select the field and choose
      Manual entry.

      The currency field displays.
   4. Currency: Enter an amount in the field. You can
      only enter numbers, not letters, or symbols.
   5. Select a currency code from the dropdown menu at the left.
      USD is the default value.

      ![A screenshot displays the Which records are you reading from section with a currency field highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/Workflow_Builder_Read_from_Salesforce_Which_records_are_you_reading_from_currency.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4NTQsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.eXErGCIk6lC0XUj1BzI0vPYvd86dx_5m-1XS-l1LsC8&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Which records are you reading from section with a currency field highlighted.")
   6. Select Save.
   7. Select Done.
4. Complete the rest of your Read from Salesforce step and select
   Apply.
5. Open the Writeback to Salesforce step to the Which fields are you
   writing to? section.
   1. Select Add or Remove Fields.

      The Add or Remove Fields page displays.
   2. Select the check box next to each Salesforce
      currency type field that you'd like to
      use.
   3. Select Save and Exit.

      The Add or Remove Fields dialog box closes.
      The fields that you selected display.
   4. Select Next.
6. Fields: Configure your Salesforce currency field:
   1. Select the field and choose Manual entry.

      The currency field displays.
   2. Currency: Enter an amount in the field. You can
      only enter numbers, not letters, or symbols.
   3. Select a currency code from the dropdown menu at the left.
      USD is the default value.

text:
2. Currency: Enter an amount in the field. You can
      only enter numbers, not letters, or symbols.
   3. Select a currency code from the dropdown menu at the left.
      USD is the default value.

      ![A screenshot displays the Which fields are you writing to section with a currency field highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/Workflow_Builder_Writeback_to_Salesforce_Which_fields_are_you_writing_to_currency.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4NTQsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.eXErGCIk6lC0XUj1BzI0vPYvd86dx_5m-1XS-l1LsC8&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Which fields are you writing to section with a currency field highlighted.")
   4. Select Save.
   5. Select Next.
7. Which records are you writing to?
   1. Salesforce field: Select a currency field from
      the previous step.
   2. Operator: Choose an option like Equal
      to, Less than, and so on.
   3. Workflow field: Select the field and choose
      Manual entry.

      The currency field displays.
   4. Currency: Enter an amount in the field. You can
      only enter numbers, not letters, or symbols.
   5. Select a currency code from the dropdown menu at the left.

8. Complete the rest of your Send Documents for Signature step and select
