You have completed the process. You have added and
configured a currency field in a Read from Salesforce and a Writeback to Salesforce
step. When the workflow runs, Docusign creates an agreement and adds
all mapped contacts and fields. When the workflow completes, Docusign
updates the record's currency field with any changes.

title: Prepare Your Salesforce Organization to Use a Start with Docusign Action
heading_path: Prepare Your Salesforce Organization to Use a Start with Docusign Action
text: Learn to prepare your Salesforce organization to start Docusign Workflow Builder workflows from Salesforce.

title: Prepare Your Salesforce Organization to Use a Start with Docusign Action
heading_path: Prepare Your Salesforce Organization to Use a Start with Docusign Action
Before you begin
text:
[Purchase a Docusign Intelligent Agreement
Management (IAM) for Sales application](https://www.docusign.com/intelligent-agreement-management?utm_source=bing&utm_medium=cpc&utm_campaign=GBL_XX_PRF_UPS_2410_SEMIAM&utm_term=docusign%20iam&campaignid=569731866&adgroupid=1178678316174006&location=84406&device=c&matchtype=e&extensionid=&creative=73667602015411&keyword=docusign%20iam&placement=&network=o&msclkid=3287ee64f2c01f69bcd686665f40e7da)

Important:

- This feature is only available to IAM for Sales customers. Contact [Docusign support](https://support.docusign.com/s/?language=en_US&_gl=1*74elk4*_gcl_au*MTY5ODQyMjA2MC4xNzY5NjM1OTA1LjE5ODM3MTQzNjguMTc2OTYzNjIwNC4xNzY5NjM2MjA0) or [Docusign
  sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales) for more information.
- Do not follow these steps if you already have Docusign
  Apps Launcher v8.4 or higher installed. Only follow these steps to add a
  Start with Docusign component or action in an
  organization with v8.3 or lower installed.

.

With [Docusign
Workflow Builder](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=bqi1705961132077.html&_LANG=enus), you can create workflows to
automate your agreement process. If you want to start workflows from Salesforce, you
can add a Start with Docusign action or component to your
layouts. Users can start a workflow by selecting Start next
to a workflow name. To start workflows from Salesforce, you must prepare your
organization.

Use this procedure to prepare your Salesforce organization to start workflows from
Salesforce:

1. As a Salesforce and Docusign administrator, [Install Docusign Apps
   Launcher version 8.4 or higher](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=ogj1586134254571.html&_LANG=enus).
   - [Install for Salesforce production](https://login.salesforce.com/packaging/installPackage.apexp?p0=04tKg000000D3Vk).
   - [Install for Salesforce sandbox](https://test.salesforce.com/packaging/installPackage.apexp?p0=04tKg000000D3Vk).
2. [Disconnect and reconnect Docusign and Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=ntr1586134210309.html&_LANG=enus).

   Important: Disconnecting removes Docusign access from all Salesforce users. Disconnecting does not remove users from your connected Docusign account.
3. [Open Docusign Apps Launcher](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=etv1586134206174.html&_LANG=enus).
4. Go to Docusign Setup >  User Management.
5. [Re-add your Docusign users](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=uwf1586134275195.html&_LANG=enus).

   Important: Users must [re-activate their Docusign access in Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=vbw1648698462634&topicId=ymi1586134213877.html&_LANG=enus). After re-activating,
   users can access Agreement Desk in the Docusign Agreement
   Requests component.

You have completed the task. You have prepared your Salesforce organization to start
workflows from Salesforce.

title: Prepare Your Salesforce Organization to Use a Start with Docusign Action
heading_path: Prepare Your Salesforce Organization to Use a Start with Docusign Action
Next steps
text:
Complete the following tasks to finish your integration setup:

- [Build a workflow to be triggered by a
  Start with Docusign action](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=tskccafc250-9431-4f0c-9518-d4526d9128b1.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to build a workflow that can be triggered by a Start with Docusign action.").
- [Build a Start with Docusign action](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=tsk32e505bf-436a-47b1-b7f6-55e6ad614ec5.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to build a new Lightning web component action to start a Docusign workflow from a record.").
- [Add a Start with Docusign action to a layout](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=tskb637a1ca-b7ba-4604-97f7-f5f2d5f899f6.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to add a Start with Docusign action to a page layout to start workflows.") .
- [Grant user permissions to start
  workflows from Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=tsk3640d081-197c-4f31-990a-0309487e92a8.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to assign a custom Docusign permission profile so that standard users can execute Workflow Builder workflows from Salesforce.").

title: Build a Start with Docusign Action (Manual Controls)
heading_path: Build a Start with Docusign Action (Manual Controls)
text:
Learn to build a new Lightning web component action to start a Docusign
workflow from a record.

title: Build a Start with Docusign Action (Manual Controls)
heading_path: Build a Start with Docusign Action (Manual Controls)
Before you begin
text:
Important:

- This feature is only available to IAM for Sales customers. Contact [Docusign support](https://support.docusign.com/s/?language=en_US&_gl=1*74elk4*_gcl_au*MTY5ODQyMjA2MC4xNzY5NjM1OTA1LjE5ODM3MTQzNjguMTc2OTYzNjIwNC4xNzY5NjM2MjA0) or [Docusign
  sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales) for more information.
- Docusign recommends using the [IAM Launcher Setup Controls](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=tska9d68585-f524-49a4-8898-496b6575d036.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to use the IAM Launcher Setup interface to add Docusign workflow actions to Salesforce layouts.") to add workflow actions. Use these
  instructions if the IAM Launcher Setup controls fail.

[Prepare your
organization to start workflows from Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=tsk2a2ae3f7-99c2-4deb-ab15-61fc26ade4d1.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to prepare your Salesforce organization to start Docusign Workflow Builder workflows from Salesforce.").

If you want to start workflows from Salesforce records, you can add a Start
with Docusign action to your layouts. The Start with Docusign action displays workflows that you can start from your
records. You can take the following actions from the Start with Docusign action menu:

- Start: Select the Start button to start any
  displayed workflow from a record.
- Add a Workflow: Add a new workflow to the Start with
  Docusign action.
- Remove this workflow: Remove a workflow from the
  Start with Docusign action.
- Search workflows: Find a specific workflow from the
  list.

Use this procedure to create a Start with Docusign action:

1. Access the Buttons, Links, and Actions page as a Salesforce administrator:
   1. Select the gear icon ![A screenshot displays a gear icon with a lightning bolt inside.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU5MDIsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.wAL-YSPjCAqPj-MgyoWpNF41zAuWlAcTfrPpk8SPquI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a gear icon with a lightning bolt inside.") and choose Setup from the dropdown menu.
   2. Enter and select Object Manager in the Quick
      Find field.
   3. Select a Salesforce object in the left column and select
      Buttons, Links, and Actions.

   The Buttons, Links, and Actions page displays.
2. Select New Action in the upper right of the page.

   The New Action page displays.
3. Create your Start with Docusign action:
   1. Action Type: Select Lightning Web
      Component from the dropdown menu.
   2. Lightning Web Component: Select
      dfsle:embeddedWorkflowLauncher from the
      dropdown menu.
   3. Standard Label Type: Keep the default value of
      None.
   4. Label: Enter `Start with
      Docusign`
   5. Name: Keep the default value of `Start_with_Docusign`
   6. Select Save.![A screenshot displays the Action page with Start with Docusign fields and the Save button highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/Create_Start_with_Docusign_Lightning_action.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU5MDIsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.wAL-YSPjCAqPj-MgyoWpNF41zAuWlAcTfrPpk8SPquI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Action page with Start with Docusign fields and the Save button highlighted.")

   The page closes. The Buttons, Links, and Actions page
   displays.

You have completed the process. You have created a new Start with
Docusign Lightning web component action.

title: Build a Start with Docusign Action (Manual Controls)
heading_path: Build a Start with Docusign Action (Manual Controls)
Next steps
text:
[Add the
Start with Docusign action](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=tskb637a1ca-b7ba-4604-97f7-f5f2d5f899f6.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to add a Start with Docusign action to a page layout to start workflows.") to a layout so that your users can start workflows
from records.

title: Grant User Permissions to Start Workflows from Salesforce
heading_path: Grant User Permissions to Start Workflows from Salesforce
text: Learn to assign a custom Docusign permission profile so that standard users can execute Workflow Builder workflows from Salesforce.

title: Grant User Permissions to Start Workflows from Salesforce
heading_path: Grant User Permissions to Start Workflows from Salesforce
Before you begin
text:
Important: This feature is only available to IAM for
Sales customers. Contact [Docusign support](https://support.docusign.com/s/?language=en_US&_gl=1*74elk4*_gcl_au*MTY5ODQyMjA2MC4xNzY5NjM1OTA1LjE5ODM3MTQzNjguMTc2OTYzNjIwNC4xNzY5NjM2MjA0) or [Docusign
sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales) for more information.

- [Purchase a Docusign Intelligent Agreement
  Management (IAM) for Sales application](https://www.docusign.com/intelligent-agreement-management?utm_source=bing&utm_medium=cpc&utm_campaign=GBL_XX_PRF_UPS_2410_SEMIAM&utm_term=docusign%20iam&campaignid=569731866&adgroupid=1178678316174006&location=84406&device=c&matchtype=e&extensionid=&creative=73667602015411&keyword=docusign%20iam&placement=&network=o&msclkid=3287ee64f2c01f69bcd686665f40e7da).
- [Install Docusign Apps
  Launcher version 8.4 or higher](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=ogj1586134254571.html&_LANG=enus).
- Add a [Start with
  Docusign action](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=tskb637a1ca-b7ba-4604-97f7-f5f2d5f899f6.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to add a Start with Docusign action to a page layout to start workflows.") to a layout.

Docusign administrator users can start workflows from Salesforce. Standard Docusign users need custom permissions to send workflows from
Salesforce. Create a custom Docusign permission profile and grant
standard users Use access to the workflows.

Use this procedure to create a custom permission profile and grant access to users:

1. As a Docusign administrator, go to Admin >  Permission Profiles and select Add Permission Profile.

   The Add Permission Profiles page displays.
2. Create a new Workflow Builder permission profile for standard Salesforce users:
   1. Name: Enter a descriptive name. For example,
      `Start Docusign Workflow`.
   2. Select the User Permissions tab.
   3. Select the Allow users to send envelopes check box.
   4. Scroll down to Start Docusign Workflow and
      select Use from the dropdown menu.
   5. Select Add.

   The dialog box closes. The Permission Profiles page displays a message: You
   successfully created the permission profile (name of
   profile.)
3. Grant users access to your new permission profile:
   1. Go to USERS AND GROUPS and select
      Users.

      The Users page displays.
   2. Select Actions next to a user name and select
      Edit from the dropdown menu.

      The user's page displays.
   3. Select Permission Profile and choose the name of
      your new permission profile from the dropdown menu.
   4. Select Save Changes at the bottom of the
      page.

      The user's page closes. The Users page displays a message: You successfully
      update the user (user name.)

You have completed the process. You have created a Workflow Builder
permission profile and granted access to a user. The user can [start workflows from Salesforce
records](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=tskcae26f13-f2e5-4afd-85a7-145a70b12977.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to start a Docusign workflow from a Start with Docusign action.").

title: Duplicate a Workflow
heading_path: Duplicate a Workflow
text: Learn to quickly build new workflows by duplicating existing workflows.

title: Duplicate a Workflow
heading_path: Duplicate a Workflow
Before you begin
text:
You can duplicate workflows to quickly build similar workflows.

Example: You have a workflow called Salesforce
MSA. The workflow is designed to generate and send MSA agreements
from Salesforce records. You want to build a similar workflow to create and send NDA
agreements from Salesforce records. Rather than building a new workflow, you select
the Duplicate option from your Salesforce
MSA workflow menu. Docusign instantly creates a
duplicate workflow named COPY Salesforce MSA. All steps,
fields, and settings from the original workflow are copied to the duplicate
workflow. You rename the imported workflow Salesforce NDA, make
a few changes, and publish it.

Note: Steps, settings, and field
mappings from the original function in the duplicated workflow. You are not
required to re-map fields or rebuild steps.

Use this procedure to duplicate a for Salesforce workflow:

1. As a Docusign administrator, go to Agreements > Workflows.
2. Select the more actions menu icon ![A screenshot displays three dots stacked vertically.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/A-screenshot-diplays-a-threedot-menu-icon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU5NTUsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.Z5YtRkzhH2YgmTKjMzPqtmUUg0BX3OsQLWqm76nC5ro&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays three dots stacked vertically.") next to a workflow and choose Duplicate.

   The page refreshes. A message displays: Workflow
   duplicated. The workflow editor page opens, displaying the
   duplicated workflow with the word COPY in the
   title.
3. Make changes to your duplicated workflow.
4. Select Review & Publish if you are ready to use the
   workflow. Select Save Draft if you are not ready to
   publish.

You have completed the process. You have duplicated a workflow and created a new workflow
from it.

title: Search for a Workflow in the Start with Docusign Action Menu
heading_path: Search for a Workflow in the Start with Docusign Action Menu
text: Learn to search for a workflow from the Start with Docusign action menu.

title: Search for a Workflow in the Start with Docusign Action Menu
heading_path: Search for a Workflow in the Start with Docusign Action Menu
Before you begin
text:
Important: This feature is only available to IAM for
Sales customers. Contact [Docusign support](https://support.docusign.com/s/?language=en_US&_gl=1*74elk4*_gcl_au*MTY5ODQyMjA2MC4xNzY5NjM1OTA1LjE5ODM3MTQzNjguMTc2OTYzNjIwNC4xNzY5NjM2MjA0) or [Docusign
sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales) for more information.

- [Add a Start with
  Docusign action to a layout](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=tskb637a1ca-b7ba-4604-97f7-f5f2d5f899f6.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to add a Start with Docusign action to a page layout to start workflows.").
- Add users to a workflow permission profile.

If you want to start Docusign workflows from Salesforce
records, you can add a Start with Docusign action to your
layouts. The action displays workflows that you can start from your records. You can
add and remove the workflows that display in the action menu. You can also search
for workflows added to the action menu.

Both administrators and standard users can search for workflows in the action menu.

Use this procedure to search for a workflow in a Start with Docusign action menu:

Note: Docusign only displays
workflows related to the record's object.

1. Choose your access method:
   - From the Docusign Setup page:
     1. [Access the Docusign Setup
        page as a Salesforce administrator](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=etv1586134206174.html&_LANG=enus)
     2. Manage Connections and the IAM Launcher: Select
        Manage.
     3. Select the IAM Launcher Setup tab.
     4. Select the Manage button next to a layout
        (Account, Opportunity, and so on.)

        The Start with Docusign menu displays.
   - From a Salesforce record: Select the Start with
     Docusign action in the upper right of the record.

     The Start
     with Docusign menu displays.
2. Search workflows: Enter a full or partial workflow name
   in the field.

   ![A screenshot displays the Search workflows field highlighted in the Start with Docusign action menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/Start_Workflow_Builder_Workflow_Button_Menu_Search_Workflows.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU5MTgsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.8ct1Pedpc5wp9TYMFCXe9IXTZ2btfprF9GvCZynw1tA&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Search workflows field highlighted in the Start with Docusign action menu.")

   Workflows matching your search query display.
3. Select the X icon to close the action menu dialog
   box.

You have completed the process. You have searched for a workflow in
the Start with Docusign action menu.

title: Add the Docusign IAM Launcher Component to a Salesforce Layout
heading_path: Add the Docusign IAM Launcher Component to a Salesforce Layout
text:
Learn to add the Docusign IAM Launcher component to a Salesforce
layout. [Prepare your Salesforce
organization to start Docusign workflows from Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=tsk2a2ae3f7-99c2-4deb-ab15-61fc26ade4d1.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to prepare your Salesforce organization to start Docusign Workflow Builder workflows from Salesforce.").

Important: This feature is only available to IAM for
Sales customers. Contact [Docusign support](https://support.docusign.com/s/?language=en_US&_gl=1*74elk4*_gcl_au*MTY5ODQyMjA2MC4xNzY5NjM1OTA1LjE5ODM3MTQzNjguMTc2OTYzNjIwNC4xNzY5NjM2MjA0) or [Docusign
sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales) for more information.

If you want to start workflows from Salesforce records, you can add the
Docusign IAM Launcher component to your layouts. The Docusign IAM Launcher component displays workflows that you can
start from your records. You can take the following actions from the IAM Launcher
component:

Use this procedure to add the Docusign IAM Launcher component to a
Salesforce layout:

1. As a Salesforce administrator, open a record, select the gear icon, ![A screenshot displays a gear icon with a lightning bolt inside.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU5MTIsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.oLh2dIhweQuAkJCdX7aEU4dnet-yHuN5XsRzFE0tGqQ&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a gear icon with a lightning bolt inside.") and choose Edit Page from the dropdown menu.

   Note: You can add the Docusign IAM
   Launcher component to any part of your layout. The following
   instructions are recommendations, not requirements.
2. (Optional) Add a Docusign IAM Launcher tab to your
   layout.
   1. Select Add Tab on the right navigation
      panel.

      A new Details tab displays.
   2. Select Details.

      The Tab Label dialog box opens.
   3. Tab Label: Select the
      Details dropdown menu and choose
      Custom.
   4. Custom Label: Enter a descriptive name, like
      Docusign IAM Launcher.
   5. Select Done.

   ![A screenshot displays a custom tab with Docusign IAM Launcher in the label field and the Done button highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/IAM_Launcher_component_tab.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU5MTIsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.oLh2dIhweQuAkJCdX7aEU4dnet-yHuN5XsRzFE0tGqQ&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a custom tab with Docusign IAM Launcher in the label field and the Done button highlighted.")

   Your new tab displays in the right navigation panel.
3. Drag the Docusign IAM Launcher component to the tab that
   you created and select Save.

title: Add the Docusign IAM Launcher Component to a Salesforce Layout
heading_path: Add the Docusign IAM Launcher Component to a Salesforce Layout
text:
the label field and the Done button highlighted.")

   Your new tab displays in the right navigation panel.
3. Drag the Docusign IAM Launcher component to the tab that
   you created and select Save.

   ![A screen capture displays dragging the Docusign IAM Launcher component to a Salesforce layout.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/Add_IAM_Launcher_component_to_a_layout.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU5MTIsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.oLh2dIhweQuAkJCdX7aEU4dnet-yHuN5XsRzFE0tGqQ&_LANG=enus "A screen capture displays dragging the Docusign IAM Launcher component to a Salesforce layout.")
4. Activate the change to your layout:
   1. Select Activate.
   2. Select a tab to set your default options: Org
      Default, App Default, or
      App, Record Type, and Profile.
   3. Select Assign and choose options from the dialog
      box.
   4. Select Close.
5. Select the back arrow ![A screenshot displays a blue box with a left-facing arrow icon.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/A-screenshot-of-the-Back-arrow-on-the-Salesforce-Edit-Page-settings-bar.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU5MTIsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.oLh2dIhweQuAkJCdX7aEU4dnet-yHuN5XsRzFE0tGqQ&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a blue box with a left-facing arrow icon.") to return to the record.

You have completed the process. You have added the
Docusign IAM Launcher component to a Salesforce layout. Users
can start Docusign workflows from Salesforce records.

title: Configure a Use Salesforce Files Step
heading_path: Configure a Use Salesforce Files Step
text:
Learn to add and configure a Use Salesforce Files step to add Salesforce files to
workflows.

title: Configure a Use Salesforce Files Step
heading_path: Configure a Use Salesforce Files Step
Before you begin
text:
Important: This feature is only available to IAM for
Sales customers. Contact [Docusign support](https://support.docusign.com/s/?language=en_US&_gl=1*74elk4*_gcl_au*MTY5ODQyMjA2MC4xNzY5NjM1OTA1LjE5ODM3MTQzNjguMTc2OTYzNjIwNC4xNzY5NjM2MjA0) or [Docusign
sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales) for more information.

Before you begin
:   - Required: Installation of the Salesforce app
      from Docusign App Center. Review the [Installing Extension
      Applications for Workflow Builder](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=bgb1697668738892.html&_LANG=enus) topic for more
      information.
    - Required: [Workflows Create
      permissions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gbb1696973048215.html&_LANG=enus) or be an account
      administrator.
    - Required: Create a workflow as described in [Create a Basic Workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uhm1697826856678.html&_LANG=enus). Add
      steps like [invitation](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=kfx1698167344720.html&_LANG=enus),
      [web forms](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gua1698120920620.html&_LANG=enus), [eSignature](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uww1698167288211.html&_LANG=enus) and
      more to gather recipients, documents, and merge fields for your
      workflow.

The Use Salesforce Files step adds Salesforce files
to workflows. Administrators open a record's Notes & Attachments and add
the record's ID to the Use Salesforce Files step. When
workflows start, Docusign adds selected files from that
record's Notes & Attachments. You can configure the following upload settings: All Files, File Name Contains, and Latest Document Only.

Example: You add a Use Salesforce Files
step to your workflow. You use the File Name Contains option
and enter the keywords `Contract Renewal`. Your workflow sends
an agreement to recipients. When recipients open their workflow task, they see a
file named Contract Renewal 2025 included in the agreement.

Use this procedure to configure a Use Salesforce Files step for
your workflow:

1. Add a Use Salesforce Files step as a Docusign administrator:
   1. Go to Agreements > Workflows and select Edit next to a workflow
      name.

      The workflow editor page displays.
   2. Select the Add a step
      ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/Workflow_Builder_add_step_plus_sign.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4NzQsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.xKyQaDseAitrUibSazlMsDSlbwyFdPxsvOQCOR4AeG8&_LANG=enus) icon.

      The Add New Step dialog box displays.
   3. Search for steps: Enter and select `Use
      Salesforce Files`.

      The workflow editor page displays with the Use Salesforce Files
      step added.
2. Name your Use Salesforce Files step:
   1. Select Configure on the Use Salesforce Files
      step.

title: Configure a Use Salesforce Files Step
heading_path: Configure a Use Salesforce Files Step
Before you begin
text:
Files`.

      The workflow editor page displays with the Use Salesforce Files
      step added.
2. Name your Use Salesforce Files step:
   1. Select Configure on the Use Salesforce Files
      step.

      The configuration dialog box displays.
   2. Step Name: Keep or edit the name. It is helpful
      to add the name of the Salesforce object, like `Account - Use
      Salesforce Files`.
3. Where are these files located?
   1. Select connection: Select the field and choose a
      Salesforce organization that you connected to Docusign. Multiple options display if you connect more than one Salesforce
      organization.
   2. (Optional) Add New Connection: Select this
      button if you want to [connect more than one Salesforce
      organization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=ogk1719256147008.html&_LANG=enus).
   3. (Optional) Manage Connections: Select this
      button if you want to [add, edit, refresh, or delete a
      Salesforce connection](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=uaw1719259450391.html&_LANG=enus).
   4. Select Record ID: Select a field from the start
      step or another step in this workflow:

      - Manual entry: Add text for your new
        variable and select Save.

        Note: The name that you enter must exactly
        match an existing field. For example, the rule fails if you
        enter CompanyName for the
        Company\_Name
        variable.
      - Select a field from the start step or another step in this
        workflow:

        Note: Steps like Read
        from HubSpot and Read from
        Salesforce display fields from up to five
        records. If more than five records are present, Salesforce
        logic decides which five to display. Selecting fields from
        multiple records helps you automate processes like mapping
        multiple Salesforce contacts to multiple Docusign participants.![A screenshot displays Read from Salesforce fields highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_Salesforce_Read_Workflow_field_selections.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4NzQsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.xKyQaDseAitrUibSazlMsDSlbwyFdPxsvOQCOR4AeG8&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays Read from Salesforce fields highlighted.")

        You can use the fields that you select here in
        later steps like Collect Data with
        Webforms or Send Documents for
        Signature.
   5. Select Next.

      ![Screenshot showing a connection highlighted for a Salesforce connection and a record ID path.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_Salesforce_Use_Salesforce_Files_Where_are_these_files_located.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4NzQsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.xKyQaDseAitrUibSazlMsDSlbwyFdPxsvOQCOR4AeG8&_LANG=enus "Screenshot showing a connection highlighted for a Salesforce connection and a record ID path.")
4. Which files do you want to import?

   Note: You can import a maximum of ten files or 100 MB.

   Filter files by: Select an option:
   - All Files: Upload all documents from the
     record.
   - File Name Contains: Upload documents that match
     file name keywords.
   - Latest Document Only: Upload the most recently
     added document from the record.
5. Select Apply.

title: Configure a Use Salesforce Files Step
heading_path: Configure a Use Salesforce Files Step
Before you begin
text:
the
     record.
   - File Name Contains: Upload documents that match
     file name keywords.
   - Latest Document Only: Upload the most recently
     added document from the record.
5. Select Apply.

   ![A screenshot displays file import filters for name and a notice about the ten file import limit.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_Salesforce_Use_Salesforce_Files_Which_files_do_you_want_to_import.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4NzQsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.xKyQaDseAitrUibSazlMsDSlbwyFdPxsvOQCOR4AeG8&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays file import filters for name and a notice about the ten file import limit.")

   The Use Salesforce Files dialog box closes. The page displays your new step.

You have completed the process. You have built a Use Salesforce
Files step in your workflow. When a workflow starts this step, Docusign adds the most recent file from the specified record.

title: Add a Workflow to the Start with Docusign Action Menu
heading_path: Add a Workflow to the Start with Docusign Action Menu
text: Learn to add a workflow to the Start with Docusign action menu.

title: Add a Workflow to the Start with Docusign Action Menu
heading_path: Add a Workflow to the Start with Docusign Action Menu
Before you begin
text:
Important: This feature is only available to IAM for
Sales customers. Contact [Docusign support](https://support.docusign.com/s/?language=en_US&_gl=1*74elk4*_gcl_au*MTY5ODQyMjA2MC4xNzY5NjM1OTA1LjE5ODM3MTQzNjguMTc2OTYzNjIwNC4xNzY5NjM2MjA0) or [Docusign
sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales) for more information.

[Add a Start with Docusign action to a layout](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=tskb637a1ca-b7ba-4604-97f7-f5f2d5f899f6.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to add a Start with Docusign action to a page layout to start workflows.").

If you want to start workflows from Salesforce records, you can add a Start
with Docusign action to your layouts. The action displays workflows
that you can start from your records. You can add and remove the workflows that
display in the action menu. You can also search for workflows in the action
menu.

Only administrators can add workflows to the action menu.

Use this procedure to add a workflow tile to the Start with Docusign action menu:

1. Choose your access method:
   - From the Docusign Setup page:
     1. [Access the Docusign Setup
        page as a Salesforce administrator](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=etv1586134206174.html&_LANG=enus)
     2. Manage Connections and the IAM Launcher: Select
        Manage.
     3. Select the IAM Launcher Setup tab.
     4. Select the Manage button next to a layout
        (Account, Opportunity, and so on.)

        The Start with Docusign menu displays.
   - From a Salesforce record: Select the Start with
     Docusign action in the upper right of the record.

     The Start
     with Docusign menu displays.
2. Select Add Workflow.

   ![A screenshot displays a list of Docusign workflows displayed in the Start with Docusign action menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/Start_Workflow_Builder_Workflow_Button_Add_a_Workflow.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU5MjIsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.apuVK9dmivkefC_up-Keq3gSVN7dNiBHXlRObyIweB8&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a list of Docusign workflows displayed in the Start with Docusign action menu.")

   The Add workflows dialog box displays.
3. Add a workflow to the action menu:
   1. Select the check box next to each workflow that you want to add.
   2. (Optional) Use the search field to find more workflows.
   3. (Optional) Filter which workflows display:

      1. Select Created by.
      2. Select Me or Not
         Me.
      3. Select Apply.
   4. Select Add Selected.

      The Add workflows dialog box closes. A message displays:
      (number of workflows) added. The workflow that
      you added displays in the Start with Docusign action
      menu.
4. Select the X icon to close the action menu dialog
   box.

You have completed the process. You have added a workflow to the Start with Docusign action menu. Users can start workflows from Salesforce
records.

title: Add Docusign Workflow Actions to Layouts (IAM Launcher Setup Controls)
heading_path: Add Docusign Workflow Actions to Layouts (IAM Launcher Setup Controls)
text:
Learn to use the IAM Launcher Setup interface to add Docusign workflow
actions to Salesforce layouts.

title: Add Docusign Workflow Actions to Layouts (IAM Launcher Setup Controls)
heading_path: Add Docusign Workflow Actions to Layouts (IAM Launcher Setup Controls)
Before you begin
text:
Important: You cannot use the IAM Launcher Setup
controls to add actions to some Salesforce objects, like Case. If you can't add an action
with the IAM Launcher Setup controls, [add actions
with Salesforce Object Manager](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=rrf1583359212854&topicId=fji1583359148678.html&_LANG=enus).

If you want to start
Docusign workflows from Salesforce records, you can add a
Start with Docusign action to your layouts. The Start with
Docusign action displays Docusign workflows
that users can start from records without logging on to Docusign.

Use this procedure to add a Start with Docusign action to a
layout with the IAM Launcher Setup controls:

1. Manage Connections and the IAM Launcher: Select Manage.

   The docusign app center dialog box displays.
2. Add an IAM Launcher action to a Salesforce layout:
   1. Select the IAM Launcher Setup tab.
   2. Select a connection: Choose a connection from the dropdown menu. You can only choose shared connections.

      ![A screenshot displays the IAM Launcher Setup tab and Connection menu highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/IAM_for_Sales_IAM_Launcher_Setup_tab.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU5ODEsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.ZQ46piEjeAWcMk5Ra0G3PmClh77HjHud45jCujUsBAM&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the IAM Launcher Setup tab and Connection menu highlighted.")

      The Create IAM Launcher section displays.
   3. Select Create.

      The Button Options section displays.
   4. Object: Enter an object name or select one from the dropdown menu.
   5. Account Layouts (optional): Select check boxes to add the action to specific layouts.
   6. Select Create.

      ![A screenshot displays the Button Options page with the Account object selected.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/IAM_for_Sales_IAM_Launcher_Button_Options_object.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU5ODEsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.ZQ46piEjeAWcMk5Ra0G3PmClh77HjHud45jCujUsBAM&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Button Options page with the Account object selected.")
3. Repeat this process for each layout to which you want to add Docusign workflow actions.

You have completed the process. You have created a Start with Docusign action and added it to a layout. Users can start workflows without logging on to Docusign.

title: Remove a Workflow from the Start with Docusign Action Menu
heading_path: Remove a Workflow from the Start with Docusign Action Menu
text:
Learn to remove a Docusign workflow from a Start with Docusign action
menu.

title: Remove a Workflow from the Start with Docusign Action Menu
heading_path: Remove a Workflow from the Start with Docusign Action Menu
Before you begin
text:
Important: This feature is only available to IAM for
Sales customers. Contact [Docusign support](https://support.docusign.com/s/?language=en_US&_gl=1*74elk4*_gcl_au*MTY5ODQyMjA2MC4xNzY5NjM1OTA1LjE5ODM3MTQzNjguMTc2OTYzNjIwNC4xNzY5NjM2MjA0) or [Docusign
sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales) for more information.

[Add a Start with Docusign action to a layout](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=tskb637a1ca-b7ba-4604-97f7-f5f2d5f899f6.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to add a Start with Docusign action to a page layout to start workflows.").

If you want to start Docusign workflows from Salesforce records,
you can add a Start with Docusign action to your layouts. The
action displays Docusign workflows that you can start from your
records. You can add and remove the workflows that display in the action menu. You
can also search for workflows to add to the action menu.

Only administrators can remove workflows from the action menu.

Use this procedure to remove a workflow from a Start with Docusign
action menu:

1. Choose your access method:
   - From the Docusign Setup page:
     1. [Access the Docusign Setup
        page as a Salesforce administrator](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=etv1586134206174.html&_LANG=enus)
     2. Manage Connections and the IAM Launcher: Select
        Manage.
     3. Select the IAM Launcher Setup tab.
     4. Select the Manage button next to a layout
        (Account, Opportunity, and so on.)

        The Start with Docusign menu displays.
   - From a Salesforce record: Select the Start with
     Docusign action in the upper right of the record.

     The Start
     with Docusign menu displays.
2. Select the three-dot menu ![A screenshot displays three dots placed in a horizontal line.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/IAM_Launcher_component_three-dot_menu.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU5MjQsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.iZh1IO9TRBULM6XCphsDL3X7RpbOK7FhviBvjsDrnTA&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays three dots placed in a horizontal line.") next to a workflow and choose Remove this
   workflow from the dropdown menu.

   ![A screenshot displays the three-dot menu and the Remove this workflow option highlighted in the Start with Docusign action menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_Salesforce_action_menu_Remove_this_workflow.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU5MjQsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.iZh1IO9TRBULM6XCphsDL3X7RpbOK7FhviBvjsDrnTA&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the three-dot menu and the Remove this workflow option highlighted in the Start with Docusign action menu.")The Remove workflow dialog box displays.
3. Select Remove

   Note: Docusign removes the workflow tile
   from the menu, but does not delete the workflow. You can re-add the workflow
   tile to the menu later.

   The Remove workflow dialog box closes. A message displays:
   (name of workflow) removed.
4. Select the X icon to close the action menu dialog
   box.

You have completed the process. You have removed a workflow from the
Start with Docusign action menu.

title: Add Docusign Workflow Actions to Layouts (Manual Controls)
heading_path: Add Docusign Workflow Actions to Layouts (Manual Controls)
text:
Learn to add a Start with Docusign action to a page layout to start
workflows.

title: Add Docusign Workflow Actions to Layouts (Manual Controls)
heading_path: Add Docusign Workflow Actions to Layouts (Manual Controls)
Before you begin
text:
Important:

- This feature is only available to IAM for Sales customers. Contact [Docusign support](https://support.docusign.com/s/?language=en_US&_gl=1*74elk4*_gcl_au*MTY5ODQyMjA2MC4xNzY5NjM1OTA1LjE5ODM3MTQzNjguMTc2OTYzNjIwNC4xNzY5NjM2MjA0) or [Docusign
  sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales) for more information.
- Docusign recommends using the [IAM Launcher Setup Controls](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=tska9d68585-f524-49a4-8898-496b6575d036.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to use the IAM Launcher Setup interface to add Docusign workflow actions to Salesforce layouts.") to add workflow actions. Use these
  instructions if the IAM Launcher Setup controls fail.

[Build a Start with Docusign
action](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=tsk32e505bf-436a-47b1-b7f6-55e6ad614ec5.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to build a new Lightning web component action to start a Docusign workflow from a record.").

If you want to start Docusign workflows from Salesforce records,
you can add a Start with Docusign action to your layouts. The
Start with Docusign action displays workflows that you can start
from your records. Users can start workflows from a record without logging on to
your Docusign account.

Use this procedure to add a Start with Docusign action to a
layout:

1. Access an object's page layout as a Salesforce administrator:
   1. Select the gear icon ![A screenshot displays a six-point gear with a lightning bolt inside it.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/salesforce-gear_.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU5MTAsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.UireTo91oaBuB3wKcK75hq3FcEMC61HUlIg9G68FACo&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a six-point gear with a lightning bolt inside it.") and choose Setup.
   2. Enter and select Object Manager in the Quick Find
      field.
   3. Select a Salesforce object in the left column and select
      Page Layouts on the Details page.
   4. Select a layout from the Page Layouts
      column.

   The object's page layout displays.
2. Find the Start with Docusign action:
   1. Select Mobile & Lightning Actions in the
      upper left of the layout menu.
   2. Salesforce Mobile and Lightning Experience
      Actions: Select the override the predefined
      actions link. If the link does not display, you do not
      need to take this action.

      ![A screenshot displays the Salesforce Mobile and Lightning actions section with the override the predefined actions link highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/Mobile_and_Lightning_actions_override_the_predefined_actions_link.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU5MTAsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.UireTo91oaBuB3wKcK75hq3FcEMC61HUlIg9G68FACo&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Salesforce Mobile and Lightning actions section with the override the predefined actions link highlighted.")
   3. Enter Start with Docusign in the Quick
      Find field.
3. Add the Start with Docusign action to the page layout:
   1. Drag the Start with Docusign action to the
      Salesforce Mobile and Lightning Experience
      Actions section.
   2. Select Save.

title: Add Docusign Workflow Actions to Layouts (Manual Controls)
heading_path: Add Docusign Workflow Actions to Layouts (Manual Controls)
Before you begin
text:
d.
3. Add the Start with Docusign action to the page layout:
   1. Drag the Start with Docusign action to the
      Salesforce Mobile and Lightning Experience
      Actions section.
   2. Select Save.

   ![A screen capture displays the process of dragging the Start with Docusign action to the Salesforce Mobile and Lightning Experience Actions section.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/Add_Start_with_Docusign_button_to_layout.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU5MTAsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.UireTo91oaBuB3wKcK75hq3FcEMC61HUlIg9G68FACo&_LANG=enus "A screen capture displays the process of dragging the Start with Docusign action to the Salesforce Mobile and Lightning Experience Actions section.")

   The Saving Layout message displays. The Page
   Layouts page opens.

You have completed the process. You have added a Start with Docusign
action to a Salesforce layout.

title: Add a List of Participants to a Salesforce Workflow Step
heading_path: Add a List of Participants to a Salesforce Workflow Step
text: Learn to add a list of participants to a Salesforce workflow step.

title: Add a List of Participants to a Salesforce Workflow Step
heading_path: Add a List of Participants to a Salesforce Workflow Step
Before you begin
text:
- Required: Installation of the Salesforce app from Docusign App Center. Review the [Installing Extension Applications for
  Workflow Builder](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=bgb1697668738892.html&_LANG=enus)
  topic for more information.
- Required: [Workflows Create permissions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gbb1696973048215.html&_LANG=enus) or be an account
  administrator.
- Required: Create a workflow as described in [Create a Basic Workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uhm1697826856678.html&_LANG=enus). Add steps like
  [invitation](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=kfx1698167344720.html&_LANG=enus), [web forms](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gua1698120920620.html&_LANG=enus), [eSignature](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uww1698167288211.html&_LANG=enus) and more to gather recipients,
  documents, and merge fields for your workflow.

You can dynamically map recipients from a Read from Salesforce
step to a Send Documents for Signature step. Docusign can import a list of contacts related to a Salesforce
record and add them as envelope recipients. Senders are not required to enter
recipient names and contact information manually.

You can use the following related list objects to add agreement recipients:

| Parent Object | Related List (Child) | Common use Case |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Account | Contacts | Map multiple contacts as agreement signers |
| Opportunity | Opportunity Contact Role >  Contact | Map contact roles (for example, buyer, approver) as participants |
| Opportunity | Opportunity Line Items | Read multiple product lines |

The following logic applies to creating Salesforce participant lists:

- The Read from Salesforce step adds up to five related records per parent object.
  If more than five records exist, Salesforce logic decides which five records to
  add.
- You can only map participant lists in the Read from Salesforce step. You cannot
  map participant lists in Writeback to Salesforce or other Salesforce steps.
- You can map up to 10 participant fields per related object, like Full Name,
  Email, and so on.
- When you select a related list object, Docusign reads fields across multiple
  objects, like Opportunity >  Opportunity Contact Role >  Contact.

Example: You want to create a workflow that
automatically sends an NDA agreement to multiple Salesforce contacts. You add a Read
from Salesforce step to your workflow. You create a Salesforce Contacts participant
list by mapping the following Salesforce and Docusign fields:

| Salesforce field | Docusign field |
| --- | --- |
| Account / Contacts / Department | participantRoleName |
| Account / Contacts / Full Name | participantName |
| Account / Contacts / Email | participantEmail |
| Account / Contacts / Business Phone | participantPhone |

You add a Send Documents for Signature step to your workflow.
You choose the Add dynamic list of participants option and
add the Salesforce Contacts list. You assign a recipient role of
`Signer` to the list.

A user starts a workflow from a Salesforce account with five contacts.

Result: Docusign creates an agreement for all
five contacts, with signature fields for each contact's department, full name,
email, and business phone. Docusign sends the NDA to all contacts
automatically, with no manual entry needed.

Use this procedure to add a list of participants to a workflow:

title: Add a List of Participants to a Salesforce Workflow Step
heading_path: Add a List of Participants to a Salesforce Workflow Step
Before you begin
text:
's department, full name,
email, and business phone. Docusign sends the NDA to all contacts
automatically, with no manual entry needed.

Use this procedure to add a list of participants to a workflow:

1. [Create a basic workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uhm1697826856678.html&_LANG=enus) containing [Read from Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=idr1713985144297.html&_LANG=enus) and [Send Documents for Signature](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uww1698167288211.html&_LANG=enus) steps.
2. Configure your Read from Salesforce step:
   1. Open the step to the Which fields would you like to
      read? section.
   2. Select Add or Remove Fields.

      The Add or Remove Fields page displays.
   3. Select the Related Lists tab on the left.
   4. Select the check box next to each Salesforce related list field that
      you'd like to use.
   5. Select Save and Exit.

      ![A screenshot displays the Add or remove fields section with Salesforce field check boxes highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/Read_from_Salesforce_Related_Lists_Contacts_fields_selected.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4NTIsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.tlsaIN6UCIEk25jQCqN9XgKED_RIMMcyfL4UffdWqbk&_LANG=enus)

      The Add or Remove Fields dialog box closes.
      The related list object that you selected displays.
   6. Select the Map button next to the related
      list.

      ![A screenshot displays the Which fields would you like to read section with a Map button highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/Read_from_Salesforce_Related_Lists_Map_Contacts_button.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4NTIsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.tlsaIN6UCIEk25jQCqN9XgKED_RIMMcyfL4UffdWqbk&_LANG=enus)

      The Map to Docusign Object dialog box
      displays.
3. Map your Salesforce and Docusign objects and fields:
   1. Docusign object: Select a Docusign object from the dropdown menu.

      The Map Salesforce fields to your Participant
      object section expands.
   2. Salesforce fields: Select a Salesforce field to
      match with each Docusign field.
   3. Repeat this process for each displayed field.
   4. Select Save Mapping.

      ![A screenshot displays the Map to Docusign object dialog box with a Docusign object and fields highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/Read_from_Salesforce_Map_to_Docusign_Object_dialog_box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4NTIsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.tlsaIN6UCIEk25jQCqN9XgKED_RIMMcyfL4UffdWqbk&_LANG=enus)

      The Map to Docusign Object dialog box closes.
   5. Select Next.
   6. Complete the rest of your Read from Salesforce step and select
      Apply.
4. Configure your Send Documents for Signature step:
   1. Open the step to the Confirm recipient details
      section.
   2. Match the template's [name] recipient to one of this
      workflow's participants: Select Add
      Participant from the dropdown menu.
   3. Select Add participant.

      ![A screenshot displays the Confirm recipient details section with the Select and Add Participant fields highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/Read_from_Salesforce_Related_Lists_Add_Participant.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4NTIsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.tlsaIN6UCIEk25jQCqN9XgKED_RIMMcyfL4UffdWqbk&_LANG=enus)

      The Add Participant dialog box displays.

title: Add a List of Participants to a Salesforce Workflow Step
heading_path: Add a List of Participants to a Salesforce Workflow Step
Before you begin
text:
b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4NTIsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.tlsaIN6UCIEk25jQCqN9XgKED_RIMMcyfL4UffdWqbk&_LANG=enus)

      The Add Participant dialog box displays.

      Note: A participant is a placeholder for someone who
      will take action at specific steps in your workflow. Docusign uses the participant information to
      assign that role to a person when the workflow
      runs.
   4. Add a dynamic list of participants: Select the
      switch icon to turn it on.

      The switch moves to the right.
   5. Dynamic list: Choose the dynamic recipients list
      from your Read from Salesforce step.

      ![A screenshot displays the Add Participant dialog box with a Salesforce Dynamic Recipients list highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/Read_from_Salesforce_Dynamic_Recipients_Add_Participant_dialog_box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4NTIsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.tlsaIN6UCIEk25jQCqN9XgKED_RIMMcyfL4UffdWqbk&_LANG=enus)
   6. Role: Enter a role name for your recipients
      list, like `Approver`.

      Tip:
      - Use unique role names: Make sure the role names that you’re bringing in
        from Salesforce are unique. If there are multiple roles with
        the same name in your incoming list, Docusign only imports the first one.
      - Use the same role name in your source system and
        eSignature template: Make sure the role names
        from Salesforce match the recipient roles in your eSignature
        template. If they don’t, the workflow will likely fail.
   7. Select Confirm Role.

      ![A screenshot displays the Add Participant dialog box with a Salesforce Dynamic Recipients list and a Role highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/Send_Documents_for_Signature_step_add_dynamic_Read_from_Salesforce_list.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4NTIsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.tlsaIN6UCIEk25jQCqN9XgKED_RIMMcyfL4UffdWqbk&_LANG=enus)

      The Additional guidance dialog box displays. Select
      OK to close the dialog box.
   8. Select Add.

      The Add Participant dialog box closes.
   9. Select Next.
5. Complete the rest of your Send Documents for Signature step and select
   Apply.

You have completed the process. You have added a list of participants to your workflow. When
the workflow starts, Docusign creates an agreement and adds all mapped
contacts and fields.

title: Start a Docusign Workflow from a Salesforce Record
heading_path: Start a Docusign Workflow from a Salesforce Record
text: Learn to start a Docusign workflow from a Start with Docusign action.

title: Start a Docusign Workflow from a Salesforce Record
heading_path: Start a Docusign Workflow from a Salesforce Record
Before you begin
text:
Important: This feature is only available to IAM for
Sales customers. Contact [Docusign support](https://support.docusign.com/s/?language=en_US&_gl=1*74elk4*_gcl_au*MTY5ODQyMjA2MC4xNzY5NjM1OTA1LjE5ODM3MTQzNjguMTc2OTYzNjIwNC4xNzY5NjM2MjA0) or [Docusign
sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales) for more information.

You can use the Start with Docusign action to start workflows
from Salesforce records.

Both administrators and standard users can start workflows from the action menu.

Use this procedure to start a workflow from a Start with Docusign
action:

Note: The action menu only displays workflows that
you can use on that record.

1. Open a record containing a Start with Docusign action.
2. Select the Start with Docusign action in the upper
   right of the record.

   The Start with Docusign action menu displays.
3. Use the action menu to search for a workflow:
   1. Enter a full or partial workflow name in the Search
      workflows field.

      ![A screenshot displays the Search workflows field highlighted in the Start with Docusign action menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/Start_Workflow_Builder_Workflow_Button_Menu_Search_Workflows.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU5MjcsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.X6bgKsIDECv4-4HGo_Dz3gG6uoecmgje2l5Su9rdXZc&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Search workflows field highlighted in the Start with Docusign action menu.")

      Workflows matching your search query display.
   2. Select Start next to a workflow name.

      A message displays: Your workflow was
      initiated.
   3. Select the X button in the upper right to close
      the Docusign dialog box.

   The Docusign dialog box closes. The Salesforce record
   displays.

You have completed the process. You have started a workflow from a
Start with Docusign action. Docusign sends tasks to
all workflow recipients.

title: Build a Workflow to Be Triggered By a Start with Docusign Action
heading_path: Build a Workflow to Be Triggered By a Start with Docusign Action
text: Learn to build a workflow that can be triggered by a Start with Docusign action.

title: Build a Workflow to Be Triggered By a Start with Docusign Action
heading_path: Build a Workflow to Be Triggered By a Start with Docusign Action
Before you begin
text:
Important: This feature is only available to IAM for
Sales customers. Contact [Docusign support](https://support.docusign.com/s/?language=en_US&_gl=1*74elk4*_gcl_au*MTY5ODQyMjA2MC4xNzY5NjM1OTA1LjE5ODM3MTQzNjguMTc2OTYzNjIwNC4xNzY5NjM2MjA0) or [Docusign
sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales) for more information.

Required: [Workflows Create permissions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gbb1696973048215.html&_LANG=enus) or be an account
administrator.

You can use Workflow Builder to automate agreement actions from
your Docusign account.

Docusign Apps Launcher is a package of Docusign
apps that you can use in your Salesforce organization. These apps include Docusign eSignature for Salesforce, Docusign Gen
for Salesforce, and Docusign CLM for Salesforce.

You cannot build Workflow Builder workflows in the Apps Launcher user interface. However, you
can start Workflow Builder workflows from Salesforce records to create agreements with
Salesforce and Docusign data.

Before you can start workflows from Salesforce, you must create a compatible
workflow.

Use this procedure to build a Workflow Builder workflow that can be started from
Salesforce:

1. As a Docusign administrator, create your workflow:
   1. Log on to your Docusign account and go to Agreements >  Workflows.
   2. Select Create Workflow.

      The Creating your workflow dialog box
      displays.
   3. Select New Canvas and select
      Continue.

      The Creating your workflow dialog box
      closes. The workflow builder page displays.
   4. Select Add workflow start.

      The Add workflow start dialog box displays.
2. Select a method: Select either From a Link or From an API
   Call from the dropdown menu and select
   Next.

   Note: You cannot select other methods when building workflows that start in Salesforce.

   ![A screenshot displays the What starts this workflow step with From a Link and From an API Call highlighted in green.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/What-starts-this-workflow-with-From-a-link-and-From-an-API-Call-highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU5MDUsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.IwmK-3Bpm6pxWyuq_tY4KZYvzSuPru91pKtVQMaKKnk&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the What starts this workflow step with From a Link and From an API Call highlighted in green.")
3. Set up variables for dynamic information: Add the following variables:
   1. Select New Variable and choose
      Text from the dropdown menu.
   2. Enter `recordId` in the field.
   3. Create another text variable and enter `objectName` in the field.
   4. Create another text variable and enter `applicationName` in the field.
   5. Create another text variable and enter `orgId` in the field.

      ![A screenshot displays the Set Up Variables step with recordId, objectName, applicationName, and orgId variables highlighted in green.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/Start_Workflow_Builder_workflows_from_Salesforce_Set_up_variables_for_dynamic_information.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU5MDUsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.IwmK-3Bpm6pxWyuq_tY4KZYvzSuPru91pKtVQMaKKnk&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Set Up Variables step with recordId, objectName, applicationName, and orgId variables highlighted in green.")
4. Will this API call be started by a human or an automated
   process?:
   1. Select Human.
   2. Select Apply.

   The page refreshes. Your From an API call start
   step displays.
5. Complete your workflow:
   1. Add more steps to your workflow.
   2. Select Review & Publish when you have
      finished building your workflow.

heading_path: Build a Workflow to Be Triggered By a Start with Docusign Action
Before you begin

   step displays.
5. Complete your workflow:
   1. Add more steps to your workflow.
   2. Select Review & Publish when you have

You have completed the process. You have built a workflow that can
be triggered by a Send with Docusign action. When a user selects a
