title: Refresh the Salesforce Connection to Your Docusign Account
heading_path: Refresh the Salesforce Connection to Your Docusign Account
text:
Learn to refresh the connection between Salesforce and Docusign after
you add fields or objects to your Salesforce organization.

You can connect Salesforce organizations to your Docusign IAM account. When you create a connection, you can build workflows that
read from and write to your Salesforce records. If you add Salesforce fields or
objects, perform a refresh so that the Salesforce app can access the new
content.

You can access the refresh controls through your Salesforce organization or your Docusign account.

Use this procedure to refresh the Salesforce content available to the app:

1. Choose your login method:
   - Login from Salesforce:
     1. [Access the Docusign Setup
        page as a Salesforce administrator](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=etv1586134206174.html&_LANG=enus)
     2. Manage Connections and the IAM Launcher: Select
        Manage.
   - Login from Docusign:
     1. Open the [Production](https://apps.docusign.com/app-center/extensionapps) or [Demo](https://apps-d.docusign.com/app-center/extensionapps) App Center site.
     2. Select the Salesforce tile.
     3. Select Manage and choose Manage
        Connections.
2. Select the three-dot menu icon ![A screenshot displays three dots stacked vertically.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/A-screenshot-diplays-a-threedot-menu-icon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU5NTIsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.dplFb0yCk4UxCvf1m19U1ZLnLLuyR4wfK1DqaFd55dA&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays three dots stacked vertically.") next to a connection and choose Reload
   Objects.

   A dialog box displays: Refresh this
   connection?
3. Refresh this connection?: Enter `REFRESH` in the field and select Refresh Connection. ![A screenshot displays the Refresh this connection dialog box with the word Refresh and the Refresh Connection button highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/Workflow_Builder_Refresh_Connection_dialog_box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU5NTIsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.dplFb0yCk4UxCvf1m19U1ZLnLLuyR4wfK1DqaFd55dA&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Refresh this connection dialog box with the word Refresh and the Refresh Connection button highlighted.")

   The dialog box closes. The Connections page displays with a message: Successfully refreshed.

You have completed the process. You refreshed the connection to your
Docusign IAM account. New Salesforce fields or objects are now available for use in
Docusign workflows.

title: Use Docusign Connect to Link Salesforce Fields to Docusign Fields
heading_path: Use Docusign Connect to Link Salesforce Fields to Docusign Fields
text: Learn to map Salesforce objects to Docusign fields with Docusign Connect.

title: Use Docusign Connect to Link Salesforce Fields to Docusign Fields
heading_path: Use Docusign Connect to Link Salesforce Fields to Docusign Fields
Before you begin
text:
[Create a Connect Configuration to Add Salesforce Merge Fields to a Workflow Step](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=xza1742958339120.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create a Connect configuration to add Salesforce merge fields to your workflow's Send Documents for Signature step.")

You can use Docusign Connect to link Salesforce fields to Docusign fields. When recipients complete and sign agreements, Docusign updates Salesforce fields you linked with Docusign fields.

Use this procedure to link Salesforce fields to Docusign fields
with Connect:

1. Log in to your Docusign account as an administrator and go to Admin >  INTEGRATIONS >  Connect.
2. Select the Actions button next to your Salesforce
   configuration and choose Edit from the dropdown
   menu.
3. Connect Objects: Select New Object
   and choose Salesforce Object from the dropdown
   menu.

   The Salesforce Object Connect page opens.
4. Map a Salesforce object to your Docusign account:
   1. Inactive Object: Select Activate
      Object from the dropdown menu.
   2. Object Name: Enter a name for your
      connection.
   3. (Optional) Object Description: Enter a
      description that explains details of the connection.
   4. On Completed Envelope Only: Only update
      Salesforce records when all recipients complete the signing process.
   5. Add if No Match: Add data to Salesforce if it
      does not exist.
   6. Select Salesforce Object: Choose a Salesforce
      object from the dropdown menu.

      ![A screenshot displays the General Settings section, with Inactive Object and Select Salesforce Object highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-General-Settings-section-with-Inactive-Object-and-Select-Salesforce-Object-highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4MjUsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.3nQCVtxau6tZlTMpAb-5jbsM4dsPUjHchMhx0l3pPbo&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the General Settings section, with Inactive Object and Select Salesforce Object highlighted.")
5. Select Where: Select the fields that are used to match a
   Salesforce object with Docusign information:
   1. Select Docusign Field: Select a field from your
      Docusign account to match with a Salesforce
      field.
   2. Select Salesforce Field: Select a Salesforce
      field to match with a field from your Docusign
      account.
   3. (Optional) + Add Item: Connect more Docusign and Salesforce fields.

      ![A screenshot displays the Docusign Recipient ID field and the Salesforce Owner ID field highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-Docusign-Recipient-ID-field-and-the-Salesforce-Owner-ID-field-highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4MjUsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.3nQCVtxau6tZlTMpAb-5jbsM4dsPUjHchMhx0l3pPbo&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Docusign Recipient ID field and the Salesforce Owner ID field highlighted.")
6. Update Fields: Choose the Docusign
   fields that write back information to Salesforce fields:
   1. Select Docusign Field: Select a field from your
      Docusign account to update with a Salesforce
      field.
   2. Select Salesforce Field: Select a Salesforce field to be
      updated by your Docusign field.
   3. (Optional) + Add Item: Connect more Docusign and Salesforce fields.

title: Use Docusign Connect to Link Salesforce Fields to Docusign Fields
heading_path: Use Docusign Connect to Link Salesforce Fields to Docusign Fields
Before you begin
text:
a Salesforce
      field.
   2. Select Salesforce Field: Select a Salesforce field to be
      updated by your Docusign field.
   3. (Optional) + Add Item: Connect more Docusign and Salesforce fields.

      ![A screenshot displays the Update Fields section with the Docusign Recipient ID field and the Salesforce Owner ID field highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-Update-Fields-section-with-Docusign-Recipient-ID-field-and-the-Salesforce-Owner-ID-field-highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4MjUsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.3nQCVtxau6tZlTMpAb-5jbsM4dsPUjHchMhx0l3pPbo&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Update Fields section with the Docusign Recipient ID field and the Salesforce Owner ID field highlighted.")
7. (Optional) Attachments: Choose how completed agreements
   save to Salesforce records:
   1. Attach Docusign Envelopes: Choose this option to
      save completed agreements to Salesforce records.
   2. Attach Docusign Certificate: Choose this option
      to save Docusign certificates of completion to
      Salesforce records.
8. Select Save.

You have completed the process. You have linked Salesforce fields
to Docusign fields. When recipients complete and sign agreements, Docusign updates Salesforce fields you linked with Docusign fields.

title: Check the Status of a Flow That Failed to Start a Docusign Workflow
heading_path: Check the Status of a Flow That Failed to Start a Docusign Workflow
text:
Learn to check the status of a flow that failed to start a workflow.

Salesforce administrators can build a Start Docusign Workflow Builder
workflow step to link Salesforce flows with workflows. When a flow starts, Salesforce starts the
linked workflow. Linking flows with workflows builds an automation chain that
creates agreements from Salesforce and Docusign data.

If you want to view the status of a workflow, you can [check the workflow process in your Docusign account](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=vcp1698707326210.html&_LANG=enus).

If a Flow fails to start your workflow, it does not display in your Docusign account. You can only view the logs in Salesforce.

Use this procedure to check the status of a flow that failed to start a workflow:

1. Select the Docusign Logs tab.

   The Docusign Logs page opens.
2. Select a log from the Docusign Log column.

   The log details page opens for that item.

You have completed the task. You viewed the details of a flow that
failed to start a workflow.

title: "This Template Is Not Supported" Displays When I Configure a Send Documents for Signature Step
heading_path: "This Template Is Not Supported" Displays When I Configure a Send Documents for Signature Step
text:
Learn to fix the "This template is not supported" error in a Workflow Builder Send Documents for Signature
step.

I added a Salesforce field to my workflow's Send Documents for Signature
step. When I selected Next, an error displayed:
This template is not supported. I cannot save my Send
Documents for Signature step and finish building my template.

title: "This Template Is Not Supported" Displays When I Configure a Send Documents for Signature Step
heading_path: "This Template Is Not Supported" Displays When I Configure a Send Documents for Signature Step
Resolution: Delete Salesforce Fields That Are Not from Your Source Object
text:
Cause: Your workflow
connects to a Docusign template containing fields from
multiple Salesforce objects. Workflow Builder can only use
templates containing fields from a single Salesforce source object (Opportunity,
Account, and so on.)

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. Log on to your Docusign account as an administrator
   and go to Templates >  My Templates.
2. Open your template's Place Fields page:
   1. Select the more actions menu next to your template ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-use-button-with-a-three-dot-menu-next-to-it.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU5NjksInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.rs3Yoez1JfBR_1oIXZBQyz9btwab9b3GC4RQvhEUX1M&_LANG=enus) and choose Edit from the
      dropdown menu.
   2. Select Next.

   The template's Place Fields page opens, displaying your
   template's merge fields.
3. Remove fields that are not from your Salesforce source object. For
   example, if you choose Opportunity as your source, remove fields from
   all other objects:
   1. Select a merge field.
   2. Select Salesforce in the right
      column.

      The field details display the Salesforce
      object.
   3. Select DELETE if the field is not from
      your Salesforce source object. ![A screenshot displays the Contact Full Name field with the Delete button highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-Contact-Full-Name-field-with-the-Delete-button-highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU5NjksInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.rs3Yoez1JfBR_1oIXZBQyz9btwab9b3GC4RQvhEUX1M&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Contact Full Name field with the Delete button highlighted.")
4. Repeat this process for all non-source fields and select
   SAVE AND CLOSE.

   You have completed the process. You have removed all non-source Salesforce fields from
   your template. Return to your workflow and complete your Send
   Documents for Signature step. Docusign
   does not display the This template is not supported
   error.

title: Uninstall the Salesforce Application
heading_path: Uninstall the Salesforce Application
text:
Learn to uninstall the Salesforce app.

The Salesforce app connects your Salesforce organization with your Docusign IAM account. You install and
authorize the Salesforce app from the Docusign App Center. You can
uninstall the Salesforce app if you no longer want to use it with your Docusign account.

Use this procedure to uninstall the Salesforce app:

1. Choose your login method:
   - Login from Salesforce:
     1. [Access the Docusign
        Setup page as a Salesforce administrator](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=etv1586134206174.html&_LANG=enus)
     2. Manage Connections and the IAM Launcher: Select
        Manage.
   - Login from Docusign:
     1. Open the [Production](https://apps.docusign.com/app-center) or [Demo](https://apps-d.docusign.com/app-center) App Center site.
     2. Select the Salesforce tile.
     3. Select Manage and choose Manage
        Connections.
2. Select Manage and choose Uninstall
   App.
3. Uninstall this app?: Enter UNINSTALL in
   the field and select Uninstall.![A screenshot displays the Uninstall this app dialog box with the word UNINSTALL in the field and the Uninstall button highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-Uninstall-this-app-dialog-box-with-the-word-UNINSTALL-in-the-field-and-the-Uninstall-button-highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU5NjQsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.n6DWoq3ix_5DDYxd5jswn0l1BU3M22orFRNMYG5zbuo&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Uninstall this app dialog box with the word UNINSTALL in the field and the Uninstall button highlighted.")

   Docusign removes the Salesforce app. The page
   refreshes.

You have completed the process. You have uninstalled the Salesforce app. Your workflows cannot read from or write to your Salesforce
organization.

title: Create a Connect Configuration
heading_path: Create a Connect Configuration
text:
Connect notifies other applications when a Docusign event you want to know about
occurs. Learn how to create a Connect configuration.

title: Create a Connect Configuration
heading_path: Create a Connect Configuration
Before you begin
text:
- Set up a webhook listener. Review URL to Publish in [Connect Configuration Settings](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=zjq1665170940873.html&_LANG=enus "Docusign Connect sends updates to other applications. Look up the settings that you use to set up Docusign Connect.") for more information.
- Connect must be enabled for the account.
- If you want to follow only selected groups of users, create the groups first.
  See [Add Groups](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=mbg1583277362677.html&_LANG=enus) for details.

Connect sends updates (event messages
or webhooks) about your Docusign activity to other
applications. This topic shows you how to create a Connect configuration so you can kick
off downstream processes in your own systems.

1. From the Accounts view, in the sidebar under INTEGRATIONS,
   select Connect.

   The Connect page displays.
2. At the top left, select Add Configuration > Custom.

   The Add Custom Configuration window
   displays.
3. Enter the settings that you want to use. For more information, see [Connect Configuration Settings](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=zjq1665170940873.html&_LANG=enus "Docusign Connect sends updates to other applications. Look up the settings that you use to set up Docusign Connect.").
4. Select Add Configuration.

The configuration saves. Consider testing the configuration
as a next step. Send Docusign transactions and observe your application's
behavior.

title: Create a Connect Configuration to Add Salesforce Merge Fields to a Workflow Step
heading_path: Create a Connect Configuration to Add Salesforce Merge Fields to a Workflow Step
text: Learn how to create a Connect configuration to add Salesforce merge fields to your workflow's Send Documents for Signature step.

title: Create a Connect Configuration to Add Salesforce Merge Fields to a Workflow Step
heading_path: Create a Connect Configuration to Add Salesforce Merge Fields to a Workflow Step
Before you begin
text:
- Set up a webhook listener. Review URL to Publish in [Connect Configuration Settings](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=zjq1665170940873.html&_LANG=enus "Docusign Connect sends updates to other applications. Look up the settings that you use to set up Docusign Connect.") for more information.
- Enable Connect for your Docusign account.
- Enable Workflow Builder for your Docusign account.

Note: If you want to follow only selected groups of users, create the groups first. See [Add Groups](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=mbg1583277362677.html&_LANG=enus) for details.

Docusign Connect sends updates (event messages or
webhooks) about your Docusign activity to other
applications. Create a Connect configuration to add a Salesforce merge
field in your workflow's Send Documents for Signature step.

The Send Documents for Signature step uses your Connect configuration to access
Salesforce fields from a Docusign envelope template. If you do not
create a Connect configuration, you cannot add a Salesforce field to a Send
Documents for Signature step.

After creating a Connect configuration, you can add a Salesforce merge field to your
workflow's Send Documents for Signature step.

Use this procedure to create a Salesforce Connect configuration in your Docusign account:

1. Log in to your Docusign account as an administrator and go to Admin >  INTEGRATIONS >  Connect.
2. Select Add Configuration and choose Salesforce from the dropdown menu. ![A screenshot displays the Add Configuration dropdown menu with Salesforce highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-Add-Configuration-dropdown-menu-with-Salesforce-highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4MjIsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.GMJX3Ly7HqSv8Ujzzr3y1TB8zmduSGwakg89mO9NSo4&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Add Configuration dropdown menu with Salesforce highlighted.")

   The Connect with Salesforce dialog box opens.
3. Salesforce Environment: Choose an environment to connect to Docusign:

   - Production: Connect to a Salesforce production environment.
   - Test: Connect to a Salesforce sandbox environment.
   - URL: Enter your Salesforce environment's URL.
     - Sample URL for OAuth: https://[your\_salesforce\_instance\_url\_here]/services/oauth2/authorize
     - Sample URL for other:
       https://[your\_salesforce\_instance\_url\_here]/services/Soap/u/39.0
4. Salesforce SOAP Web Services API Version: Select the appropriate API version from the dropdown menu.
5. Salesforce Login Method: Choose a method to connect Docusign and Salesforce:

   Important: When creating your Connect configuration, enter the same Salesforce administrator credentials you used to [install the Salesforce app](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=jxo1706295810553.html&_LANG=enus).

title: Create a Connect Configuration to Add Salesforce Merge Fields to a Workflow Step
heading_path: Create a Connect Configuration to Add Salesforce Merge Fields to a Workflow Step
Before you begin
text:
the same Salesforce administrator credentials you used to [install the Salesforce app](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=jxo1706295810553.html&_LANG=enus).

   - OAuth (Open Authorization) - Recommended:
     1. Select Connect with OAuth. The Salesforce login page displays.
     2. Enter Salesforce administrator credentials and select Log In.
     3. Allow Access?: Select Allow.
   - Salesforce username and password - Less secure:
     1. Salesforce Username: Enter your Salesforce administrator username.
     2. Salesforce Password and Security Token: Enter your Salesforce administrator password and security token. Append your security token to your password. For example, if your password is MyPassword and your security token is XXXXXX, enter MyPasswordXXXXXX.
     3. Select Connect.![A screenshot displays the Connect with Salesforce dialog box with Production, API 54, and OAuth login method selected.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-Connect-with-Salesforce-dialog-box-with-Production-API-54-and-OAuth-login-method-selected.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4MjIsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.GMJX3Ly7HqSv8Ujzzr3y1TB8zmduSGwakg89mO9NSo4&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Connect with Salesforce dialog box with Production, API 54, and OAuth login method selected.")

   The Connect for Salesforce Configuration page displays.
6. Complete the setup of your connect configuration:
   1. System Settings: Choose Active from the Connection dropdown menu.
   2. Sender Selectable Items: Select the checkbox for each Salesforce object that you want senders to access.
   3. [Choose other Connect options like Associated Users, Envelope Events, Recipient Events, and so on](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=vob1727899215236&topicId=ctv1583277395112.html&_LANG=enus)

      ![A screenshot displays the Connection field set to Active and the Sender Selectable Items section highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-Connection-field-set-to-Active-and-the-Sender-Selectable-Items-section-highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4MjIsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.GMJX3Ly7HqSv8Ujzzr3y1TB8zmduSGwakg89mO9NSo4&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Connection field set to Active and the Sender Selectable Items section highlighted.")
7. Select Save.

You have completed the process. You have connected your Salesforce
organization to your Docusign account. You can add Salesforce merge
fields to your workflow's Send Documents for Signature step.

title: Link Multiple Writeback to Salesforce Steps
heading_path: Link Multiple Writeback to Salesforce Steps
text:
Learn to link two or more Writeback to Salesforce workflow steps.

Before you begin
:   - Required: Installation of the Salesforce app
      from the Docusign App Center. Review the [Installing Extension Applications for Workflow Builder](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=bgb1697668738892.html&_LANG=enus) topic for more information.
    - Required: [Workflows Create permissions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gbb1696973048215.html&_LANG=enus) or be an account administrator.
    - Required: Access to a suitable template to use for your workflow
      step. Review the [Template Requirements](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=xnw1709852160602.html&_LANG=enus) topic for more information.

You can use a Writeback to Salesforce step to update Salesforce records
with information from your Docusign workflow. The Writeback to
Salesforce step updates Salesforce fields, creates new records, and more.
Administrators link workflow fields to Salesforce fields in the Writeback to
Salesforce step. When task recipients enter data in workflow fields, Docusign updates the information in the linked Salesforce
fields.

You can configure more than one Writeback to Salesforce step in the same
workflow.

Example: You want data from your workflow to write back to
different fields from Account and Opportunity records. You build a writeback step
linked to the Account object and add one group of fields. You create a second step
linked to the Opportunity object and add fields there. A workflow participant enters
information into a Docusign web form. The participant enters an
Account Number and an Opportunity Amount. Docusign updates the
linked Account and Opportunity fields.

Use this procedure to add multiple Writeback to Salesforce steps to your workflow:

1. [Configure a Writeback to Salesforce
   step](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=pen1706132774147.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to add and configure a Writeback to Salesforce step to update Salesforce records.").
2. Add a Writeback to Salesforce step as a Docusign administrator:
   1. Go to Agreements > Workflows and select Edit next to a workflow
      name.

      The workflow editor page displays.
   2. Select the Add a step
      ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/Workflow_Builder_add_step_plus_sign.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4NjcsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.YwNIwpOLDG8izw7TqZlZeL0Y_MwAHGVALfTIJ3ZK1pg&_LANG=enus) icon.

      The Add New Step dialog box displays.
   3. Search for steps: Enter `Writeback to
      Salesforce` in the search field and select the
      Writeback to Salesforce step that displays.

      The workflow editor page displays with the Writeback to
      Salesforce step added.
3. Name your Writeback to Salesforce step:
   1. Select Configure on the Writeback to Salesforce
      step.

title: Link Multiple Writeback to Salesforce Steps
heading_path: Link Multiple Writeback to Salesforce Steps
text:
The workflow editor page displays with the Writeback to
      Salesforce step added.
3. Name your Writeback to Salesforce step:
   1. Select Configure on the Writeback to Salesforce
      step.

      The configuration dialog box displays.
   2. Step Name: Keep or edit the name. It is helpful
      to add the name of the Salesforce object, like `Account -
      Writeback to Salesforce`.
4. Where will you write to?
   1. Select connection: Select the field and choose a
      Salesforce organization that you connected to Docusign. Multiple options display if you connected more than one Salesforce
      organization.
   2. (Optional) Add New Connection: Select this
      button if you want to [connect more than one Salesforce
      organization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=ogk1719256147008.html&_LANG=enus).
   3. (Optional) Manage Connections: Select this
      button if you want to [add, edit, refresh, or delete a
      Salesforce connection](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=uaw1719259450391.html&_LANG=enus).
   4. Salesforce object: Search for an object or
      choose one from the dropdown menu.
   5. Write settings: Select an option from the list
      that opens:

      - Update or Create: Update a record or
        create a new record if none exists.
      - Update: Update data on an existing
        record.
      - Create: Create a new record.
   6. Select Next.

      ![A screenshot displays a Writeback to Salesforce workflow step with the Connection, Salesforce object, and Write settings highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_Salesforce_Writeback_Where_will_you_write_to_Salesforce_object.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4NjcsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.YwNIwpOLDG8izw7TqZlZeL0Y_MwAHGVALfTIJ3ZK1pg&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a Writeback to Salesforce workflow step with the Connection, Salesforce object, and Write settings highlighted.")
5. Which fields are you writing to?: Select workflow fields to
   map to Salesforce fields. When workflow recipients enter data, Docusign updates Salesforce records.
   1. Select Add or Remove Fields.

      The Add or Remove Fields page displays.
   2. Select the check box next to each Salesforce field that you'd like to
      use as a workflow variable. You'll be able to use these fields in later
      steps.

      ![A screenshot displays the Add or Remove Fields page with two Salesforce fields selected and highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/Workflow_Builder_Writeback_to_Salesforce_Add_or_Remove_Fields.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4NjcsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.YwNIwpOLDG8izw7TqZlZeL0Y_MwAHGVALfTIJ3ZK1pg&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Add or Remove Fields page with two Salesforce fields selected and highlighted.")
   3. Select Save and Exit.

      The Add or Remove Fields dialog box closes. The fields that you
      selected display.
   4. Fields: Add a variable or step for each
      field:

      - Manual entry: Add text for your new
        variable and select Save.

        Note: The name that you enter must exactly
        match an existing field. For example, the rule fails if you
        enter CompanyName for the
        Company\_Name
        variable.
      - Select a field from the start step or another step in this
        workflow:

title: Link Multiple Writeback to Salesforce Steps
heading_path: Link Multiple Writeback to Salesforce Steps
text:
field. For example, the rule fails if you
        enter CompanyName for the
        Company\_Name
        variable.
      - Select a field from the start step or another step in this
        workflow:

        Note: Steps like Read
        from HubSpot and Read from
        Salesforce display fields from up to five
        records. If more than five records are present, Salesforce
        logic decides which five to display. Selecting fields from
        multiple records helps you automate processes like mapping
        multiple Salesforce contacts to multiple Docusign participants.![A screenshot displays Read from Salesforce fields highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_Salesforce_Read_Workflow_field_selections.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4NjcsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.YwNIwpOLDG8izw7TqZlZeL0Y_MwAHGVALfTIJ3ZK1pg&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays Read from Salesforce fields highlighted.")

        You can use the fields that you select here in
        later steps like Collect Data with
        Webforms or Send Documents for
        Signature.
   5. (Optional) Overwrite if field value exists:
      Select this check box if you want the data entered by a workflow
      participant to overwrite existing Salesforce data.
   6. Repeat this process for each variable displayed.
   7. Select Next.

      ![A screenshot displays the Account ID Salesforce field and the Overwrite if field value exists checkbox highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/Workflow_Builder_Writeback_to_Salesforce_Which_fields_are_you_writing_to.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4NjcsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.YwNIwpOLDG8izw7TqZlZeL0Y_MwAHGVALfTIJ3ZK1pg&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Account ID Salesforce field and the Overwrite if field value exists checkbox highlighted.")
6. Which record are you writing to?: Create a rule that
   matches a Salesforce field with an equivalent workflow field. This allows
   Workflow Builder to interact with Salesforce data.
   1. Salesforce field: Select a Salesforce field to
      match with a Docusign field.

      Note: When you select a
      (reference) type field, a new field opens
      below it. Choose a child object from the new field.
   2. Operator: Choose an option like Equal
      to, Less than, and so on.
   3. Workflow field: Select a Docusign field to match with your Salesforce field. The following
      options display:

      - Manual entry: Add text for your new
        variable and select Save.
      - Select a field from the start step or another workflow
        step.
   4. Select Done to save your rule.

      ![A screenshot displays a Salesforce field, an operator value of Greater than or equal to, and a workflow field all highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/qwr1706590641805/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_Salesforce_Writeback_step_Which_record_are_you_writing_to_Rule.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTU4NjcsInNoZWFmIjoicXdyMTcwNjU5MDY0MTgwNSJ9.YwNIwpOLDG8izw7TqZlZeL0Y_MwAHGVALfTIJ3ZK1pg&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a Salesforce field, an operator value of Greater than or equal to, and a workflow field all highlighted.")
7. (Optional) Add more rules:
   - +AND: Workflow Builder only imports Salesforce data that
     matches all of your rules.
   - +OR: Workflow Builder imports Salesforce data that matches
     any of your rules.
8. Select Apply.

   The Writeback to Salesforce dialog box closes. The page displays your new step.

title: Link Multiple Writeback to Salesforce Steps
heading_path: Link Multiple Writeback to Salesforce Steps
text:
your rules.
   - +OR: Workflow Builder imports Salesforce data that matches
     any of your rules.
8. Select Apply.

   The Writeback to Salesforce dialog box closes. The page displays your new step.

You have completed the process. You created and linked two
Writeback to Salesforce steps for related objects. You can repeat this process to map
other objects like Contact and Case. When this step is triggered in a workflow, it
updates records for all objects you mapped. Workflow participants are not required to
enter data for these fields.

title: Connect Configuration Settings
heading_path: Connect Configuration Settings
text:
Docusign Connect sends updates to other applications. Look up the
settings that you use to set up Docusign Connect.

Docusign Connect notifies other applications when a Docusign event you want to know about occurs. You can use Connect to kick off downstream
workflows. This topic describes the settings that are available for custom Connect
configurations. To learn more, see [Create a Connect Configuration](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=xwi1583277389681.html&_LANG=enus "Connect notifies other applications when a Docusign event you want to know about occurs. Learn how to create a Connect configuration.").

title: Connect Configuration Settings
heading_path: Connect Configuration Settings
text:
leId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=xwi1583277389681.html&_LANG=enus "Connect notifies other applications when a Docusign event you want to know about occurs. Learn how to create a Connect configuration.").

| Setting | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Name | A name for the Connect configuration. |
| URL to Publish | The web address of your Connect listener. The URL must use the HTTPS protocol and SSL/TLS connections. It may include query parameters. Connect uses port 443 for HTTPS by default. You can specify port 1443, 2443, 3443, 4443, 5443, 6443, 7443, 8443, or 9443 in the URL instead. Example: https://listener.example.com:1443/listener.aspx The SSL/TLS certificate for your web server must chain to a certificate authority on Microsoft's trust list. Self-signed certificates do not work. You may use free certificates from Let’s Encrypt. |
| Enable Log (Account-Level Connect only) | This option enables logging, which can help you troubleshoot any problems. This setting is on by default. It is automatically enabled for Organization-Level Connect configurations. |
| Require Acknowledgment (Account-Level Connect only) | This option is selected by default. Requires Connect to wait for an acknowledgement from your listener after sending a message. Connect records a successful message transfer when your listener returns an HTTP 200 status code. This functionality is automatically enabled for Organization-Level Connect configurations. |
| Data Format | The Data Format you want to receive. This setting is available only for custom Connect. Important: You cannot change this setting after you save the configuration. The options are:  - REST v2.1: JSON (default) - Legacy: XML |
| Event Message Delivery Mode | If the configuration is for a third-party integration or partner, verify the format to use. Important: You can't change this setting after you save the configuration.  The options are:   - Aggregate: Groups event messages for periodic sending. The   information about the event is from when the system sent the   message. - Send Individual Messages (SIM) (default): Sends individual   messages for each event. The information about the event is   from when the event occurred. This option is only available   for JSON configurations. |
| Trigger Events | The events you want to follow. |
| Include Data | The data you want Connect messages to include. The available options depend on the Data Format you chose. |
| Associated Users/Groups | The users or groups of users you want to follow or exclude.  - All Users (includes new users):   Connect sends updates for all account users.  - Select Users/Groups to include: Connect sends updates for the   account users or groups that you select. - Select Users/Groups to exclude:   Connect does not send updates for the account users or   groups you select. |
| Include HMAC Signature | Generates a Hash-based Message Authentication Code (HMAC) and includes it in the message header. |
| Include OAuth | Enables OAuth for Connect messages to your web server. Enable OAuth for the account first. |
| Include Basic Authentication Header | Enables Basic Authentication for Connect messages to your web server. |
| Enable Mutual TLS | Adds Mutual Transport Layer Security (TLS) authentication and authorization to the configuration. Mutual TLS only initiates when your listener sends a valid Certificate Request message during the TLS handshake protocol. Docusign cannot initiate Mutual TLS because it is the client. When you use it with OAuth, the access token request to your authorization server includes the X.509 certificate. |
